ratings.comment: Easy A class, just do the assignments and show up and you'll be fine. The hardest part is Ackerley himself. I've never seen a professor not care about a group of students until I had him. He gives the most menial assignments and spends most of class rambling about how "cool" and "creative" he is. I learned absolutely nothing this whole semester.

His style of teaching makes learning easy. Prof. Ackerley keeps it interesting and you learn without realizing it. Read  your chapters, do your work, participate both orally and written and you will learn while receiving a good grade. His classes promote good discussions and real life application of subject matter. I would take him again pronto.
Great Teacher. Makes his class fun and interesting. Never boring.Show up and turn in assignments and you will receive  an A! Take him he is great!
Great teacher!Take his class!

He lays it out on day one: you get what you want to out of the class. There is a lot of information given to you and it is up to you to take something away from it. His class layouts are well organized and you know what's expected. Great teacher!
excellent teacher

He is the best professor ever.  He is extremely organized and grades fairly.  I have 2 classes with him and am planning on taking a third class with him next semerster.  He is super entertaining!  He calls class edu-tainment.  Best classes ever!

Professor shows no care, no heart, is rude and dry with emails, and has a false syllabus. Would not round a 79.9%! Would not recommend. Definitely take the other Econ professor, he is said to be an amazing professor!

75% of your grade is the average of 3 exams. Exams are difficult, but they are manageable if you study. Addler is not the best professor lecturing and explaining wise so if you struggle to pick up the material this class will be difficult. Overall, very standard class, just study as much as you can for exams and you will be fine.
Class was easy, but even if it was hard it would've been fine. Explains things really well and engages the call with interactive examples. No complaints, definitely take his class
We played lots of kahoots in class. He made coming to class fun. Course had some hard parts like a lot of graphing but it is very doable.

Tbh ECON 300 was hard. Exams are worth an insane percentage, and you can only get EC if you are on the podium in kahoot. Addler is great in office hours, but so many students come the day before exam days that there's a line down the hallway. Make sure to come early if you need help!
Exceedingly straightforward class. Pay attention(to either the textbook or his lectures) and you'll do just fine.

Professor Addler was the best professor I've had yet. Clear grading in the syllabus. Always in class. Easy to get extra credit. Pay attention to lecture and do homework. If you repeat the homework he has unlimited attempts on to study getting an A on all the tests should he no problem.

Mitch Addler is such a great professor and makes this course very doable. One annoying thing is that he  does not round grades at all which kind of sucks, but theres an optional final to do to makeup for it. He posts all of the lectures which is super helpful, if you follow study guides you'll get A's, and homework is so easy. Would recommend.

Awesome easy class. Pretty much all you need to do is show up and pay attention. Tests are 70% of grade, but insanely easy. Literally got 106% on one of them no studying. Engaging and makes things super easy to understand as well.

Prof. Addler is a good professor. Everything was explained in simple terms or with analogies that made it easier to understand. If you just pay attention in class, you will do fine on the exams. However, they are 75% of your grade, so if you aren't a good test taker, you might want to take this online.

Currently in the Econ 300 class and it is truly frustrating that he does not post the lectures before the classes. He chooses to post them after we finish the chapter, how does that help me?? I have to now take notes on the stuff we learn 3 weeks after we started learning it, it doesn't make sense to me? Nice guy, but should take the critique.

Prof. Addler is a good lecturer, but the lectures/notes are also all online. If you just take the notes & do the homework, it's an easy A. Gave a significant amount of easy EC as well. The class is overall very easy and the one assigned essay is a breeze. The 3 midterms were also super easy if you put the bare minimum amount of effort in studying.

Took his class online and I had a great experience. I watched his lecture videos and took notes and I was good to go for his tests. The videos can be long, but they do a really good job at teaching you everything you need to know. Would also recommend the regular session online as opposed to the 5 week online. Great guy :)

Pretty hard to get anything below a B. Just pay attention to concepts and that's about it. Offers a supplementary final if you didn't do well. if you take notes in class you won't need to open the textbook.

Mitch is really good at the lectures however the tests you take (3 midterms and a final) count for 75% of your grade in the class. The homework's aren't very helpful and the study guides provided DO NOT match the test, so your basically studying for no reason. I haven't taken the final yet but the tests are the WORST. i wouldn't really recommend

His tests are worth 75% of your grade divided between 3 tests. homework is not helpful and neither are the study guides. you could take the most detailed notes and they still wouldn't be helpful when studying for your exams 10/10 would not recommend.

Mitch did a really great job lecturing for this class. If you attend all the classes and take notes, do the easy homeworks and study for the exams you'll do great. I noticed people did a worse job on tests if they skipped class often. Also there are tons of extra credit opportunities.
He lays everything out very simply and clear and helps you prepare for exams.

If you were going to take this class with this professor, i highly recommend trying to get into it when it is online, It makes it a whole lot easier. I took this class 2 times with the same professor and doing it online is the better option.

75% of your grade comes from exams (3 midterms and final), but he drops the lowest exam (Includes the final) so if you're happy with your grade after the 3rd midterm you are done with the class. Attendance isn't "mandatory" but there is participation credit that you get from doing the Kahoots during class (not every class has a Kahoot)

posts lectures weekly and you have two online assignments a week. one major paper and three exams before the final. if you follow his directions in writing youll get 100 on the essay. is very easy to put off.

Adler is fine, only thing that irks me about this class was that the online homework assignments(never take you that long but just pesky) would be on the same days during the week even when there were exams. Coursework wasn't too difficult; though some Econ concepts can be tough to grasp, Adler does his best condensing them down.

I think Prof. Addler is a great guy, he is super nice and keeps lectures pretty engaging. I like his class however,  he goes over 5 chapters of in-depth content then gives a large exam that's closed notes and book. He gives a study guide but pretty much just puts every topic on the study guide. get ready for lots of exam prep if you take his class.

Very entertaining lectures, brought food a couple times. During office hours he was very helpful. Studying the concepts was much easier after more in depth explanation in office hours. Homework was a little difficult for me b/c the inquisitive grading scale. Although, exams were much easier if you fully understood homework.

Nothing against the teacher,  but there is no point in taking Addler's Econ when you can take Jarvis, which is online and much easier due to the tests being open note. Furthermore, there is no paper or final for the online version and is overall much easier than Addler's in person Econ course.

Addler does a good job teaching the class and is very available for office hours. Attendance is not taken every time, but randomly on Kahoots. Manageable workload, just prep for test and you will be fine.

Professor Addler has great lectures that are easy to go back and rewatch. He moves slowly through his lectures and breaks everything down so it's easy to understand. He gives lots of easy extra credit and is great at explaining key concepts. I would absolutely take any class he offers again.

Mitch is such a nice and funny guy. He goes into depth with the material to help you understand and tries his best to answer any questions. Inquizitive homework can get very annoying but there's plenty of Quizlets to help you study.
Sports Econ was a really good elective to take even if youre not into sports, I don't care about sports and still had fun. Addler taught it in a way that helped the Economic principles be understood.

The class is 75% exam based but if you listen to his lectures, do the homework, and read the textbook you won't struggle at all. Also, there are lots of extra credits in this class, he explains economic terms in the most simplest ways and even shows real-life examples, would definitely recommend taking them.
He give us all the lecture and explain as easy as he can! I want to  hear his class again

Solid Professor, he does grade attendance but it is only worth 5% of your final grade. Can skip a large portion of lectures and still get a good grade in the class. Although it's probably why I got a B and not an A. If you just show up to class and do the homework / extra credit and take the exams should be easy A.

Econ200 was a great course, and I would highly recommend taking it with Professor Addler. His lectures were always very engaging, often bringing students on the lecture hall stage to demonstrate economic concepts in fun ways; he also made lots of pop culture references in his slides. Overall a very friendly professor with a great sense of humor.

This class is not too hard if you put in the effort. The homework is very helpful, and everything is structured to get you the highest scores possible. There is lots of extra credit opportunities, and he is overall a very effective lecturer.

He lacks so much empathy as a person. I genuinely think that as much as you may try he is SO rude. I would say the exams are difficult in this course, be sure that you understand the material or you will literally end up failing. I know several people with 89% in this class and he refused to round. I just do not think he is sympathetic whatsoever!!

Addler is a great professor! He makes boring topics interesting in his lectures, but if you miss lectures you'll miss a lot of information. The inquizitive homework is very time-consuming but helpful and you can go for as long as you want until you get a grade you are happy with. Personally, I think the tests are really difficult & the class.
Mitch is a great professor, he uses many examples, and he also posts his lectures on D2L, would definitely take him again.

Course starts out with proofs, which are difficult if you've never been exposed to them, but otherwise it's a breeze. The rest of the course is mostly conceptual with some easy computations thrown in. Lectures are really interesting with lots of real world examples and activities thrown in. Mitch is also super chill and connects with students well.

This class is kind of hard kind of easy. He does a really good job teaching it. Go to all his lectures, despite the exams being open note. The lectures are very helpful and you can ask questions during lectures he won't make you wait to call on you. He goes a little fast so I would recommend voice recording the lecture while you take notes.

Mitch is a super down to earth, relatable yet very sharp professor. The inquisitive assignments and textbook readings are very time consuming, but do carry a lot of useful information outside of class. Lectures are top tier and tests are difficult but doable. Last semester I failed online econ200, but this semester with Mitch I passed with an A.

So glad I took ECON 200 with Mitch! His lectures are engaging and he uses current events to explain the subject matter. His exams are relatively hard, but if you study and go to class you'll definitely get the grade you deserve. He takes attendance through Kahoots, so I would recommend going to class if you want participation points! Easy A :-)

Mitch is a really great professor. His lectures are very interactive and interesting. He explains the material very clearly and he really cares about his students. There are only two midterms and a final, along with homework and participation points. He offers extra credit too! Pretty easy class if you study and come to class.

Great Professor, includes clips from movies and shows to make learning about economics fun. He also does fun competitions during class to explain concepts and gives out prizes and money. Exams are not difficult and homework is manageable. Best professor I have had.
One of my fave econ professors at UA. His lectures are really clear and easy to understand. 10/10 recommend

Very funny and made the content easy to understand. The grades consist of three exams, participation, and weekly homework assignments on InQuizitive. There was technically a required textbook, but I did not read it and still ended with an A in the class. Participation is done via Kahoot! and if you get first place you get an extra credit point.

Easily my favorite professor this semester, lectures are very entertaining through his in-class activities and pop-culture tie-ins (push-up contest, donut-eating contest, paper airplane contest, etc.), and concepts are easy and clearly explained. If you have taken AP Macroeconomics in high school the content is very comparable.

Great and very entertaining teacher, keeps the class engaged. Class is fairly easy once you learn the basics of ECON. Reading due every Monday and two midterms and a cumulative final. One of my favorite classes.

Professor Addler is a good professor. Attendance is crucial to understanding the content. I have had to dedicate a lot of my time into this course to ensure that I'm doing well. Looking back, I probably could've taken this course over the summer to focus all of my energy onto it. It's a worthwhile class, but it requires a lot of work and attention.

Mitch is a cool guy and a solid instructor. He keeps the material interesting when it sometimes isnt. I appreciate his use of free materials for attendance rather than forcing everyone to buy a stupid clicker. The homework format ensures you can get 100% no matter how many questions you get wrong. Exams are considerably easier than homework.

I don't know why he doesn't have 5 stars. The guy explains tough concepts in economics and makes it so everyone can understand it. The homework is extremely helpful and the tests are fair. He also gives extra credit for getting top 3 in the kahoots.

Honestly, I have no Idea how people rate him 5 stars. He doesn't post his power point slides, you have to take notes while he is talking. Never see a professor doesn't post his slides. You just have to read the book. I would rather to take the course online instead of wasting your time with him.
Great course, awesome instructor!!

He's a good lecturer, and he cares about making sure you understand the material. He will answer any question, and he makes it easy to get extra credit. He gave us 5 points extra credit on our first mid term for wearing something Hawaiian that day of exam. I took his summer class, so it was very intense, but he's a good teacher.

This class had super interesting content. Attendance is mandatory, and he cold calls very, very often. A lot of the questions on tests were on random things like small details in cases, and it is very difficult to prepare for them. I would take this if you're very interested in international marketing, but not if it's important to you to get an A.

He did not follow the syllabus, and we would be given large case studies to read and analyze a day or two prior to class. We had to buy each case study, it was not included in the materials we already bought for class. He loves to cold call students. I honestly liked him at first, and started to dread class as it progressed. Take an easier class.

Professor Agarwal is an incredible instructor. It's evident that he likes teaching and wants to be there. He's incredibly knowledgeable, approachable, and caring. His tests can be complex, but he is always open to student suggestions for improving the course. If you take his class seriously, you will learn so much.

Carlos seems hard but he does it to make you learn. He is really approachable once you get to know him. Class is a single group your with the whole time so pick wisely as that will make or break if you move on to the video competition.
Avoid this professor

I have not experienced similar issues in other courses, including advanced and AP-level classes. In this course, expectations were often unclear, communication was limited, and grading felt inconsistent. Greater structure and responsiveness would significantly improve the learning experience.

I've done well in other classes and even AP-level courses, so this wasn't a workload issue. The problem was unclear expectations, little to no support, and inconsistent grading. It was frustrating not knowing what was expected or how to improve.

In my experience, this professor communicates expectations very poorly and often lacks clarity in explanations and feedback. Questions are rarely answered, help feels discouraged, and grading seems biased against written responses. The overall environment feels uncomfortable and unproductive.

In my experience, this professor is extremely unorganized, gives unclear instructions, and is rarely available to help. Emails go unanswered, questions feel dismissed, and interactions often feel uncomfortable. Grading seems inconsistent and makes it hard to know what's expected.

In my experience, this professor is extremely unorganized with constantly unclear instructions. Expectations change without warning, and grading feels inconsistent and arbitrary. It often seems like your grade depends more on the day than on the work itself.
Does not give clear instructions. Unorganized

Worst teacher I have ever had in my 4 years here. Spent hours trying my best to follow all instructions, yet still been receiving 0%'s (4.0 gpa). This professor barely speaks proper English and cannot give clear instructions. He also never responds to emails, and it is nearly impossible to get hold of him to try to talk about your grade.

Very disorganized and complicated d2l page. Unnecessarily strict grading. Gives 0% if 5 minutes past deadlines and 0% if he assumes it is Ai based on his own judgement.

Seems like he is knowledgeable and experienced but expects students to put in 10+ hours per week into a basic online course that could be drastically simplified.

I have never had a more biased and difficult professor in my entire academic career. He wants to be technology heavy and friendly, yet allows for no phones or laptops in class. He is a horrible grader, it is entirely dependent on his mood. He went out of his way to humiliate our group in front of the class multiple times.

Even though this is a mandatory class to graduate from Eller he grades as if you are on Shark Tank which is stupid because no one cares enough to try that hard. Its a 2 and a half hour class where you can't use any electronics. His ego is massive and he needs to be brought back to reality that his class is our last priority.
One of the kindest professors. He expects quality but also deeply cares about learning.
Alsua is the best, real life applied innovation.
He manages to make difficult topics easy to understand in a practical way. Tip: Go with the flow, and active participation matters!
This is a fully online course. Yet Carlos feels “present.” Lots of announcements to keep you updated. So many announcements can be at times confusing, but overall a great class.
Good class. I took it as an elective and it was worth the 3 credits.

He will not let you text or use your phone in class. If that bothers you, or you use class time to chat with your friends or do online shopping, skip this class. If you want to learn about being innovative. This is the course for you.
The best. He knows his stuff. Not easy, but worthwhile.
Bs class

One of the worst teachers in Eller. Can be summed up in his first line "This is a d2l heavy course, no laptops". D2L is our online platform, we barely touched it. Dates are never correct on the syllabus and never announced in class, nothing in class is used, 20% of each grade in the rubric was how he was feeling that day, heavy disorganization, etc
You get out of this course what you put into it. I learned the basics of entrepreneurship in a deep meaningful way. Excellent course.
Alsua is the best. Excellent professor and an even better mentor. He is demanding, but in all the right ways. You work and you learn. The best value for my time this year and  for most of my career.
Alsua will make you work for your grade, but if you follow the steps and put in some work, you will not only succeed in this class. You will also succeed at understanding how innovators work.
A remarkable mix of an entrepreneur with real-world experience and a solid academic.
A very experienced professor. Awesome lecture!
You DO have to work in this class, but you will be learning from the best.
The best!
Stern yet incredibly caring. Very knowledgeable.
Awesome!
Dr. Alsua is one of the best professors I have had. He is demanding, but I have learned more about business and entrepreneurship in this class that in any other class.
This professor goes above and beyond for his student and is very understanding highly recommend!
Lots of work, but I have learned more about entrepreneurship from this class than from any other in the program.
Finally someone who knows how the business world actually works, and who will not put me through endless lectures!

Dr. Alsua has a keen entrepreneurial mind. He is well travelled, well rounded and very informed about the real world out there. I didn't want to take this class at first, but now I know it will help me quite a bit further along in my career

Professor Alsua isn't a bad a professor at all its just the class can be long when its from 3:30pm to 5:30pm. There are plenty of important textbook readings with assignments to do and the group assignments which are most of the grade aren't bad. He's a pretty easy grader so no need to stress too much about it.

I feel like accounting can't be taught properly in lectures, definitely should be average class sizes. You can tell she does try when teaching. Exams have answer choices that appear when you do the wrong or not enough of the equations which messed me up as this was not the case in accounting 200. I would study for hours and it did not help on exams

The tests make up most of your grade. As a professor, she's fine. The lectures were incredibly boring and honestly not very helpful, which is frustrating when attendance is randomly taken. You'll learn more from the homework than from anything you'll do in class, so I'd recommend just taking the L on the attendance points and save your time.
She talks too fast and does not really care if the material is understood. Seems like she is there because she has to be.

Heather has been my favorite professor so far. She truly cares about her students and makes ACCT 210 much more manageable. The class is tough, so I recommend studying ahead and practicing regularly. Lectures are posted online, but even though only five attendance days are required, I suggest going as often as possible.
So chill. easy content and lots of EC
Professor Altman is one of the best professors for Accounting, just took it this Fall 2024 semester, she indeed is the best!

Unfortunately for the students, she does not seem to care about whether or not we actually learn and comprehend the course material. She seems to be one of those teachers that just clocks in and out of work/lectures and just goes through the motions.

Prof Altman is a very average professor. You won't become an accounting expert after this course, but you will know what's needed for the exams, which are in person, but not extremely difficult. Prepare to take notes, or cram if you're that kind of guy. Attendance is needed twice a week, and the location was rough, but it's manageable.
Very straightforward class and professor, homework was not hard and exams are fairly easy. Just show up to lecture and you will do fine.

Heather did a great job explaining the concepts and applying real life scenarios to her lectures. She always had a positive attitude when teaching and provides tons of resources and wants you to succeed. As long as you pay attention, study and put the extra time in you will be good. She offers some extra credit at the end too. Take her class!

Altman puts effort in her lectures but I wouldn't consider her good. Due to the large amount of content she would skim over core conceptual to get to the calculations faster. She has great lecture videos, but the other teachers post the same ones on their D2L. Do yourself a favor and take the other ACCT 210 teachers (Katie or Alison)

Managerial accounting is hard. Given that, Heather does really good making sure we can power through it. She is a very kind person who deeply cares about hher class but since the material is extremely hard it may seem like shes a bad teacher. The lectures are long but the material is also long so it equates.

Unfortunately, the classroom is very very very crowded, which can make it hard to pay full attention during lectures. 
Additionally, I've noticed that the course relies heavily on tests to see how much we understand, and I find the tests to be quite difficult.

Heather is a very sweet and caring person, she just wasn't my favorite professor. She clearly cares and puts a lot of effort into her lectures, but it doesn't translate well. She goes very fast and doesn't teach what's mentioned in slides or textbook. Lectures confused me so I ended up relying on myself to teach the concepts to do well on tests.

Professor Altman was a wonderful accounting teacher and is very knowledgeable in the content she covers. I found her classes to be very slow, which made it more difficult to focus, but she taught her content well and was great at answering questions. Tests will be a HUGE part of your grade, the extra practices are incredibly helpful for studying.

Professor Altman is a nice person, and she cares for her students to do well. I had an easier time in ACCT200 paying attention during lectures because of the teaching style however if you apply time to practice outside of class, you will do fine. Accounting is different for everyone though. Some people have to study for days and some not at all.

personally, she talked very quickly during the lecture and I got spoiled with an extremely small accounting 200 class, so it was hard to adjust. She is very kind during office hours and offers detailed answers that help you understand why you were wrong. Make sure you take detailed notes during the class and pay attention to specifics.

Heather is caring for the most part. The course format is almost identical to ACCT210. Lectures are monotone but she tries her best, and you cannot expect great excitement out of basic accounting. Study the slides and you'll be fine for tests. Overall, decent and easy course for what it is

Like someone else said, exams are like 70% of your grade and they are all taken with honorlock. Be prepared to do a lot of studying. Class average for most of our exams were 61%-70%. She offered 5 points EC if we took the student class surgery at the end of the course

Altman is honestly a great teacher for how hard managerial accounting is. You need to put in work outside of the class to do well & ACTUALLY pay attention in class, but she explains everything well. Exam & formula heavy, but if you study it's straightforward. Only complaint is she talks a little quiet, but other than that she's great & I recommend!

Exams are the only assignments that count for anything in this course.Exams make up over 70% of your grade. All other assignments count for almost nothing. During our very last exam she offered 1 point of extra credit if we did the course surveys, I'm assuming because most of the class was nearly failing.

Professor Altman is amazing! She is very experienced and passionate about the material. She gives you everything you need to succeed in this class. Accounting is difficult but if you show up to lectures and study for exams you should be just fine.

If you like accounting and numbers, this class will be easy. However, the teacher is incredibly boring and spends 30 minutes at a time talking about things that could be taught in 5 minutes. Useless class as well.

I learned nothing in her accounting class, nothing. Exams make up 76% of your grade in this class, while homework, discussion posts, and other assignments make up 24% of your grade. You're doomed if you don't pass at least one exam well. She offered little assistance and had zero flexibility. In this class, honor lock is in effect, just a heads up

Accounting 210 is a difficult course to teach. But Professor Altman flew through the lectures to the point where it felt like she needed to get through so many slides so we could stay on course. My classmates and I ended up having to teach ourselves most of the material. If you out in a lot of extra work, you can succeed in this class.
she kinda just talks at you during her lectures, not to you. she barely explains and moves very fast when the class time is super long. the worst class I've ever had

Altman was an amazing professor. Everyone knows ACCT 210 is hard, so anyone who wrote a bad review likely got a bad grade in the class and blamed it on Altman. If you attend class, do your homework, and study hard, you will do well in the class. Also, give yourself around 4 solid nights to study and watch the review videos!!! it helped me a lot!

You have to take acct210 and it's difficult. Everyone knows that. But she's the best professor you can pick. Explains things well, knows her shit, fast with email replies, respectful. You have to study each lesson and prepare for each exam. Attendance is random on D2L so NO need to buy those clickers. People who hate probably didn't study for exams

The worst professor I've had in all my years of schooling. That's not an exaggeration. She falsely accused me of HACKING/cheating on the zoom room for our final exam, failed me from ACCT210, and kicked me out of Eller. After I explained to her that I didn't cheat and was planning to get a lawyer to clear my name she took back what she said and did.

A hard class made even harder by mindlessly boring lectures, but I guess that's the nature of accounting. Heather isn't a bad teacher and knows the material, but nothing is really done to make it palatable. It's a put your head down and grind class. Be ready for the tests because they count for most of your grade. HW/quizzes aren't too bad.

Accounting is an incredibly difficult class. There is so much information and the tests are closed note making them very difficult. every other aspect is not bad -homework, quizzes, etc. but the tests are just awful.

She's great as a person but she seems like she's reading from a script. There's no change in tone. I get that Accounting is numbers abd concepts, but even when she's talking about real life examples, it's the same tone. Every sort of content is relayed like that so you don't know what content you're taking in. I had to rely on the textbook.

She will talk for an hour and a half and you won't understand anything because you'll fall asleep. In line with the school's accounting department, just plan to completely teach yourself everything. Unfortunately attendance is mandatory. Definitely try to take another teacher that doesn't take attendance. Class is definitely not easy.

I took Heather for 210 and she is one of the all around best professors I have had. Not only does she explain the concepts exceptionally well, but she is very sweet and it is clear that she cares about her students.
She sucks. do not take. its like a difficult accounting class on steroids, gives lots of quizzes and work. Quiz averages are extremely low, and shes so boring. I give her a 0/10.

After transitioning to online she included quizzes which were hard and gave us very little time for them! Her exams always averaged to a D yet on the next ones she wouldn't make it easier or give us more time on it. Honestly, this class is a headache! I was 2% away from a B and she offered little extra credit.

She sucks, it seems as if the class got harder after the whole COVID-19 stuff. The quiz averages for the whole class was like 60% which is not even passing, and her lectures are the most boring thing on this earth. not engaging whatsoever, and her exams are very difficult. If u can skip this professor do so.

WORST TEACHER EVER! DONT TAKE HER!!!! I RECIEVED AN 89.5 IN THE CLASS AND SHE DIDN'T ROUND ME UP! COMPLETELY PATHETIC TEACHER, BORING, AND NOT ENGAGING AT ALL!!!!!!!! DO NOT TAKE! I REPEAT DO NOT TAKE

Pre- Covid-19: boring, wrote on paper notes in lecture, lacked explanations, voice is unbearable, hard tests, tedious hw. Now, mid-Covid: VERY difficult tests, ZERO empathy, HARD quizzes, not enough time to do lengthy problems, written completely different than how material was taught, cut NO slack despite the pandemic. she doesn't care about u!!!

Heather is really awesome and super nice. While her class is challenging, going into her office hours helps. She's willing to work with confusing topics with you and help you figure out ways to understand the content better. ACCT 210 is a little bit harder than 200 because of the content, but she tries new things to make the class easier.
lectures are painfully boring and it's difficult to retain any of the information with the way she teaches it.

In-class discussions are completely pointless. Hard to follow but you're required to attend for necessary attendance points. I never learned much from listening to her in class and would have to teach myself all of the chapters for the tests. When the online structure hit she made it much harder for students. After students were failing, she curved

When the class was in person her lectures were extremely difficult to pay attention to. It basically would consist of watching her take notes on a printed out powerpoint off of an Elmo projector on the front of the classroom. When everything got shifted online it basically has been the same thing but with an added level of difficult (added quizzes)
Not understanding whatsoever. Made class way harder after things went online for some reason. Horrible professor all-around

Professor Altman has one of the most inefficient teaching methods. I have not learned a single thing from this course. I even earned an A in ACCT 200 with a MUCH lower rated professor. I teach myself everything in this course. Not to mention, shes the only professor who made class/exams more difficult after COVID19 rather than lighten the load.

She's one of the best teacher in the accounting department, but it is a really difficult class. The class was much more doable before COVID, but the transition online wasn't the best and she did add more quizzes. She's great during office hours and very open w/ emailing you. She added a 6 pt curve to the exams to help. I would take her again.

Ms. Altman's lectures though very hard to sit through were not terrible but when switching to online due to COVID-19 she decided to change the entire course structure. This new structure making the tests even harder and even adding quizzes. It appears to me and many peers the class is no longer about learning and about her giving us a letter grade.

Heather is probably the worst accounting teacher at the UofA. In-class lectures are long, boring, and unenthusiastic. It's currently 2020, and she's still using a projector from 1945 to present a lack of material to the class. The tests are unbearably hard as she doesn't know how to actually teach the class. Overall an awful course.

I have been a student in her class in person and online because of COVID-19. In-person, lectures were extremely long and lacked energy. It was second to second of her talking. Now that all classes are online, she has decided to make the class even harder adding quizzes that you have no time for. It's very frustrating and would not recommend her.

I dislike her so much her way of teaching is so difficult she would always confuse me with her lectures. Literally one of the worst teachers I've ever had, I'm pretty good at accounting and I still found this class super difficult due to the way she taught. If you are thinking of taking her please SKIP. She sucks. I basically taught myself.

As a woman taking her course, she truly inspired me. Not many professors seem like real humans, but I love that she shares about her daughter. She also explains what she has done with accounting, she is very credible. She is so nice and not intimidating. She just wants respect and her kids to succeed! Best accounting teacher at u of a, hands down.
Very nice. Knowledgeable about the subject matter. Tries to break it down into easily understandable formulas. The formulas are all very similar & it can be very confusing.

She is such a great professor for this subject. She goes into detail and actually goes step by step to make sure you understand the information. I would HIGHLY recommend this professor. She makes in-depth study guides, all you have to do is put an effort into this class to make sure you learn the material and you'll do well!

Overall, Heather gives very clear explanations of the material in lecture. The study guides, homework, and **lab assignments (highly recommend!) prepare you well for the tests. However, lectures are a little rough due to their length and her expectation for participation. It just gets awkward. Also, she takes clicker attendance very often.

Great teacher, recommend to do the accounting labs for extra credit. There is a lot of material to cover for this course so it will be lecture heavy so use the labs to synthesize each chapter. She is always in a good mood and will always give you a lot of in class problems.
Heather was one of the best professors I have had. She really cares about her students and makes the material very easy to understand. The class is difficult but she makes it so much easier

Altman is the best choice for 210! She makes you work for your grade as she goes through slides and material quick. You have to stay on top of the course and attend office hours/ lab to fully grasp material. Second test is by far the hardest but others are manageable. She cares about her students and give plenty of opportunities for help!
Test heavy class. She is a good teacher, just goes very fast in class during notes.
Very hard class but heather is probably the best 210 prof at uofa. She is super nice and gets things done on time. You gotta work in this class.

Being completely honest, you have to haul ass to pass this class. But it makes you feel great when you do. Mrs. Altman opened my eyes to accounting and personally I love it so much! She tries her best to get you to understand, and does not grade easily but honestly it makes you work harder. Recommend her for ACC 200/210 or 250. She's awesome!
I love heather, I never understood ACCT200 and in ACCT210 I am doing so amazing!! Definitely try and get her as your prof.

Heather is a great prof. but this is a tough class. She goes through the material very quickly, leaving it up to you to really grasp the material by putting in the time outside of class. However, she makes herself and the preceptors very accessible outside of class. You'll have to work for your gra, but Heather makes this class a little bit easier.

She's a very good professor. She teaches a very difficult class but that has nothing to do with her personal teaching style, Accounting is just difficult in general. She is willing to work with you outside of class to further explain concepts and she is extremely nice about it as well. Just be sure to actually read the textbook chapters, it helps

Hands down worst professor I have ever had at the University. She is boring and does not know how to teach well. It sucks that she was the only ACCT 210 professor because I did not have an option to learn from a good professor.

I did not enjoy having Heather teach Accounting 210. She zips through the class to cram as much material in as possible and doesn't leave much room for questions. Tests are difficult. There isn't too much homework and most of it is fairly easy. She is a kind person but I would not recommend her as a professor.

Heather Altman is one of my favorite professors at the U of A. She knows her material well and is great at explaining it to her students. This class is not an easy A, although an A is attainable if you are willing to work for it. Go to class to fill in slide notes, and there is an average amount of homework.

This was not an easy class but Heather is a great professor and cares about her students. Participation matters in the end when it comes down to your grade. She takes random participation points w/ clickers during the lecture. If you miss class a lot you are going to lose some points. Tests are challenging so much sure you study & ask questions

Heather is the best! Accounting is really hard, but Heather makes it easier to understand. She breaks questions down, does them in different ways, shows easier ways. Really a good, caring instructor. Go to office hours if you don't understand something, she will spend time helping you! Really cares about her students! 10/10 would recommend.

Altman is a great teacher! She goes over the material thoroughly and gives a lot of opportunities to do well in the course. If you ask her a question she will always answer it and lead you to the correct thinking!

This is another class that sucks but is mandatory. Super difficult and extremely boring, but Heather really cares about you as a person and it is obvious that she is a sweetheart. Aside from that, the class is absolutely dreadful and makes no sense. Super math heavy. Just pass it so you can get in to Eller!
ACCT210 is a more difficult course, but Prof. Altman is a great teacher. She cares about her students and is really helpful in office hours.

ACCT210 is a hard class but Heather tries to make lecture clear. Her handwriting is hard to read so make sure you listen when she speaks. Exams are tough but the guidelines are not clear as to what to study so read the book. She will not stray form the syllabus and it's "rules"

Highly recommend reading the book. She is a wonderful person, don't be scared to go into office hours. However, her lectures are boring and hard to keep your focus on. Try your best to pay attention in class because lectures do help and it's hard to teach yourself everything a couple days before the test.

Good professor, seems kind and reasonable in class. There are clicker questions about once a week that you must attend class to get points for and random attendance points. The tests determine a big portion of your grade so make sure to study. She seems really knowledgeable and passionate about accounting which makes the class more enjoyable.

Heather is gifted at teaching. You can tell that she truly cares about her students performance and she is always willing to answer any questions. She handles her lectures in a very organized way. Tests can be tough because it is accounting but if you put in the work you will do fine!

Heather was by far one of my favorite teachers/professors of all time. She made me actually enjoy accounting which was a class I dreaded taking. She made the material easy and extremely understandable and I am now majoring in accounting because of her. The way she teaches makes everything make sense and it feels like your actually learning.

Heather is a great instructor for a fairly difficult subject! She understands that it is hard, so she works to make sure everyone understands! I didn't take enough advantage of the resources available - office hours, lab, TA office hours, tutoring. Really, with all of the available help you can do it! And Heather is great!

Professor Altman is among the best teachers here at the U of A. She is so sweet and knows the material she is teaching and it shows. 2 tests and a final, homework about twice a week. Show up to class to fill in the powerpoint notes (very helpful) / attendance taken at random. I've decided to major in ACCT after taking this course with Altman!

I took Altman for my first accounting class and she is very very smart in her field! Definitely makes it simple to understand and will answer questions that you may have! She really seems to care, the class may seem hard but she will help you to get the grade you can

The teacher is fine but the class is HARD. You need to teach yourself and practice as you go and a lot. You have to practice, go into office hours, and just make sure you get it as you are going and don't wait till before the test. Attendance is usually whenever there are clicker questions, but there is random clicker question attendance as well.

Heather is one of the best professors I have had at the UofA. She is extremely clear in grading criteria and content. A very well-organized class. The material is a little dull but Heather makes concepts clear. Take her for ACCT210 if you are able!! Never used textbook, just get the wiley code and online textbook!

Good and clear lecturer. She is passionate about the material, but moves through it a little slow. Class is not overly difficult, nothing but  formulas to remember, but the exams are difficult if you let the material get away from you.
Good teacher class in general is super boring.

Heather is the nicest professor I've had while at UA, she is very clear on what is expected to receive an A in this class. However if you want a good grade you have to study for the tests as they are over half of your course grade. She is also super helpful in office hours
Very easy class and Ms. Altman gives very clear explanations.

Heather is a great instructor! Lectures are easy to follow. Mini pre-lecture quizzes every week and chapter activities you do alone or in a group at the end of class (quizzes and activities are done at home before bringing into class). Exams are over half your grade, so give yourself time to study. Attendance taken at random via your clicker.

Heather is by far one of the best professors I have ever had. Beyond being approachable and sweet, she explains the material in the most helpful way possible and is clear about expectations. Assignmentsquizzes make attendance importance but her lectures are enjoyable and go by fast. She provides practice tests, very similar to exams. Love her!

Heather is one of the best teachers I've had, and she is so adorable.  Accounting is a tough class, but she makes it very easy to understand.  She goes over every assignment in class so you understand it and is always willing to answer questions. The tests are difficult but definitely manageable if you study and go over the assignments beforehand.

Sure this class is a lot of work; there is something to turn in most every day. Even though Heather's lectures don't exactly make Acct more interesting, they are SO clear in explaining the material (way better than the textbook). You'll need the book for the HW though. The practice exams she gives are super helpful. Would recommend her

amazing professor, she gives a practice exam that gives you a really good idea what the exam are going to be like. class is kinda mandatory, she gives clicker quizzes once a week and group activities once a week but they are easy points. TA is awesome too. favorite class!

Feedback was vague and inconsistent. Emails and questions were ignored. I felt punished for being concise and rewarded for verbosity. The class discouraged reflection and focused on repetition. Expectations were unclear, and engaging learning methods were lacking. I spent more time guessing than actually learning.

This class has clearly not been updated or even looked at for years. The only communication we get from the professor is clarification of something because the assignments are unclear, the readings are worthless, and the grading is seemingly random. I have received the exact same feedback and grade for the past three assignments. A complete waste.

I guess he's given up on teaching. This course is a bunch of random articles to read, a few dead discussion boards, and grading with no real feedback/care. Unfortunately, it seems that most students are confused by the readings and the assignments. Lectures are essentially a waste of time with no worthwhile material to incorporate into summaries.

Very difficult class as there is little lectures or reading assigned and the 4-5 papers a week don't align at all with the assigned readings.  As a result, grading is a very mixed bag as even if you use the readings and follow the rubric it's not clear what's expected.  Good luck!

The class did not even reflect the title of the class. Professor Benson never talked about the textbooks, instead every lecture was a chit-chat about current sports and the athletes. Only 1 assignment and 2 exams so there's a lot of pressure to get good grades on those.

You should never take this class, I cannot stress enough. Most boring lectures, the 2 exams are worth 70% of your total grade. No homework so you have no idea what you are going to be tested on. His entire lecture he never actually talks about anything useful. If you take this class, enjoy studying 297 slides for the midterm exam!!!!

If you want to take a class that takes three weeks for a grade... SIGN RIGHT UP. One of the laziest grading professors I ever had. I will make sure to avoid all of his classes as he puts fourth zero effort in communication or grading.
A fun class with a fun teacher, would recommend to anyone thinking about taking him.

"Showman" is the perfect word to describe Lehman Benson. He will assign a significant amount of writing assignments but clearly never reads them and will respond with canned feedback. His class content has little value and I am frustrated with the fact that the University of Arizona allows for him to continue instructing as such.

Dr. Benson was a great professor who really cares about his students. He gives a lot of extra credit if you give good input during a class discussion. 50% of the class is based on tests that are on his lecture slides as well as from reading the textbook, the negotiations aren't on the tests. Negotiators are easy if you read the packet he gives you.

Professor Lehman is quite the showman. His delivery is comical but often strays away from the course content. He rarely covers the necessary content because the class is often led down rabbit holes that are unrelated to the topic. His quizzes are extremely frustrating, but if you write a paper, don't worry... he does not read them.
One of my favorite professors thus far. He is caring and so funny. He really knows what he is teaching and uses lots of current real world examples for better understanding.

He's good at what he does, as many have already said. He's friendly, approachable, and knows the material. The only "unfortunate" thing I noticed is that he doesn't realize his own worth. His in-easiness as a professor is noticeable. Mind you, it doesn't inhibit his ability or performance, but you can feel it lingering just under the surface.
Awesome guy. Tough but fair, really enjoys what he is teaching. Good sense of humor and super easy to talk to. Tests are tough but all info is in the book. Go to class because you will learn a lot
Likable teacher and keeps you interested in class. READ the textbook and you'll be fine.
Benson is the man. Hes funny makes boring material interesting. One of my favorite teachers and if you study you will do fine on tests.

Awesome professor. You can tell he is into what he teaches and he makes it enjoyable for the students as well. Even though the classes are long, he keeps them interesting. Tests were not too hard, as long as you go to class.
Awesome guy, really nice. Tests are a bit hard but curves them big.

He's probably the nicest, most approachable teacher I've ever had. And he's funny. You can tell he likes teaching & genuinely cares about his students. Exams can be difficult but he explains things pretty thoroughly in class so pay attention.
Just like everyone else said, He is a likeable teacher. Very nice guy, and easy enough class with some effort. Seems kinda nervous all the time.
Good teacher
He is nice and cares about students.
He is an extremely nice guy, just not the greatest of teachers.  You can tell teaching isn't the highest on his priority list.  Seemed to slack a bit while teaching.
great guy, funny, approachable, knows his subject (although sometimes stops saying something in fear of "crossing the line"). very intelligent and highly recommended for any class!
No Comments
No Comments
TA's are doing the teaching, this guy is totally unprepared. Just read the book during lecture and you will do a thousand times better! He is a joke.

If you pay attention and read the material you'll get a good grade. But he's very figgity and does not explain the material that well at all. In fact, half the time it seemed like he was looking over the material for the first time.
He is very unhelpful
Benson is the man. Only teacher in first semester COHORT that even tried to help me.
No Comments
Nice guys but ineffective as a teac
Nice guys who is obviously passionate about Game Theory

Do not take this class with professor Blume. Most of the time it seemed like he didn't know the material he was "teaching". There is lots of calculus in this class that is never covered. Going to office hours was almost as pointless as going to class. Exams are short but difficult because the material is never taught.

Games and Decisions is a class disguised as a game theory class, but is actually a calculus class with a very small side of game theory. Professor Blume is unable to teach either of the subjects. His TA is also unaware of how to teach the subject matter. The class is very disorganized and students are basically on their own.
He does lots of interactive games in class and prepared us well for the exams.

The class was very interesting, but Professor Blume has a terrible way of communicating the important topics you need to know to pass the class. I spent hours going through the textbook to try to decipher what exactly he was trying to say in any given lecture. I recommend taking game theory with another professor if possible.
Lectures were hard to understand and he got stuck often.  Not really a helpful professor in office hours either. Unfortunately unavoidable.

Dr. B is kind, very intelligent, and most interactive/effective professors I have ever had. She wants her students to do well and provides everything in order for them to excel. Her lectures are to the point and concise, allowing for easy review and studying of the material. The cases help reinforce the knowledge presented in the class.

She is extremely passionate about finance and has a great sense of style btw! She is always in a good mood and willing to help. BUT class is really tough and her exams are definitely way harder than expected. A lot of note taking and clicker questions for attendance.
Dr. Bonaime is the best professor at Eller! Don't hesitate to take classes with her, she will make sure you understand everything in her class!!
Really nice teacher, extremely knowledgeable in finance. Tests are extremely tough and not very similar to her notes or study material provided. Do not skip class.
SHE IS THE BEST!!!

LOVE Bonaime. She is hands down one of the best professors I have had at the U of A. She is always willing to help and chat with you if you put in the effort. Its obvious that she is very knowledgeable in her subject and what you learn in her class really sticks with you. She doesn't use a textbook, just class notes that I think ill keep forever.
Not easy to get A at Professor Bonaime's class!
I love Professor Bonaime!
She is excellent and amazing!
Very nice teacher!
She is an awesome professor!

Professor Bonaime is my favorite teacher in U of A. She is very good at teaching and helping students. To be honest, Fin412 is not a easy class, you can't skip class or any homework. Anyway, you will learn a lot from her class!

Great teacher, really passionate about the subject, and she is able to break it down so its understandable. I must say that the final I took about 2 hours ago was extremely difficult, and there was no reason to make it that hard.

This class doesnt appear difficult at first but you HAVE to go to class. The in class notes are not posted online and you get points off after 1 missed class. She told us the final would be conceptual heavy but then proceeded to make half of the test questions math-based problems.
Good professor

The lecture material is very similar in content and density to material learned during first semester of Eller or in undergraduate classes. Still, Laura is a cool professor, and has some great examples. Expect to make two group case presentations and a final in-class case slide deck with your team at the end of the semester.

Laura is an amazing professor who is extremely nice and very informative. While the quizzes in the class are kind of annoying and there is a midterm thats all open ended questions, she still is a very fun professor to speak to and the class as a whole isn't bad. 9 Quizzes, a midterm, 2 assignments, a final project and participation really isn't bad

Laura is the best! She's young and cool, really knowledgeable about the course and great at teaching it. Her lectures are actually interesting because she uses real world examples and stories and participation and discussion are huge. This is basically one of those classes where no answer is wrong, you just need to justify it. Projects are fun too!

One of the best business professors I've taken so far. She is very open with grading, assignments are not that difficult, and will round up your grades without having to ask. Super helpful for getting into Eller and other classes, would love to have her for more classes.

Dr. B is honestly amazing. It's an easy class if you simply focus during the first 2–3 weeks, since the writing structure stays consistent throughout. She's fun and really tries to make the class enjoyable. If you attend class and visit her office hours before assignments are due, there's no reason not to enjoy this course and also get an A.
Grading is very slow. Attendance is all of your grade.
Dr. B is the best. She is so sweet caring and flexible. Go to all of her classes and it is an easy A in the course.

This course showed me how to write business professionally and this teacher made it very clear and to the point. The workload is not a lot and if you just go to class you will easily get an A. Great teacher!

Dr. B is AMAZING!! If you have the chance to take her class, DO IT. You will learn, you will pass, and you will feel appreciated and cared for. I have no bad words for Dr. B, this class is my favorite class from all I have had in a long time. Definitely recommend Dr. B!!

Dr. B was absolutely incredible and so much fun in class! She was always chill and willing to work with students, she truly wants to see everyone succeed. She's great at teaching the content in a way that everyone can grasp and makes it fun to engage with. If you have to take this class go with Dr. B you won't regret it!!

Definitely take Dr. B if you have the chance!! I was considering taking the online section, but Dr. B's in person class was so fun and engaging. Attendance is technically optional but you do get points for showing up (APs). Every assignment is simple and she gives time to work on it in class. I wish she taught upper division classes!!!

This might've been the easiest course I've taken in college. Dr. Brodersen is incredibly friendly and caring. The lessons are very straightforward with what she wants you to know and I completed most of the assignments in 10 minutes max. She gives good feedback and will let you revise anything. I came to class just to talk to her, she's so nice.

Cheryl is the nicest teacher that I have had at this school. She is extremely caring for her students and rewards her students for coming to class. She is super funny and wants her students to succeed!

Cheryl is the best! Such a genuine heart for her students and she is hilarious as well. If you go to class you will do well. She is a fair grader and tells you exactly what she wants to see in your writing. The classes are clear and structured, 100% recommend this professor.

Professor Brodersen will change your life! Take the Happiness Asset for your seminar...it's 3 honors credits instead of only 1! Fun, introspective assignments. Class discussions about the weekly book chapter. Small class size with class collaboration. She is so understanding, accommodating, and supportive!

She is one of the best professors I have had at UofA so far. She genuinely cares and teaches you how to be successful in life with the material she teaches rather than for a grade. She doesn't really ever give anything lower than an 80 on anything and was so entertaining that going to class was fun and worth it.

Dr.Broderson really cares about her students. She will do everything she can to teach you how to be good at communicating. Gives great feedback and great grading criteria. Really wants to see you succeed. She is a great person to work with. Highly recommend taking her for your BCOM class.

Dr. B was the best professor I have had in college. Not only is she a good teacher, she is so nice and understanding if you need an extension due to life difficulties. She is clear and concise in her lectures and is more than happy to work with you through assignments. She is helpful in office hours and wants her students to succeed.

I LOVE DR. B. She is so funny and is literally the sweetest teacher ever. She's very open to communication and questions. Also very understandable if you miss class for a personal reason. Best professor I've had at the UA by far. If you ask her to look over your homework before you turn it in or "fishbowl" it, you get an A. Would def take again.

Professor Brod is my favorite. So easygoing and knows we are college students. Always cracks jokes in class, grades very fair, and accommodates valid excuses and problems that pop up during the year. TAKE HER CLASS!

Dr. B is a great professor! She cracks jokes and is extremely easygoing. You get a lot of time to work on assignments, and she is an easy grader. While attendance is required, she is a very understanding professor and will accept your absence sometimes without question. Deductions for lateness are also hardly applied.

She is a great person, but no so much of a professor. She barely takes time to go over the material and then gives out C's for no good reason. We will do examples of homework in class and she tells us this is what we should do and then takes off points for doing it. The class content itself is cake. Losing my 4.0 to this mad woman.

Cheryl is a gem! She gives you a lot of time to work on assignments in class. Her assignment instructions are super clear, and she goes over examples so it's easy to get a good grade as long as you try. Attendance isn't really mandatory but you'll do much better if you go to class. Ends class early sometimes and doesn't make us go on Fridays.

Cheryl was an amazing professor. She really cares not only about the grade you are receiving but if you understand the material. She did a great job adapting to zoom and cared about how we were adjusting. All around good person:)

Cheryl is a great teacher. Lectures are clear and explain the homework assignment for the week. She has classes were she edits students homework so the class knows what an A looks like, but is also super helpful with it in office hours. Theres one group project, but overall easy class
She's a very great professor. She gets off-topic at times but she's very caring and funny. She's there for you if you need the extra support. I would 100% recommend her class.
Cheryl is hilarious and caring, BCOM is an easy course and she makes it worth going to the lectures
Great Teacher! little points in the class so it is easy to get a B if you don't try.
You have to take BCOM so when you do try and get Cheryl. She is one of the best BCOM teachers and actually wants you to succeed. She will give you feedback and make class fun.

She cares a little about class, but over spent more time talking about herself, family and her MBA students she teaches than ever actually explaining anything. Do whatever you can do to not buy the book, as the school and the BCOM teachers have collusion going that force you to purchase materials written by the professor. Super unethical.

Not a good professor who always talk about some other things that are not related to the class materials in class. She is a hard grader. No matter how well you write your paper, she will not give you more than 80.

BCOM214 sucks but you have to take it, so take Cheryl, she's the only professor who is aware of the issues in the course and cares enough to help students who get screwed. There is a disconnect between what is taught vs grade expectations but she will work with you. D2L lecture videos are absolutely torturous to sit through - you have been warned

Tons of quizzes - she is awesome though. makes class basic and easy. The stuff you learn is cool, but super easy. She is sympathetic, will help where and when she can. She's super laid back and easy to deal with. She's the best professor affiliated with the bcom content, all the rest I hear horror stories about.

Professor seems like such a great person, but there seems to be a bit of a fundamental disconnect in what's taught, vs. what is supposed to be learned. She's definitely a great person for feedback, but better instruction is a necessity.

The BCOM 214 Program at U of A needs some serious reworking, but Cheryl is one of the better options for who to take. She's willing to work with her students while also giving you ample opportunities to really get good feedback (if you go talk to her). She often lets out class early and has funny stories she's willing to share. Definately take her!

Was one of the most useful classes I have taken at the UofA since I want to work in financial analytics. Lecture videos are online and then you get time in class to work on the assignments. Grading is very clear and is based on completion, you also get extra credit for turning assignments in early and small deductions for each day it's late.
three words. WORST PROFESSOR EVER
This teacher does not want you to get an A in his class. He knows a lot but DOES NOT KNOW how to teach. Sets unrealistic expectations and takes points off for tiny mistakes. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS

Hard and a lot of work. David is extremely intelligent. He does the most to drill concepts into students through assignments and resources he provides. If you attend class and work through the models, you will learn. Group projects are heavily weighted, choose your team wisely. Pop quizzes are a slap in the face. Learned the most in this class.

This class was the best structured most useful course I took while at UA. Extremely valuable for any job in which you will use excel. David is extremely responsive, knowledgeable, and supportive of your career. You will get exposure to excellent job opportunities through this course.

Yes, this class is hard if you don't follow through, and put in the extra effort needed. If you only want this class because it doesn't have tests, then don't take it. Prof. Brown is a really good professor, and really cares about students. It is very assignment and project heavy, but very rewarding. He won't turn his back on any of his students.

Worst professor and worst class I have EVER taken. No one in the class really knows what’s happening and he just throws stuff at you to figure out. If you ask questions, he will make you feel stupid. Please if you see David teaching a class, just do not take it and save yourself unnecessary stress.

DO NOT TAKE ANY OF DAVID’S CLASSES PLEASE. Unless science somehow allows us to get the ability to read minds you are destined to fail. He took off points on all my stuff for not having things he never mentioned. Truly enjoys seeing people suffer. His class ruins me week.

He definitely enjoys ruining GPAs just because you can’t read his mind. He will make sure to make sure you fail in his class and will go out of his way to be rude to you. Truly one of the worst professors at Eller. Sad how he doesn’t have the mental capabilities to understand we are students. He literally ruined my spring break.

Worst class i have ever taken. He really expects everyone to have a phd and know finance as well as he does. Doesn’t understand anything about how people learn. Overall he’s a typical rich yt man who has no understanding of the world around him. Worst eller professor.

Honestly an awful professor. He will grade you and take points off on things like not having a bar graph or random stuff that he never mentioned. Unless you are a mind reader dont take any of David’s classes. You won’t know what he’s grading on until he takes a ton of points off. Also will make you feel like an idiot all the time. Hate him!

Definitely agree with all the other ratings. He also has a phd in finance, but clearly not in science. Constantly complains about masks even though he got COVID. Smart guy when it comes to finance. Not so much when it comes to understanding science.

Believe me, I thought the other reviews were wrong but they were definitely correct. He makes the class unbearably hard. He talks like we all are phd students and know everything there is to know about finance. He has quizzes where you barely have enough time to do anything and really is no help at all. Get any other professor.

He makes the class hard for no reason. You literally have to gamble for your grade and risk it. Eller really is hiring anyone now. He clearly wants you to fail and will put forth all his effort into making sure you do not succeed.

The videos posted are completely useless in helping you solve the assignments. I found myself having to look everything up online instead of relying on the videos. They seemed to have changed them from direct walkthroughs to now Mr. Brown puts up videos which have little to zero correlation with the homework assignments. Nice guy but useless prof.

The time threshold on the assignments are completely ridiculous. You need to copy and paste all your answers to meet the time limits and even then there are assignments that this was difficult. A one credit class but took just as much time as a 3 credit one. An "easy" A in the sense grading is binary but this class needs to be worth more credits.

First time they implemented FIN360L with just excel assignments and exams. Most difficult course i'ver even taken in college. Exams are impossible and timed and the homework don't prepare you for the exams whatsoever.

David is a good guy; however, the class is way too difficult if logic is not your strong suit. The class is too fast-paced and the practice tests don't necessarily prepare you for the tests. There are too many group assignments so the free-rider problem is real.

While David is a good guy his class is very difficult and too fast-paced. It is hard to figure out his assignments despite studying the assignments. The lectures are too group-heavy focused, not very helpful and indeed the group exercises expose the free-rider problem. If logical is not your strong suit, I recommend not taking this class.
Tough but rewarding.

Alright, probably the second time actually writing something here in RateMyProfessor. Why? Cuz this is class is insane. Chose this class and hoped to get some advanced skills about EXCEL. I guess I can't say this class is not useful, but the way he constructed it was just making everyone exhausted and bored.

Where to begin. David is a decent guy but his class was the hardest class I've ever taken in college. I spent HOURS working on assignments only to get nowhere. His office hours are not helpful, he makes you feel dumb for asking to clarify his instructions on his assignments, and he assumes that everyone has worked extensively in Excel.

Materials tough but she makes it somewhat easier, I had a stellar group so that made it a better. Pay attention and you'll learn the material you need but don't slack off because the material just cumulates over the semester so you'll need to know stuff from the beginning for it to make sense at the end. Class is hard, but she's a good teacher.
She is the most caring and helpful teacher you will ever have.

She is a very thorough instructor and seems to really care about whether or not the material makes sense to students. However, she is so perky first thing in the morning that sometimes it's hard to stomach. Of course- most people probably like that.
the best class ever!!!!

This is one of the hardest classes EVER. We do as much work as expected by a graduate student & the tests/debates/journal are hard too. Yet, this class has also taught me the most useful information since I've been in UofA. The class will be hard, but if you can get through it, its totally useful to further your career after college.

Dr. Brucks was astounding, maybe the best teacher I ever had in 6 years of college.  She came up with brilliantly concise, relevant examples of the material off the top of her head.  Always infinitely prepared and available.  Sharp as a tack, engaging and fun.  Highly recommend her and the courses she teaches.

You need to understand stakeholder incentives and the impact of accounting entries. Be an independent learner, take good in-class notes, and grasp what's taught in class. Practice problems until you master them independently—this is excellent exam prep, alongside reviewing his slides. The research HWs and Excel project are easy points. Do them.

This class is very similar to Katie Maxwell's 400A class in terms of difficulty, so if you did well in that class you should be just fine in James' class. The exams are hard to study for bc there are not many practice problems, and the exams are mostly conceptual written response. James is very kind and funnt so his lectures are tolerable.

Not a lot of practice homework or examples, which made exams difficult. Exams are a lot of "why" instead of "how", and mostly written response. You'll need to study the impacts of accounting decisions on stakeholders a LOT. Go to every class and you should be fine. Cool guy, but I would definitely have preferred more practice.

I had a good experience in this class because professor Z taught it. You can tell that he cares for and respects his students. Even though the class requires participation & presentations, you're never made to feel bad if you give a wrong answer. Z makes it clear he's here to help you and provides great feedback.
basically a review of high school calculus with a different book that was a way too expensive. quizzes were easy. really it was a nonstress class if you just stayed on top of things.

Makes Calc II tolerable, not the best class but not the worst. Spent alot of time on vectors and it got old fast but good explainer. See him after class because he doesn't check him emails much it seems.

Shannon is an incredible lecturer. Her class is challenging, she is understanding, and genuinely wants you to succeed. She's extremely helpful during office hours and always makes sure you understand. The exams are tough, this has been my hardest class so far even with hours of studying. She is the sweetest and enjoys teaching this class.
Super nice but the tests were very difficult and didn't feel prepared for them

Chen is a wonderful professor and person, and her lectures are always engaging and interesting. However, the content can be challenging with how many rules, exceptions, and exceptions to exceptions there are. By far the most challenging course I've taken, but also very light coursework-wise (4 projects, 3 exams, and pre- & post-class quizzes).

This class was difficult but Shannon made it very engaging. Lots of new material and pre and post class quizzes- i got 100 on all of them so definitely do able. Go to office hours and ask questions it's where I learned most of my stuff. You get a note card for the exam.  Tax returns are easy she lets you compare with students in office hours.

Acct 420 is a tricky class with a lot of moving parts. However, Professor Chen is so nice and passionate about taxes that I often felt engaged during each lecture. Overall enjoyable class even if I didn't do very well on the really hard exams.

Shannon is literally the best! There are a LOT of rules. I would suggest studying early for exams each unit, as there will be a lot to remember for each test. Very fair exams and projects, and she made understanding and applying the content very simple. So helpful. Study how she solves the problems in class (& do optional HW!). Very enjoyable class

Download her slides before class and take notes during lectures to focus on how she approaches problems. I don't recommend studying the textbook or taking notes without her slides. Do practice problems and study the concepts in her slides, as it's a rule-based class. Homework is optional, and there are many extra credit opportunities. She is kind.
Professor Chen is great and even with her gentle voice, she maintained respect from her students. She made tax fun and is very helpful during her office hours.

Shannon is a great professor and is a large part of why her class was my favorite of the semester. Although the content can be challenging at times, she goes through it very clearly and is always willing to help, especially with quality-of-life things like recording each lecture and allowing you to go to a different.
My favorite class during my first semester at Eller. Shannon's lectures are never boring, so it's always fun to goto class. There's also a ton of extra credit. Be excited to learn tax with her!

Shannon makes taxation fun and easy to learn. The format also gave a lot of flexibility. Class has pre-lecture videos and lecture is spent reviewing quizzes and highlights from pre-lecture. Professor Chen is also easy to contact and very helpful in office hours.

Shannon was a lovely professor! I think she is relatively new but I really enjoyed going to her lectures every week, she made it seem like she was happy to be there too. The content is not any worse than your other accounting courses, and with the right study habits and getting fine grades on quizzes you'll be fine. Lots of extra credit too!

Never seen a professor get so much respect from her students. Some students gave her flowers on our final exam day, which shows how much she cares for the class and her students. Challenging subject, but definitely worth the time :) 10/10

Shannon is an incredible lecturer. Her class is challenging, she is understanding, and genuinely wants you to succeed.
