She's extremely helpful during office hours and always makes sure you understand. The exams are tough, this has been my hardest class so far even with hours of studying. She is the sweetest and enjoys teaching this class.
Super nice but the tests were very difficult and didn't feel prepared for them

However, the content can be challenging with how many rules, exceptions, and exceptions to exceptions there are. Very fair exams and projects, and she made understanding and applying the content very simple. So helpful. Study how she solves the problems in class (& do optional HW!). Very enjoyable class

Never seen a professor get so much respect from her students. Some students gave her flowers on our final exam day, which shows how much she cares for the class and her students. Challenging subject, but definitely worth the time :) 10/10

Mei Cheng is the best accounting teacher at UA.  She makes things SO easy to understand, and her class is COMPLETELY fair.  If you want an A, you have to work really hard, but you get a cheat sheet for the exams!  She always puts her students first and answers questions all day every day.  She completely engages her students and is so kind.

Very thick accent which makes understanding accounting that much more fun. Says she gives partial credit which is completely untrue. The material makes sense to her but she rarely communicates it in an effective manner. Also makes a lot of mistakes while lecturing indicating even she does not understand financial statement analysis.
He might be a super difficult grader but, it is because he cares he genuinely gives the best feedback that I have ever had. I truly feel like I became an amazing presenter.
Absolutely great professor. The point of harsh grading is to watch you improve. Perfection comes from his feedback!

Mikel is the best professor I've ever had. He truly cares about each student, and his teaching stood out during case comp where everything he taught helped us shine. BCOM 314 can be intimidating, but he made the environment supportive and connected. He genuinely wants you to grow as a individual and if you apply his feedback, you will succeed.

At first, I had mixed feelings about Mikel when I started his class. I remember thinking it was going to be a long semester, and it definitely was, but looking back, he was the best person to help me grow as a presenter, especially for the case competition.
You want to get poor grades after over performing, go ahead.

Mikel is an amazing professor. I would take his class again if I could! He cares about your success and gives you valuable feedback on your presentations. If you care to grow, he will help you get there. Make sure to go to office hours/set up meetings with him because he will help you and it'll get you closer to the A.

Mikel by far is the best professor I have had at the UofA. He really cares about you and wants you to succeed. This class could be tough and frustrating. However the work you put in will show what you get out of this class. Mikel is very accessible, make sure to go to office hours if you want an A in the course. By far one of the best professors!!!

I've genuinely never had a teacher who loves his job and cares about his students as much as Mikel. Mikel is definitely the hardest grader for BCOM, but if you put in the work you will succeed. This class is a time commitment but feels so rewarding after every presentation.
Extremely tough grader on the presentations.
mikel is the goat

This teacher was one of my favorites! I felt like he cared about our success and was cheering us on! He expects your best but is willing to help you get there. Really enjoyed this class and Mr Chertudi as a teacher

Mikel was great at sharing deadlines, setting his expectations and grading criteria for the class, and giving in-depth presentation feedback. He is a great presenter himself, setting a great example for the rest of us. He helped all of us in the class bond better. Bonus points for the Crumbl cookies he brought us at the end of the semester.

I found him to be a fantastic professor. He gives very insightful and clear feedback, and is visibly dedicated to coaching his students to be the best they can be. His grading is tough, but there is a clear rubric backing it. If you read the assignment directions carefully and make sure to utilize his feedback, you'll do well in the class.

He acts nice and like he cares but really doesn't. He plays god with your grades and if you don't "exceed expectations", which is impossible. I showed up to every group meeting and put in an ample amount of effort and got 90% for participation. If you are stuck with him for BCOM, I am sorry for you. I would try my hardest to switch teachers.

Ignore the bad reviews he was the best teacher I have ever had and this is my 3rd year at U of A. He goes out of his way outside of class to individualize his teaching for those who need it and made it so that any student that wanted to succeed could. Very friendly teacher, and very eager to help anyone who wants it!

Ignore the bad reviews this teacher is possibly the best teacher I've had so far in my 3 years at U of A. He truly cares about each students success and will go to great lengths outside of class to ensure that every student who wants to succeed can.

I had the pleasure of being with Mikel in the Spring of 2023. This class was very group-heavy. As in if one group member did not show up on presentation day your grade will get slammed. Also, there is a ton of work that Mikel pushes you to do great. I had good laughs with him he is a great guy but he expects excellence out of his students.

Mikel is by far the best BCOM teacher, however others will disagree because he is such a difficult grader. He really pushes you to do well and if you respond to feedback you'll get an A. His students are far better presenters than the other teachers' which really showed during case comp. Was a much more enjoyable class than I expected.

Sure, here's the updated version:

Professor Chertudi seems friendly initially, but grades harshly and craves attention. He is not biased, but sucking up affects grading. He deducts 2% for each missed class, making it the worst attendance policy. I don't recommend his class. Look for another professor He tries to make students feel dumb for his ego

Professor Chertudi is one of the best professors I have ever had at UofA. He deeply cares for his students and growth throughout the semester and sets the class up where students can always ask for help and assistance. If you put in the work and engage and ask questions, you can do so well in his class. I highly recommend him as a professor.
This man's class is an absolute nightmare. I have never struggled so much in a class and he seriously made me think about switching majors.

Mikel is so kind, has a great personality, and truly cares about you perfecting your writing craft. He is quite a tough grader, I couldn't attend office hours but set up one-on-ones with him and he gave me great feedback and works with your schedule to help you. I have never met a professor who gives such quality feedback on EVERY assignment.
Highly recommend switching to a different professor. I had a horrible experience with mikel and would not wish the same for anyone else.

Mikel is one of the best professors I have had at the U of A. BCOM is a hard class and you definitely will need to work hard to finish with a good grade. Mikel gives good individual feedback and truly wants his students to succeed. I am so glad I got him as my professor for this class. BCOM has a lot of real world application as well.

I genuinely can't imagine going through BCOM with a different professor other than Mikel. He was always willing to help us grow both personally and professionally. He gives you as much feedback as you need and you can tell he really cares for his students. He is extremely approachable. I am so so glad I got Mikel as my professor!!!
Mikel is condescending, rude, and totally unqualified to be teaching at the university level. He graded based how much you kiss up and thinks he is way more important than he actually is.

BCOM is an intense class, but Mikel does a great job at motivating students to grow as professionals, which made the class that much more rewarding. If you truly put the work in, you will get an A. It's a grind, but totally doable. Mikel really cares about his students and does everything he can to help them succeed.
do not let these other reviews trick you! this is the worst class I have ever taken with BY FAR the worst professor I have EVER had.

One of the best BCOM Professor. Highly recommend to be in his class if you can. 
Use him the right way, be respected in his class, and you will obtain the right outcome
Challenging class but great professor. He truly cares about his students and you will do well if you are willing to work and have a growth mindset.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH THIS PROFESSOR!
Grades based on how much you kiss up and not the work you submit. pretty sure he just reads the name and assigns a grade based on that

Still fairly confused with his approach to teaching this class. He tells you how great your presentation is to your face then turns around and kill your grade. Its honestly really embarrassing that he cant even be honest to students about their grades and has to hide behind a computer to be critical.
Not a good experience. Wouldnt recommend.

The professor is extremely biased. Really not sure how hes qualified to teach this course. Quite possibly the worst class I have taken at the university. The fact this is a required Eller course is mind boggling.

Overall awful class. Professor is unorganized and scatter brained. Class is run inefficiently and grading criteria is just as unorganized. I would avoid at all costs if you dont want to be stressed out all semester.

I was really looking forward to this class but was disappointed. The content was there but lectures were unorganized and confusing. The grading criteria was also quite unclear. Overall, not my favorite.

Mikel is an amazing teacher and one of the bests I have ever had. He is a hard grader, but he truly cares about the success of each of his students. He is extremely kind and tries to bond with his students. I have always been scared of public speaking, but by the end of his class, I have never felt more confident. Loved this class so much!

Mikel is an outstanding professor. He will always go the extra mile for his student's success. Although this is a difficult course, if you put in the time and effort, you will be successful. Take advantage of his workshops, feedback, team mentors, etc. Expectations are always clear! He is energetic and enthusiastic. You can tell Mikel truly cares.

Mikel was an awesome teacher! I love how lively and interesting he makes the class. You can really tell that he cares a lot about his students and thoroughly grades each assignment. To be successful, meet with him before every presentation and make sure you have a ton of questions prepared! He is sorta a tough grader but gives you more at the end.

If you have BCOM with Mikel you are in luck! He makes himself very available and will go out of his way to meet individually or with your team outside of class time. He genuinely cares about his student's success and gives great feedback and support to help you get there. BCOM is hard but Mikel makes it better, truly the best professor I've had yet
Mikel is the best BCOM teacher at UA. He genuinely cares about his students and goes the extra mile for those who reach out to him. Best teacher I've had at the university by far

Mikel was an outstanding professor. BCOM is going to be hard no matter who you take it with, but Mikel is extremely understanding about the workload. He's very available outside class, gives thorough feedback to help you grow, and does his best to support your team. You only need one group member to have the book to reference a few times.

It doesn't get any better for BCOM than Mikel. He's tough but fair and you learn a lot more than you realize at first. If you are able to find the time, rehearse with your preceptor before your first presentation because trust me you are a worse presenter than you think and they will give you important feedback.

Chertudi cares a lot about his students and is very dedicated to helping them become better presenters. He relates to his students and improves everyones confidence. Every 314 class starts out difficult because you aren't used to all the presentations but by the end of the year presenting is super easy.

Three things for you homies going into BCOM 314R. 1) This class is what you make of it. You can learn a lot if you work hard, and Mikel does an amazing job of inspiring his students to work hard. 2) Mikel wants to help you learn. If you go into his office hours on a weekly basis, you will find the class MUCH easier! 3) MIKEL IS THE BEST 314 TEACHER

Takes forever to grade things. A tough grader, but that's true for any of the BCOM teachers. An 85% is 'meets expectations.' IT IS POSSIBLE TO GET AN A. Work hard, communicate with your group, and USE HIS HELP. Team got several A's on projects bc we used our preceptor and his feedback.

Mikel is amazing. He is a tough grader (norm for BCOM), but gives lots of feedback and is always willing to help. Makes you reach your full potential and is supportive. Makes lecture interesting. Used text once, attendance is mandatory (allows one absence) but he is lenient for good reasons. Will be his preceptor, excited to work with him again!
Professor Chertudi is an amazing gem of a human being!
One of the few professors that I have had that actually cares about your progress. A tough grader, but is willing to help you as much as you need. Overall, a great professor.

it is beyond belief this guy still has a job teaching something he is not an expert. It's an insult to other instructors to rate him as such. He was more a facilitator and didn't teach much of anything. He was terrible at time management, hardly discussed assigned readings, and  never was able to get thru his lecture slides.

Mikel was incredibly helpful in this course. The boot camp writing assignments really prepared me to write more comfortably in a business environment. Mikel is always open to helping any student, so long as the student reaches out for help. The only negative about this class is he takes forever to grade and respond to emails.
An absolutely incredible teacher with a class that is incredibly useful in real life, but its literally impossible to get n A. Sorry 4.0 students, this is your where you'll get your first B.

Mikel seems like a good teacher all year but takes forever to grade assignments. The biggest problem is that no one in the class could manage an A on any paper regardless of how much time they put into it. Then he posts final grades a week after the semester and completely burns you for any chance at an A. Pretty sure he just grades off of names.

He seems like a good teacher but refuses to give A's on any paper. I question the grading of the course. Takes forever to post grades. At the end of the semester he chooses your participation grade and conveniently enough the students he likes happen to get just the points to get an A regardless of them coming to class??? I wouldn't trust him.
Great teacher, really cares about his students and tries hard.

Pathetic.  Most people in this class were more qualified to teach this class than this terrible professor.  I actually think I am worse writer after taking this class.  The professor does one thing very well, contradicts himself.

He is a horrible teacher. All he does is respond to students he is afraid of and ignore those that question the terrible teaching he attempts to do. The grading is also arbitrarily based on those same student dynamics and not the rubric he gives. You should avoid any class with this bad teacher and U of A should fire him.

This was the hardest class I've taken in Eller. Pair that with a teacher who writes test that are purposefully tough and has an iffy attitude, and you've got yourself a doozy. Preeti is often condescending, and prepare to study 8+ hours for each midterm. If possible, take this one with another professor. Ridiculous.

1. Prof. Choudhary is a kind and experienced woman. she can be a bit hard to follow.
2. If you show up to class, study a few days before the exams, attend office hours as needed, and have half a brain, you'll achieve a B(+). Not sure if finance is for you, otherwise. 
3. 50% was curved to a 78% on the final. She gives plenty of partial credit.

The content is already difficult but she makes it more difficult Exams cover about 30% of the practice exam, homework, and notes. A cheat sheet is allowed but not helpful since you base it on the practice exam. She is often rude and gets frustrated if you ask for clarification more than once. If possible, take this course with another professor.

Preeti is unfair when it comes to attendance, with certain days netting more attendance days than others, like making days on the week of school breaks more points which is completely unfair for out of state students. Exams are very difficult and she moves very fast. She is clearly an expert, but it was a negative experience as a student overall.
Overall a horrible experience. Preeti is dismissive and rude a majority of the time. Tests are far harder than in class content. If you are able to avoid her, absolutely do it.

This was the worst class in the entire MBA program. The quizzes were proctored and often had nothing to do with what we actually studied.
She gave out extra credit assignments throughout the course, to make up for how brutal the final was.
Overall, it felt like the course was built for freshmen, not for working professionals in an MBA program

She knows her acct knowledge fs, but that doesn't mean she knows how to teach. Class has a lot of content so you do move fast, but her notes are all over the place and hard to learn from. Like any acct course def requires lots of attention. She does have favorites and is sassy towards some students. Raise your hand in class, has extra points.

Hopefully she reads this, as she is a very caring and kind professor - her tests are just horrible.

She gives students a studyguide that had literally (seriously) zero questions on the tests. She counteracts this by giving you a cheatsheet, but since you think the studyguide will be like the test, you base your cheatsheet on it, making it useless.

I took Dr. Choudhary's financial accounting course as part of a MBA program. The lectures well well organized and clear. The grading was quite fair.  She was prompt and responsive to questions. There were some Harvard Business School simulations etc which were not that helpful. Overall, the course met its objectives and teacher was effective.

Preeti is a very kind person but a really bad professor. She has eight weeks to teach of a great amount of accounting and does not take this as an opportunity to teach broad concepts and general theory. Instead, she goes into unnecessary depth, makes quizzes purposefully hard, makes us pay out of pocket for an AI to grade our projects.

Prof. Choudhary's class was painful. Deadlines and grading expectations were unclear and inconsistent. Her lectures were confusing, often contained mistakes, and did little to clarify complex topics. Grades weren't posted before the final, adding stress. Communication was terse, and the workload unmanageable. Choose another professor if possible.

I don't usually write these, but I don't feel these reviews are fair. Content accounting majors cover in two semester is squished into this single-semester course. We learn a lot very quickly. Choudary will give you everything you need to succeed, but you have to really prioritize this course. Take advantage of homework sessions, do all EC, & STUDY

Class is always hard to follow. She makes tons of mistakes on her slides and clicker questions. The exams are unreasonably difficult and very different from the questions we do in class. This class made me hate accounting.

Choudhary is... not good. The upside is that she seems to hate all of her students equally, which eases the pain of being treated with a sort of detached contempt each time you ask her a question. No advice but to weather the storm if you're in one of her classes, it can only get better.

I have never written a review for a professor, I felt it was necessary for Professor Choudhary. I loved accounting until her class. I understood the material so well but would fail tests because of the obscure questions. The homework website is AWFUL and not user friendly at all. It was far more confusing than helpful. DO NOT take her class!

Dont take ACCT 400C with Prof. Choudhary if all your ACCT courses have been online. Was her first time teaching the class, she definitely knows her stuff, but when it comes to teaching 2 years worth of content its hell. Used to have a 4.0 GPA, offered tons of extra credit, that didnt help. Resources dont help. Doesnt consider student feedback.

This class was so difficult and the exams were nothing like what you learn in class. This class was already extremely difficult, but I dont feel like I was actually taught anything of value. Try to get another professor if you want to keep your GPA up.

She lives up to her negative reviews. All around bad teacher who brings an attitude and gives very hard exams. Exam content is way harder than anything discussed in class. She was the only option available but absolutely take someone else if you can.

Poor teacher. Slides and lectures do not correlate at all to what you are tested on. Test material is significantly more difficult than homework or in class work. She does not explain things well and has an attitude when you ask questions. Would not recommend at all.
Terrible teacher. Has no idea what her material is or how to help her students. She hinders her students learning and does not consider student feedback.

Dr. Choudhary does an excellent job at ensuring the entire class understand the material. Even though some students did not like the fact that she actively calls on students to answer questions, I did appreciate this as it means there are not just a few students carrying both the in class discussions and the group projects.

What made the class most difficult was that she had errors in just about every other slide in EACH of her chapter ppts. I'm the type of person who needs to teach myself from the slides at home, and she made that pretty much impossible. Every class she gives clicker questions on uncovered material & its graded for correctness. I could say more...

If you have the chance to take this course with another professor, by all means take it. The course work was difficult with so much content to digest in a short period of time; however, she made the course more difficult with ridiculous clicker quizzes, little extra credit, and test that is ridiculous.
Professor consistently made mistakes on lecture slides, homework assignments, etc. Became easily frustrated and did not seem to have an effective teaching style. Many in the class received a C.

He's funny, and lectures can be enjoyable, but the class is extremely disorganized. Assignments appear with almost no notice and are very difficult with harsh grading. The website is chaotic, and weekly quizzes are announced only hours before class. Overall, the class is stressful, disorganized, and requires a lot of time.

Unfortunately, I took this class despite the warnings. If you value your time, avoid it. Professor Currium is extremely disorganized, grades were rarely posted, and the grading is harsh. With frequent quizzes and unclear lectures, I often left class feeling like my time was wasted. Please choose any other professor.
Assignments are released with minimal notice, a ton of work, and extremely tight deadlines. This professor is not recommended if you have a job or obligations outside of his class.

The worst professor I had the displeasure of being forced to take a class with. He very clearly set up the class to maximize stress, making sure you do way more work than a 3 credit class needs. Posts reviews and announcements extremely late and during weekends, expecting us to give his class more then 5 hours a day. Unfortunately 1/5 is too high.

Hands down, the best professor! His course is difficult for sure, and his exams and homework are pretty intense. Initially, I felt the course was unnecessarily more difficult than it has to be. But when I was preparing for job interviews, I truly valued what I had learned through his coursework. His material and teachings made me interview ready.

Dr C is very smart and can teach you anything but he will sometimes forget things. He would forget to put assignments on d2l until the day of even though he told us days beforehand he already did. He also showed up to office hours late. Very funny sense of humor, kinda quirky I like it! This class contains a lot of group projects fyi

Overall Currim was an eh teacher. Just prepare to move fast because he flies through the lectures and often we were left behind. Everything builds on top of each other so once you'd miss a few things you'd all of a sudden be a couple chapters behind. It may be because of him teaching the Graduate Program that he believes that we can keep up.

His class is "organized chaos" (only way i can describe it). Assignments sometimes not in the gradebook so you can't accurately know your grade but the class and assignments are mostly fairly easy. I got an A on both exams, and he gave us candy after each exam was graded so that was cool. Also >20m late to any online meeting so ymmv with OH.

Very poor guidelines for homework. His quizzes are clearly intended to "be tricky" despite his frequent claims to the opposite. He is a tough grader who loves busy work, and his lectures were disorganized. I got an A no problem in this class, but he was an unnecessarily difficult professor to work with.

My goodness this class is so complicated. Everything is so poorly organized and quizzes are so difficult. For someone who has no prior coding experience this is a nightmare. Don't bother with this class please. If you are an expert in programming you can do well in this class. Otherwise do not waste your time and enjoy your life please.
this class was so bad, i genuinely had more fun in ACCT210 and im a MIS major

Made the class harder than it needed to be. The class is flipped, so you only spend one day in class each week. When you're in class, there's an expectation that students already learned the information. The professor would ramble about irrelevant things in class, making the learning environment challenging. Exam questions were also worded weird.

Absolutely the worst class I have taken in my life. For a higher division class at Eller, this was completely unacceptable. Poorly structured, lectures cover nothing for exams. Doesn't teach. Very condescending. He messed up case comp grades and only changed them after the entire class complained when it was entirely his fault inputting them.

Currim is the worst professor I have had in college. His exams don't line up at with his class material and he is proud our class average was a D. I have gone in for help and all he did was make me feel dumb, left crying from this guy. He can't take any responsibility for his exams being poorly written or the fact that half of us are failing.
I asked him for help, and he gave me with a very generic response. He basically ignored all I had to say and just replied by giving me sarcasm.

Worst professor I have had while at Eller. He is a nice man, but an incredibly lazy professor. He uses class time only to answer questions. Nothing is actually taught in class. Additionally, the tests are all about memorizing facts and not using any intuitive skills. I'm sad my tuition money was used to pay this man's salary.

I have never written a review on here but I had to because he makes the test a huge part of your grade and the class average is usually around a D. He barely even teaches you the materail and just goes over group assignments.

He's a nice guy, but his class is one of the worst classes I have taken while at Eller. The material has little real world application and he drones on in lectures while rarely switching slides. He teaches niche concepts that I will probably not use much in the future.

Faiz is probably the most unengaging professor I have had in Eller so far. I am an MIS major and find no value in taking this course. One time he spent 45 mins on the learning objectives alone, thankfully lectures are pre-recorded. His exams are basically a gamble of getting a lucky draw from his 200+ question pool, and he has no exam reviews.

Very niche and specific homework assignments that do not teach actual transferrable skills for software applications such as Microsoft Access, Excel, and Power BI. His in-class lectures lack any sort of engagement. I have never been so disappointed in an upper-division class, it feels as if nothing that is taught will be used within a career.

This was a fun class and gave me lots of experience with new software. He was very active in the "ask the professor" discussion and the material made sense. I would take him again and liked his lectures.
Class may be difficult for those without SQL experience, but professor is helpful and approachable. Much of the grade depends on the exams and the homework where as the term project is only worth 15%

Future EDM students, don't give up - I got a C on the midterm and a B on the final. I didn't even turn in the last 3 questions on triggers and procedures by the end. I still managed to pull an A after the curve. Be forewarned: the front-end part of your project is only taught within the last week! Currim sure knows how to "teach".

This is by far the most challenging course I've ever taken. If taking in an 8 week format, you will unlikely have time for anything else. Don't slack during week 1 or you'll never catch up. That being said, I feel like I truly got my money's worth and learned many valuable skills. Dr. C. is kind and approachable. Take advantage of office hours.

Kind guy, awful class. Tis a semester-long class hastily stuffed into 8 weeks, rather than making class concise & clearly grouping weekly materials. No clarity on deadlines & exams. Midterm shook the class with content covered week after. 40% of ur grade depends on collab with 2 groups! so less control of ur grade. Unnecessary complexity & stress!
Very hard and frustrating class. The tests are so unlike the material you are taught, so it is very difficult to study for them.

I am so disappointed in the quality of Eller's courses and teaching. MIS304 is a poorly structured class that serves no true purpose or benefit for students entering the professional world. You are provided with surface-level information that won't pertain to 90% of your professional career. No point in attending because lectures are pre-recorded.

Professor Faiz is a joke. The class is flipped which normally means you do work in the class and learn on your own. However, in reality you do work on your own and teach yourself everything. His lectures are completely worthless and he does not discuss any course material. It is the end of March and we have not had one full lecture with content.

Dr Currim is a tough but fair grader, and a very knowledgeable person in the field. This class takes dedication, but the material makes sense when you spend the time to understand it. Lectures are really important, along with weekly materials.
You don't even know what happen then the quiz is closed. You spent time in class tho, you still can't figure out the quiz cuz everything is not in the lecture. GOOGLE IS THE BETTER THAN HIM

Confusing lectures, confusing slides, confusing dates for quizzes and assignment due dates. Quizzes are set for you to fail the class. If there was a choice, would rather taking two other inspiring class instead in the master program.

Truly one of the kindest professors I have ever had. The material may be hard, but he really does his best to encourage you and make you want to work hard. He is definitely one of the nicest professors Eller has.

Absolutely awful class. Exams consisted of subjects that we were never given homework on and questions that weren't on the review PDFs. You'll wait over an hour in office hours to not receive a straightforward answer and feel even more confused. Learn SQL beforehand because you're pretty much on your own in this class.
Probably the worst class in the MIS program

This has got to be the hardest, most frustrating class Feedback given on homework is sparse so you can't learn from ir. Too many trick questions, lectures are so boring. if you have no programming experience BEWARE, you are basically teaching yourself this class. You need to go from beginner to advanced within the first week of the course. TERRIBLE

Faiz was hard to pay attention to during class, but it was cool how he only makes you come to one day a week. A lot of extra credit opportunities too, to make up for the hard exams. Fairly easy if you seek out help if you need.

304 is 111 but harder. Faiz makes the material easier to conceptualize and man looks like he enjoys MIS and life. I like how attendance only matters for Tuesday or Thursday with online lecture taking up the other day. The assignments, though valuable, can be time consuming and annoying.

He taught MIS 304, not 307 this semester. And the class was SOO BORING. Assignments were too much and complicated, and he deducted points for teeny-tiny aspects. TESTS WERE EXTREMELY HARD AND TRICKY!!! Case Comp was boring and he seems to enjoy it while the majority of us DONT. If he still teaches, it would be best for u guys to drop it and wait!!!

Best professor I've ever had. MIS531 is maybe the most infamous course in the MS MIS program. It is extremely challenging, but at the same time, incredibly rewarding. I would not recommend taking the online version of this course.
He's just amazing

Only have to attend class on one of the scheduled days (you pick one for the semester). Currim is very knowledgable is his subject matter & his lectures are engaging. He offers extra credit just for asking questions in class! By cutting his class sizes in half and encouraging so much participation shows how much he truly cares about our learning!
Excellent professor. Loved his class.

Difficult class. Assignments take up most of your time because they are incredibly difficult. Quizzes are at random and most people did not do well. He does curve the grades which makes it easy to get a B but that does not mean you will not be stressing the whole semester. Get ready to work because it will not come without great efforts.

He makes the class so difficult, which is absolutely unnecessary. All the week you must struggle with his homework since he does not teach very well. The amount of works he asks in his assignments is unreasonable. It seems he does not undestand that students have other classes and homework assignments, and this is not only about his damn class.

Faiz is professor of principles and teaches well but the problem is that course seems outdated to me and needs to be updated in accordance with the needs of 2017-2020. Pretty boring slides and skips few slides when not able to finish the course in time.

Very Passionate about what he teaches. He is a very boring lecturer with tedious assignments. I would recommend getting a PC for this course. Also, he changes the Case Assignments so up until the day they're due. So I would recommend procrastinating unless you don't care to make his changes.

Faiz Currim created an overly difficult class. The University of Arizona seems to maintain this brutal class because it shows that not everyone can get an A. I despise the class and my classmates do as well. It's unnecessarily difficult with the intent to boost an ego that leaves the students upset and wastes their time. Could be taught better.

It is a good class. I was introduced to Enterprise Database management in graduation level with this course.  The slides are helpful. Not that difficult as it sounds. The ER and Relational concepts are cool. The assignments are a bit time-taking, but makes you 'feel MIS' after doing them.

Worst online course for a masters I have taken yet for this program. And I have take 4 other courses out of the required 10 for a masters. Unless you have extensive knowledge of SQL do not take the course.

Also had Professor Currim for MIS 307. Great teacher who really cares about what hes teaching. Knowledgeable, funny, rather easy going. I wish he taught more MIS classes, Its professors like Mr. Currim that keep our ranking at #1. Class was not that easy, but who said being #1 was easy.

He makes sure the MIS department keeps its #1 status. Makes databases interesting and keeps a positive attitude throughout the semester. He knows what he's doing. Not an easy class, but hey... gotta beat those ASU grads.

Great teacher. Teaches at a reasonably fast pace but fully understands that the class is introductory database and grades accordingly. If you attend class, study, and pay attention (aside from grasping the actual technical concepts) you will do fine. He curves the class to a B which is nice. Funny and very intelligent.

Professor Delaney was an amazing professor. Had amazing energy for 9:30 in the morning, and truly is like waking up and drinking a cup of coffee. I gave him a 3/5 difficulty, not because he gives a lot of work, but the class overall is just a lot of work, so make sure to prepare for that. Otherwise, great semester, truly one of the best professors!

Delaney is a great guy and cares a lot about his students, but his grading expectations are tough. Yes, BCOM is a hard class, but Delaney expects you to go above and beyond for an A. If you follow his guidelines and rubric, it's a B. Expect to go to office hours often. Lectures are sometimes unclear but overall, Delaney is not the worst out there.

John is very kind, but his class can be tough. He's a strict grader, and I had to do weekly Zoom meetings with him just to get close to perfect scores. Attendance is mandatory with only three allowed absences. He sometimes struggles to communicate clearly in lectures, which is hard to follow. I'd suggest taking a different BCOM professor.

Amazing professor, I recommend taking him if you can. I even was a preceptor for him and I highly recommend him. He cares about his students. Make sure to participate and try your best and you will receive an A. Sometimes his instructions can be confusing but make sure to ask questions and he is happy to help.
This is a hard class becuase it is meant to challenge you but John made the most of it! He was super kind and caring and his energy at 8AM is UNMATCHED!

He is so fun! For homework, meet with him. He tells you what to have based on his grading. Rehearse for presentations. I got mid 80's on presentations and feedback would be "don't sway". Put in work for the video and website because they are important. Overall, you are going to spend time out of class working on things so plan accordingly.

If you get RANDOMLY placed on a bad group, GL. His takes off points on everything, very vague instructions, no helpful insights. The class is pure BS, no matter how hard they try to convience you website, personailty survays are benfital to your career. The truth is that NO ONE GIVES A SHT. This guy never worked a day in corporate, his is a joke.

I love Delaney! He's the sweetest and the best teacher for BCOM. The grading is hard, but that's the point of your first semester in Eller. The criteria for projects are a bit vague, so make sure to go in for presentation check-overs with enough allotted time to make changes after receiving feedback. Would recommend him as a prof!

This class can be super intimidating at first, but looking back was super fun to be apart of. Delaney brings amazing energy to every class and is always available outside of class when you need more help (highly recommend to take advantage of this). BCOM 314 projects are tough so be ready to step outside your comfort zone! So fun overall

Delaney is not good at lecturing, but he still requires students with strict attendance. To be honest, I'm really frustrated. This is the worst class I've ever taken. You need to guess how to get the project scores, there is no standard for rating. I have learned a lot, but I hope to never see Delaney again.
Probably one of the hardest class as the grading is extremely random and the accessibility of the preceptors are extremely limited.

He was a really nice guy, except extremely inconsistent in grading. The assignments given should be easy however, he docks points off on everything. He is extremely vague and his lectures do not relate to assignments or tests.

The nicest BCOM professor in Eller. Super personal/supportive and gives great feedback. Assignment details are a little scattered and you should ask for clarification, but he's easy to access outside of class. Focus on presentations and the bigger projects, besides that, just being in class and doing easy side work makes up most of your grade.

Tough grader. Can't deny that we've all improved our public speaking and presentation skills tho. He's known for giving most students high B's regardless of how close they are to an A. Gave multiple assignments due in a few days without knowledge prior. Wasted a lot of class time with the constant and unnecessary 2 min presentations. Tough class
Professor Delaney is literally one of the coolest professors you could ever have. Its easy to understand the material and he's always there to help if you need it.

This professor is a plant and his class is clear scam. He has a rigged attendance policy and he makes sure to be as vague as possible in order to fail students who he does not personally like. This professor was clearly put in this position to filter out students who Eller admin have deemed as undesirable, but you don't have to take my word for it!

John Delaney was such an amazing teacher. You need the book, but only for small presentations on the chapters. He was so happy all of the time and I loved coming to class. He is a tough grader, but all of the BCOM teachers are. If you are to get any teacher in BCOM, he is the best one to get.

John is an amazing professor! He makes you work hard for your grade though. He is probably one of the best Corporate Communications profs out there. Make sure to work with him and the preceptors and come with positive energy and you will be fine!

Avoid having him at all costs! While it is possible to receive an A in the course, you have to work WAYYY too hard to do so. His grading criteria is extremely unclear and he takes an eternity to grade most assignments. Very passive aggressive personality too

John is a nice enough guy, but makes his class unbelievably hard. He'll always find something to mark you off on (even if it's just a style choice). Although it's certainly possible to get an A, it's also a ridiculous amount of work. He doesn't really explain the assignments well, so you feel like you're guessing most of the time.

John is by far one of the best professors that I have ever had throughout my college career - the class does make you work, but you come out on the better end. He is always available and understanding and tries his hardest to make everything engaging in class and really wants you to succeed.

If you want to have any chance of winning case competition, avoid Delaney at all costs. His feedback is often times very contradicting and doesn't align with the rubric. Nice guy but he really makes you work.

One of the more time demanding classes during first semester of Eller. You have to really work to do well. The challenge was that he never made it clear what the expectations were for every assignment, resulting in receiving a grade of an 80% at best. It was difficult to assess your grade in the class because he takes months to grade anything.

BCOM is one of the harder classes as a first semester Eller student, but John does a great job of making the class fun and engaging. Although I absolutely hated presenting, my skills grew so much in his course and I'm still not done with the semester, but I can just say that I'm only going to get better.

I had Delaney for bcom at 8am. You must go to class to avoid losing points. Class was very tough and demanding but delaney was supportive and inspiring. My only gripe is how hard he grades compared to other instructors. He's extremely approachable and made our class feel like a family. (White knight of biz)

I really enjoyed taking Dr. Garcia's class. Some of the readings were incredibly difficult for me, so it's worth making a friend or two in the class to work through it with. He is very kind, interesting, and cares about his students. The work he is doing is very, very important.
A great human being and teacher! Really cares about his students and holds thought-provoking very helpful discussions.

A good class. Be prepared for lots of group discussions and debates. Makes you think critically about current problems with schooling in america. Class is easy and makes you think. work is basic and weekly. very easy. only a two papers and you have plenty of time to work on them

This class is very easy. All you have to do is do the work and you will pass. He took forever to get a 5 page paper grade back to me, it took about 6+ weeks. His class is interesting and very easy. Expect to read a lot and discuss a lot. Very fun and a good person

She is the best teacher ever! This review is actually for the mgmt elective 432 her negotiations class. She is honestly one of the kindest teachers ever. Going in person does matter, but the classes are fun! Definitely an easy A
Lots of powerpoint (some at least 100 pages), so be prepared to spend a lot of time studying. Although she cared much about the students, the class was so boring and difficult.

Many people say this class is hard, although if you show up to class, take simple notes, and study for 2 hours before the exam you will do well. She is one of the best professors I have ever had, has a lot of energy and puts a lot of effort into her lectures. Even brought her dog to the last day of class 12/10 good boy he said hi to everyone.

Doyle easily has the most fun, engaging slides at Eller - most Eller professors just cut and paste bullet points onto a blank slide. She's clearly put a lot of work into building her slide decks. Out of all the pointless filler classes they make you take during first semester Eller, hers was my favorite. No other class quite as interesting as OB.

Dr. Doyle's class isn't easy, but I learned a good bit from it. Honestly, it's one of the better classes I've taken at UA. Her class is engaging, and doesn't simply lecture to us. Going to office hours, attending class, and reading the assignments is crucial. But, no more so than anything else I've taken.

I wish this review could steer you clear of her, but given that Eller makes your schedule and there's only one MGMT 310 class to take, if you get her, you're stuck with her. Get ready to be talked at for 75 minutes while she blows through poorly designed presentations (Often 100+ slides) at the speed of light. I hated this class.

Its not an easy A but she is an incredible teacher. The material is interesting, fun activities, clear grading. Test heavy but there is A LOT of extra credit. One of the best teachers at UA and Eller. I had problems with my group and she is approachable and understanding. Brought speakers and tried to make class fun.

I really enjoyed Dr. Doyle's class. Many of the activities she had us do weren't necessarily the most relevant to the material we were learning, but they were impactful in a way that I will always remember them. All of her expectations are laid out for you, so there are no surprises when it comes to grading projects or assignments.

I really liked Sarah Doyle! She clearly cared about her students and did her best to make sure everyone was feeling confident on the content. She sometimes has activities that dont really pertain to the class but are pretty engaging. Her tears are somewhat tricky so make sure you understand the material. Overall not a horrible class!

Great teacher, her power points are the best and have a ton of info to study. She makes fun activities sometimes so that we don't get too bored with lectures. Just read the book chapters and go over hw questions for tests and you'll be fine. Group projects are a lot of work but she gives you a lot of time to do them.

This was definitely one of the more difficult classes I have taken. He is very knowledgeable and knows how to teach the subject, but unless you have a photographic memory, you are going to have to study for hours. Your entire grade is three exams. Although I enjoyed his lectures, his teaching style did not work for me and I really struggled.
He's possibly the most intelligent of all the ECON professors and the exams are extremely straightforward, in fact he provides the questions beforehand.

Professor Drabicki is extremely intelligent, and knows the subject matter in and out. Public Sector Economics is the hardest class I've ever taken, but if you go to EVERY single lecture, you should do fine. Attendance is not graded, but you will fall behind extremely quickly if you miss class, because he posts nothing online.

Drabicki is the smartest professor I've ever had. I've taken two of his classes and they are VERY straightforward. Exams are difficult, but once you answer all review Qs and know HOW to study/understand them, exams are easy. ATTEND REVIEW SESSIONS, he will even say sometimes what Qs will be on the exam and how he wants you to answer them.

This professor is a genius. He comes to lecture and writes everything on the board off the top of his head. He gives the questions that will be on the exams, but you have to have a REALLY strong understanding behind the concept to do well. DO NOT take this class if you're not good at understanding how graphs can connect to complex economic ideas.

Nothing is posted on D2L. I went to every lecture and review session, wrote down everything he wrote down, reviewed it for hours, and was still lost. I studied more for this class than all my other classes this semester by far and still had to drop the class. If you're reading this I would recommend taking literally any other class than this one.
Difficult tests. No answers for review questions. Only review sessions but you can't make sure everything is correct. The exam will be different from the review questions a lot. Don't TAKE THIS CLASS

Very straightforward teaching style. If you're willing to come to class everyday, take detailed notes, and study those notes IN ADVANCE (no cramming) you WILL get an A. This is coming from someone who has failed multiple classes in their college career. (Tip: make notecards for the review questions. My friend and I did this and we both did well).

Professor Drabicki is extremely knowledgeable but was awful at adapting his class to an online setting. I took this class in the Fall in what was supposed to a "live online" format. Instead, he held no live lectures and posted pre-recorded lectures from his summer class on some very difficult material. Seemed lazy, would not take this class online!

Attendance is not mandatory however if you miss one class you will fall behind in the material and he does not post anything on D2L. His class doesn't even pop up on the main D2L page so good luck trying to find what your grades were. He is very funny and shows a lot of passion towards the class however he is very technical and a very strict grader

This is the first class I've failed in college, but I would (and will be) take it again. Drabicki is so knowledgeable and actually successful in the private sector in his field. He bleeds red, and he doesn't care if you come to class. NO participation, just tests and quizzes. They're really hard. Get ready to work hard and learn a lot.

Even though I didnt receive the grade I wanted I walked away with much more knowledge than before in the field of economics. Drabicki is a tough grader and the class requires a lot of studying but it will pay off.

Prof. Drabicki is great. He really knows his stuff and his lectures are always engaging. No assigned reading, and he gives out review questions that make it very clear what you need to know. He's not a generous grader, your answers have to be precise and complete to get an A. He goes into at least one entertaining tangent per class, which is fun.
Similar as ECON 443. He is fast but teaches efficiently during lecture time. As long as you memorize all notes and go over the review questions. You will be fine.

How can you get a A: Go to class, take notes everything what he talks in class, and memorize the notes. He is a fast instructor, and seems he does not like questions during his lectures. He does not have office hours but his TA has one.
Professor Drabicki is an amazingly clear lecturer, but because of this there's no assigned text so it's imperative that you attend class regularly.
I adore this man and I absolutely loved his class!! It's not an easy A, but hard work absolutely pays off in this class!

Don't take this class if you're not going to make it to every single lecture. No book. There's nothing to study from except for the notes you take in class. No points for attendance but taking in class notes are essential to pass his tests, which aren't easy. Pretty much only take this class if you're a morning person.

His lectures are long, but incredibly useful.  He writes down absolutely everything you need to know on the board, which makes note-taking extremely easy.  He bleeds BRIGHT red and anyone with any amount of liberal in them may be somewhat uncomfortable.  Regardless he's an awesome professor and I highly recommend.

Superb professor. He may be so right wing for your political tastes, but Prof. Drabicki is excellent. He knows his stuff and is genuinely interested in your success. Don't miss class and write EVERYTHING down.

PHENOMENAL teacher.  Hard course (PPEL 301 = intermediate microeconomics). Exams are verbatim questions from review sheets, and lectures go question by question. DO NOT MISS class, KEEP review sheets at all times. Don't buy the book (his recommendation, he was right). You will work hard but learn a ton from an amazing teacher.

I don't rate professors very often, but Professor Ellis is one of the best I've ever had I thought I would. He commented on every single discussion post which shows he cares. His lectures complement the reading very well and are engaging.
Prof. Ellis is great! Really takes time to help you understand the concepts. Highly recommend, learned a lot in his course.

Highly recommend taking any class that Aleks teaches.  He is one of the most knowledgeable and helpful professors I have ever had. His classes are both intriguing and applicable to the real world.  Aleks takes the time to make his own tests, so if you pay attention in class you will most likely get a good grade. Take his class!

He is pretty nice. You only have to attend once a week for the most part, but attendance is taken and there are in-class assignments. There are only 2 tests. As for his helpfulness, he wouldn't read parts of my final paper in order to see if I was on the right track. Get the chapter book but not the textbook. A little bit of extra credit offered.

Buy the novel but not the textbook. Attendance is pretty much required for points but class is interesting. Tests are harder than you'd think, but he repeats questions on the final. Project is fun depending on your group. I'd recommend this teacher for 310D. He's fun to look at, too!

BOTTOM LINE: the points are pretty easy to get in the class...there just aren't many of them. Tests can be semi-difficult, so do well on those and get 100%'s on everything else to get an A. there is some extra credit, but just try to do well all along. An A takes some work, a B is pretty easy

Very easy class... went to class every monday and every other wednesday. Team projects are easy even if you have a lame team... Wednesday you have activities with team in class that are easy and some are also fun... He is a great teacher.. showed episodes of the office and simpsons in class for demonstration of poor management
Very easy class, watched episodes of The Office, The Simpsons. Only have to go to class on Mondays and every other Wednesdays. Easy assignments

This class isn't exceptionally hard, however, the reviews are extremely outdated. The exams are no longer open note and now require Honorlock with an external camera. He gives you approximately 4 textbook modules and 10 or so readings per week. The lecture videos are just him reading the textbook with no slides. Don't think this will be an easy A.

Professor Fortunato does a fantastic job expanding on cybersecurity if you've taken 415 before this amazing class. This was a very fun class as well and pretty easy since the exams and quizzes are open note. Another amazing thing about the labs is you do get to do tech work to really feel like a cybersecurity analyst. I'd take this class again too.

Took MIS 517 online with Professor Fortunato. Easily one of the better professors in the Online MIS program, and definitely the best professor when it comes to the cyber security focused courses. Easy to get an A if you do the work, exams/quizzes are open note and he does not use Examity!

I took 417 online and it was very straight forward. For the quizzes just have all the class lecture pdf's open and CTRL+F your way to an A+. Labs were challenging but really cool as we spun up our own VMs and implemented some security policies. You do write a lot of essays. Should be straightforward to get the A, and you'll learn a lot on the way.
I took MIS517 System Security Management with Prof. Fortunato. He was a great professor and I learned a lot from the class.
Super easy A. No need to read textbook just watch his lecture videos. So much extra credit i think i had 5%. Spent abt an hour a week on this class ended with like 106%.
Fricke was a good prof, I enjoyed how simple he made concepts through his videos. I found his exams to be somewhat difficult, however he did offer a lot of EC which made up for it

I took the online version of this class and I really enjoyed it. It is just like MGMT 359 if you have taken that class but minus the sports references. Watch and take notes the video lectures and you'll be golden for the tests. Pretty easy class if you take the time to get an A. Also offers extra credit. I recommend the online class if you can.

I really liked this class. He does a great job referencing sports law topics and many different cases that involves sports. The tests are not bad at all and he offers 2 15 point extra credit opportunities throughout the year. The projects with your group are also engaging and pretty easy. Definitely recommend this class.

Class is very interesting and simple! It was so easy to do well on the exams. He gives you an outline of everything you need to know. Literally just had to read the book each week, take a quiz on the chapter, and do a discussion. Take notes from the book and study those. Go to class for the ethics stuff, it is just once a week.

Best professor & class! He makes it super engaging & uses real world examples to help you understand concepts. He also wrote the book for this course so it is very easy to read and understand. On top of this, he is such a great guy and you can tell he truly cares about his students! I can't speak highly enough about him & this class!

Professor Fricke is an amazing professor. He is very engaging and it's great listening to his lectures. There is a lot of options for extra credit. Also, you should defintely read the textbook, not only because it helps for the exams, but also because it's written by the professor and the style is very engaging and easy to understand.
I enjoyed his class as it was straightforward and focused on quizzes, exams, and discussions without any group projects.

Took 402 in the fall, Fricke is a really great lecturer and provides a textbook that is easy to digest, unlike many textbooks that are made to be complicated and confusing for no reason. Wish he taught more undergrad classes, I enjoyed this one! Read the chapters and come to class (a few skips is fine he gives drops) and you'll ace the class.

Fricke is the man fr. I took his 3-week class over break and he makes learning the concepts very engaging by using real world RELEVANT examples. For exams, he doesn't try and trick you and goes over what to expect for each exam. Just a fundamentally strong professor who deserves a raise at eller tbh.. I never write reviews so trust me, he's good.

I took a 3-week online course with Fricke. I not only loved the content, but how it was taught. His lectures were the only pre-recorded ones I have ever had (even 2 years post-Covid). All my other professors made a powerpoint and used very dull voice overs. Fricke's lectures were engaging, interesting and you can tell he took time and effort.

This class is basically all online recorded lectures, but he splits them up into a couple videos so they are not too bad to watch. The written assignments are fairly easy as well if you are detail oriented (however the first one they grade really tough). There are a lot of EC opportunities to help keep your grade up as well. Great professor!

Professor Fricke is so amazing and provides a lot of really interesting information in this course that can be used later in life. He is a very engaging teacher and wants people to enjoy his lectures. This class is not too much work, you just need to show up to class and make sure you do the biweekly written assignments.

If you put in the TIME AND EFFORT youll do amazing on everything. For the group projects, they grade the first one very hard because they dont tell you anything about it. So unless you cheat on it, youll get a lower score. AFTER that, they tell you what they wanted. TIME AND EFFORT will come in handy for the assignments after the 1st assignment.

Dont know what the 5 star review is even talking about when they say gives good feedback. We asked multiple times and never got any. The rest of the class is EASY. Im guessing 5 star cheats on them or knows Fricke a little too well. They set you to fail the first assignment, but after that its on you if you do bad on them.

Ok theres so much going on here. I have to say the lectures are interesting. I do have to agree with the one stars that the group projects are not graded the best. My groups done pretty well, but thats mainly because we got in touch with past students. They really do not tell you what they want at first. While its a cool class, it can be better

Agree that the video lectures are interested but definitely disagree with the point that if you work hard youll be fine. Unless youre given the rubric by a preceptor theres just no way to get a good grade in the group assignment. Points are taken off for the most irrelevant items.
Worst class ever!!! Makes this useless class hard for absolutely no reason. Out to make sure your grades get destroyed.
Hate this class! Probably one of the worst structured classes I have ever taken. Get ready for your GPA to drop bc Frickes main reason to exist is to make sure everyones grade suffers

Absolutely one of the worst professors at Eller. Constantly wants you to fail and will purposely make sure that your hard work goes to complete waste. His group assignments are created to make sure your grade drops. No clear rubric but will take points off for random reason. Truly the worst class ever!!!

Seriously one of the worst professors and classes I have ever taken. The group assignments are created to set you up for failure. He refuses to give a rubric, doesnt ask the questions he wants you to answer and then gives low grades because you were unable to somehow read his mind. Worst class I have ever taken.

Professor Fricke's video lectures are really helpful and informative. It is important you make sure you take notes on the video lecture as they not only help for the group assignments but more importantly the exams in the class. He is also very nice and fun to speak to and the exams really aren't that difficult once you get the hang of it.

Only teaches theory but doesn't teach you how to perform them, so most of it is meaningless.Completely scatter brain to the point of severe hinderance of the class. This is a data analysis class where your grade can not be broken down to data.Straight up used chat gpt to teach us instead of explaining things himself.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS.

Ghosh makes Ochem seem simple. Getting a B in the course was relatively easy, and an A is possible if that is your goal. DO EXAMPLE PROBLEMS on every practice test you can find before each exam. Sometimes, he uses the EXACT same questions as a previous year. Email when you don't understand something, he replies within the hour during exam weeks.
Dr Ghosh is retiring.  I am lucky to have taken the last class he is teaching. Good luck Dr Ghosh. Thank you for an amazing semester.
Dr. Ghosh is a great professor. He makes difficult concepts seem easy, and his exams are very similar to the practice exams he provides.
I'm going to miss professor ghosh, attend class or you will be lost. It's tough but he is so helpful and amazing.

PROFESSOR GHOSH IS THE BEST!!! ochem is a tough course and Ghosh is the professor you want to take it with. There are 4 exams + a final and one exam gets dropped. There are two extra credit opportunities for the last two exams, both 10 points each for completion. He is so nice and funny and responds to emails so fast, especially during exam weeks.
Ghosh is the best professor ever! He gives you so many resources and if you do practice exams, you will be set in the class. Very funny and wants you to succeed.

Ghosh is kind, great at explaining the material, doesn't make tests incredibly difficult (very similar to practice exams, so use that to your advantage), and provides a solid amount of extra credit too. Easily the best prof to take for ochem, you need to put in the work for a good grade but his class isn't nearly as overwhelming as other profs'.

Professor Ghosh was always very supportive of his students and wanted everyone to truly understand the content and succeed. I felt that his lectures were very clear, if a bit repetitive, and I appreciated that he gave a few extra credit opportunities which were not too much work. Overall, I really liked this class.

He was great. Ochem is really tough, but you will make it through this class if you put hard work into it. Use the practice exams and email him with questions. He always was helpful and quick with responses. Again, ochem is tough so take it with him 100%.

All I can say is that he's truly a GOAT. Someone said he is Indraneel Goat and I cannot agree more. Amazing lectures, common sense, understandable and such a hilarious guy. The only things that are graded are the exams (4 midterms but 1 dropped + final). Study your butt off from the practice exams because the real exams are so similar!
Ghosh is amazing!!! TAKE HIM AND YOU WILL BE OKAY!! super caring, and he is logical versus making you memorize everything under the sun, he realizes we are not all chemistry majors!!

This class is so difficult, but so is any organic chemistry class. Dr. Ghosh is an angel, he really cares about his students and their learning. The grade is 3 tests (1 of 4 is dropped) and the final, that is it. Study your butt off for those exams and you should get an A :)

Neel Ghosh? More like Neel GOAT. He is super nice, funny, and his lectures are amazing. He has been super understanding and accommodating for students' needs during COVID. The material is difficult but he makes it manageable.

everyone told me second semester ochem was harder than the first semester but to me it was the opposite. second semester material is harder but ghosh did a fantastic job of teaching us the material and making sure we did well on exams. the last 2 exams are killers but he offers lots of help and practice for them. 10/10 would recommend

I ended up doing much better in this class than I did in first semester ochem. The lectures are thorough and easy to understand, the tests are fair, lots of study resources are provided, and he was very understanding and helpful when transferring his class online during this semester.
Ghosh is great! His exams are tough but they are extremely fair. Take this class!

He deserves the world. He replies so fast to any and all questions and is a good lecturer. I wanna buy him his little coffee that he brings to class every day because he is the gift that keeps me from crying.
Best class my Sophomore year. Lots of study material and clear explanations. Do practice exams and go to reviews before exams.

Includes Homework as part of grade unlike Dollinger. Still a hard class because it is Ochem but I liked having homework it focused me to do the work for the grade unlike in Dollinger where you are just graded on tests
unorganized and never clear grading criteria. Taking online was bad. Professor is very willing to help but I don't feel I genuinely learned much

Grading criteria unclear, routinely inconsistent in written and verbal instructions on assignments and tests so you have no idea how to prepare and dismissive of students when they try to ask for clarification. This class was a joke! It shouldn't have been a graduate requirement, it's more like a freshmen/sophomore class.

Great professor. He requires participation in the lectures in order for students to best understand and memorize course material through the examples that emerged from the discussion ! He randomly calls names during lectures to see if you are in class or not. So don't skip class.

Not a difficult class. Took weeks to grade exams. Gradebook updated at the end of semester. Cancelled class via email the morning of 2x. Didnt order text on time w bookstore thus no text until 3rd wk of classes. Ignored multiple direct emails. Take this class online! Dont waste your time w this guy. Hes either disorganized or too busy to care.
Prof. Eyran is great! He is very engaging, funny, and gives a lot of opportunities for extra credit. Somewhat of a tough grader, but overall, a great instructor.
Eyran is a great lecturer and keeps class interesting!

Super chill and fun guy. The tests can be a bit difficult because of how much content is covered but he offers a ton of extra credit to make sure you do well. Lectures are okay because he is funny, but the actual topics are a bore.

Professor Eyran Lives to troll. Most of his lectures are made to feed his ego to make the class laugh. Anytime there is a formula he doesn't actually show how to apply the equations. You can survive the class on extra credit like Caleb Love survived at pigs(2024)(barely). Lectures dont supply the exams well enough. Good guy if you like Comedians:)
Absolute chiller. class participation is mando

Eryan is super funny and lighthearted. He means well and offers so much extra credit. He has Tophat in class for participation grade (polls) and will pull random letters for attendance. As long as you take notes and listen to what he says and apply it outside of class you will do good. The homeworks take a long time on McGragh.
Very quirky guy but i loved him. It was hard because of it beign an 8am but the prof sure had lots of energy. We did fun activites in class like making paper airplanes

His actual class can be challenging, but he offers so much extra credit, you'll always pass. He's extremely responsive to email and quite nice in office hours. It's pretty clear he wants everyone to get a good grade in his class.

Such a funny and easygoing prof! Easy A but if you don't want to take the final would recommend watching his online lectures before class, taking notes on the slides during class, and going to the zoom reviews (as questions on there are on the exam). Also when he asks ?s not on the slides -- those are on the exam too ;)

Eyran tries to be engaging and is pretty entertaining despite the dull subject material. Tests are each worth 20% of the grade but if you understand all problems in the lecture slides, you should easily get an A+ on them. HW problems are usually much harder than test problems but can be good practice. So much extra credit offered. Very easy overall

Funny, lighthearted professor who gives digestible lectures. The (McGraw Hill) homework assignment problems are often much more difficult than whatever you'll run into on an exam in this class. Case competition(not what you think, you just record a voice over of a presentation in a group) is the easiest, low-stress thing ever. Neat class

Eyran was very energetic during lecture which always made me laugh and brightened my day. He offers a large amount of extra credit throughout the course. The exams are relatively easy as long as you run through the slides with notes at least once. My only complaint is the difficulty of the homework is often higher than in the lecture material.

So much extra credit, finished with a very high A and did mediocre on exams. Each exam is 200 points, so if you dont want to take the final, STUDY EARLY and pay attention in class. Follow the project rubric EXACTLY for a good grade. Overall, not a bad class (esp at 8am), and Eyran is pretty funny and really engaging.

Eyran is caring and funny and his lectures are tolerable. The exams are worth a lot so you should definitely study for them. He graded the case analysis really hard so make sure you follow the rubric to a tee. Do all the extra credit and get chegg to help with the homeworks.

I really enjoyed this class, eyran wants his students to succeed by implementing a TON of extra credit and having fun activities in class. Even though the exams are pretty difficult, he does a good job with showing you what to study to help his students succeed.
Funny professor with somewhat bland lectures (mostly due to material). Lots of opportunities to earn extra credit and overall very easy.

He has video lectures that are long but easy, he walks you through the examples and he added playback speed control when someone asked. His papers and discussions are the same thing, only thing was you had to reply to everyone in your group to get full credit. Take notes, do the hw and you'll be fine. Very active in Ask the instructor discussions.
awful lecturer, goes way too fast, picks on students, the course work is impossible, get ready to study

He's rude and snide when you ask him questions, the content is incredibly boring, and the homework assignments are all tedious and annoying. He is okay in lectures and makes jokes to be "funny" but try not to have to ask him any questions! You have to scan in for attendance based on random last name letters.

Do all the homework and show up for the participation points. Do well on the exams by going over his slides and doing the exam review quiz. He offers make up papers (takes about 30 mins to write one) to improve your exam grade. The exam review quizzes are also on quizlet and they help so much for the exams. Tons of extra credit in this class too.

Eyran is an amazing professor and is really funny! He's personable and was always available to help students. Eyran incorporates many real-world examples in his lectures and it helped me learn so well. The exams are somewhat difficult but he provides a lot extra credit to help with the difficulty. One of my favorite professors in my undergrad.

He's a funny guy but the lectures are so bland. Lots of extra credit. Exams are mid, not easy but not hard either. Study his exam reviews and lecture slides and you should be good for exams. Besides being a fun and cool professor, he's mid.

Eyran was AWESOME! He made the boring material actually interesting. He offers so much extra credit in this course, so it's hard to get less than a B if you do the work. Make sure you do ALL assignments in this class! Exams were not too bad, but the pre-lecture homework was so annoying to complete because they took forever to finish.

People on this forum are complaining about an average college course. Eyran does not make tests to completely pull the rug out from under you, just study and use pre-recorded lectures. He offers a lot of extra credit so you can save your grade, this is not an insanely hard class to get a good grade in. Put in the work and it will pay off.

Didn't like the professor or the class- the lectures were boring but attendance was required. There were never any real world applications so I was often left thinking 'when am I ever going to need to know this stuff??' The tests were difficult but he offers TONS of extra credit that absolutely saved my grade.

I wasn't a fan of the class, but I did have a lot of respect for Professor Gisches. He's really funny and does his best to get students interested in operations. The exams are difficult, but there's so much extra credit, so it isn't a hard class to get an A in.
Had to take this class for my mba- not great.

This class is ALL OVER THE PLACE. The content on exams is extremely unclear and the exams are filled with grammar errors. The amount of content on is exams is beyond overwhelming and the questions are straight up riddles. Horrible at explaining how to use the formulas and concepts.

Gisches is not a good public speaker and rambles/mumbles most of his lecture. It can be very hard to understand anything he says in combination with his accent. The class is not really about supply chain and focuses more on textbook diagrams and problems. He never relates actual content to the real world and clearly never worked outside academics
Tough grader. Lectures go way too fast and he is very hard to understand. The final is also a large portion of your grade and no curve when everyone bombs it.

By far the worst professor I have ever had, and wish that this class was not a requirement. This class made me second guess why I even decided to go into Eller because of how poor the teaching was, and the lack of care for a students' success. Horrible teacher, resign if you have a problem with students asking questions. IT'S YOUR JOB.

He is a nice man and his exams are easy as long as you understand the material in class and on the homework. He also hands out a lot of extra credit so don't be too concerned about getting anything lower than a B. This class is probably my easiest class this semester and I give credit to this man for making my life easier.

He has all these expectations for you that he doesn't communicate. It's hard to understand him. He sounds like he's yelling, he doesn't make sure kids are understanding it. He said an assignment was extra credit (said it in print on d2l and syllabus) and then gave everyone a 0 who didn't do it. It wasn't extra credit. He and his syllabus are a lie

I haven't finished the course yet, but here's what I can say. The homework is pretty tough but representative of what's covered. If you read the textbook thoroughly and watch his lectures, you'll do well. The lectures themself are incredible and some of the best I've ever had at U of A. He explains everything well, and it's surprisingly hands-on.
