Class is not difficult and lectures + reviewing homework will be enough to do well on tests. Tons of extra credit opportunities (around 8% available this semester) and class attendance was not mandatory. Among the easiest Eller classes and Gisches made the class enjoyable with polls and videos

It is possible to succeed in his exams if you put the work in. Offers lots of extra credit, and his lectures are fun to attend. I passed all my exams with a 90% just by going over the Learning Assessments and reviewing the PPTs.

Best professor ever! This class is like a breath of fresh air when you are getting absolutely destroyed by Eller. And his tests are NOT hard at all! All of the conceptual/non-math questions are listed explicitly on the review guide and you get a formula sheet for the math. Just pay attention in class & study for like 2 hrs/test and I got As on all.

He may be a nice guy but that doesn't come across taht way in his exams. They are extremely difficult and majority of what is on it is really hard to prepare for. He goes through the content very fast and expects us to know everything going in. I am taking some of the hardest classes in eller right now and get a's on every other exam except his
One of the worst professors Ive had in my entire life. His tests are something you can not prepare for no matter what you do. He does a terrible job explaining things and makes class unbearable.

Professor Gisches is great! Has a dry sense of humor. made the class fun w/poll questions & videos. lots of EC, lots of book assignments that are easy but time consuming (Do not skip them). If you do the book assignments, poll, & extra credit, you can get a 70 on exams and still pass the class with "B" or "A". 5 exams, last is optional.
Good

Eyran is the one thing that makes this class okay at 8 AM. He's so funny, and the class isn't that bad. So much extra credit and cheat sheets on the exams! Definitely take advantage of both of those things to get an easy A.

Don't be fooled, Eyran isn't just an MIS professor, he is also a comedian. This guy is so funny, I actually didn't mind having his class at 8AM! If you listen, you'll notice that he continually cracks jokes and you can tell he knows he's funny which makes it even better. There is also so much extra credit. Material can be tricky at times.

The only thing I dreaded about this class is having it at 8am because it was hard to pay attention. I think as long as you're not clueless in lectures, write down everything on the study guide in your cheat sheet, and do all the extra credit, you should be able to get an A. Show up to class for clicker questions and in-class activities.

Really important here to separate the class and the professor. The class? Absolute garbage. Really tedious and plug and chug formula heavy. That being said I liked Eyran a lot. Did his best to liven up class with videos and is a pretty funny guy You get a cheat sheet for exams MAKE SURE YOU DO ALL THE EXTRA CREDIT. Will take you from a C to and A

Everyone hates on 373 but I enjoyed it. The material is kind of dry especially if you aren't into statistics and the curriculum forces Eyran to move really fast in class because there's too much material to cover in 1 semester even at this level. Follow the study guide, review slides and use the extra credit lectures for practice. Easy, easy A.

He's not terrible. The class is insanely hard. TONS of EC you can do, thankfully. You won't understand a word he says and the math is compounding. This class should honestly just be online. He does give a cheat sheet which is awesome. He's not a bad guy, but 8am's are rough.

Eyran is fantastic. Makes the class incredibly easy; cheat sheets on tests, homework you can click through in 2 minutes, SO MUCH extra credit. You honestly have to try to not do well in the class. Easy A and funny guy

Gisches is a great professor and has a good sense of humor. Show up to class because the are always clicker q's. He sometimes shows clips from tv shows in class relative to the course content, which makes lectures less painful. Offers plenty of extra credit!
Gisches is really funny if you actually listen to his lectures.

Eyran is a good guy and did his best to keep an 8 am class entertained. Used lots of interesting videos and made participation questions very easy. Gives you a cheat sheet for exams and finds any reason to give extra credit. 11/10 would recommend.
Eyran is burdened with teaching the most uninteresting material at the university. He owns it though. He's lowkey really funny. Show up, pay attention, and take advantage of the extra credit. Easy A

Gisches knows a lot. I sometimes did not vibe with his teaching style, but that is not against him. I would go to TA office hours and using his awesome powerpoint slides, they would help me with what I didn't understand. This class does require reading and commitment if you want to understand and get a good grade, but it is worth it. Great info.
I thought the class was a little tough but he is a great teacher and truly made the class interesting. He was also very helpful when I needed help on my homework.

I liked the way he taught MIS 373 online! He was very helpful and inspirational. His midterm and final exams were definitely harder than expected. If you study well for those, and do the work you will get an A. I just did not study well for those and resulted with a B. The course is also intriguing. I highly recommend this class and the instructor!

Very Good lecturer and teacher. Funny in his own way and interesting. The tests are not bad as long as you go over slides. Also take advantage of the cheat sheet he provides. Do ALL the connect homeworks they aren't too hard
The class was a lot of busy work. Make sure you use your cheatsheet and study for the exams on top of that. Very funny dude in lectures- he'll slip in jokes but no one pays attention.

He is strict & somewhat intimidating when you speak w/ him, however, he is very helpful and considerate. Take connect HW & OA reports serious because you need those points to boost you up when you take the exams. Despite being able to use a 2-sided cheat sheet, test for me were difficult. This class has the most busy work of any other class by far

Gisches class is pretty straight forward. Plenty of extra credit and you can use cheat sheets for exams but get started on them early. The home works are annoying but easy. Also offers extra points on missed exam questions

Nice professor, teaches well, offers extra credit, and lets you use a cheat sheets on every exam and optional final. Actually study for the exams, that's what brought me down. Class is mandatory and try not to miss the homework due every Monday at 8pm.

CLASS IS STATISTICS, ALL OVER AGAIN. HIS TA'S (YANG AND ROOPALI)SUCK! HE DOES HIS BEST TO MAKE THINGS TRICKY ON TEST, SO BEWARE! OVERALL, HE IS A NICE GUY, AND HIS CLASS IS FAIRLY EASY. GREAT MATERIAL, BUT GET READY FOR MATHEMATICAL DIAGRAMS,AND YES, MORE STANDARD DEVIATIONS. HE LETS OUT EVERY CLASS EARLY; VERY COOL!!!

Super easy, class is mandatory via clicker questions, but if you have enough points dont go. he gives out almost a tests grade worth of extra credit, homework is online and easy. You can bring in a cheat sheet for the tests. Its pretty hard to get anything other than an A

I don't know what the one bad review on here is all about. Barry was a great teacher and if you prepare a little bit the class is smooth and very entertaining. Got some pretty good skills out of this class and he's a very funny guy.

If you get sick for a week, he won't help you with catching up with what you missed. Instead, he'll ask you to repeat the same question 10 times before he understands it. Unless you can read his mind or know how to  negotiate prior to taking his class, this is a class to avoid.
Barry was absolutely one of my favorite teachers. I loved this class and learned life skills from him. I would recommend it to anyone and would take it again. Awesome person and teacher

I have now taken both of his classes, negotiations and business law. He always shows up in a good mood and presents in a very fun way with stories and cases that have provided value to me as a person. He is unapologetically himself and is a fair grader/teacher. His classes fill up fast because of his reputation, 100% would recommend.

This is a pretty chill class and it is basically mock negotiations and discussions. Professor Goldman is very casual and likes to joke around. He is not online savvy at all, and will require you to print things out to turn them in. Sometimes the grading criteria was pretty confusing, but overall it is not too difficult to get an A in this class.

For mgmt 430, the class is only graded out of 100 points and quizzes and the midterm make up 66 of those points. The information in the lectures does not pertain to the quiz questions. This class has extremely easy concepts, yet because of how it is graded, it is difficult to get an A.

Barry is a great professor. The negotiations are super interesting. There is a heavily weighted single quiz that is difficult and based the textbook that we aren't given assignments to look at, but other than that quiz it is relatively easy. Just show up to negotiations. His voice is pretty monotone and hes deaf but he's a chiller. Long live Barry.

Barry teaches this class really well! He keeps it interactive, however it is very lecture-heavy. There are 2 tests throughout the semester and one group project. Class never goes the full time, so you are usually there for only 45 mins max. Attendance isn't mandatory. You will need the textbook to know about the court cases he expects you to learn.

Barry is a great professor! He takes attendance on days you do the in-person negotiations so other than that, you don't NEED to be there. There are no tests in this class. He is a super chill guy and pretty funny. He keeps the class lighthearted and really interactive.

Dr. Goldman is a great lecturer/professor. He keeps the class engaged and he never runs the full course of the lecture. Usually you run for about 1.5 hours MAX. He's an honest guy and really cares about the class/teaching. Definitely recommend this class for management students. You learn a lot about negotiations day in and day out. Most useful.

Class routine: go through the day's role playing exercise and listen to his lecture for a half hour or so. Great material, awesome teacher. If you're interested in the content of the class and want to learn it, you should do well.

He is tough but a fair grader and is accessible outside of class, exams are pretty tough but he does tell you what you need to know in lecture. Overall, I had a great time in Ken's class and learned the most out of any of my other classes

This professor will assign 4-6 assignments due every Sunday that all take hours to complete with little to no guidance. He will make you pay for a simulation that has three parts to it. When asking for help and guidance he doesn't give any. His teaching style is unorganized and cluttered with little direction.

I want to file a restraining order on this professor I dislike him so much. Unnecessary amount of projects and assignments for the least useful mandatory class at this school. The fact that he says you will fail if you don't complete all assignments or you can't pass is ridiculous. Ken, retire pal.

Lecture heavy class, but Ken is a good lecturer. Material can be tough. I've taken 3 of his classes and enjoyed all of them. He is always available during his office hours and responds promptly to emails. Overall, just pleasant to work with too.

What a joke. This guy couldn't make it in the real world even at the corporate wasteland Raytheon, so now he teaches. Don't bother asking any questions or you'll be given completely wrong, BS answers.

Class 473A was graded fairly. But it was different in 473B, grading was hard and sometimes unfair, exams were partly in the slides, and homework problem was not taught in the class. Students can't get a clear explanation of any confusion from the instructor.  You can take an A by self-learning from the textbook or online tutorial.

It is possible to get an A in this course if you put in the work. There are only 300 points in the course and 100 of them are the final (he provides a 50-page study guide). The assignments are challenging, the grading is very tough, and he does not clarify areas of confusion. He encourages you to work with your peers to do better. Not my favorite.

Ken's 473A class was taught beautifully. I found the class to be fair in grading and expectations.

His 473B course was much worse. I feel like there was a large lack of structure. The largest complaint is that the homework was often confusing and the book didn't help at all (way too high level). He teaches content after the work is due :/

I've taken 473A and 473B. The class structure consists of 3 exams, case studies, position papers and a final group project. Exams are fair, case studies are interesting, and group projects are good if your group works well.    I got A's in both classes easy grader as long as you put effort into your work. Ken is great my fav lecturer at Eller!
A good professor, class assignments are straightforward, but there is some added complexity on tests. Slide heavy class.

Class is VERY heavy on the readings and memorization from the readings. Tests are not incredibly intense but memorization is essential. Practical knowledge portions of the class are pretty easy to apply knowledge to. He has weird grading that is very tough on even the slightest errors. There are weekly quizzes that are hard.

The worst professor I have ever had. Does not explain anything, expects you to be an expert with tools like Microsoft Project without teaching you anything. Don't waste your time, you won't learn anything from this guy.

Worst teacher ever. Does not explain anything clearly. You have to ask 5 questions to get 1 question answered. He is a tough grader who does not care for students and if your on his bad side then he will not be nice back. Do not ever take a class with this guy.

He can be somewhat condescending or vague when answering student questions in the discussions. There is a lot of homework and reading, too much at times to really learn. You will need prior finance knowledge and strong excel skills to succeed. He seems to have a very serious, severe approach to teaching.

Very fair and expects hard work. A lot of homework to help enforce concepts and apply lecture and reading material. This was one of the best courses at UofA. Definitely tough, but like anything else, the effort you put in is reflected in your grades. The course had extra credit with him filming locations after his weekly introduction.

He's mid. Seems to know the material well. He's very serious personality. He didn't let us review the final so no idea how my test was graded and if it was fair. Jam packed with weekly homework and near the end, everything is so busy. He also takes off points if you post a response on the day an assignment is due. It was a stressful class.

If you put as much effort into learning the material as Matt does into teaching it, you will fail. 

That says it all.

One of the hardest classes you will ever take. The textbook really is far too much information to absorb effectively, and the lectures are cruelly absent of what you really need to know to perform well. Honestly, just be happy if you pass and put it out of your mind.

I thought students might have been dramatic with reviews but now I understand. When speaking to him it felt incredibly condescending and was not even listening to me but talking down to me. Likes to scare his students. I don't think those are qualities a teacher should have.

Matt is a strong believer that students should "struggle" with the material before receiving any kind of assistance. His lectures are unhelpful. His tone is often condescending and demeaning towards students requesting assistance.

Nice guy, but this professor cares more about himself than the students. He clearly does not understand individual student needs, and requires the class to do things a certain way so that he can stay organized. It's a lot of work to meet his standards, and if you're not first to answer on things, you will not earn points. It's a race, not a class.

Professor Haertzen means well and about Finance, but oh my the homework is so hard. If you ask questions about it he doesn't really help much; he just gives you a broad answer. Only 1 take home exam at the end and the smartbook and discussions aren't too hard at all. He's mid.

Attendance not mandatory but in-class participation is part of your grade. Rambles on and on about conceptual topics and speeds through any calculation problems, therefore completing the weekly homework can be difficult. Exams include: true/false, multiple choice, free response, and word problems. Choose your group members wisely (lots of projects)

He does not do any examples for you, and when he does, no work is written out, he just gives you the answers and doesn't write out the work. When test time comes around basically everything is cumulative and his best advice is "get it right so I don't have to grade it". If you're not in Wall Street Scholars, don't expect to do well in this class.

FIN 460 was a challenging course. The weekly homework assignments were difficult and lots of students had to use the TAs for help. The final exam is 30% of your grade and is very daunting to study for. The professor was very knowledgeable of the subject and responds quickly. Not my favorite class, but possible to get an A if you dedicate the time.
He is okay

All around nice professor. If you have even basic previous knowledge about stock market and investments this class is very basic and can be painfully boring. He teaches a lot of formulas and problems which he even says himself can be done easily on excel. This is really where he lost me as a teacher. A lot of busy work and not much conceptual work.

This is my first online course for my graduate program and I think he is a really great professor. There is quite a bit of work but as long as you put forth the effort, it shouldn't be that difficult. Tests are open book open notes and if you still struggled on some of the questions, he is more than happy to go over things and help you succeed.
Does a good job breaking down financial concepts and providing step by step instructions. If you pay attention and put forth the effort you will have no problems

The professor was 15 minutes late to a meeting I scheduled, suggested I just do certain projects myself since he couldn't grant me an extension and when I said I was struggling, he suggested I just take the W.

Matt is truly an innovative teacher. He makes you complete a cheesy quiz game in order to get a code to start your exam. He also makes students do all of their homework on excel which great to force students to master it. You will need it in the workforce anyways. If you have any questions on homework, he will be more than happy to help you out.

I recommend taking a different professor he is very critical on the written portion of the quizzes and grades harshly when its not specific enough but only when its too specific he accuses you of cheating.

Grade is based on 6 quizzes and 2 exams. If you miss class then you'll miss a lecture and fail a quiz. Quizzes are easy as long as you write down everything he says to study! I passed without the textbook doing this. He drops the 2 lowest quiz grades which is very helpful. Very passionate and funny professor

Took zoom class. Class sessions are basically him talking through slideshow of different art pieces. He defines key terms used (will be on quizzes). Attendance was mandatory since slides weren't uploaded. I used to not care about art at all until I this class, now I appreciate it much more! Just take notes, pay like 50% attention, & you'll be ok

I took 6 classes with this Professor: Painting, Independent Study, Printmaking, Art History 1 & 2, & Figure Drawing. I was denied an Art Major when I was young, & returned to college at age 66. The Textbook was huge, but thorough. The Professor gave good lectures, sometimes humorous. He inspired me to seek a Degree in Art History. Thanks!

This professor starts the first class by saying that NO ONE in his class ever gets an A unless they are very smart. He teaches his class with passion and it is interesting to listen but his quizzes and work dont match some things he taught. Some of the quizzes are simple as an true or false questions but he makes them complicated.

Bill makes the class interesting. There is a test every week on Thursdays, all you have to do is answer five multiple choice questions and a short paragraph about what you see in the picture he shows. As long as you read some of the stuff in the textbook and write the key terms in your paragraph you'll pass.
Tried to make a boring class interesting. Miss a lecture, fail the quiz. Quiz every Thursday on the previous two lectures. Pretty nice and passionate but take another class for the art credit.

I had professor Bill for my ARH141 class since the beginning I was really confused. We would take notes one class and the next one we had a quiz but the way he grades is really complex/ not clear, even the best guy in class had a B. He is a really cool person but I did not like the class at all.

I've taken almost every class this guy offers. Art History, you don't need the book just pay attention to the lectures. Painting/Drawing, he doesn't always explain what he expects from you so you're kinda on your own. If you really care about painting he will tear you apart with his critiques but so long as you turn in 4 paintings you'll pass

Blomquist is AWESOME, nice&funny. My class was twice a week ,Tues and Thurs, which Tues we would take notes and Thurs would be test day, and take notes right after the test. The test consisted of 5 T/F questions and one art piece analysis. EVERYTHING he says write it down. Without the book, I earned a B+ by studying my notes of EVERYTHING he said.

I was very skeptical while registering for this class but Professor Blomquist really made art history interesting and enjoyable for me. The only graded assignment are weekly quizzes which are 5 true or false and then one picture to describe. The tend to be fairly easy if you actually listen.

Had for painting class. Student-controlled room, you set up easel, have paints - SPECIFIC kind of paints and brushes. He will tell you the material, and assignments. Usually 4-5 paintings for a semester. He is hard about references. You MUST have one, from a master painter. Doesn't teach you, you learn on your own but he will direct you/help/advice

I love this class very entertaining and interesting, Professor explains everything clearly and in a way we can all understand. If you ask for help, he will help. He only gives quizzes on Thursdays based on your notes (make sure to take some notes). You do use the book, but only to study. Plus, if you miss a class you can watch it later online.
Really fun class. Great professor, always made it interesting. would highly recommend,

He is a great professor, extremely clear and detailed. You have to attend every class or fall behind. There's a quiz every week, 5 true&false questions and 1 slide description, you HAVE to nail the true and false question or it will kill your grade. If you do not study for quizzes do not expect and easy A. He assigns no Homework. Easy class overall

Professor Blomquist is excellent! His lectures are lively and very informative. Not only that, he facilitates class discussion very well - you won't be afraid to speak up! Another plus is that he is more concerned about his students understanding the ideas and history of art rather than just memorizing a bunch of little facts. Highly recommended!
Very detailed, but easy. I haven't had to use a textbook yet, everything is shown on slides.
Good teacher. He knows his stuff when it comes to drawing techniques, and I don't think anybody left the class without having improved their drawing skills.

Textbook is used for reading quizzes every few weeks. She posts study guides for each test, and as long as you use them, they shouldn't be a problem. Other than that there's a few essays - nothing that should be too difficult as long as you can do analysis.

If you are interested in the subject matter, take this class. Dr. Plax is extremely passionate about what she teaches which makes her one of the better professors I've had here at the UofA. However, the tests are extremely hard and there is one 7page paper that is difficult as well. Prepare to study for 10+ hours every week.

Her lectures aren't the most interesting if you aren't into art history. She knows her art history though. I would recommend going to every lecture because there's always at least one question from each lecture on her exams. She gives you all the questions for the exam prior to the test and they are all free response.

By far, one of the hardest classes I have ever taken. However, 5 years later I can recall all the information and have an incredible love to Baroque Art. Lovely woman, and overall an amazing challenging class.
She was very nice and available to help and review. Though the lectures are very lengthy, they are interesting. Tests are difficult, lots of details. All in all, I thought she was above average.

Her lectures are EXTREMELY boring!!! As previously mentioned it is difficult to stay awake. Her tests are ridiculous, she does give you study guides which is nice. However, you must spend hours preparing for the test even with the study guide because the slides and terms given are rarely mention in lecture or the book!!! Insane amount of writing.

Easy A if you don't mind writing 75+ pages total throughout the semester. Lots of reading - you will read nearly the entire textbook and several additional articles. Most writing involves relaying what you read, with little analysis. Hard to pay attention, let alone stay awake. TA who did grading was very helpful and nice though.

Awful. Worst class I have EVER taken. Her lectures are never engaging- her voice and accent are grating -she doesn't put the PPts online - absurd amount of writing (75 pgs by the end)- has no clue what is going on with her own class schedule -Please, take an ARH class with Moore, Ivey, or Albers instead!

5 page text analyses almost every 2 weeks 10-12 pgs essays for 3 take home exams 2 group discussion paper 5 pgs each LOTS of notes n terms to define in exam esays so you need to go to class n you get 3 absences total or 5% deduction for each absence boring just reads shows pics on ppt n 100+ pgs to read in art hisotry textbook on average GOOD LUCK

Worst class I have ever taken. Her voice is very monotonous, the material is boring, waaaaay too much work. We had a 10-30 page journal article to read before each class meeting. The class is long and boring, the tests are ridiculously difficult. She grades way too harshly. Nice woman, but would not recommend this class to anyone.
She loves art but she focuses too much on architecture and floorplans! ew boring!
She is funny so class isn't too boring.  but the tests are pretty hard so you need to study.
You will get way lower grades, but the tests are made to be that way with a very good curve. If you go to lectures every time you will do great in the class, that is the main thing.

This class was honestly great. The lectures were fascinating and Dr. Herbst was always more than willing to answer questions in detail. Don't worry about scary test scores, the curve will generally bring alarmingly low grades up to A's. Overall, this is a fantastic introduction to labor economics by someone who clearly cares about the subject.

His lectures are fine, but his exams are much more difficult than anything else we learn. He also doesn't tell us what to expect on the exams. However, he's not an awful person bc he has MASSIVE curves where he will curve the average to an A at the course end. It's almost impossible to not get a B, but it can be a little difficult to get an A.

I like this Professor as a person but I cant give a great rating. He doesnt provide much insight as to what will be on exams. Its a bunch of questions we have never seen before. Be ready to fail the exams unless you have prior knowledge. He gives a huge curve at the end. HUGE. Im happy about it but the unknown was pretty darn stressful.

PROS: Really cool guy very detailed on the information he provides Tests can be easy IF you study enough HW is connected w/ lectures and textbook CONS: Only graded on two HWs and three exams (Online only) Some subjects can be vague on the book and lectures Ex. Lorenz Curve DO NOT START STUDYING A DAY BEFORE MAKE EVERY EXAM COUNT

Dr. Herbst did an excellent job teaching labor economics and making it interesting since it can be a pretty dry topic. He made sure that everyone who wanted to understand the content did. Although attendance isnt mandatory, he sparsely quizzes so I recommend attending lectures. All in all, Dr. Herbst is fair and wants every student to succeed.

Max is the best, most caring professor I've ever had. This course is very difficult, but it's definitely manageable with Max as your professor. He cares about all of his students and is willing to help in any way he can. Definitely go to office hours often and study a lot for exams.

Max has entertaining lectures and gives great feedback following your quizzes and exams. Appreciated all the office hours he held and how quick he got grades back to you. He is a very caring professor that is always happy to help you if needed. The exams are challenging and a lot more difficult that the practice exams but he gives partial credit.

Don't get me wrong he is a great person super nice and offers a lot of support but he makes the quizzes and test way more challenging than they need to be which gets so frustrating. You need to know the stuff like the back of your hand because there will always be some kind of trick once it comes to the tests which is annoying.

Max is an amazing professor who is not only challenging but also fair. He makes every lecture relevant and entertaining with his nearly limitless energy. Max bends over backwords for his students and even when his wife is giving BIRTH to his CHILD he didn't stop grading assignments in a timely manner.

So kind! His level of care for students genuinely impressed me. Good lecturer, but the class was challenging. There are so many resources and you can rewatch lectures. Practice and understand the process, don't memorize problems, as his exams aren't the same as the practices! Will be the same content, but questions won't be asked the same way.

He's one of the top 1% of professors. He provides individualized feedback, holds many office hours, and offers helpful worksheets. To prepare for quizzes and exams, complete the practice ones on D2L until you can do them perfectly on your own and review slides. Understanding practice problems and key concepts is key to success.
Max is the BEST professor you will take! He is so energetic and truly passionate about accounting... 10/10.

Max is one of the best professors you will meet during your academic career. Max is genuinely the reason I made it through the last few weeks of my undergraduate. Max is more than a professor he cares about you in and outside of the classroom. Thank you Max for everything.

Max goes above and beyond for his students. Especially during the pandemic, he set up so many office hours, and was always welcoming to students. More than that, he was legit excited when students came in to ask questions, and was always there with a whiteboard to help explain. Tries so hard in lectures to help you understand. Take his classes!

If there was ever a time I truly believed that angels walked among us, it was taking this course. Max is so passionate, not just by his teaching, but moreso by his students. He truly believes in every person that walks through his doors and would probably take a bullet for one if it came down to it. I feel lucky to have had him as a professor.

Really cares about his students. Ver;y enthusiastic and genuine. The material is difficult and so are the quizzes and exams, but he is very lenient about grading and partial credit. He also grades on a curve. Attendance counts for participation points but isn't 100% mandatory.

One of the best professors in Accounting department ever! He cares about everyone and gives the useful feedback. The most impressive thing that Max did is he creates quiz and exam problems by himself...

Brian is a passionate, knowledgeable professor and a strong role model for future teachers. While he emphasizes caring and relationship-building, the class often felt repetitive and lacked depth in exploring new ideas. I learned how to be a compassionate educator but not how to effectively teach science in my classroom.
If I could give Brian a 10/5 I would. I love this man. Truly the most inspiring and best professor I've had at NAU.

Brian is the best professor that I have ever had. I have had him as a professor for several courses and his classes have always been my favorite. He is respectful, caring, and obviously loves what he does. I have never learned more in a classroom environment either. 100% recommend.

I couldn't say enough good things about Professor Stone! Every time I left his classes, I felt a huge sense of purpose in becoming a future educator. He's easily the most respectful and understanding professor I've had. Take any and every course you can with him, you won't regret it. His methods courses were extremely eye-opening.
The best professor I have ever had! Very inspirational for aspiring teachers.

Hands down the best professor I have ever had!! I have learned so much from Dr. Stone. If NAU had more professors like Dr. Stone NAU could change the world!! Take any class that you can from him you will not regret it!! Truly cares for his students and how they will impact their future classrooms.
Prof Stone remains one of my favorite instructors from my graduate program

Brian is amazing! He is probably one of the best teachers I have had in my life. He is respectful, understanding, engaging, and passionate and knowledgable about what he does. He has completely changed my mindset when it comes to teaching methodology and I couldn't be happier about it. If you have the opportunity to take his class, do it!!!!
Best. Teacher. Ever. Cares about all students. Creates a pathway to success for all students. Overall super genuine and honestly the best teacher.
The best professor I've had at NAU! Brian is very passionate about education and changing it for the better.

Brian is one of the best professors I have every had. I have had him for four classes now and he truly cares about education and what is best for his students. You can go to him for anything and he will always be understanding. I would 100% recommend him to anybody to take one of his classes. Great guy and an amazing professor.

Brian has great ideas regarding progressive education. He always has research to back up his ideas. He really gave me a lot of confidence and excitement going into teaching. I feel way more prepared from these two classes vs every other class put together. He doesn't believe in tests or homework and has an alternative to every traditional tactic.
The methodology class, not learning about science itself. Hands-on activities, which is always fun. We even judged a science fair in Corondado. Definitely a great teacher!
AMAZING!! He is a great professor and I would absolutely recommend his classes and anything that he teaches. HE is inspirational to future teachers and I can't wait to be in his lectures again

Brian is an awesome teacher! Doesn't really believe in homework (Few assignments outside of class) makes learning interesting and fun. Would definitely recommend taking him for any methods courses you can. Really enjoyed his class.
Love Brian! Doesn't believe in homework!
He is an amazing instructor. He changed my views on homework and take him when you can for your methods courses.
Brian is a great teacher. I learned a lot this semester.
Brian was a great teacher! Really amped up my interest in teaching and gave everyone a chance to share their opinions. Very respectful and helpful!!!

This is one of the most helpful professors I have ever met.  He is fair and respectful.  I took all of my methods courses with him and I am a better teacher because of it.  Take his classes where ever you can.

If you want to gain brain cells instead of losing them, I suggest you do not take this course. Hills is a very AI-reliant grader/Instructor. Extra credit is only given if you do revision based on AI suggestions. Her grading is so niche and nitpicky no reason If you want to keep a good GPA and a full head of hair, I recommend you stay away from her

Took BCOM 214 online; it was not a bad class in terms of homework; however, the graders are very picky. Would not recommend for people trying to keep a 4.0 GPA, although it is doable, it is a lot more work.

Instructions on assignments are very unclear. It seemed the facilitator who graded my work was trying to find the smallest things to take points off for. I disagreed with most of what I got points off for. There were grades in the grade book that were not in the course schedule. Would not recommend.

the most uncommunicative communications class there is. There's a lack of instructions for assignments & some are just incorrect. Links don't work, professors are late to meetings & they obviously grade using AI rather than reading work themselves. Also, the entire case competition is a research-driven project, not a communications-driven one.

Too subjective as a grader. Refuses to provide constructive feedback and becomes hostile if you ask her to elaborate on grades. You can be an honors English student, and she will still find something to nitpick rather than taking the entire assignment into consideration.

Her videos say one thing but then the assignment says another. Very nit picky, she WILL find something wrong. This is the second class I have taken with her and I have never gotten a 100 in anything other than participation.

This class sucked. I had a project with 4 other people, across 4 different time zones and work schedules and we had to coordinate a live 15min presentation, given in the middle of the day on a Monday. I don't know if she's ever even considered we have jobs? There were also 2 checks in we had to do live on Zoom with her in the middle of the workday.
great and kind professor. Very fast feedback and had office hours every day.
Extremely professional and very fast grader. Always responded within 24 hours. I learned a great deal from this course.

Ms. Hills took so much time working with us! she responds within minutes on emails and is always happy to set up  Zoom meeting, morning, noon, night, and weekends. She has an impressive resume, having worked in the US Senate and she isn very witty. best online teacher ever!
Best teacher in elller
Great teacherrrr
The single worst professor I've ever had. Unclear instructions and grades harshly for no apparent reason. When asked about very questionable grading she has no response just ignores it.

No clear grading criteria or assignment instructions, and when asking for clarification you get no helpful answers and sometimes attitude. If you do the assignments correctly, you get a 90%, which makes no sense, shouldn't you get a 100%? There were multiple graders who graded differently and had different criteria so grading is very inconsistent.

Its an 18 week course down to 7 weeks so obviously little time to write papers (about 1 per week) but clear directions on what to do and allows extra credit when you fix the errors on your papers when she grades them. Its an easy A

Teacher grades ridiculously hard but won't give you any sample material to look over. She will want a paper done a specific way give you none of the resources to know how she wants it done and then after you somehow figure it out she will mark you done 20 points for having one sentence that could be improved. does not respond to emails or contact
Class is very easy but you learn a lot. You get a random group project but as long as you put in effort you will be fine.

His class is great! Lectures are well organized with questions built within the lecture modules to test your knowledge. Most of these questions show up on the weekly quizzes and exams. Quizzes are typically 30 questions/1 hr time limit and exams are 50 questions/2 hr time limit. Total course 7 Quiz, 2 Exams, 4 Labs and group project. Non proctored.

Take this class if you need an MIS elective!! Literally so easy, all the quizzes and exams are on quizlet (no examity) and the labs are so easy and lowkey fun. I don't even think he looks at the labs because I got 100% on all of them 10 mins after submission. Plus the class is self paced so you can work on your own time.

Professor Hoopes does an amazing job teaching cybersecurity to help get an idea of the cybersecurity industry. This was also an easy and fun class since its just 3 labs, 7 quizzes, 2 exams, and a group project. No examity along with the exams and quizzes being open note, one of the best and most fun classes out there. Professor Hoopes is fantastic.

Pretty easy but also most importunely interesting class. No examity, open note exams and quizzes and a group project. Though the video lectures can be long, professor Hoopes still does an amazing job explaining everything as best as he does in the lectures. Really does an amazing job giving a basic idea of what to expect in cybersecurity if you go.

Professor Hoopes is a really good prof and the class it self isn't that bad at all. The quizzes are pretty easy since the provided lectures can help and they're open note just like the exams as well. The video lectures too are very helpful as well in order to get a better understanding.

Definitely recommend this class! As long as you do all the work you'll probably get an A.. I took it in 7 weeks online and it was pretty easy. Theres 3 labs, 2 open note exams, 1 group project, and weekly quizzes. Everything on the quizzes/exams comes directly from the notes provided for you and the labs were graded pretty easily.

Professor assigns online quizzes that make no sense, very little in the quizzes actually matches up with online lectures not to mention they are riddled with grammatical mistakes and incomplete sentences. Very little feedback given on assignments. Really disappointed, unfortunately the only professor that teaches this online course for the program.
The worst professor I have ever had, he got confused when doing math problems with left the class confused very often. Going to class is pointless
Bad lecturer. Inexperienced & unprepared. He makes financial concepts even more confusing.

Sweetest professor EVER! Taking her 471 course was the best decision I've made at Eller and I feel sorry for the students who had 471 with someone else! She incorporated a lot of fun activities/videos in her lectures. Songcui is AWESOME!!
This was by far one of my favorite classes at UofA. Professor Songcui is an amazing teacher and person.

She is slightly hard to understand due to her accent but very well intentioned. Her participation schema is lousy and requires active attendance every class but she shows lots of fun videos and movies.
Only BNAN teacher obsessed with giving out 0s any other one is better

Professor Gray is an amazing professor. He taught his 8 am class, and he taught the material exceptionally well, and it was applicable to real-life situations. He made everything easy to learn and was always willing to help, especially during final group projects. I loved the class and everything you learn can be used in the real world.

The BNAN277 lab was pretty easy, with weekly case or Tableau assignments. Professors Hunter and McClintock were very engaging and informative through the recorded lectures, showing how to do all but the writing portions in depth. Both professors were also very helpful during the final group project for BNAN277.

He is a great help when it comes to the discussions. He is always available to help. The highlight of the semester is when he fell flat on his bottom when he tried going to student to help in the class. Really good guy and he has is own youtube channel if you are having trouble with excel. IT IS A BIG HELP! Would definitely take this class again.

I have never seen a more hardworking and engaging professor. He makes sure that students get access to as much practice as they can. He is extremely accessible to ask questions and truly cares about his students. AMAZING and funny lectures. This university doesn't deserve him.

Great professor. Clear criteria, very engaging and inspirational. He knows how to follow up with students as well and make sure everything is ok. I will definitely take a course with him again in future. Easiest 5 stars I have ever given.

He was amazing for stats. Never really enjoyed stats but this semester was so much easier because of him. Go to class and pay attention, quizzes are online and open note and shouldn't confuse you! Gives quite a few EC points & good feedback. Answers quickly as well. Group project kinda sucked because my group, but if you reach out he is helpful.

He is awesome! He's so easy to contact out of class and never took more than like 2 hours to get back to me. Grades very clear and gives a lot of feedback on everything. Quizzes are online and open note. Discussion is super helpful. There is no final but there is a group project that is a lot of work especially if you get a bad group.

This class is fantastic! Gray makes the lectures interesting and helps make stats relevant to real life. The quizzes are open note, and he provides an extremely helpful formula sheet. There is homework after each chapter through the online textbook, but it isn't too difficult. He answers his emails very quickly and always helps when needed!

Statistics is boring and repetitive but Gray made this class easy and enjoyable. He teaches in a way that makes the concepts relatively simple to understand and his lectures are quite entertaining. He responds to emails quickly and will happily help you whenever you need it. The formula sheet will be your best friend for the exams.
Overall awesome and funny guy. His lectures are supper engaging and he tries to apply the things learned to real life. If you're looking for a stats professor, Gray is the one!
Gray is such a goat, he teaches everything you learn in 276 in a week and it is so much clearer. I could not imagine taking this class without him.

Professor Gray wants his students to learn and actually puts in the work to make BNAD 277 engaging and interesting. There is a lot to learn in this class, and you definitely have to put in the time and energy to do well. If you have to take a stats class, Dr. Gray is the way to go.

One of my favorite professors. Very casual teaching style, made lectures super engaging. Made a formula sheet that had everything you needed to know and was allowed for use on every assignment. Despite the formula sheet making class easy, I learned a ton about stats principles and how to use Excel. He and John are both fantastic

This guy is at the rare intersection of teaching something well and teaching something that's going to be actually useful to you at Eller. I took his course years ago - what he teaches is foundational and so natural that I've never forgotten it. I know grad students who didn't go here for their undergrad who can't read a logistic regression output.

You have to take Bnad277. He's the best professor you can pick compared to the other professors. You have to put in the work. All lessons and practice are recorded and posted online so you don't need to goto class in person. Exams are online and open book. Only thing is the hwk. Time consuming af. Other than that, he's an amazing professor.

Stats is a hard subject for many, but Dr. Hunter's approach makes it all the more easy. You can tell he cares about his students a lot and wants to set them up for success in and out of the classroom. Quizzes are a bit difficult, but open book open note. Dr. Hunter has somehow made statistics extremely easy to digest even with little background.

Dr. Hunter is a very engaging lecturer, and does a good job of making the material make sense. That being said, don't get too confident and make sure you are getting good grades on homework early on. The lab for this class is a headache. But, would recommend Dr. Hunter!
tests were difficult, but material was explained clearly during class and provided a ton of study materials so we could be successful.

Gray is fantastic and one of the best at Eller. He truly cares about the subject he teaches as well as his students. If you spend literally 15 minutes studying you will pass all the quizzes. You can earn 100% on weekly HW by retaking it. Everything is open note/book.

Gray is a good professor and is all around very likable. The subject matter is hard, but I felt that the tests were unnecessarily difficult and were often filled with confusing/trick questions. If I had to go back I would have chosen another professor. However, Gray still is an all around good choice. The labs and HW are not too difficult.
BNAD has difficult content, but Gray goes out of his way to make it as easy as possible for students. He is an EXCELLENT teacher and may actually be my favorite teacher I've ever had.

Gray is the absolute best!! This was my second statistics course, and he taught everything the first class was supposed to teach in two weeks of this class. Cannot say enough positive things about this professor. When we're in-person, he brings his husky during the final and lets you pet it :) Genuinely cares about his students!!!

This is how professors SHOULD have handled online classes. Great lectures that you could watch outside of normal class time. Very accessible and truly understanding (I had health issues and he always catered to my needs). Material was tough but I'm glad to have had him as the prof.

Dr. Hunter is hands down the best professor I have ever had. You can tell he is passionate about what he is teaching and really wants every student to pass his class. He and John make a good team and do a great job explaining the course material and are always available to answer questions even late at night. Def sad they can't teach all my classes

TAKE THIS CLASS! This is the best prof I have had yet. I wish him and John taught all my classes, that's how great they are. If you put in some work, you can definitely get an A. He gives extra credit and is easily available. The course is organized and expectations are clear. The class is literally structured to help you.

Professor Gray was by far the best professor that I have ever encountered. Day or night he and John (another professor in the course) were more than ready and happy to help you through Discord. There were countless times where I posted something on there past 8 PM (latest 11 PM) and immediately got a response within minutes. Unmatched commitment...

Professor Hunter has great video lectures and video walk throughs of all assignments. He also has a ton of office hours on discord and answers questions quickly. Live lecture was never required and extra credit opportunities were offered. I would highly recommend Professor Hunter.

He's a great teacher, definitely knows what he's doing. He genuinely wants you to pass his class. Provides a ton of study guides and video walkthroughs of him explaining the answers. All tests are open notes, open internet. The only way you'd fail with him is if you didn't try.

Gray is an amazing professor. Stats is not an easy class to take, but Gray is always there for help. He is great in office hours and will respond to emails in a timely manner. Tests were around 60% I think but he made sure you were prepared for them. I would highly recommend showing up to class as you can get some points & tips on hard test areas.

If you had Brent Marinan last semester, expect to actually learn stuff in this BNAD class. Gray is awesome and very sweet. His tests and homework are very straight-forward and he is alwayssss available. Seriously, like I have emailed him at 10 at night and he gets right back to me. He's just awesome, but actually teaches you things lol

This class is hard simply because of the subject, but Gray makes this class a million times better than it would have been. In my BNAD 276 class, I had a different professor and didn't learn the basics needed for this class, BUT Gray did a phenomenal job of teaching us and making sure we understood everything.

Gray is very funny! Stats is not a fun class, and he doesn't just sprinkle extra credit if you're not understanding it. I highly recommend attending all lectures if possible because he really rewards kids by coming to class, he'll tell you important tips for homework and tests. He is a little intimidating cause he's so smart, but overall nice!

Gray is absolutely the best lecturer I've ever had. He explains everything thoroughly, and repeats important themes. His study guides were true to the tests, and he is dedicated beyond belief to making a tough topic accessible to everyone. Engaging, easy to follow, just show up! Office hours were basically one on one pick his brain until you get it
Gray is the best professor I've had in my 5 semesters at UofA. He works hard to make sure you understand the material, and he offers plenty of help to students outside of class.

Gray is the man. I love him. Stats isn't easy & it requires hard work because the material is difficult, but Gray enables his students to succeed. Go to class, do the homework, study & you'll pass. He is also so caring & understanding. I had issues arise around the time of a test & he let me take the test a week later. He's an amazing professor!

The best class I have taken in college. Professor Gray modified the class halfway through and it was great. He taught us about personal finance and started every day with a something interesting that related both to Economics and what was going on in the real world around us. Warren Buffet Documentary and Index fund podcast are the best.

Gray is amazing. His lectures are very useful and he provides a lot of material to help you really learn to material and prepare for the exams. You can tell Gray really cares about his student's success. This is the only math class I've excelled in because of the way Gray teaches.

Gray is awesome. The textbook is so easy to read, stats almost seems like a breeze! He gives many opportunities to get help outside of class, holds review sessions, and offers extra credit. To add, he is hilarious in lectures. Highly recommend!!

Professor Daddy is kinda sassy but only when it comes to protecting John the lab instructor. If he hears that there is even a WHIFF of disrepect towards John then steam will come out of his ears. Otherwise, the lectures and labs are helpful/engaging and I'd highly recommend reading the textbook because he wrote it to be relevant to the class. A++

Works harder than any prof out there. Does so much practice, reviews for every exam, does additional office hours the night before the exam. If you put in the time, he will help make you successful. The class is a lot of work, but it is a 4 credit class, so don't get lazy or you can get behind quickly.

Professor Gray straight kills it. Stats is not easy, but he makes everything so relevant to the business world. Doesn't get bogged down in theoretical stuff, but makes sure you understand the concepts and how to apply them. Gives test review questions with detailed explanations so you aren't stuck guessing why you got it wrong. Take him if you can

Gray is an awesome professor. Stats can be a very difficult and abstract subject; Gray makes it easy to understand and learn the concepts. Tests are worth 60% of your final grade, but he explains everything very thoroughly. John is great too, he explains things slightly differently than Gray which is great if one topic didn't make sense.

Gray is an amazing professor, he's a young guy who really cares about the class. I thought I would get a way lower grade because of how hard stats are for me, but he drops lower homework grades and gives some extra credit which helps a lot! What I like the most is that you can get a good grade even if you don't do so well on the tests.

Professor Gray is AMAZING. I cant say enough good things about him. Stats is difficult, but with him and A is possible. Come to class, take good notes and listen. Homework is really easy as long as you pay attention in class.
He said riiiight 150 times in one hour

I've had Gray twice now, and he is simply great at what he does! Both classes were not that easy, but he is good at making the material relatable and he makes coming to class something to look forward to. If you can take one of his classes, do it! Even if you have to wake up early.

Professor Gray is a great teacher especially as stats is extremely hard to learn. I did struggle in his class with exams, but the labs and extra credit make up for it. The first exam was tough but they gradually get better. Gray is funny and cares about his students as he held extra office hours and review sessions for us outside of the class.

Mr. Hunter is probably the best professor Ive had at the UofA so far. He has the ability to make students laugh and the ability teach some pretty boring content in a very effective method that is easy to grasp. He cares about his students success and also gives opportunities for extra credit. He is an overall great dude who is good at what he does

Fantastic professor. The class is not easy and it takes a lot of work, but if you show up and do the homeworks/labs it can makeup for a less than stellar performance on the exams. First exam is by far the hardest so don't slack off early. Office hours and review sessions are really helpful and he is always willing to help you succeed

Gray is a fantastic professor. He really cares about student's succeeding in his class. The class is very difficult and he does a great job of dumbing it all down for you. Quality professor. Definitely recommend taking him if you can. Go to his office hours to get help because he is super helpful. There is also a fat curve at the end of the class

Solid teacher, lectures were easy enough to understand. Really tries to dumb down some unnecessarily diffucult and wordy concepts to make it easier to understand which is appreciated. Stats is a hard subject but Hunter makes the knowledge somewhat more accessible. Truly wants you to succeed and has lots of office hours.

First semester here, and making a great start. He's passionate, and absolutely knowledgeable. You really need to study the homework, as well as the practice tests before an exam. The exam's are a strong majority of the weight of the class, as math classes usually go. This class is not easy. Don't fall behind.

First semester professor - dude cares, that's for sure. He does whatever it takes to make you pass the class as it's a gate-keeper for Eller. The content is hard, the subject is hard, the work is hard. Study, do the HW, do the Labs, and you'll pass. This is a math class and if you barely got by math up to this point you're going to struggle.
Super helpful and really cares about students. Although some topics are hard, but he gives super clear explanations.

His course is super hard and has a huge amount of hw. DO NOT take his ECON 520, which is said to be a probability and statistics course for "entering graduate students" but actually covers almost everything. I can't believe that the mid-term exams can cover the two probability and statistics courses that I took in the math department.

His teaching style is like putting everything in the class if you know something already. Probably we are expected to learned the middle level of econometrics before. We usually lost in the class and feel helpless. But he welcomes all kinds of questions and wrote down every steps for us. TA is super helpful for HW. Exams are open book and easy.

I have done 16 problem sets. Each problem set contains at least 8-10 questions with bunch sub-questions under each. Usually spend two days on one problem sets. Also, his lecture is very ambiguous and unnecessarily hard, and you can give him suggestions all you want, but he won't listen. Oh, you also need to learn R and python all by yourself.
Really nice guy, clearly loves his job.Class is easy, gives a note sheet for exams. Two midterms, no cumulative final.

Jarvis has great lectures. There is 3 lecture videos a week, and 2 assignments for homework a week. You go in person once a week to class to go over what you learned in the video, class attendance/presentation is a kahoot. Jarvis is funny, makes class bearable, and breaks down econ easily.

Super funny and engaging lectures. I struggled with feeling like I had enough resources to prep for the 2 exams but he gives a study guide. Only in person once a week and it's super easy to get questions answered through his discord. It's easy to do well on hw, the main concern for me was doing well on the exams.

A nice and funny guy, class was mandatory, he doesn't give any extra credit or do extensions, so be sure to do homework and assignments on time. The homework content was fairly easy, tests were harder, but he was allowed a cheat sheet. Overall, he was definitely passionate aboout the subject, and the course load was manageable.
He is passionate about teaching, caring and hilarious. I would definitely take his class again

Overall I really enjoyed this class, Jarvis makes it really engaging and the workload is pretty manageable. However this semester there were only two exams, a midterm and the final, on 6-8 chapters that weigh 20% each. So if you do bad on the first exam, it is really hard to bump up your grade up after that, there's no extra credit either.

No materials uploaded to D2L, so def go to class, he takes attendance by Kahoot, and you actually learn stuff/can take notes from that. Overall, very nice professor, very funny, helps you as much as possible. Tests are lowkey hard, but you can have a cheat sheet. (40-question multiple-choice on a bubble sheet). Massive class. Would take again!
Definitely my favorite prof. I liked the way he did lectures, they were really engaging. He's pretty funny, I would take him again if i could
My favorite professor t
Great professor! Always made sure to say hello and make sure the lectures were interesting. Weekly homework, which was easy and took an hour. Weekly video lectures with questions.

I was nervous since Jarvis just changed the tests to in person however pretty sure he made them simpler now. He curves tests and the homework is busy work but easy to complete. Class is fairly simple if you put in a little bit of work to study for the tests and make sure you complete all your work.
take his class!!

He's a really nice guy who tried his hardest to make the in-class portion of his class fun. Everything is online except a one-day-a-week lecture on Kahoot. If you use your resources, this class should be an easy A.
He made his class fun with good lectures. There were a lot of notes, but he still managed to make class fun. You can tell he really cares about his students.

He is a great professor, class meets once a week and it's a Kahoot while he talks about ECON and other fun stories. If you have a hard time staying engaged I would recommend his class! The HW is very manageable and overall a very easy class if you apply yourself and stay on top of deadlines, he doesn't except late work bc he has too many students!!

My favorite socially benevolent dictator, SBD for short. If you like Kahoot you'll love this class, it's all you ever do. Super easy A, and he wears funky clothes all the time. Likes to talk about his 1232131 kids tho, but thats fine. Tests were online and open note. He curves too. Love this white guy!

Justin is a great professor. He is engaging and makes learning economics interesting. He tries his best not to make it another boring business class and adds personality to it. The tests were kind of hard, but I still passed them. As long as you show up and do the work, you will do well.
Lectures are pretty interesting, just hate the program he uses for homework as you had 3 tries to get a question right or you lose all points to that question.

I loved Jarvis, his lectures are very engaging and he teaches concepts well. But don't be fooled by the one class a week as opposed to twice like the other professor has. There's about an hour or more of online lecture videos you have to watch with questions throughout. The tests are kind of hard, but they are online and open note...

I am not good at math and dreaded the idea that I had to take Econ. If you're like me, Dr. J is your best chance. It's hybrid. About 5 assignments due every week, even test week. Tests are online. His TAs are going to answer your questions and concerns but he is super chill Im sure if you needed extra help he'd be there.

I took this class for my business major and I will say that this was the only class that I loved to go too. I never missed because his class is super fun and easy. everything can be Chatgpt and the tests seem hard but he doesn't honor lock them so its easy to pass them and just look everything up.
LOVE LOVE LOVE this man! This man made me love Econ!
Guys please take his class, he's amazing. You won't regret it. I'm not even an econ major but I lovedddddd his lectures
Guys please take this professor, he's amazing, you won't regret it. Im not even an econ major but he was great
Justin Jarvis makes economics come alive with his humor and engaging teaching style. The tests are challenging, but with helpful resources like unstuck ai, I was able to excel in the class.
Jarvis makes such an effort to make his students experiences amazing! He's sooo chill and the sweetest ever, I am a slow learner and his teaching style made this course so much smoother for me!!!

This class isn't too difficult. Attendance is graded via Kahoot. Videos, learning curves, and homework are due each week. There are three exams and none are dropped. The study guides aren't that helpful. There are no extra credit opportunities. But if you put in the work, it's not too hard to pass the class.
Jarvis is an amazing professor and makes difficult topics easy! He is very passionate which helps you enjoy the class more.

Easy course for a foundational. Jarvis was accessible through discord and his TA team was always great for assistance. Tests are straight forward enough and relate to the key concepts. The video lectures due during the week can be tedious due to how they're set up to be graded, and no retakes. Luckily all assignment types have drops. Good class !

Jarvis is fun and engaging. There are 3 videos, learning curves, and end of chapter assignments due every week. Stay on top of it but he does send email reminders every week. Attendance is counted via lecture Kahoots. There are 3 tests, they're not easy so don't slack as none get dropped.

He is one of the funniest and most interactive professors I've ever had. There are weekly homework assignments that don't take too long and 3 tests throughout the year. The class is easy if you put in the effort to understand the concepts each week.

Super nice teacher. Kahoot is used for attendance once a week, and all other work is online. Exams aren't too difficult if you use the textbook, and the assignments don't take that long. He is passionate about the topic and wants to make people laugh.
loved him and all the kahoots
Jarvis is a really good professor who really cares about you. That's all you need to know, Econ 200 is not hard and he makes sure it isn't.
I enjoyed this class, but be prepared to take lots of notes because exams are difficult without them. The lectures are super easy if you have some prior knowledge of the material.
JARVIS IS AMAZING. The Economics of Strategy was one of the most fulfilling and informative classes I took during my time at the university! Highly recommend :)

Professor Justin Jarvis is good at explaining course material in an engaging manner. However, his exams are challenging and often come with limited time for completion. Despite this, his humor and genuine concern for his students' success create a supportive learning environment that promotes growth and understanding.
He loves to teach and you can see that through his lectures. It is easier to learn when the professor actually likes what he's teaching.

Jarvis is a funny guy who somehow makes basic economics decent to learn about. The tests are pretty easy, but be prepared to put a decent amount of time each week into the video lectures since they are for credit.

I found the class easy and I can tell Dr.J likes teaching and Econ so that makes class better. I didn't like how we had to watch lecture videos and then sit in his class for an hour listening to him repeat the same things we just watched for homework. I wish he would just choose one for students to do.

Best professor ever. he made the class super well organized and made it easy to turn in homeworks. Exams were taken online which is super nice and stress relieving. He had fun in his class and ocasionally brought in guest speakers which was cool.
Great guy and a pretty solid Gen Ed class. A nice overview of basic ECON. Be ready for the tests, but lots of helpful resources. Should be fine if you show up and do the work

Professor Jarvis is the best professor I've ever had at the University of Arizona. His ECON 407 taught me so many knowledge by using real world examples such as studying models from different companies across many industries. From all ECON student this class is highly recommended to take.

We only met on Thursdays and play a Kahoot! Based on the lecture videos he records. He makes economics exciting. The class is fun and engaging. Attendance is required. Tests are weighted heavy but the homework and panopticon lecture videos balance out the weight of the exams.

Meet once a week on Thursdays to take a quiz on the assigned reading. The articles are super interesting and I learned more in this course than probably any other course at the Arizona. 3 tests which aren't memorization questions, they're about the application of real-life economic theories. Would highly recommend ECON 407.
I loved this course, Dr.J is amazing and the content was interesting 100% recommend it.

LOVE CLUB 200. Prof Jarvis sets Econ200 up in a way that it is easy to succeed! His lectures are entertaining and almost fun, as its a Kahoot once a week. The online homework can be difficult, but if you take notes its manageable. 
The exams are difficult, but he is always available to answer questions. Definitely take Econ w Jarvis!

Jarvis is truly a fantastic teacher. I struggled with many of the concepts, and after going to his classes I always felt so much better. Weekly online lectures + quiz, 1 in person class per week, and 3 exams. Cannot recommend this guy enough.
AMAZING teacher. Cares about his students and actually wants us to learn not just pass the class. Jarvis is really a great example of what a teacher should be.

I've had Jarvis 2x, & he's always my favorite course. He explains concepts with examples that just make sense. 407 is super fun, you read 1 case/week you're quizzed on, it's a conceptual heavy class. He's lively and makes the material enjoyable. Read the cases, participate in discussions, watch the lecture vids, & you'll be good. He's the best!!!

You can tell that he really loves his job, super energetic and always has the best vibe. He really cares about his students. There are 3 exams. Lots of videos with quizzes that are worth point and 2 other homework assignments that are due on saturdays. try and pay attention because there is NO EXTRA CREDIT. Attendance is taken by kahoot!

Jarvis is a good teacher and his lectures are fine but there's a lot of online homework and videos you have to watch and work on outside of class. Inquizitive is one of the most annoying homework platforms and Smartwork is even worse (the 2 websites he uses). Overall the class isn't bad if you just need to take it for an easy online econ credit.

Jarvis made a boring topic fun and easy to understand. All the materials were clearly laid out and we knew what was expected. If you do the study guides (he does not give you the answer key to the study guides), you will do good on the tests. He was a great professor, and was easy to get in touch with his preceptors for any questions you had.

really great professor! i had to take the class for prereq for vet school and did not come from a business background. he is really great at explaining and makes class fun with Kahoots, music playing that you request, and discord which makes him very accessible outside of class. makes a large lecture hall feel small.
Super good and informative class. He puts a lot of time into making the content understandable and relatable. If you are taking ECON 200, this is the class you want.

The class is easy enough and Dr. Jarvis is a very fun and engaging lecturer, but there is definitely a lot of hw. He works mainly through discord and online lectures (one in-person a week), and teaches very well. For those taking honors, there are only 3 extra papers that are easy enough, just don't procrastinate them. Definitely recommend!

If you want a great, awesome, enthusiastic professor who cares about their subject, TAKE ECON 200 with Dr. JARVIS. Sure Econ 200 is tough at times, but there's lots of help. The kahoots are fun to do and the material is actually very enjoyable and applicable to real life. Overall, hands down best professor I've had thus far in my college career.

Dr. J. is the most engaging college professor I have ever had. He tries to make his class fun, and you learn a lot from going to his in-person lectures as well as by watching his pre-recorded video lectures. There is a decent amount of homework, but if you pay attention in class and while watching the lecture videos, you will get a good grade.

Three tests that are worth a majority of your grade. You have a lot of drops in this class which is nice. Second test hard, first and third relatively easy. Have organized notes. There were a lot of people including myself who were really close to getting an A (like .10 percent away), and he refused to round up grades, pretty upsetting and weird.

This class is fairly easy but the material can be overwhelming and difficult to retain. Attendance is a part of your grade, so plan to go to class (class is only one day a week). Justin is very enthusiastic so the lectures aren't bad at all. There are 3 open note exams, worth 40% of your grade, so be prepared with thorough and organized notes.

He makes it a point to make this class personalized and more individualized even though its a huge lecture. 
Great professor, super enthusiastic and intriguing. Genuine guy.
One of my favorite professors I've had. Made class super interesting despite teaching to an auditorium of like 200 students. Made the content super easy to understand
Jarvis is great and the only professor you should be taking for Econ200. I spent minimal time on this course outside of class, just show up and pay attention and its an A.
Get Jarvis for Econ. Great lectures, funny guy, really good teacher. If you do the work you should get an A no problem

This class is a college student's dream. The homework is relatively quick and easy, you only have to go to class once a week, and you get three excused absences. Jarvis is funny and always available to answer questions through the class discord. He plays music and uses a Kahoot to take attendance. There's no reason for you not to take this class.

Professor J was a great. I loved his lectures. He is very organized, has a modern style of teaching course material through watching video lectures online. He is funny, caring, and inspirational. I look forward to taking more classes with him.
Plays music in class, is extremely nice, makes you only go to class once a week. Take it!

Take Justin for ECON200. He explains concepts really well, he's funny, and it's enjoyable to watch his lectures. If you want to get the A then you'll have to take some decent notes and put some effort into understanding the material, however, it's still an easy class.

He was much better at teaching 200 than 407. You watch lectures at home and take a quiz on each video which prevented you from asking clarifying questions. You show up once a week to take a quiz from an assigned reading. He provides study questions for exams but doesn't post solutions, he will answer one if you explicitly ask about it.
Great class. Great professor. Learned a lot.

Dr. J. is a caring, intelligent man who makes ECON 200 an interesting class. He is easily accessible outside of class and wants you to succeed. He is interested in getting to know you as a person and making sure you succeed, not only in his class but outside of it as well. You only go into class 1 day a week and participation is through Kahoot!

I would definitely take Prof. Jarvis again because he made ECON 200 easy and enjoyable. He also applied the material to real life, making the concepts more relatable. Although I didn't particularly like the asynchronous, online format of the lectures, the lectures themselves were great. Overall, he was a 10/10 prof with great care for his students

Jarvis is super nice and a really great lecturer -- you only meet once a week, and watch recorded lectures before the weekly meeting. Some concepts are a bit difficult and you'll have to pay attention, but super manageable. weekly inquizitive and smart work, and unit tests are online

If you have to take econ 200, get jarvis. He has class once a week, and online content for the other part of the week. just do the work and you'll be golden. you get 3 missed classes but then the rest hurts your grade. also, kahoots!

If all professors at UofA was like this man, the school would be top 10 in the world. He is how all should be. Caring, funny and with an important twist of ADHD (I don't think he actually has it tho) which makes him super energetic. He loves us and we love him!
Dr. J is a one of a kind professor. Wish more of the professors could be like him.

Took ECON 200 online and the class was easy, but tests were tough. If you pay attention and study, you should do fine. Jarvis is a nice, caring professor and office hours are through a class-wide discord.
Jarvis makes a boring class very fun!! He seems like a really great guy and I would definitely highly recommend any class taught by him.

I LOVED this class and Dr. Jarvis. I took this class live online on zoom this past semester. Historically I haven't liked online classes but Dr. Jarvis made the material so interesting and I learned a ton. There were lectures to watch and about two homework assignments a week. We had in total three exams.

Dr. J is the best teacher I've had at the U of A thus far. I had no interest in Econ prior to the class, but his enthusiasm and teaching style genuinely made class fun to attend. The material is reasonably challenging while not being overtly difficult. Keeping up with assignments and paying attention in class will help a lot.
