Lots of lectures and homework, but he teaches very well! If you go to class and keep up with the assignments you will do well. The study guides he gives help for the tests. Each week is laid out the same so there are no surprises. Great professor!

Dr. J is super nice and caring and he really cares his students' wellbeing. He is also super funny and his voice is very energetic and passionate. He is a music fan and will never make you sleepy. At his ECON 200 at ALL COST!!!

Amazing professor and great at explaining complicated concepts to make them incredibly simple. As long as you do the work and pay attention to lectures you will get an A. Not too much work and only held class once a week. 10/10 professor!

Completely recommend taking econ with Dr. Jarvis. Calls it Club200 and plays music at the beginning of Zoom like it's a club. Very nice. Good energy. Work is not too hard, and the tests were not too bad. Just put in the work and go to the Zoom sessions and you'll be good.
Jarvis is amazing and if you pay attention to lectures you will do fine on then 3 test.
Took it live online, might be my fav professor so far. Fun lectures, which is rly hard to do online.

Dr. Jarvis is an awesome professor. In the honors section, the essays weren't too hard, and there is plenty of course material to be graded that a few assignments won't mess up your grade. He is super communicative and even had his own Discord server! Attendance isn't mandatory per se, but there's Kahoots every week you are graded on.

Class is only graded on a weekly quiz and three exams. You might do everything you need to do to know the information but still get a super bad grade. I do not recommend this class, the grading is unfair and it is not motivational.

Dr. Jarvis is amazing. Lectures are fun and you'd actually wanna learn. The overall atmosphere of this course was enjoyable. Dr. Jarvis is really engaging and tries his best to make this class memorable. However, there were a few assignments to do, but it wont take long. If you have a chance to take this professor, take him!!!!!

Very cool guy. Lots of work every week. Makes class fun and interesting. When the HW gets hard, its not very easy to get an explanation as to what you are doing wrong over discord but if you ask early you might get help from your peers.

Dr. Jarvis makes it so anyone can understand Econ! His lectures are put together so well and he is always trying to help you succeed. There is a good amount of homework, but it is imperative to help you understand the topics. Overall one of the best teachers out there.
He is a great professor. Makes it really easy to succeed in his class as long as you do your part. Also a really fun and interesting class in my opinion.

I didn't know anything about ECON before this class and honestly wondered why it was a mandatory class for my major. I am so glad it was because this is a class I actually would count as useful. We take so many unnecessary courses in college that we are lucky when we get one that actually will help us in the future. Dr. J was awesome!

I absolutely LOVED this class. Before taking this class I didn't know a thing about economics and Justin Jarvis did an amazing job of teaching the basics of economics. He is very caring, always helpful and quick to answer your questions, and does a great job at keeping you on top of everything. There is quite a bit of hw, but nothing too impossible

This professor is literally the most upbeat and greatest professor to have for this intro Econ class. He uses discord and that is a great platform for managing several hundred students. All you have to do is watch the lectures and do the homework and you'll easily get As on every test.

He's really good teacher and he makes learning fun and I actually understand econ now I would definitely take a class with him again. Theres a little bit of work outside of class but he drops a lot of your worst grades
Dr. J was a super good professor. He always knew what he was talking about and would answer any questions you had in class. I took the class Live Online T/Th but we only met on zoom on Thursdays.
I had him TuThu but we only met on Thursdays. You just watch videos before class, and then he goes over the topics and we play kahoot. Really simple and straightforward.

I am still taking this class during covid, the professor is nice and chill but can be extremely passive aggressive at times. he tends to ignore questions he doesn't want to answer and the person that replies to emails is very rude and unhelpful. His tests are easy though that's the good thing.

His class is very easy to stay on top of as you are graded on attendance, the online homework and tests. It was a challenging course, but if you really pay attention to his engaging lectures, it will make sense. He clearly cares about ECON and it made it much more enjoyable.

Dr. J is the absolute best. There's a fair amount of homework but it's all pretty easy. There are three tests over the semester (no final!) that make up 45% of your grade, so you need to do well on those, but they're not too bad. He also gives a study guide before the exam with some of the exact same questions.

How could I say anything bad about professor Jarvis? He is awesome! I loved taking this class, and he really tried to make it interactive and interesting for everyone. There's a lot of homework for his class, but he allows multiple assignment drops to make up for it. take his class!!!

He is very caring towards his students and wanted us to succeed. I was nervous going into this class because I heard ECON 200 is hard, but he made it super easy to understand. Deadlines are clear so there are no unexpected quiz or homework that you could potentially miss. His class was well structured and organized.
I loved Dr. J. This class was easy as long as you did the homework, watched the lectures, and studied. Dr. J is enthusiastic and clearly has an in-depth understanding of the topic.

Dr. J is an amazing professor who is super passionate and cares a lot about his students! There's a bit of homework to be done each week but it is manageable. Just make sure you keep up with the lectures and homework and attend class and you will do fine!
Justin is a great professor. He really wants his students to learn. Just go to class, watch the videos, and do the reading and youll ace it. He is always available to help and take in feedback.
Jarvis is a cool guy but he assigns way more homework than the other econ 200 professor. He generally explains things well but if I could go back I wouldve taken Dirk not him.

I love Dr. J! I wasn't planning on having this much fun in an econ class! Dr. J is super respectful, energetic, fun, and a just overall a great guy. There is homework every week and three exams but they are easy and curved.

Dr. Jarvis is an amazing professor. He made class enjoyable and despite us being in the middle of a pandemic, he utilized all his resources into making the class run smoothly. There is quite a bit of homework, but not to the point where you are drowning in it. Tests are easy/doable if you take notes and do homework. I would highly recommend!
Dr. J is the GOAT. Great lecturer: passionate, patient, and engaging. Can't wait to take his higher level course in the future!

Dr. J is awesome! He truly cares about his students and the assignments are actually beneficial to learning the material. His lectures are engaging and the material on the tests is always stated very clearly so you know for sure what you will see.

Dr J is amazing. I was supposed to take this class with Mateer during the covid semester and honestly Mateer is a good guy but he doesn't know how to teach online. Jarvis is amazing, makes class fun, and you actually learn in this class. Weekly easy assignments and tests are not hard at all. Easy A

Dr. J was a BLESSING to have for my first sem here. Only have class Th & all other lectures are posted online. Due dates for all work are so consistent that there are no surprises. And, he is the NICEST prof. He genuinely cares about his students, makes class fun, answers all questions, and wants you to succeed. Do the assignments and you chillin.

This professor is such an amazing guy. He truly cares about how his students are doing. He assigns three lecture videos with embedded quiz questions but they are not hard to get right. His tests and the rest of the homework is fair and he gives resources that you can use to be successful.

Dr. J is such a nice guy. He makes his lectures very intriguing and I don't even like economics lol. Crucial you pay attention though, or you will fall behind. He teaches very well so as long as you put in the effort, you will be chillin.

I have no idea how he does not have a 5/5 rating on here. Professor Jarvis is awesome he's a great guy and a really good teacher. His lectures are engaging and somehow he made it so I look forward to going to his class even over zoom. He is very caring and organized.
Jarvis is honestly the sweetest person but his teaching method wasn't for me. If your someone who loves lectures and having your grade rely on mainly you test scores, then he will be great for you.

Dr. J is the nicest teacher I've ever had. He really cares about his students. The topic isn't the most interesting but he does his best to keep your interest. Grade consists of 3 tests, weekly HW and kahoots. If you go to class and do the work you'll do fine

Nice guy. Dont take his class tough. His lectures are boring and doesnt prepare you well enough for the tests. Other professors actually put the study guide questions on their tests, not this guy. I wish I knew this so i couldve gotten a better grade with someone else.

Dr. J is an awesome professor! Though there is homework due every week, as long as you stay on top of learning the content you will be fine. Dr. J is one of the best professors Ive had at U of A so far. He is so lively and engaging (even at 8 am when I had him!!) I would definitely recommend taking this class!

Dr. J is the nicest professor ever and he made going to class fun for such a boring topic in my opinion. He does lots of kahoots! The tests are really not that hard if you actually study like you're supposed to and he's very approachable for questions. I'm not sure why he would have bad ratings when he's a great person and great professor.

He is one of the best professors at UofA. I always enjoyed going to his lectures as he tries to make them interesting. You have to go to class in order to get the content needed for the test however the material is not complicated. Homework is online and if you do that along with studying all of the lectures, you will pass with flying colors.

Professor Jarvis is a very nice and energetic guy and I enjoyed going to his lectures, though I started to get bored during the last month of the class due to the repetitive nature of some topics. Just go to class most of the time and you should be fine. He tries to make class interesting and does kahoot quizzes a lot. No papers which I liked.

Justin Jarvis is a great teacher. Ill be honest I never went to his class, but the classes I did he was great. He gives out extra credit for all of the tests which helps, if you miss class you can find the lecture slides if you search online even though he doesn't post them and if you just study for the tests for a day or two you should be fine.

Jarvis was a great teacher for this class. He is extremely energetic and cares a ton about the material and students, and is always asking for feedback to make the class better. Kind of baffling that people don't like him as the class is one of the easiest in the whole university, and he tries to make it fun

I am getting all A's in my other courses so I know that I am not the issue here receiving a low C possible D with this course. He doesn't do a good job preparing you for the test at all. Instead of just making a clear study guide to help you with the test, he makes you look back on every lecture slide as a review. He's a great person but bad prof.

He is an amazing teacher and I feel like most of the bad comments on here are from people who are not paying attention or going to class. I studied the study guides that he gives and got an A on all of the exams. The notes do help, make sure you get all the formulas down. I love his teaching method and how energetic he is!
You "have" to go to the lectures in order to get the notes. The only downside is that the notes don't help at all on the exam.

He is a cool dude overall but he is not the best prof. I can never follow what he is teaching in lecture, even when I try. The online homework is difficult and a waste of time. The way the information is presented is unclear and confusing.

I recommend his Econ class, if you can take it!! He tries his best to make the topic enjoyable and you can tell he likes teaching. He's welcome to any feedback, and is really nice! His slides are well put together but he doesn't post them online so you have to show up if you want them or can get them from a friend. Attendance isn't mandatory
He is a good and fun teacher, but the amount of homework he assigns is too much. Also, his exams are really difficult. But if you go to class and really study then you will get a good grade.

He is overall a great guy, just not a great prof. He talks about his wife and kids too much. There is a lot of material and the tests are each worth 25% of your grade, which most people fail. I have heard other professors for econ are better so take it with someone else and save yourself from a bad grade.
Very nice guy but never left me feeling prepared for the exams. Take the class with Mateer

Dr. J is the best!!! Econ is not my favorite topic, but he makes it very interesting. He makes sure the class knows he actually cares about us doing well and takes our input into account. Highly highly recommend.

His lectures are always really well put together. Attendance is not mandatory, but he doesn't put up the slides so in order to learn the material it is best to go to class. You can tell he is passionate about what he does, and for an 8 am is was not a bad class at all. I recommend :)

Insanely easy IF YOU SHOW UP TO CLASS She tries to make it so no work ever goes home, and you do the assignments as a class. You submit the assignments, and get checked in class meaning you walk out of the classroom never having to care about the course. I had around 5 absences which after communicating through email, she walked me through the work

I liked Jennings as a professor but the class felt very disorganized. Super easy work if you follow along with her, so GO TO CLASS! The chances of her having the directions or any way to get into contact with her is unlikely. I got a B when I very much deserved an A. She also told me I would keep an A even with missed one assignment... she lied.

BCOM214 is a lot easier than other classes Jennings teaches. This class is very easy, with little to no homework, as the assignments can easily be completed in class if you follow along with the prof.

The class was quite chaotic and felt overly aggressive at times. The instructor rarely taught and instead had us do frequent impromptu presentations. Additionally, the course material seemed poorly structured, and I didn't find the content to be conducive to learning. I'm concerned that I won't gain much from this course in the end.
We was a great lecturer who made everything simple and cared about her students.

She was super laid back and understanding. Class was beneficial and she didn't grade harshly. As long as you do what she tells you and turn the assignments in you will get an A. Instructions were unclear sometimes, and d2l was a little messy, but she cleared everything up quickly. Didn't update the grade book often. Other than that I recommend!
Understanding, caring, and easy class. Top professor at this school
Catherine is amazing! She is super relaxed and chill. She waived off our midterms and finals. I freaking love her. Easy A!

Professor Jennings had a hard time controlling the class. We often missed deadlines. For our board of directors presentations, time was not allocated equally. I emailed the TA regualry for clarification. No grades were input into D2L until the week before finals. I had no idea what my grade would be until it was posted on UAccess

Dr. Jennings is very knowledgeable, and really funny. But, she is very relaxed and often cancelled class and gives you a lot of wiggle room. She graded most assignments the week before grades were due. Overall, this is an easy class but I don't feel like I learned much.

The best professor I have had. I absolutely loved class every day. Dr. Jennings made class enjoyable and truly inspired her students. I would jump at the opportunity to take another one of her classes. She gave thoughtful feedback as well as incorporated student feedback into her teaching.

The class structure was not very good. The syllabus was not followed and many changes occurred throughout the semester. She did not make herself available as a resource and never gave feedback on work until the end of the semester where she gave most people a B and some an A. Thought it was a waste of time. Also, 4 TAs that did nothing at all.

As long as you show up and give effort, she will love you. She's so sweet and funny. If you get her as your teacher you're very lucky and will crush the course. She does have a few essay, but show up to class (again) and you'll be completely fine.
Very kind and considerate. I loved her class.
She is super nice and a great professor! Would take a class with her again!
She's soooo great best teacher I've had so far
best proffesor on campus

I LOVE KATIE
she is the nicest teacher ever! If i could i would take all my engl classes with her. She is so caring and doesn't really give many assignment outside the class beside of the major projects. Her classes are not boring at all shes a fun teacher. As long as you attend the classes you'll get an A. Definitely recommend taking engl with her
Always very prompt with great lectures!
absolutely amazing
Always a joy and a delight. Felt like I was learning and I could get help. Appreciated the lectures preparing me for the tests!!

The lectures were very helpful, and corresponded to the quizzes and exams. Highly encourage doing the practice exams because they are very similar to the exams. Homework was kind of hard but you get three chances on each problem.

Lots of discussions and group work, but all of the assignments were very clear and relatively easy to do with the reading/lectures. Time management is the most difficult part of this class - I paired it with a quantitative course and didn't feel like this one was necessarily a "break" from work the way some other qualitative OMBA courses were.
Highly recommend! If you have the option to take a class from him I would jump on it 100%

Overall great course going over the economics of business strategy.  Professor Johnson is very responsive to questions and has office hours.  Course is made up of 6 weekly reading assignments and video recordings plus video responses and a group presentation which combines concepts from all prior 6 weeks. Lots of work but easy grader.
Just dont take his classes one of the worst I have ever taken

4 exams, can retake 1. Go to class & participate. Problems you do in class are the ones on the exams. Grades consist of homework, in-class participation, and exams. Your grade is heavily based on exams. Use TA's for clarification. The class is hard but Ryan is a good professor if you use the resources.

ECON 361 can be a tough class, but Professor Johnson makes the material easy to understand and the practice exams contain all the same concepts as the actual exams. He gives plenty of opportunities to succeed in the course.

70% of your grade is based on 4 tests, the other 30% are just showing up to class and homework. The tests are pretty easy, but his online lectures, in person lectures, and the homework/review make them a cake walk. He is pretty entertaining and even as an MIS major this was probably tied for my favorite.

If you have him in Harvil, the wifi sucks. If you semi pay attention in class + watch all the "optional videos" for each chapter in D2L, you're good. For exams, watch and take notes on the optional videos, study the slides he posts from class, study his exam reviews. I'd consider this a pretty average class, maybe leaning on the easier side

Had him for ECON361. Your grade is only from exams, HW, and clicker questions in class that have extra credit if they're right. Class is all lecture and notes, but if you listen and find students who understand the material, you'll do well. He's pretty dry but a nice guy, just pay attention and do the study guides bc they are the same as exams.

Johnson makes it so difficult to learn economics. His lectures are all over the place and he does a poor job of explaining the material. There are no lecture slides to revisit to help reinforce content, so you're stuck looking at his hand-drawn supply/demand graphs. If it wasn't for the class TAs, I would not have passed this class

I would not recommend his class. He talks on and on and doesn't go over the material. He just has us do problems and not big on vocabulary. Be prepared to teach yourself. I am at a high B and I would take this class as long as if it were taught by another professor.

He is an amazing lecturer, knows and CARES about Econ and you can tell. He is always ready to help and all you have to do is put in the effort. His in class work and quizzes are exactly what are on the exams so if you go to class and pay attention then you are ready to go! Johnson loves talking to students so office hours are fun!

You really only need to go to class and you will do okay on exams and don't need to do anything outside of the lectures. The exams are copy/paste from class. Not a fan of his teaching style which is 75 minutes straight of econ graphs on a projector but he has a good personality and makes it less boring than it could be with another professor.

No formula sheet for exams and it's asynchronous. The course material is pretty tough and he doesn't answer anyone's questions. He complains all the time about how people do badly on their exams but he is the real problem. He has no business teaching this class.

this class is asynchronous. He doesn't even have office hours. in the asynchronous lectures, he goes super fast and doesn't even explain all of the content clearly. he just expects us to know everything. his study guides are unclear. be prepared to teach yourself. he's not even a teacher. avoid him if you can. a different person is needed to teach.

He is very nice and very fun to talk to. The class is a bit difficult and rough mostly from the homework. However, if you're struggling go see him for office hours and you'll be good. Also make sure to take notes during the lectures and you'll be prepared for the open note exams. It also really helped to have the clicker questions extra credit.

This class was still very difficult online due to the homeworks and the exams. However, the exams were open note which made it a bit better and at least he does reduce the overall grade for each exam giving you a little boost. It was still bad though the first two weeks were video lectures not live but still helped as do the live lectures.

Worst pandemic teaching I experienced. It was obvious that he wrote off the semester & students. We had no synchronous meetings for the first 3 weeks, and he instead released just 10 mins of video each week. Our first Zoom class was an exam review, and when students expressed their anger with this, he disabled the chat for the rest of the semester.

Be prepared to teach yourself. This class is so hard, he does NOT teach you want he tests on and what the homeworks are on. Just goes over the bare minimum and expects you to figure out the rest. He is also not very nice. Ask a question and he makes you feel so dumb for asking it or that it's an inconvenience and waste of his time to answer.

(Junior) Easily the best lecturer/prof I've ever had. His lectures are engaging, he gives out extra credit every class for participation credit, and honest about what's actually on his exams. The hw is easy too, but the main take away are his exams are fair and similar to his reviews. If you go to class and pay attention it's hard not to get an A

Amazing teacher. Have taken classes with him twice now and I honestly think he is one of the only professors in Eller that genuinely cares about your success. You have to go to class and take notes though, but the lectures are very interesting.
Cool guy. Super nice. Teaches you the quiz, great content.

You really should go to class because of the clicker questions and some stuff that is on the test he only says in his lectures, it's not in the notes/book. Definitely study DAYS before the exam (don't cram the night before). Do the homework and make sure to answer the clicker questions correctly as much as you can.

Great professor for a tough class. Utilizes the clicker questions well to teach, and you only need to answer 60% of clicker questions correct to get full credit. Econ can be tricky but the review slides should help prepare you. 4 exams and an optional final, lowest is dropped

Go to class. Dr. J doesn't waste time and gets right to working problems, the same kind you will see on the tests. The in-class Q's can get hard with only 1 min or so to do them. If you start struggling, he usually has them on D2L in advance & you can do them before class. To study: drill all inclass Q's until you master them, then redo HW --> EZ A

He's like your friend's Dad. He's funny, nice, respectable and care. He teaches well and it's a terrible subject. He helps as much as he can and he's silly. ECON still sucks, but he makes it suck less.

This was one of the classes I really looked forward to. While attendance wasn't mandatory, It was definitely recommended. The class was a lot of fun too. He has a fantastic, engaging teaching style. Since I took an 8 am, I would occasionally sleep through my alarm. After I woke up I was always disappointed because I really looking forward to class.

Took it online so never met him in person. However, the class is well structured and you learn a lot. Work includes weekly reading/HW assignments which aren't too difficult, three Examity proctored exams (all multiple choice), a 4 page paper, some discussions, and a prediction-market game to earn extra credit on the paper. Final is optional.
Professor Johnson has made labor economics one of my favorite classes thus far. The lectures are entertaining. Quizzes and tests are similar to homework assigned, and in class discussions.

Professor Johnson is definitely one of my favorite professors I have had at the UA. He is easy to track, fun to listen to, and makes his class extremely interesting. The workload and tests are fair and manageable if you show up and pay attention. Definitely recommend taking this course!

Lecture slides are confusing. Has horrible handwriting. Does not inspire in subject matter, class is super boring. Practically taught myself everything outside. Not too hard to get an A in if you apply yourself a bit extra.

professor Johnson is a great teacher. Class is 100% mandatory as he writes all his lectures on the board, and will once and awhile have pop quizzes that can be key points on what will be on the test. He wants everyone to succeed and is very straight forward with what you need to know. I really enjoyed this class, and would take it again if i could.

He's a great professor. Advice: go to class and take notes, do the homework because there will be pop quizzes on it and use the quizzes to study for the tests. He is extremely willing to help, I went to his office hours once a week and he was always so helpful and explained the concepts even more for me. I would definitely recommend this class.

Awesome professor. Teaches the class really well and breaks down the concepts pretty well.  He is more than willing to answer any questions and provide help to students who need it. He also actually cares about the students to learn the material. Econ 382 has some harder concepts. Do the homework to prepare for exams!
He's willing to help if you seek him out. I took other econ classes so I didn't need to work as hard as others but it was still a challenge. A little math thrown in there so be prepared for that!

Great professor! HIGHLY RECOMMEND! Definitely qualified (if not over qualified) for this course and is very willing to help when you need it. He teaches very well & creates an environment where you're comfortable. You learn so much! Hw answers are online, quizzes (indv. & group) here and there but are not bad and an optional final. :D Take him!!!!!
very rude and unprofessional.

Incredibly amazing professor. If you are passionate about finance and not just some rich kid, this class will be a challenge and very interesting. Kahle is a great professor and explains things very well. A majority of students refuse to pay attention and put in the effort to learn and then get mad at her for their shortcomings.
impossible to learn from. tests are nothing like the homework.
Dr. Kahle gives pretty straightforward lectures, but sometimes the homework requires a little extra thinking or reading. Tests are easy A-B if you learn how to do the homework.

I wasn't very interested in corporate finance so this class was hard and very boring. Some multiple choice but mostly free response tests. There is also a large company valuation project that takes quite a bit of time. Be sure to get a good group. Overall, I'd say the class was more difficult than average.
Excellent professor, very helpful.  Serious in class, personable in private.
Kahle was probably the best professor in terms of learning new practical information. Excellent clear lectures. Exams aren't easy.
good class
This is a hard class but Kealey is very passionate about what he is teaching. Very open during office hours.

This class was the most interesting MIS class I have taken yet. It is a very niche subject but Professor Kealey is a very personable person who is more than willing to help outside of class. 5 relatively easy and interesting assignments that vary in time. Open note exams but would definitely be familiar with the content before them.

A professor that knows his content but doesn't seem to be the best at teaching. Exams are open note but don't expect that to mean it's easy. We had d2l quizzes after every class, 5 big assignments that takes 2-3 hours to complete each, and 3 exams.

This was one of the classes that I knew was going to be the hardest class of the semester from day 1. It's super broad and the course goes by fast. Without a doubt, Dr. K knew what he was talking about but I struggled to keep up with him. Fortunately, the tests were open-book, so I could get high Bs or low As. Don't bother buying the textbook.
Professor Kealey is engaging, clear, has high expectations, and gives valuable feedback. I learned so much.

He is very passionate about the subject matter, which in turn makes you excited to learn the material. He’s extremely helpful and wants his students to succeed. He takes the extra time to ensure you understand the material.

Prof. Kealey is a great professor who takes time to ensure students understand what they are doing and schedules "office hours" if neccessary to help. Lecturer modules are pretty light. Expect weekly quizzes and assignments, midterm and final (examity proctored), and final report instead of group project, roughly 8-10 pages. I would take him again!

Professor Kealey is one of my top favorite professors without a doubt he is one of the best. He is very nice and very fun to speak to and really informative if you're struggling with the assignments or confused on what to do. The exams are open note and the quizzes overall aren't bad. I took him for 307 as well and enjoyed that course just as 416.

Paul is the best! He's literally so nice and genuinely cares about his students. He gives personalized feedback for every assignment which is rare nowadays. The workload isn't bad at all either, there's two easy little quizzes due each week, 4 graded labs, 3 exams, and a group project.

He is grossly negligent with wrong test answers, isn't responsive or helpful on what you did miss, and seemingly doesn't care at all to even word his questions sensibly. Took another class of his in person and enjoyed the subject matter, but the charm of his blunt predisposition is really just a cover for a lack of competency or care for his job.
Tests hard af, impossible but curved. Class was FIN460 but doesn't let me add it.

TERRIBLE I took his 460 class, don't take this I don't know wtf I was thinking. Listen to these reviews. Worst class and worst teacher in Eller by far. List goes on for the reasons, just save yourself the stress and don't take or drop if you can.
WORST TEACHER .. DONT TAKE FIN460

he is the worst professor I have ever had. as a senior, this is the hardest class I've taken, and will probably screw my graduation date. no d2l resources, grades on median, doesn't help, laughs at you in class. drop rate is probably insane, do not take this class even if your life depends on it.

Do not take Professor Khan. He absolutely does not know how to teach and you will regret taking his course. He is very disorganized, does not use any sort of powerpoint in his lectures, and hardly updates his syllabus. I would not take Professor Khan again unless my life depended on it.

Lectures are great but he doesn't hold back calling you out if you don't know something. He simplifies every concept. Case study we did was a waste of time because he didn't bother explaining it once. Gives 0 examples or resources so take a lot of notes. 0 feedback for anything graded. Lack of real word connections with concepts taught. Easy class.

Aamir may seem intimidating but he really cares about your understanding in the class. He explained every concept very throughly. Attendance is a must as most of your grade is over attendance and participation. I definitely learned a lot from Aamir and I recommend him.

Aamir is really the best professor I believe I have had at Eller. He is a harsh grader and can be intimidating but the amount of info about core financial concepts I have retained from him is staggering. He starts from the very bottom and teaches you every concept extremely detailed. This is truly the only finance class you need for all of college
HE IS THE WORST!

GO TO CLASS Khaan has been my most impactful professor at Eller by far He will cold call people in class but it is just to be sure that you are paying attention and that the class is understanding the material. Basically no homework, you must do good on the tests to pass. He grades on the median curve which was scary at first but worked in my favor

Khan is a great teacher if you want to actually learn something. His methods of teacher are dated which caused a lot of frustration for students who needed extra material (if you do, the textbook is pretty good). Test is where it matters. You will do really well if you go to class, if you dont you'll do horrible.

I took this course a year or two ago, for most professors id forget them by then. Mr. Khan on the other hand is truly unforgettable. Although a tough grader, hes really understanding. Go to office hours, speak to him and youll do just fine, he truly cares. Has really informative classes and a very fun yet serious atmosphere.

He definitely knows his stuff but is a very harsh graders. Tests and quizzes are timed such that you barely have enough time to finish. He cold calls on people during and asks random questions, if you get the question wrong you lose participation points. Overall, an interesting class made much more difficult than it needed to be by the professor.
Very awful teacher.
I don't think Aamir is a good teacher. Sometimes, he can be very rude and disrespectful, and he always intimidated students how hard his tests would be. I don't really like him :(
He was so unprepared and disorganized throughout the entire semester, and he always forced students to answer his questions in class. Sometimes, he was kind of rude to us. I really do not like him :(
Aamir was the worst teacher that I've met at U of A. Don't take classes with him!!

Great lecturer. He does not have slides, so attending class is vital. The tests are challenging, but showing up the majority of the time will put you in a good position for them. I do not recall any homework, so put some effort into test preparation and the main project.

Professor Khan is one of the best professors at the University of Arizona. I took him in the Fall of 2019 and he was nothing but amazing. His exams are fair and he makes sure everyone fully understands concepts. Essays are very fun and creative and directly correlate with the class. He calls on people frequently make sure to do the readings.

Aamir is the best professor I've had at the U of A. He wants you to understand the material and will go out of his way to get to know you personally. He is lecture heavy and you will have to read a LOT in his classes, however the exams (at least in 150) are pretty straightforward. You just have to listen to what he has to say and you should be fine

I've never used rate my prof before to write a review, just read them but I feel compelled to say that this is definitely the best teacher I've had at Eller. at the beginning of the semester I disliked him because I thought he would be unreasonably hard but the exact opposite I actually learned so much, weird style of teaching. You have to LISTEN.

By far one of the best professors I've had at Eller. Although Aamir's class was very challenging, he really does care, wants you to know the material well, and succeed beyond his class. He's approachable outside the classroom and helps you connect with recruiters and alumni in the Financial Services Industry. I highly recommend this class with him.
Aamir is the best professor at Eller hands down. Extremely engaging and makes class exciting. Class is tough but in the end you will learn more in this class then any other class offered at Eller.

Aamir's method of teaching is unlike other professors at Eller. He'll make sure that everyone understands a concept before moving on to the next. He truly cares for each student. Make sure you attend each class to do well. His exams are by no means easy but if you understand the concepts and ask questions, you will do well!

Just finished this course. Pretty easy class. Put in decent effort and you'll do fine. Professor emailed back quickly whenever I had a question. Exams were 20 questions long but if you knew the material it was easy.

As long as you actually make a solid effort in class you will be fine. He does a wonderful job of being available for any questions you may have and will work with you. Very knowledgeable and approachable. (he was my capstone professor fin 498, I didn't take 311 with him but I'm sure he's just the same there too).

I was concerned as I did poorly on the first test, but I ended up with an A.  The professor was helpful, but mostly just took extra effort (math not my strong suit).  And LOL @ the other review, the quizzes are not "impossible" you get three attempts, and you can view the correct answer explanation after your first attempt... who is "trash" here?

Took the 3 week course & was able to pass with a B, despite not being the best at math. Had a lot of homework assignments and quizzes, but if you do them all you'll do well on the exams. He gave helpful feedback. Study! He gave us the option to have cheat sheets and allowed us many different options for extra credit. He is an incredible professor!!
I was very nervous about finance, but it was taught in a simple way that I could understand and I enjoyed what I learned.

He is an amazing professor hands down. Willing to work with you as long as you voice that youre wanting to do what is possible to pass/learn material. Not a tough grader at all and very lenient when it comes to grading. He himself is always accessible and answered my emails within a max of 24 hrs, and 100% understanding. 10/10 would take him again
This class was more difficult than the average course I have taken at Eller (I am a senior). However I learned significantly more than in the average course. Funny how that works.

As an ex-accounting major, this is the one of the hardest classes I've ever taken. I'm taking it as an accelerated summer course. The tests are like 11 problems, so there's really no room to make mistakes. The practice problem videos are so poorly filmed you can barely see the questions or his calculator. The exams are nothing like the quizzes.

The professor was available quickly by email and Zoom when I needed his help. Everything was very organized which is important for a short online course, I could find the things I needed to earn an A. You do have to study, but I found the exam study guide and practice problems were in line with the exams which are worth a lot of your grade.

I've taken FIN 311 on campus and online and I can confidently say that the online version is structured entirely differently with more advanced concepts compared to on campus. Kinnear doesn't do a great job at preparing you for exams, which account for nearly 45% of your grade. He also isn't fond of emails or providing any meaningful help.

I thought he did a great job teaching the class. Being a 3-week online summer course made it much harder. I feel as though if it were taken in a normal 16-week format it would be a relatively easy class (if you are mathematically inclined). Expectations are very well laid out, something I really appreciated in this fast-paced course.
Pretty decent class but kinda hard, he is OK as a prof but tries to help

Extra credit for attendance, and going to class helps. Exams a bit hard, but he curves them. Exams  PPT and quizzes, textbook irrelevant. Lectures sometimes include topics outside the scope of the class/exams, sometimes this made me more interested in finance/was a nice change of pace. Dry humor and very easy on the eyes. Available for questions.
Excellent professor who is highly knowledgeable in finance.

Dr. Klasa is very knowledgeable about what he teaches and does a good job of laying down the foundational information to then connect to new data and approaches in finance that are not as common. He is very logically oriented, provides lectures and interactive handouts in class. He provides insightful feedback and details about in exam materials.

Sandy is a super nice guy! As a non finance major the class was definitely challenging but his test reviews are SUPER helpful. Just go through them a lot. The tricky thing is most of your grade depends on your financial plan. Good luck. Pick a good team!
Really nice guy who seems to truly care that you are understanding the material and conceptually grasping it all, whether a Finance major or not.

Tamar is a great professor! Most of her classes and exams are lecture heavy so therefore you must go and participate in class. She is very understanding and will try her absolute best to make sure you understand the material. Overall, she is really great professor and I would definitely recommend to take MGMT 486.

Getting through up to 5 hours! of online lectures each week was a slog, especially with the background noise (instant messages, voices, banging sounds). YouTube was better for understanding concepts.  Homework spikes heavily during some weeks.  Feels like a heavy 3 credit hour class.  Final exam almost guaranteed to result in grade level reduction.

This instructor looks very new to teaching and behaves as a high school teacher. Her assignments request to solve each question in 3 different ways.
Requires an executive summary starting in assignment 1. 
Makes this class very tough and requires the time of 3 other classes.
Assigns long team projects along with tough assignments in the same week!
No clear instructions on how to complete homework. Most things in home assignments are not covered. No review of assignments.

Only once a week. Requirements are attending class, talking in class, teaching/presenting to the class in a group (once), and writing a paper about the topic you presented on. SUPER EASY and great professor. Teaching the class is not as intimidating as it seems because she gives you all the material you need, and you just put it in a PowerPoint

Graded by participation *only*, don't take this class if you can avoid it. If you're not his bestie within the first two weeks then good luck getting an A. He doesn't teach, just rambles and asks quiz questions all class, and assumes AI will teach us and doesn't bother teaching us himself. I regret ending up in his course, learned nothing

He has no rubric of his grades. Everything is based on his mood, does he like you or not, etc.
There is one time he even said to one of my classmate that- "oh I thought you are a B student". That was the second week of the class.
Be aware of how he grades your homeworks.
Like what the hell? Worst kind of professor you can get.

Fogged brain dude, super smart. Treats 20 year olds like they are finance professional in their 50s. Don't take. Impossible to even understand what he is saying outside all the jargon. The TAs are just as worse, arrogant uptight schmucks

Extreme tough class. Made you spent tons of time studying but make quiz creative so that you lose every points and wasted your time studying. . The grade consists of 8 am weekly quizzes only, so don't expect to pull up your grade through easy assignments or attendance. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS unless you have to.

Professor Lamoureux is the best. He is a truly genuine person who loves the subjects he teaches. He cares about the students as well. Contrary to what some students think, his classes are easy to pass. Take his class if you truly want to learn.
He is smart but he obviously doesn't know how to teach. He made students wasting ton of times on unnecessary struggling.
Excellent class, very knowledgeable, challenging quizzes and exams but worth it

Super smart guy.  Really liked his class.   A better graduate professor than an undergraduate.     Very engaged in meeting students, but you better be ready to work.  The only 'B' I ever received in Finance (the rest 'A's).

Very smart, definatly on a different plain than most students (me).  He made an honest effort to explain things though and had a genuine desire to see students learn the material.  Take his class but be ready to work hard.
hard prof. knows his stuff and can teach you it. high investment. high return.
Very interesting stuff.He made me love investment analysis!
No Comments

She knows her material and is engaging, but her internal biases show in inconsistent grading. She is harsher on women, interrupts them often, and is more flexible with male-dominant teams. 

Feedback is rarely provided and often too late for correcting your next assignment, and AI-generated work often earns higher grades, which she cannot detect.

She is a greater teacher. Sets you up for test and dosent try to trick you! She interacts with class which makes it not a snooze fest. Class for the most part is mandatory but when I was taking an elective and had to decide which one this would be my first choice. Also a note econ law policy making is a good elective.

Makes her Econ class super enjoyable, even over Zoom. You can tell she cares about all of her students, her lectures are awesome and she listens to students feedback. Would definitely take another one of her courses!

Langer is incredibly intelligent and knows the ins and outs of every topic she covers. The class is fairly easy if you simply pay attention and go to office hours for help. She will help you with anything in office hours and wants you to get a good grade. Larger also curves exams and grades by the end of the semester.

Langer is a great professor all around. She cares about you and that you actually learn and understand. She is always willing to explain anything. I would recommend taking any class by her. They are not easy A's per se, but if you show up, study, and put effort in you will get the grade you deserve. Weekly HW and a midterm and final

Loved this honors class. Dr. Langer is an amazing professor. She makes herclass enjoyable with group games and activities to make the economic subjects seem more real and relatible. She truly cares about her students success and understanding and that comes through. Tests are hard but manageable and homework helps your grade so never neglect it.

Dr. Langer is an excellent professor who makes economics very accessibleunderstandable. She provides tons of opportunities for questions and clarifications in class and during office hours. I highly recommend this course.

She's a really nice professor and loves what she does. However, this class is very tough and at the beginning of the semester she tells you that you probably will not get an A. There is not much that goes into the grade and the midterms are very difficult.

Allison is a great professor. This was easily the most useful course I have taken at college. There are daily quizzes where you apply relevant law from the textbook to current events. Attendance is important, missing more than 2 quizzes will hurt your grade in the class. You can tell that she really cares about her students. 10/10 course.
Quiz everyday. But Lee is just a great professor. The quiz is not difficult and is a great class to get exposure to law. I highly recommend this course. Textbook is used every day.

She is incredibly knowledgeable and supportive of her students. She definitely seems like she wants to create a lasting impact in her students and leave them with something to remember for a while. She lets everyone voice their opinion and makes the class discussions interesting, highly recommend her.

This class wasn't too difficult, but required a decent amount of work to get an A. You are given a quiz in class every single class, so you have to show up. You discuss the answers to the prior quiz during class, so each class gets a bit more repetitive. She also reads what you put on the quiz in front of the whole class, which made people unhappy.

Mr. Lemoine was my favorite professor I've had so far. His lectures were super interesting and unique plus he makes the simulations you use to complete projects. He was funny but his handwriting definitely sucks. Homework was usually not too bad, but it was two readings and two small quizzes each week about the reading.

Econ 150C2 was by far my favorite course of the semester. Professor Lemoine's lectures were always engaging, and the content of the course was incredibly interesting and relevant. He makes the homework and projects very clear, and is always super accessible if you ever have any questions. Overall an amazing professor with a good sense of humor.

Is no one going to talk about how his hand writing is unreadable? You think Doctors have bad hand writing his was on a whole other level of "huh?" "What is that?" Instead of doing slides like a regular teacher he decides to write everything on his own and shows it on the big screen where no one can take notes because we don't know what it says.
Lecture Heavy. Made everything expected of the class clear in the syllabus. Would always seem him changing his shirt outside the lecture hall every time before class haha. Would take again!

Professor Lemoine makes it very clear what is expected in the syllabus. The weekly quizzes are very easy, as he puts the answers to them in the lectures. Attendance was not mandatory, as all the lectures are recorded and posted online. Simply do the work and you will earn an A. Anyone else who says otherwise is either incompetent or lazy lol.

There are weekly quizzes and for every class, there are smaller quizzes. They don't hurt your grade, but it's easy to forget to do them. SO DO THEM! They can eventually hurt your grade since its so many to keep track of. Happened to me. Other than that I would've gotten an A. Lectures aren't as interesting and there are 3 essays due.

Derek Lemoine has a true passion for what he teaches and you will learn a lot. He tries to make it interesting but the content itself may bore you. Very few opportunities to receive grades so kind of a hit or miss, especially for the reading quizzes in class. Attendance is taken through class poles/ quizzes with a clicker. Hated the group project.

Was a pretty hard class I'd say. Thought I'd get a C, but my grade I think was a B but got curved to an A. If you work hard and study you will be fine. Couple project grades, quizzes can be kinda hard (5 mins long) and only one exam (final exam). Economics but very focused on economics and climate change.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU WANT AN A!!!!! This class is WAY too hard for a Gen Ed and is bringing my GPA down. TAs grade everything & very little in grade book so you literally wont pass.
TA's do eveything except stand up and lecture for an hour. Not a lot in the gradebook so if you miss one assignment it really hurts you. Didn't lean economics mainly focused on climate change.
Fairly easy class, mandatory attendance maybe 2-3 times, but be sure to go because there is sometimes reading checks. You will also be given extra credit for every time you participate in class.

Attendance isn't mandatory which is nice, but you might as well not show up because there isn't a single chance that you can sit through an entire lecture without falling asleep. Most boring class I have ever taken. Also a hard grader with very few grades in the grade book.

This class is tough to sit in for an Hour and 15 minutes. He stands up there and lectures the whole time and you have to go because there are clicker checks. However, work is easy, there are a couple of online quizzes and the group projects don't take much work. Get a few friends to take it with you and it won't be bad.
This class had very few grades. If you didn't understand the unit and didn't do well on the quiz, it was not good.
Very tough class. TAs do everything

I would not take this class if you are looking for an A, i put in the same amount of effort as all other gen eds and still got a C when I am typically an A student. Not much in the grade book and hard to do good on the things that are in there. Skip class and I can assure you that you will fail.

Professor Lemoine is a cool guy and does a good job at making his lectures interesting. There isn't much in the grade book and the quizzes and final are easy as long as you actually go to class. Going to class isn't hard because his lectures aren't stuffy and are usually pretty interesting. Easiest and most fun gen ed I've taken.

the thing that sucks about this class is he makes you read a book a long with it about climate change so the class is not only just econ concepts but you need to go the next step and apply it to the book/climate change and it makes it way more complex than it should be. he is super boring. uses clicker. takes attendance. pop reading checks ...

There are very few items in the gradebook; 2 group projects, 1 essay, 3 worksheets, 4 7-questions quizzes and the final. Because there is a small amount of points available you can't miss many points. He was very knowledgeable of the material. Also, he offers up to 1 extra credit point a class for answering or asking a question which is nice.

Mr. Lemoine is a great person, he shows his passion for economics, but he can get carried away at times. His TAs do all his grading for him and they are very picky with answers on exercises. It requires all of your attention to get good grades.

Very enthusiastic about his class but an awful teacher. His class is very hard to follow and makes very little sense. You really have to go to class and take notes or you won't pass but you'll fall asleep if you go. DO NOT TAKE this class if you want an A.
Hes a great professor. He is also very enthusiastic about his class. You really need to take notes though because it was a lot of terminology needed to understand

He is a good teacher and tries to explain concepts with good examples. Pay attention in class and take notes. Things weigh too much on the overall grade. Don't F up any assignment if you want an A. I missed a textbook homework, a quiz, 13/20 on essay rough draft, got 40% on the final and got a C. 40% of students got a D on the final. Clicker used.

I diid not pay attention at all I just did the assignments and I failed almost all of the quizzes ! I also did extra credit ! I went into the final with an 79% so I studied the guide and got an 75%! But the curve is amazing !
Do not take, you will fall asleep in class and the final is impossible to pass
The TAs in his ECON 150 class are bad and not responsible. Also he is a tough grader. The lectures are boring  too. Such an useless class.

Such an easy class but can be difficult to stay interested in the material. He is super passionate about all the material & keeps all the info up to date. One group paper, one solo paper. Super easy quizzes every 3 weeks or so & the final he curved SO much. Grading scale is interesting because I got an 84% but somehow it counted as an A!

Lemoine does his best to make the subject interesting and gives good examples that make things more easy to understand, but the subject is honestly super boring and the class feels like it lasts forever. Class participation is important. Not necessarily a difficult class, but the final was harder than I expected, especially compared to the quizzes.

I went to this class 3 times all semester and missed two of the four quizzes and still got an A by doing everything he assigned and by studying the lectures he posted online. He's a funny guy but the subject is boring. Attendance is not mandatory, and he grades very lightly. He also gave a hefty curve at the end of the semester. Easy class overall

attendance isn't mandatory but participation is and that's given by daily clicker points, and that's 10% of your grade. a few clicker quizzes and a few homework assignments. one group essay that isn't too bad. lectures are quite dull and I never found any substance within them, had better luck learning online and with the book.
There aren't many grades. He gives boring lectures. Although the TAs were very helpful.

I really had to take notes and pay attention to pass this class. There is no participation, but he does give extra credit for students who show up when there's low attendance. He's super clear and understandable about complex concepts, it was easy to follow.

I put in about 1-2 hours a week into this class and got a B which could have easily been an A, which I was just a few points away from. If you need an easy gen ed that you might learn from a little I recommend this class.
Covers just about everything I learned as a senior in highschool. Nice professor but the topic is just so boring that it puts you to sleep by itself.

Great class if you're interested in the environment and economics. Attendance is not mandatory but definitely helps. Class is graded on a curve so its not too hard to get a good grade. Dr. Lemoine is pretty engaging and sometimes funny

He's aight. Really nice guy but he'll throw in some random quizzes whenever he wants and they're super hard sometimes. The classes were pretty boring but doable. I'd recommend it if you're interested in econ but if you're looking for an easy A, probably not.
He is a cool dude, and he knows what he's talking about, but it is very boring and too long of a class.  I do not look forward to attending the class ever.  Extremely hard to take solid notes.
Professor Lemoine is a sufficiently adept professor, but the policies he implements on class conduct are enforced in a distracting and inefficient manner.

Very efficient speaker,Lemoine is able to communicate the material very well through power-points and  in-class activities.As for the class itself, its a complete joke;Only a few assignments(graded carelessly)the entire semester.Even though I advocate for difficult classes,I do recommend this one as the material is interesting if you pay attention

Really Nice Guy. He is super cool and does not require much work out of you. You are required to do a debate, but if you're not much a a talker then you can become a judge and write a one page analysis of the debate with a group. There's one homework assignment and one essay. The final is a big deal through. Overall, pretty Good class.

no. do not take this class. so boring. pointless information. worst lecturer ever. he puts everyone to sleep. its unbearable staying in class for 1 hr and 15. everyone leaves always. awful explanations. just no no no no no.

SOOOOO BORING! I've been to every class but 1 and all he does is talk and talk and talk. Everyone is either on their phone or computer in this class. You get points for attendance. So far this class is really easy but we have yet to turn in a paper that is 30% and the final which is 30%.

Very Easy Class, attendance is not mandatory and there are only 3 quizzes and he drops one of them.  This class is a joke, take it and you WILL get an A.  He also offers extra credit opportunity.  I learned absolutely nothing in this class.

Derek is such a good teacher. He has a good way of relating things so they make sense. He uses clickers constantly throughout the class. There are only a few assignments so make sure you do well on them and you're set. I'm really glad I took his class:)
Lemoine is a cool dude forsure. It's long class period, but he keeps it interesting and his lectures are crystal clear!

This class is not bad at all! We didnt even have our first assignment until half way through the semester. Lemoine's lectures are interesting and easy to pay attention to. I definitely recommend this class as an easy Tier 1.
Total boss

The worst professor ever. One of the most disrespectful professor in the entire university. Do not take chances with her and never try to seek any sort of mentorship from her. She thinks too highly of herself and would put you down every time. I hope one day she faces the heat for this behavior and realize how she is playing with the students.

This is a very tough course, but Yong does a great job of lecturing and balancing it with actual activities. Really easy grader, for example, group presentations were casual wear and no one really got less than 80-90%. Tests are really easy if you study and there are 6 pop quizzes that are straight out of the short assigned reading articles.

Professor Liu is a great teacher and person - he cares a lot about what he's teaching and who he's teaching. He's a very intelligent and patient person. I learned this subject really well with him, and would definitely recommend it - His classes are really competitive to get into, so make sure you enroll early! His tests are pretty straight forward

Super nice guy, willing to help, or answer any questions. Hard to understand/pay too much attention in class. If you're a marketing major this class is basically the same as your first mktg research class in Eller. If you're not a MKTG major Im guessing this class will be a little bit difficult. criteria for grading isn't super clear

Gave the class printed powerpoints for all the lectures.  While some took notes on them, you really don't have to because everything on the test is right off his slides.  His accent is fairly strong, but he will take no offense when you ask for clarification. Yong is a GENIUS and I really enjoyed his class.

Kinda boring class. His accent is pretty strong but he can occasionally make you laugh. You could probably skip every day of this class and learn the test material out of the book, to be completely honest.

His accent is a bit think so I suggest sitting up front. BUY THE STUDY GUIDE!!! His tests are practically ripped right out of the guide. Two projects - so, so on difficulty level. The lectures are interesting if you can understand him. 5 pop quizzes - these can make or break your grade. 3 exams and no final.

You have to decode his speech. His foreign accent is not very clear. But, he is very intelligent and an extremely nice guy. His tests are fair if you just study the night before and go to class. Does do pop quizzes though, so be wary. Class is 2 small projects, 3 major tests, 5 pop quizzes and a participation points and that's it. No final.

Prof. Mackie wants you to succeed, but the class is HARD. You get a cheat sheet for exams and she holds reviews that tend to be VERY similar to the actual exam. Take advantage of the extra credit she offers; it'll help. TAs are hit or miss, though.

Professor Mackie has a clear, structured D2L that outlines week by week material to cover including lectures, topics, and assignments. Everything is manageable if you just follow her clear guide. On exams you're allowed a double-sided cheatsheet. Really great experience, it's a breath of fresh air to have a course so well organized!

There are no whiteboards allowed on exams which is quite ridiculous for a math heavy course. Some of the questions on the exams are on material not covered in class. You are allowed a note sheet on exams, but they do not help at all. She makes this class very difficult even though the majority tend to struggle with accounting. Would not recommend.

The class was pretty easy, although you have to complete a lot of assignments. She was very caring and friendly, but the exams feel like you are the main charcter in Squid Game. There are at least 10 people watching every move you make at the exam, eventhoug there are 4 exam verions. Otherwise, the class was very good.

My business course is outdated and not enough walkthroughs for difficult questions. No whiteboard allowed come exam time and this really slows down how you solve problems. Not enough exam material to practice on harder questions that students struggle on the most. Some exam questions are different from what was taught in this course.

I had basically no interactions with this professor and heard more from the course facilitators. Of the interactions I did have with Prof. Mackie, she did not seem understanding of personal situations at all. It seemed as though she already had her mind made up to give me a 0 on graded work but let me explain my situation out of courtesy/policy.

Honestly, do not take her class. She shows 0 compassion for students, she does not know what she is teaching, literally reads off slides, she does not care for you to succeed she just cares for filled seats and getting paid. I quit my major because of her, she gives 0 motivation, 0 understanding. No good words. Exams do NOT have cheat sheets!!!
easiest A of your life. do the homework, show up to class, and get a cheat sheet for the exams. like cmon, what else could you ask for. BABY FOOD

Lectures are quite long, and she tends to go through topics quickly, so it's easy to get lost if you're not paying attention. She's really helpful and allows for a lot of outside-of-class assistance. You are permitted a one-page cheat sheet and plenty of resources to help you study for the exams!

Pretty much whole class is exam based and exams are totally different then the reviews she provides. To add, she lacks any minimal amount of compassion a teacher should have, forced me to take exam 2 hours after finding out my grandpa had died. This tanked my grade and I was .1 away of a point from passing, she said it was out of her control. Awful
exams are nothing like exam reviews. all are multiple questions which is pretty hard. overall good prof

This was one of the most inconsiderate professors I have ever had. As an online student, the majority of us have families, careers and other obligations in addition to our schooling. I did not feel that Professor Mackie took that into consideration.

Cost Accounting is difficult. This is the best online instructor I have had and not sure why she has such a low reviews. She tells you what you need to study on. You just have to put in the work. Lots of video examples of problems in excel. 1 of 3 hard exams but allowed curve from all students results + past results.
Lectures were slightly boring but that's accounting in general. Thought class was very fair and cheat sheet on exam was clutch. She was Very helpful with any questions you may have.

The lectures can be boring but she explains everything very simply and easy to understand. The study guides for the exams give you everything you need to know for the test. We also got a  1 page printed page of paper we could use on the test. She really wants you to succeed in the class and understand the principles of accounting.

Professor Mackie did a good job at explaining accounting concepts, and was easy to get in touch with if there was any questions or issues. I also enjoyed the good extra credit opportunities, and being able to review my exams outside of class.

Alison is a great professors in terms of her personality. She is passionate about her teaching and very supportive. Yet, her lectures are sometimes confusing due to her unclear instruction. Overall, since she just recently became the official lecturer at Eller, it's acceptable at this point that she still has some areas for improvements.

ACCT 310. The worst accounting professor at Eller. She teaches the class in a disorganized manner. You are quizzed on things never discussed in class. She literally tries to trick her students on purpose. Do not take her if you do not have to.

Alison is a nice person but not that helpful when you ask her content questions. She makes a lot of mistakes during lectures and students have to point it out for her to realize. She basically just reads the slides and is not that knowledgeable about the content. Exams are 50 questions with only 75 min and it is almost impossible to finish on time!

Alison is clearly new to teaching, but she is kind. Every lecture she makes lots of major mistakes while completing problems that often aren't pointed out unless a student realizes. Beware of the after class quizzes as they will kill your grade, but besides that, before class quizzes and tax return projects aren't too bad. Exams can be difficult.

Personally Mandel is not the best option for a professor in your Eller career. You'll understand. 

When asking Mandel to meet and discuss assignments he tends to ignore you. He is a harsh grader and when confronted and he still ignores the student. It will be super hard to get an A in the class, due to his harsh grading and absence in the course.

Mandel will work you hard. While he is tougher than other BCOM instructors, he means well. I took many lifelong skills away from this course which are all in thanks to this instructor. Recommend, but get ready to work.

Mandel was such a great teacher. He knows how to lead and despite what the other reviews say, he created a great learning environment and pushed students to truly improve. You will do a lot of public speaking and group work in this class, but it will make you a better professional. Exposure therapy fr works.

Mandel grew on me throughout the semester. At first, he comes across as unnecessarily hard. In the end, it appears to be a "baptism by fire" thing. Mandel is more difficult than other BCOM professors which can be a good thing if approached right as he does want his students to succeed. BCOM will take up a ton of your time in 1st semester Eller.

Easily the hardest BCOM class, to the point it seems like there is a disconnect between him and other instructors. For the case competition, he admitted the way he taught the class would not win when you are being graded by professional judges, which just shows that his material is very off from real standards.

He can be a little abrasive, especially in the feedback, but he isn't out to get you. He lightens up a bit on the grading as the semester progresses and it is possible to do well in this class as long as you put the work in. I'm personally not a fan of his teaching so I wouldn't take him again but he will set you up to succeed.

This class (first semester of Eller in general) will push you to your limitssssss. Mandel is a super harsh grader but that being said you will definitely improve as a student over the course of your class.

Extremely difficult class. He clearly has favorites and treats them as such. Extremely harsh in grading, takes points off for the tiniest details. If things aren't done the way he likes he will give you a bad grade, even when other teachers teach it the opposite way. Very difficult course but you do learn a lot and the feedback is beneficial.

Horrble. I have vivid memories of him docking points from a whole team's presentation because he didn't like how one of the team members had positioned her arms. During presentation days, he always picks one team (not even necessarily the "worst" group) to verbally eviscerate in front of the entire class for 5-10 mins. Must make him feel important.

Was terrified of this class but gotta say I learned so much and walked away a better speaker. Work with him outside of class, use your resources, and put TIME into your group work, and an A is possible. Mandel and this class are tough, but just follow his guidance and trust the process. You will have to meet w/ your group almost everyday to succeed

Really cares for his students, but good luck getting a B in this class. Others teachers that teach this class hand out A's to their students, but he makes it impossible to get an A. No matter what you do you will not get an A, but his feedback is very helpful in allowing you to grow professionally.

Professor Mandel truly wants his students to succeed. He will purposefully grade you poorly in the beginning, but if you stay on top of your assignments an A is definitely possible. He gives extremely helpful feedback. I went into the class with poor public speaking skills and left very confident in my ability to use them.

Mandel is a touch professor who prides themselves in giving their students the skills to grow. This class was challenging at times but I learned so much along the way. Everything I learned I will take with me into the real world. 

The class has quick deadlines that can be overwhelming at times. Presentations and projects happen very fast.

I don't know why Professor Mandel has such bad reviews because he is amazing. He is only a tough grader because he wants to push his students and make them grow. Take the first couple grades with a grain of salt, but as long as you work hard and create a good connection with him you will be just fine. He really cares about his students and it shows

I wanted to like this class but Mandel makes it extremely difficult. Extremely slow grader and his feedback is average at best. He's ironically bad with communicating and doesn't hesitate to play favorites. Get ready to stress about possibly losing scholarships as he will intentionally give you a low grade on the beginning assignments!
Probably the worst teacher to take 314 with.

He is an extremely tough and nit-picky grader. Only a small portion of his students get A's in the class. It's almost as if it satisfies him to see his students fail, because he will fail you with any opportunity he gets. He also assigns an absurd amount of textbook readings every day. He is an unadaptable teacher. Run before it's too late.

Awful teacher. Teaches what not to do rather than what to do. Tells you what he expects of you on the day the assignment is due. Takes a LONG time to grade; took three months to grade one paper. Very nit picky for no reason. 2/15 of his teams won at case competition which reflects his teaching.

Slow grader, unclear, inconcise, confusing expectations, taught the opposite of what was expected in case comp, not accomodating grade-wise, good bye mental health, domineering, zero extra credit, not caring or considerate, and said he cares. Readings and quizzes easy (very small part of grade). Teammates evals big part of grade!

Honestly not a good teacher. It's a business communication course (basically the only way he can grade you is if he likes how you write). MAJOR POINT: He purposely gives everyone bad grades in the beginning and then progressively increases your grade towards the end of the semester. Try and get a different teacher.

EXTREMELY long time to grade, teaches what not to do rather than what to do, no clear grading/expectations (set up to fail), gives very rude/malicious feedback. unaccommodating throughout Covid. you'll never meet his expectations, ur never good enough. His grading was beyond unreasonable. hardest 1st sem eller class. UAccesss switch professors rn!!

I love Mandel! He will push you to be better. He is a tough grader in the beginning but he wants to see you grow. You can get a lot out of this class if you work hard. The 1st half of the class is group work heavy, the 2nd half is individual. Tip: Use your preceptors! They know what he is looking for and can help you!

This class was by far the most rewarding class I have ever taken. Mandel is a great professor that pushes you to grow and master your BCOM skills. This class can be overwhelming at times, but try to have a postive outlook because everything you learn in this class is a valuable skill. Mandel makes this class fun and is one of Eller's best!

Nice guy but VERY tough grader. He's the kind of professor that won't give out A's till the end of the semester because we have to "grow" to earn them. At least he did teach us real-life valuable skills and not random lecture crap that we memorize and forget the next day like in other classes.

This was a great class and I really benefited from everything I learned. He does not start off easy so be prepared to take a few hits at the beginning, but by the end of the semester you will know exactly what his expectations and requirements are to get a good grade.

If you go to professor notes you can see the type of prof and person he is. Terrible class, terrible content, terrible person. If you can't take constructive feedback, then go back to doing what drama majors do. I'll take my americano with no cream, thanks.

Mandel is one of the best teachers that I have had at the University of Arizona. It was definitely a hard class, but I feel like my presentation skills actually improved. You really get out of it what you put in.

takes a long time to grade assignments, teaches what not to do rather than what to do, and gives very condescending/snide answers to questions and feedback. He routinely broke his own presentation guidelines and didn't give any feedback to grades posted after class was complete. Simply doesn't care for the students and is lazy; switch professors.
Most condescending teacher ever. Answered questions snarky and doesn't care for the students.
Mandatory you buy the book that he wrote. Mandatory you buy the program for presentations that his previous student made. This is collusion at it's finest form.
An incredible teacher in every possible way!

Mr. Mandel is the best consulting BCOM professor in Eller. Yes there is a lot of work but he helps his students SO much and really wants everyone to do well. He is a nice guy and an understanding teacher. He is nit-picky with grading but thats because he wants his students to continually improve. The most influential teacher i have had in college

While he is passionate and knowledgeable about presenting, he doesn't understand that BCOM is not everything.
