He needs to take a long, hard look at his teaching choices and realize that making students spend more time on BCOM than all other courses combined can be extremely detrimental to student success going forward, which is morally wrong.
The best Eller has!

Tough grader. If you're unsure of something, ask for clarification. At the beginning of the semester, you won't know what hit you. But this class will TRANSFORM you. Your writing and presenting skills will improve significantly. You get out of it what you put in to it. He's prompt with replying to emails. Overall, I loved having him as a professor!

Your guiding light through first semester Eller is going to be Michael Mandel. Your writing and presentation skills will improve significantly and you will find a true reward working with non-for-profits. I can not stress how wonderful, patient, and caring Professor Mandel is. Practice practice practice, be honest, and do your very best.
He's a great teacher

One of the best classes I've taken at Eller and Mandel is the man! Not only does he dress to perfection, but his lectures are entertaining and he personally helps you with the assignments. The skills you will learn in this class will help you tremendously in the business world. I highly recommend taking the CSR track - much more rewarding!
Not necessary to get textbook. He is always willing to help upon making an appointment. He's hard grader and is hard to please. He also sends long emails every week.

This class was difficult, but will help in the long run. Michael's door is always open when you need it. If you go to see him, he will take the time to help you figure out the best way for you to succeed in your class. Though he is a slightly harsh grader, he only does it to ensure that you are well prepared for the rest of your Eller classes. :)

This guy is just not a good teacher. He is very hypocritical and is susceptible to errors that he continuously gets mad at us for. His teaching style is less about what you learn, and more about what you are doing wrong. He has a degree in drama... but teaches business. Not the usual quality of Eller. If you can get a different professor, do that!
One of the best professors I've had at the University of Arizona.

Professor Marinan was amazing at teaching excel and python programming. Super helpful during class as the material and exams can be difficult sometimes and will offer extra credit. Take him if you get the chance.
Awesome professor. Favorite this semester. Class goes by quickly as always working on real world data assignments in groups. Replied so quickly to emails.

This professor was good and let us make the syllabus at the beginning of the semester, but he wasn't that great at teaching. I've never taken a stats class before, and he just read off the slides the entire time, and sometimes his slides were outdated and confusing. He did provide lots of extra credit, so he makes the class easy to pass.

Overall really nice and very understanding guy, but taking this at 8am was brutal. The lecture presentations are pretty vague and he doesn't give much detail, which leaves you to figure out how to do it on weekly group assignments. Honestly, take this for extra credit but you're better off going with any other BNAN 276 teacher.

Professor was super supportive. Many of the assignments were working together and interesting. Stats isn't the easiest but he made an early morning class much easier to get up for. Take him if you can.

Great Professor to take for BNAN 276. Would recommend him to everyone to take him. He offers extra credit left and right as long as you go in person. Great lectures, uses real-world examples. Even though I do not really enjoy business stats at all, he made it possible and easy to pass this class.

Without a doubt, this professor was the most reasonable and understanding professor I ever encountered. He also provided positive feedback and responded as quickly as possible. I took him for BNAN 277, which I highly recommend, as he actually cares and communicates effectively with his students. I would give him a 11/10 rating easily.

Professor Marinan made a challenging course enjoyable and easy to follow. He was incredibly responsive, explained everything clearly, and was very flexible with deadlines. I felt supported throughout the entire course and would definitely take it again. My favorite Professor at this university.
Had Brent for a stats class and a programming class. Explain concepts with realistic examples. Really cares about the students.

This was a good enough course, the material is not my favorite but it's easy to get through with Brent. His classes are very fun and you will get free snacks from him all the time. Lectures can be easy to get lost, but he's there for clarification after and posts them online. Exam/assignment heavy, although all assignments are given a video guide.

Professor Marinan made this class fun by keeping the classes entertaining, while still teaching us so much about Business Statistics. He really cares about his students succeeding, and I enjoyed my time in his class.

Professor is engaging in large class and made the stats material interesting connecting it to real world events. The exam guides are a great refresher before the exams along with the lecture slides and videos.

I loved this professor!! This class does require you to study outside of class because his lectures are short, but he gives lots of extra credit to make up for it. He really cares about his students and will help you if you're struggling. His assignments are easy to follow with videos too, so it's hard to get lost. Highly recommend for BNAN 276.
He is the best professor at this school. SUCH A NICE GUY. He actually cares about his students. Such a good instructor with lots of EC. Very good communication and very fair on grading.
Brent is the best professor I had last year.  Was intimidated by some of the topics but made it easier to understand.  Used the Tableau he taught during my summer intern.
Literally so much EC. Don't try to cheat on take home quizzes. he knows

He is a GREAT teacher. He is so generous,if you show up, you can get extra credit! He is amazing, he gave us snacks and April bags(birthday bags), he even brought his dog as an assistant. Class was fun! He is amazing!! If you are taking BNAN 276, take with him, you won't regret!
THE GOAT! show up to class and get extra credit. easy A. awesome cool guy, would take again. So easy.
Brent is very likable, open to student suggestions for how class should work, and flexible. At first glance, the concepts can be difficult to digest but Brent does well explaining them. Good class.
Just a great guy overall. BNAD 277 is a ridiculously useful class to take if you are already in the workforce.

Had him for BNAD 276 and 277 (online) and he was a dream. In 276, he brought us snacks and treats which was really nice! 277 was pretty much a repeat of 276. Since it was a summer session, we were only graded on the tests and labs (and EC!!) Make sure to study the synopsis and lecture notes/powerpoints cause they are helpful for both classes!

This class is incredibly doable, and Professor Marinan is extremely helpful. I took this class via the online option and still found success. Professor Marinan does not assign too much homework, making this course incredibly manageable.
I took BNAD 276 with Brent over the summer as a 7 week course. The class was fast pace but manageable as it was well organized. There is extra credit opportunities and the grading is very fair.

Fantastic professor. He does everything in his power to help students succeed. The class is organized and taught very well, lectures are clear and there are plenty of opportunities to get help and earn extra credit.

Brent was a super nice and friendly guy. You can tell he really wants his students to succeed. He lets you take the tests online and offers a lot of flexibility. He really listens to the students so if you want something changed you can change it. SO much extra credit

Best professor I could ever ask for tbh, tons of extra credits PLUS snacks on review days before the exam. Whoever rate this man below a 4 is def never in class because the exams and Has are so easy. I would take this class all over again for the experience.

Literally my favorite professor, he is a good-natured and funny guy. He made the subject more fun to learn and had conversations with the class about class policy so you know he really cares. The hw assignments are video led and although the tests can be challenging he gives you what you need to know in class.  I hope to have him for other classes.
If you have the chance to have this professor TAKE HIM, he da best
Really cool guy, terrible professor. Lectures do not reflect the tests.

I enjoyed this class a lot. He never made lectures boring. If I could, I would take another course with him! He even brought his dog the last day of class! For exams, study the study guide because it will help!

Dr Marinan was extremely engaging all semester. Dr. Marinan also really helps you understand the software Tableau Public, which is a really useful tool in the business world. His step by step videos of the projects make it easy to understand both what you are looking for and how to navigate the software. I would definitely take this course again.

Brent has consistently been one of the best professors I have had at the UA after taking him three times. He is knowledgeable about statistics and marketing strategies. The best at breaking down difficult concepts in lecture, while being very personable.

Brent is super chill. Best professor I've ever had here. He quite literally asked us at the beginning of the semester how we wanted it to go. We said, online tests, multiple choice, lots of EC, etc... he said okay and that's how it's been all semester. Highly highly recommend.

Breaks down complex topics well. He summarizes the info from the book into digestible synopsis' so we don't have to read the book, which streamlines the process. The info course content can be daunting at times, but Professor Marinan made the course interesting and straight-forward.

Brent is an amazing professor!! The first half of this class with the other professor is horrible, but his teaching was seriously the light at the end of the tunnel. His style of teaching and extra credit opportunities saved my grade. He also made the lectures infinitely more interesting to listen to.

I'm not going to lie, this may have been one of my favorite courses at the UofA so far. Brent was an AMAZING professor and honestly just seems like a genuine guy. He takes the time to ensure understanding, he's funny dude, offers tons of EC opportunities. I'm not gonna say it's an easy A, but you won't regret taking it with Brent.

Brent is awesome. He gives so many extra credit opportunities, and really wants you to succeed in the class. Projects aren't too difficult at all, and there are grace periods for lateness. Tests aren't too bad if you're confident and/or study, and there are plenty of other grades to cushion that. Super helpful and respectful guy.

Brent might be my favorite teacher/professor I have ever had at any level. He is an incredible, friendly, funny guy who is knowledgable about the subject. The class is quiz heavy and the material can be difficult but if you study and work with him you will do just fine. Lots of extra credit and fun games like jeopardy.

Probably my favorite professor I've ever had. Super engaging and inclusive during his lectures, actually looked forward to this class. Super helpful and receptive to questions. It would be a mistake not to take this class with him!

Super nice, instead of 3 big tests he gave easier quizzes to cover the same material. Teaches tableau which is useful and cool (students complained about it) but it was extremely useful. Quizzes are just like his practice problems

Perhaps one of the most understanding professors I've had. His course is structured very well and his exams are not overly complicated. His lectures are always very engaging. He offers so many opportunities for extra credit, but as long as you attend lectures and take notes, you'll be fine. He's managed to make the course interactive and engaging.
Brent is amazing! He is so kind and understanding. He teaches so well but please go to class, that is where you will learn the majority of the material. He really wants you to do well.

Brent is a kind and respectful teacher who aims to see all of his students succeed. His lectures were efficient and informative, and his goals for the semester are extremely clear. I really recommend taking any class with Brent.

Pre-session 3 week course - Easy to keep up with assignments and lecture videos are very helpful. All the tests are online and open note plus you get 2 hours and that is plenty of time to look up answers that you need to double check. I would highly recommend precession with Professor Marinan.

If you need to take BNAD plis take it with Brent. He gives so many extra credit opportunities, and his class is overall fun to take. He's a very nice professor and I would definitely take another one of his classes in the future. Don't miss out and take a class with Brent!
The class was pretty easy for the most part but there are many symbols you have to remember. He gives extra credit often so I highly recommend going to class. Overall a great professor!

Brent is one of my favorite professors! He makes the class much more manageable by doing small quizzes as opposed to big exams. He also offers many extra credit opportunities. Take him for 276 and 277 both if you can.

Prof. Marinan is an extremely nice professor. He responds to emails quick as well. He gives out a significant amount of extra credit when he does random attendance checks throughout the semester. The class is heavily weighted on the tests so make sure to do very good on those and study!

Brent's class was usually fun, and he gives tons of extra credit. He makes the tests and homework extremely easy, often dropping or cancelling assignments so I wasn't forced to learn very much in the class. Aside from that he is passionate about stats and is helpful and accesible if needed

Brent is a great guy and fun professor. However, his teaching style is a bit all over the place and makes it difficult to retain the information. While he does give out a TON of extra credit, I have been in the class for 3 months and feel like I haven't learned anything.
He's amazing! an Absolute Gem!

He is the best close your eyes and take him. He gives hell lot of extra credits, the papers are not very tough but you have to study. He does a lot of efforts posts videos of all the methods. Grades very well. He is an angel. One of the best professors I had.
He is an absolutely amazing, best teacher ever. LITERAL ANGEL. Very understanding and gives lots of extra credit. Recommend 100/100, he is so freaking nice i cant.

I took both BNAD 276 & 277 with Brent and got A's in both. Pay attention to the synopsis he puts out. Most questions on the quizzes/exams come from that. He gives extra credit to those that show up a lot of the time so don't skip class.

This is by far one of the best professors I have taken at UA. He is hilarious and makes the class enjoyable. Statistics is an annoying subject but he actually makes you want to be there to learn the material. He gives a lot of extra credit which is a plus because it helps you out when you need it most! Would definitely recommend this professor!
Brent loves to gives out extra credit like a frat boy loves to ask who you know here!!!! Hes the coolest guy ever & kills it every time, definitely take his class

Brent is one of the best professors I've ever had. He's a great guy who one could easily tell cares about his students. The material can be tough but he gives enormous amounts of easy extra credit and also is always there to tutor if you need help. I actually enjoy stats more now because of his class.

Brent is a tough teacher but he makes sure you know your stuff and will offer a lot of extra credit and is very accessible outside of class. If he gives you the option of taking several quizzes or 3 exams during the semester, take the quizzes, they're smaller and typically easier so you can get an easy A. Just come to lecture and watch the videos
Clear grading with opportunities for extra credit. Don't have to put in much work but if you study smart, should have no problem getting an A.

This class is really tough material wise. I feel like I learn how to actually do and apply stats from the lab, but take very little away from the actual lecture. Can pass without knowing much because there is a lot of random extra credit. Show up and you'll pass at least. Brent is a really nice guy and very approachable to get help.
Brent's class was my favorite of the semester. The class was not easy but he made it relatable to real-life applications and made me a lot more interested in statistics.
I found this class to be my hardest one at the University of Arizona thus far. One good thing I can say about Brent is that he gives out a lot of extra credit.

I loved having him as a professor. He will help you do well and clarify when needed. He tries his best to make stats interesting and does so successfully. One of the best teachers I have had at the U of A. He also makes sure the majority understands concepts and will review needed materials when he senses confusion.

Brent has been one of my favorite teachers at the U of A. He constantly offers opportunities to gain extra points on tests and is willing to move the entire classes test back if it falls on the same day as an acct 200 or econ 200 test. He drafts effective study guides for tests and one day brought his dog into a lecture for de-stressing purposes.
Overall not an easy class. The class lectures don't help very much, you have to teach yourself the material. He gives a lot of extra credit.

Brent switched to only quizzes for the class. They were similar to exam difficulty, but only 1 chapter at time. Many opportunities to do well in class. Great professor at U of A. No regrets having him over 2 semesters.
Brent is an excellent teacher and gives quite a few extra credit opportunities. He also makes it readily apparent what will be on the test, so there is really no excuse to not succeed in this course.
Brent was such a great professor! BNAD 276 was so easy with him and gives you many opportunities to help your grade.

Took class as a senior. Brent has been one of my favorite professors at U of A. I don't miss these large class sizes though. Took AP stats in HS and this class was at a much higher level. I don't have to take the next stat course, but hands down would take him again. Keep up with the work.

Brent is a funny, nice professor and really cares about his students, but is an AWFUL teacher. His lectures are inconsistent, and he does not make a lot of sense explaining and working through problems. Stats is difficult as it is, but the structure of his class made it even harder to be successful. Gives a lot of extra credit.

Brent was awesome, he made class fun and enjoyable. Not going to lie, this class was difficult but he is super helpful and if you visit any of his or the preceptor's office hours you should be good. If you are going to take stats, make sure it's with him!!

If you have to take a BNAD class Brent's the guy to do it with. He'll do anything to help his students learn & he's also super funny which makes a hard class a lil better. This class is challenging, but as long as you do ALL the practice problems he posts and go over the HW questions before a test it's manageable. He also gives some extra credit

Brent made BNAD fun.  He is really funny and fair to all his students. He wants everyone to do well and doesn't take himself too seriously.  If you study and keep up with the connect assignments you should do just fine.

Brent is amazing! He is willing to help you and will work with you when you are not understanding something. He will help his students who he sees are struggling and will be very clear about what is going to be on the exam.

Brent is fantastic at making sure people understand the material and get good grades. Homework and tests are curved, study hard and you will do fine. He is very laid back and sometimes unorganized/late to class, but show up and turn in every assignment, even if its incomplete, and youll do fine.

Brent was a great teacher for BNAD 277. Honestly, if you go to class, do some studying, and take it seriously you will get an A! Brent is always willing to help out if you ask him, you just have to ask him! He can get side tracked in class but his tangents are funny.

I have not regretted a single thing to choose this professor at MWF 9am class. Brent is amazing and he is amazing in teaching us the difficult materials presented in class. His entertains the class through his humors and is so far the best professor I have taken at the University of Arizona. He serves the definition of a true, caring professor.

Brent strives to make stats as clear and easy as possible. He is funny and relatable. If you ask he most likely will give you an additional resource or post materials from lecture online. Read the book because that's where most test questions come from. Walks you directly through the labs so you can get an A every time.

He is really a great teacher to teach the basic business statistic knowledge. He is kind and patient to answer your question. He responses your email quickly. Practice and review will give for each exam. If you review good, the test will be a piece of cake. He curves and gives extra credit to help his students get their expected grades. 100% enjoy!
Exams were not easy, but definitely fair.  Attend the lectures and you will learn alot.  Have him next semester again and looking forward to it.
Good teacher. Easy to get an A in the class if you do his study guides.

Overall, the material was not hard, but what got me was the exams.  Majority of the class would get pretty good grades on the exams, but I didn't do well since I get nervous on them and definitely should have been more prepared.  500 Total Points: 400 points on exams (4 exams) 100 points on other things (homework/case studies/ attendance)
I love Brent. He is the best professor I've had at U of A overall

Professor Brandon is alright, will call you out. Hard to reach outside of class. Definitely tests your critical thinking skills, which was good. The class itself is stressful though and group projects are not worth the stress. The fact this is a GenEd class does not equate the the stress and group work needed. Its undergraduate not a graduate class

I took this as a gen ed, you learn about random stuff and there is lots of homework. He can be a semi-tough grader, but he doesn't provide a lot of feedback for homework. You do some group projects, he critiqued the projects in front of the whole class. He completely bashed our group, we still received a good grade but it seemed unnecessary

Dr. Marshall is a great person but he grades heavy and will call you out. He expects the best of you and wants your participation. He assigns homework that is due every week and it is a lot of work. 2 huge group assignments that take time to do out of class. There are no exams except the final, which is an oral exam, Mainly presentationsbe prepared

Great overall concept and approach, but impossible to contact outside of class both text/email. Heavy reliance on group projects. Team members not being as contributing? Oh well you're responsible for them and share their grade. You'll be a parent chasing them. Would not take again because of the group projects. Not worth the stress and anxiety.
I cried over this class

This is probably the best course I have/will ever take, Dr. Marshal honestly posed as a role model/mentor for my peers and me. No matter what you are studying, take this course. You will apply this material in everything you do and it will benefit not only you but others around you.

Although his class was very hard, it was very useful. You need to show up to class ready and with everything he asks of you. The things he teaches are interesting and so are the lectures, but you definitely need to work hard and prove to him that you want the A.

This is one of the hardest 150 you can take. It is so much work for a gen ed. DO NOT TAKE. Yes, the prof wants you to succeed in the class, but he is such a harsh grader. He is also so strict and rude sometimes. I spent more time doing work for this class than my other important classes. Each attendance is worth 1 point.
He's horrible. Period. Not understanding all.

Dr. Marshall was not a bad professor considering he wants his Scholars to succeed. It is just how he goes about it. He acts more like a boss than a professor which for a class of this nature is a good thing. Just a lot of demanding work, but I learned a lot from this class. I dont recommend this class for anyone that is not a freshman however.

Super interesting class. But, Dr. Marshall rarely responds to emails and doesn't communicate grading criteria very well. He gives harsh verbal feedback and is pretty scary. But, I learned a lot from this class and it was otherwise enjoyable.

Dr. Marshall has high expectations for his students which is in no way a bad thing, but when students don't meet those expectations they frequently called out in front of the whole class and ridiculed. He plays the role of a strict judge more than a teacher that is there to genuinely help us learn. If you are an introvert you will struggle here.
He is a super chill professor and his class can be fun as long as you dont stress super hard.

Dr. Marshall may be seen as someone intimidating in the beginning of the year. The reason for this is because he is trying to make students grow and get better as an individual. Most of the comments here are negative but I suggest to take this class if you wish to become a better individual. He will make you work for the grade, but you will benefit

Don't be fooled by the bad reviews, Dr. Marshall is an outstanding professor that challenges students to think critically about their decisions. The people who drop his class are people who give up when faced with a challenge... You don't want to be that person. If you want to understand the fundamentals of success, take this class. Good luck.
Learned nothing from this class completely useless...

Extremely demanding class and tough professor. I would advise to take two(regular) or maybe three(very easy) Gen eds instead. DROP OUT!!!! before it is too late. Wish I could... Lots of homework and group projects(nasty ones with nasty teammates).

Thoughtful, forces you to be accountable and responsible, personal activities like a letter to yourself to be mailed back to you in 2 years, a portrait drawing activity, a yearbook-like signing on the last day of class. Is demanding and harsh at times, but is very worth-while. Brandon cares about his 'scholars'.

I had mixed feelings about Dr. Marshall throughout the entire semester. But in the end, I am very happy I did not drop the class. His signature slogan is,"I want you guys to work for it." And he truly does. He is disorganized and doesn't reply to emails, but this class is extremely rewarding and truly makes you problem solve

I loved this class. Brandon is picky about little things but they are meant to teach us how to follow simple directions. If you read the articles, do the weekly assignment, and participate in the class you'll do great. Overall, very easy A if you can follow simple directions.
Brandon is extremely rude and never responds to emails effectively, i thought this was going to be a fun and exciting class because he seemed s nice the very first day but i was very wrong.

I wanted to like this class. I really did. But Brandon just ruins it. Although the class is about communicating with other people, he doesn't do it effectively with us. His expectations aren't clear. He belittles you and makes you feel like nothing. He means well I think but I just don't get him. Save yourself the stress and don't take this class

Never skip his class or be late, he'l l drop your grade by a letter, very strict in fact too damn strict, gives out a lot of homework but also extra credit opportunities as well. Dont take this class if you are looking for an easy A, He'll make you work for it, a lot and even then you aint gonna get it.
Fire the professor and evoke his teaching credentials. Very terrible at communication. The class is easy, but take it another professor.

Not really an understanding professor and not an easy grader.  Although he stresses the amount of time you need to put into assignments, he isn't specific on exactly what it is he wants.  You 100% need to show up and participation is a huge part of your grade.  I got a solid B and I gave pretty good effort.

Participation is key, otherwise you'll be losing points in this class. This is not a bad thing though, Marshall manages to ease you into getting out of your comfort zone through his activities. By the end of the course everyone in the class was significantly closer. Class was interesting but you do need to know the content and participate.

This class is somewhat difficult. You have to participate and go to every class if you want to pass. Marshall hates repeating himself so make sure you listen. You will also spend lots of time meeting with groups outside of the class. Overall it was a pretty fun class just lots of work

This class was one of my hardest classes but in the end I truly enjoyed it and would take it again. Brandon is not the best at replying to emails and his grading can be subjective. You learn a lot about controversial topics. You must understand that this isn't an easy gen ed but definitely worth taking.

I was placed into this class since I am in ELITE. This is the hardest class I have taken. Brandon doesn't have the courtesy to respond to your emails and his grading is extremely subjective. He doesn't even follow his own rubric. If he doesn't like you will not get an A. This class would be better if he wasn't so strict. Take with discretion

This is not an easy class, has a lot of projects and unexpected presentations. so be prepared with notes. This semester the book was use for all the homework's. Participation is a MUST!. skip more than 3 times your grade will go down. the teacher is awesome seems intimidated at first but he is very helpful. Don't forget to always give your opinion.

Every class is out of 4 points and if you don't have at least 3 insightful comments to add to the conversation, you will get no points. Sure there are no tests, but that means there's no way to get past lost points. A few students dominate the class and make it impossible for you to talk. The way he grades makes the class overly stressful.

Thought MIS 150 would be my easy A. Turned out to be the hardest class I've ever taken (in a good way). Dr. Marshall is very, very good at what he does. He is charismatic, intelligent, and kind, which makes his lectures on very sensitive subjects some of the most honest and revelatory conversations you'll ever have in school.

Have an opinion on issues, and support those issues, and you won't have an issue. There's group projects that require a lot of out-of-class time to work on, and you're with that same group the whole semester. There's no textbooknotetaking, but if you're not participating in the discussion you can't get a good grade.

As an out of state student this was a frustrating class. Multiple times he will cancel class day of or email you day of that something must be printed. Respectable teacher although I would not recommend the class if you have a full schedule and busy life. Sometimes the work is much more than expected.

Very big on class participation, doesn't like to have to repeat himself, grades are worked for, the class itself is very interesting and makes you think, the class changed me as a person, he is a fun teacher, I only had 2 quizzes, a lot of homework on D2L but it is not all at the same time,  and chances for extra credit.

If you want an easy freshman year, don't take this class. It's extremely time consuming and his grading is subjective. If you still want to take this class, participate A LOT and he'll like you. If not you're screwed. It is a super interesting class if you want to challenge yourself and it counts for diversity and a 150.
It's an awesome class, it will take some work and studying out of you but it is worth. The class is really interesting.

This class was very easy for me. It is recommended to ElITE students and I would recommend staying in it. He can be a hard grader at times, but his class is very interesting and I learned a lot. It's discussion based and only one assignment a week except the two projects.

This class is great. It is a discussion based class so there are not tests or quizzes. There are weekly homework assignments, but they are interesting and can change your perspective on many things. There are also two huge projects that determine your grade. Just follow the directions and participate in class and you will be fine.

Do not take this class. It is the recommended class for ELITE students but it is awful. Marshall is very biased. He grades you with very little feedback (even when you contact him) and if he doesn't like you you're done. The class has so much potential to be awesome but he honestly ruins it. I really disliked this class and wouldn't recommend it.
She cares about students and wants them to do well
This might have been my favorite class I have ever taken. It was difficult but not impossible.

Katie is one of the best mentors/professors you will ever have. If you have the chance, take her course, you will learn a lot. Show up to class, do all the homework, and it will reward you. Exams are tough, but if you do the practice problems you will be fine. 400A is the hardest class I've taken, Katie made it easier.

Katie Maxwell is one of the best teachers in the Eller college. She is extremely respectful, caring and knowledgeable about her subject. Go to her office hours, do the handouts, and pay attention in class you can easily get an A. I got a B because I bombed an exam and never studied for quizzes, but 10% more effort would've gotten me an easy A.

There is a lot of content you have to learn at a quick pace, so I highly recommend taking notes before classes and then following along more closely for additional notes during class. The assignments and exams are very manageable and pretty easy if you stay on top of the content. Professor Maxwell is also engaging in her lectures and nice 1-on-1.

Katie is easily the best professor I've had so far. She truly cares about what she's teaching and makes lectures fun to go to. The class itself contains a wide variety of material, but she is great at explaining the reasoning about topics, making it easier to digest.

Katie is by far the kindest and most caring professor I have ever had. She always goes the extra mile to take care of her students. This course is extremely difficult but Katie's support makes it possible to do well.

Currently in her class and 85% of the students are learning nothing from assessment & evaluation. Her assignments are unclear and she merely speaks at us for 2 hours. The material is extremely redundant and her teaching style is dry.

Hard class but Katie makes this class worth it. It is evident she cares about her students and their success outside of the classroom. This is a hard class but she makes it enjoyable and engaging. Stay on top of things and follow the schedule and you will be fine! Take advantage of office hours.

Katie is my favorite professor at Eller. She is so loving and caring. She really wants all of her students to succeed not just academically but also as individuals/adults as well. She loves her students like they are her own children.

I did not do very well in this class, although there were many review sessions that I did not attend. Professor Maxwell was very accomodating with extra test time. Show up to class and every review session possible and you can NOT cram for this class. Overall, the material itself was very confusing but, Professor Maxwell was very kind and leniant.

My favorite professor by far! Her love for teaching is clear and is there to help you succeed. The class can be tough at times, but def doable. Take advantage of her office hours! They helped me soo much throughout the semester. Be aware that the amount of time you put into this class is what you will get out of it.
Teaches very well. Helps all the time to make sure you understand concepts!

Dr. Maxwell was clearly passionate about the subject and encouraged students to learn through practice, which I found was the most helpful part of class. She was always happy to take my questions and really cared about students learning the content. If you just listen and participate, you will learn everything you need to in class..

She is AMAZING and I absolutely loved how the class was structured. All assignments were clear; lectures and homework clearly built on each other and prepped you for the exams. I wish every quantitative MBA course was like this one.

Katie is amazing! Such a great lecturer and has SO many resources for quizzes and exams. Go to class consistently and put in effort outside of class and you should be fine. NOT a cram class though, so stay on top of everything. She has so much experience and knows what she is talking about. Always willing to help! Wish she taught every ACCT class.

Katie is a very good teacher. She truly cares for all of her students. Although the class is hard she does a very good job to help those who are struggling. I would recommend paying lots of attention during her lectures as a lot of what she says relates to what is on her exams and quizzes.

She was a wonderful professor. Had clearly organized lectures that followed the book. She used multiple resources to help you learn the material, including homework, reading, and exams. Very accessible to explain questions. Tests were challenging but fair. Going to office hours was very helpful.

Katie is an awesome professor who explains accounting wonderfully. Accounting is very hard and she makes it easier. I have never had a teacher offer as many resources as she did. I always had additional problems and learning sessions. Her class is fun to be in and I wish she taught every accounting course!
Best professor I've had at UofA. Accounting is a tough class, but she makes sure that every question is answered and is very kind. One of the best personalities on campus.

I have no idea why she has bad reviews. She breaks down the content in a way that makes sense. Accounting is very difficult, and any new subject covered within accounting seems impossible to learn at first. She made it possible with time and practice. She provides tons of practice worksheets and goes through them step by step. I'd take her again!
Katie is AMAZING, the best accounting teacher I have ever had! If you have an option to take a class with her as the instructor then DO IT!

By far my favorite teacher I have had at the university. Definitely a hard course put she provides every resource you need to be successful. Lots of office hours, practice materials, and content videos. As long as you go to class and take advantage of resources you will do well.

Dr. Maxwell is super sweet and caring as a person but the course itself is definitely a weed out class. Most of the material is unnecessarily hard and you have to pass with a C or better to move onto the next accounting course sequence. The course is really test-heavy and theres not a lot of room for extra credit.

While Katie is a very nice person by nature, the class itself is extremely overcomplicated which makes it too overwhelming. The online format didn't help with this either. Very tough grader and not great at providing opportunities to make up for lost points. Choose another teacher if you can.

Katie is so cute. She is so sweet and caring AND she is a great teacher. Compared to other teachers, I think that she somewhat accidentally over complicates things, but she still rocks. Make sure you stay on top of the content because it definitely catches up with you!

Lots of how-to information is learned in this class. She walks you through all aspects of the problems, but you will have to put in the time. This content is beyond challenging, but there's no one I'd rather struggle as hard with than her. She is one of the best professors I've ever had, even though this class is the hardest I've ever had.

Katie is one of the most caring professors I have ever had, definitely gives off a "mom" vibe! Her class is likely the first upper division accounting course you will take at UofA, but she is so approachable that the more difficult content is doable. Thanks Katie!
Her class are clear

Katie is the best professor you will find at UA. She cares so much about her students' success. There are a lot of clickers before classes and the book is only used for practice problems that aren't graded. Make sure you go to class and the optional review sessions every week.

Lots of how-to information is learned in this class. She walks you through all aspects of the problems; but you will have to put the time in doing all the ungraded homework assignments on time to make sure you understand it.

Katie is the best professor I have ever had. Her class is very difficult content wise but she makes it very easy to understand. Office hours are super helpful and she is just a very caring person. She really wants all of her students to succeed in and out of her class. Would take her as a professor again in a heartbeat
Greatest instructor ever. Very clear lectures. Katie always cares about students and open to any questions. But you really need to work hard and practice to get an A

She is extremely helpful and caring. If you don't understand the material, you can seriously have a phone call with her to resolve the problem. I don't think this class is an easy A but if you do enough problems and ask for help, you can get the A. I would take any of her courses again if I could. She is one of the best accounting teacher on campus

She is a great teacher, great advocate for 1st semester accounting majors at Eller. Is dedicated to see you succeed but she makes you work for it. Do not let non-required homework fool you, if you don't do it you will have trouble on tests.  Go to her office hours, she's super helpful. Be nice to her and she will return the favor.

Katie is easily the best teacher I've had through U of A.  Ironic because I didn't actually pass the class BUT she is the sh*t.  I wish all my teachers were as understanding as Katie.  If you have the opportunity to meet this women, do so.
GREAT JOB TEACHNIG
one of the best accounting teachers very educated and knows what she is teaching i enjoy her class  work hard for an A tho
Kindness, superb knowledge, excellent interpersonal, with a bonus of a very attractive accounting professor is the sum up for Katie Maxwell.  I wish Eller has more professors like her.

KATIE !!! is one of the best teachers at Eller, guys please take advantage of this lecture and learn as much as you can in this class, because u wont get as much help in 400B and nobody is like Katie, wish she teaches 400b :(((

Katie is really the best professor I have ever had. Lectures and OH are always helpful. Always stay current with her recommended End of Chapter HW, which are great practice problems for exams. I wish I could have her in 400B as well.
great teacher will make u work hard for an A

Katie is one of the best professors at Eller she will make u love accounting and will do everything to help even on weekends she will come to the UNI for a review before the exam she's just awesome. the material is a little hard but Katie will make u understand it everything in this class is great i hope she teaches 400B

Amazing teacher! Her class is very difficult, but she is one of the best teachers in the accounting department. Go to office hours, they're helpful and a good way to get to know her. Worked hard and learned a lot in her class.
very tough class
Katie is a great teacher! The quizzes were pretty hard but she drops 2 quizzes and is very helpful with exam reviews. Do the practice problems she recommends and you're golden.

Katie is no joke. If you do what she asks prior to class and make an effort to understand all of the material before class, you'll do well. In-class activities she asks you to complete before class may come up as pop quizzes. Practice all you can, then practice again and again. She is not generous with grading.
Katie is a great teacher she sets high expectations for her students while treating everyone with respect. Her teaching style motivates you to participate in class. BOTTOM LINE SHE IS AWESOME!

Katie is the best teacher I have ever had. She is so nice, she explains everything very clear, and is very fair. She will do anything she can to help you out. Acct 400A is a challenging class in general but Katie makes it enjoyable and managable.
Awesome teacher!  Is always there for help and explains the material really well. The class material is hard but she teaches it in a way that is easy to understand and learn.
She really wanted the students to learn the subject. She went out of her way to come in on weekends for study sessions. The work was hard but she was the ideal teacher.

If you don't have a strong work ethic, you better get one quick (you're going to have to work for your grade). Katie is awesome though. Great teacher,enthusiastic, helpful, and comes to every class with a smile.
Great Prof
Always gave detailed instructions and was a great professor.

I've had a 4.0 for 10 years, and it was ruined by his random grouping on the group project and the weirdly hard grading policy. It's comical to pair 6 strangers together (probably with bad grades) and have 20% of your grade crutch on that. His class is a joke.

He is an amazing professor. In class lectures and discussions were very helpful to learn the material. Always willing to answer any questions and explain them very well. Attendance is not mandatory but there are some lectures with Top Hat codes. Would absolutely recommend this class.

John Mcclintock is a good professor. He has plenty of helpful outside resources to help but the class was not very difficult. The class consists of weekly homework and a business report write up that is accompanied by a walk through video. Attendance at lectures is mandatory but discussion attendance is not.
Professor McClintock was amazing!! He's a very nice man and very helpful. He has numerous ways to contact him if you need help with anything. 11/10 would recommend.

While a good chunk of the content is similar to BNAN276, the content was interesting and Professor McClintock made it engaging. There is a lot of homework through the case/Tableau assignments and online assignments, but it's pretty manageable if you're on top of things. Quizzes were online and the final group project was fun, but unclear rubric.

Professor McClintock is very kind and genuinely cares about his students' success. I'm not the strongest in statistics, but he was helpful and supportive throughout the course. I recommend attending lectures—the in-class practice was really useful for the open-note exams.

This dude rocks he's so sweet and caring and u can tell he just genuinely loves what he does. I took BNAN277 and him and Prof Hunter just make sure everything makes sense and you can get the attention/help you need because analytics is hard. TCannot say enough good things about him he's such a good prof and just honestly just a good person.
McClintock is one of the best professors I have had at this school. He is very well organized and actually wants to help students to do well. Would highly recommend him.

BNAN 277 is a pre-requisite course, try your best to turn in your homework's on time. Its very easy to fall behind and you should consider taking this class with Mr. McClintock as there is no major exams except 4 quizzes with about 30ish questions and one final group project. If you want to succeed highly attend his office hours and class times.

McClintock's classes aren't very hard. He does two class days a week, and one optional discussion. He does two types of HW, discussions which take ~1.5hrs, and Ch HW, which takes ~1.5hrs too. The exams aren't that hard either. Not bad for a second-year stats course.

John is a great professor who really cares and tries to connect with his students. He tries his best to make his lectures interesting to students. I thought he did a really good job teaching this class, especially since I didn't understand BNAD 276 all that well.

He's just here to give us an education and wants us to succeed. This class is a pre-req so I'm glad he just got straight to the point with what we needed to learn. if we got through the material early on day he would let us leave, always respectful of our time.

Easy class over all. All test are open book but still difficult because the answers aren't readily accessible. Discord is an easy way of communicating with him and will answer quick. Pay attention on days he says to come to class.
He is the goat. Actually passionate in what he teaches and is incredibly approachable. One of the greatest teachers out there and deserves all the recognition he gets.

John is absolutely incredible at teaching business stats and made the class easily understandable, especially for someone who didn't understand much of the BNAD 276 prereq. He's very responsive in the discord chat and provides helpful walkthrough videos for all of the case assignments. Great prof, 100% recommend.

John McClintock is one of the most caring professors and is ready to help. He will respond to your emails almost immediately sometimes. As someone who didn't understand statistics very much, he made the class more enjoyable and helped me achieve.

John is amazing and truly has a heart for his students. I have never had a professor who is so helpful and accommodating outside of class time, if you need help he ensures that you get it. His lectures were very helpful and clear, overall the class is not too difficult as long as you pay attention and do the homework!

John walks you through the majority of the case assignments each week. Walkthroughs are available online or in person. He is very tech savvy and can help you solve any problem you may have with your case assignment.

John makes the BNAD277 lab interesting and applicable to the real world. He walks you through the statistical scenarios, and has pre-recorded videos if you can't make it in person. His instructions are very clear and thorough, and he is always willing to answer questions. You can tell he really knows the material he's teaching!
McClintock is a legend, he gives you everything you need to get an A and more. I wish I got to see more of him, but as an MIS major it makes sense that I would not unfortunately.

John makes the lab portion of BNAD277 a total cake walk. He is informative, concise, and caring. The labs are easily the best part of the BNAD277 curriculum. These are real world scenarios that will prepare you for the business world. Attendance was optional, and the pre-recorded videos were very helpful.

Take this professor!!!! John was an excellent professor!! You can tell he enjoys his job and ensuring you are successful in the class. He is really good at explaining the labs and making the assignments easy to understand. I wish he could teach all my classes!!
John was a great assistant with Prof Hunter. He was helpful outside of and in class, would recommend taking classes with him.

John does a great job providing video walk throughs for every lab assignment. He is accessible outside class and answers questions quickly. Live lab lecture was never required and assignments were always graded fairly. I would recommend taking this class.

When I took BNAD 277 John taught the Lab with Martin Hunter and they are an incredible duo! John is the sweetest guy and is always willing to help. Ask questions if you are confused. Learning Excel and Tableau from this class has been extremely helpful. Go to the lab, he explains everything if you go.

John is so nice and willing to help anyone and everyone! Would recommend him to anyone! He is the sweetest man out there! Truly wants you to succeed and understands you are not a professional with Excel! Always willing to help!

John is very nice and is very helpful too if you need help with excel or the homework in general. He also does an amazing job too with trying to teach the material the best he can so we understand it.
Great teacher! Literately perfect

John is probably one of the nicest and most caring professors I've ever met. Great person and great lab professor. He will help you succeed. He holds office hours which are really helpful and will even stay longer when there's an overflow of students needing help. Dedicated to helping us succeed to say the least...

John is the sweetest man to ever walk the earth. We do not deserve him. I have him for lab and he walks through everything in a way that makes sense. If you raise your hand, he will literally ask if he can help in a room full of 300 other students if the TAs have not gotten to you yet. He is very patient and makes the class more manageable

John teaches all of the BNAD 277 Labs and is great. He is always willing to meet and review the material if you miss or just don't understand. The labs are really beneficial because you learn a TON of excel skills. If you skip the labs you are dumb. He does most of the work for you and they can really help your grade! !
John is super nice! He teaches lab for 277 and is caring. He will help you out and wants you to do well. lab aint bad if you try

COOLEST GUY EVER. I really hope he becomes a professor someday, his students would adore him. He's super funny and such a knowledgable teacher. He goes above and beyond. He'll be at his office hours 24/7 to help his students with the labs + other questions. Him and Gray are a bomb combo loved them both! John is one of the best teachers out there!

The content of ACCT430 is very interesting and lectures can be engaging. But, there's nothing to gauge if you are understanding concepts correctly and the exams are much more difficult than they should be. No extra credit and must participate every class to get 100%. Go to office hours early and for group projects as very very vague instructions.

My favorite thing about this class was when it was over. Have to participate almost every class for full credit. Group projects INCREDIBLY vague with poor instructions- go to office hours for help! She's helpful there. MCQ portion of the tests were reasonable but the FRQs focus on very specific things so if you don't know it you're screwed.

There are 2 group projects and she gives few instructions. Lectures arent too bad but I wasnt a fan of how she tracks participation. She makes you pass notecards forward so she keeps track of who participates but its very distracting. Also, often people ask questions you already know just to get credit. For the exams, you need to know everything.

McGrath gives super vague instructions on both projects and study guides for exams. Super lecture heavy and full of stories about herself. Harsh grader and person, will humiliate certain groups during the presentation part of the course. I have serious concerns about her even being a professor, as she got her CPA revoked but teaches audit/ethics?

Decently interesting lectures but the tests are really difficult. No resources to help you do well on the test and questions are about very specific things. not really sure why she makes the exams so difficult. also very vague project instructions

I did not enjoy the constant participation because it was such a large portion of the grade. Her project instructions are frustratingly vague and unhelpful. Additionally, her exams are quite difficult specifically the written questions are extremely specific on one sentence said during class.

Majority of your grade is on participation which is only given to the same 10 students that just repeat what the first person said the whole class. She attempts to humiliate kids not only when they're in class but also when they are gone. Honestly just the rudest professor you'll meet and will never be able to recommend her for anything.

I wish I knew this about Ann before I started...but she is the only Audit/Ethics professor and was involved in fraud and had her CPA revoked. Look up "Ann McGrath Arthur Andersen Scandal" and a Los Angeles article will show she had to relinquish her license. What kind of stuff we are supposed to learn from someone involved in fraud? Think on that.

Worst accounting professor I had at UofA. Seems to thrive on students struggling and doesn't create a supportive environment. Publicly calls students out for wrong answers even though participation is mandatory. Exams are nearly impossible. Might be time for #retirement.

I ignored the bad reviews when I enrolled in this course. I shouldn't have. For your well being, do not take this class or this professor if you have the option. Every point counts from the very beginning.

No one likes this professor, she loves talking about how she's from Brooklyn and that's her only personality trait. Her class itself is fairly interesting, but the tests are hard for no reason. Both classes she teaches should be relatively easy, but instead the tests are hard and she doesn't provide good review.
Awful professor who doesn't care about students' feelings at all. Vague/nearly no instructions on projects. Exams are extremely hard for no reason.
Exams unnecessarily hard. No exam reviews. Vague instructions for projects.
Absolutely awful professor, entitled, self righteous and aims to make students feel terrible.
don't take this prof

Pretty much the same feedback as everyone else. Vague instructions on projects, tough exams, and lots of in class participation. Annoying class all things considered, but at least the audit cases are fun and audit as a subject is interesting.

Make sure to review those slides before all exams as well as the cases. She gives. VERY vague instructions on the projects, so do your very best to plan ahead with your group. Also, you should aim to participate each class (she keeps track with cards). Participation was worth nearly 15% of total points.

Just make sure you understand the material.
She was an extremely tough grader. There were 2 group projects to complete and the 2nd one had super unclear expectations. I did find the course informative though.

She is a terrible professor. to do well on a test, you need to study literally everything because study guides sucks. The instructions on projects are so vague and she gives no resources to be successful. She does not care about your success. Good Luck. Would prob be interested in audit if it werent for her.

She's a terrible professor. To do well on a test, you need to study literally everything so start way in advance. She is not helpful when you go to office hours, and the assignment instructions are so vague. She does not care if you are successful because she gives you no resources. Good Luck. Would prob be interested in audit if it wasn't for her

This class is interesting but annoying. While the class is easy for time-management since there's only 2 assignments and 2 projects, that was a problem I found with this course. There was no practice to the lectures that she would give and there was no study guide, only an exam topic sheet. Also doesn't help that she offers no extra credit.

She can come off very stern but she definitely wants you to succeed and fully understand the material. She also expects you to do a ton of research for the case studies and provide multiple references. The downside of this course is that the exams are worth a great chunk of your grade. She is very responsive to emails and Ask the Instructor.

This class was the worst class I have taken through my three years of college. There is no study guides provided for any of the tests and she gives a very vague and useless overview of what is going to be on the test. You can tell who she does and doesn't respect in the class which can get very frustrating. I would never take it again.

Professor McGrath is probably the best accounting teacher I've had. She does well to keep the class engaged and is very funny in lectures. There are attendance quizzes every single class which can be kind of difficult. Very very easy to get a B in the class with how many points are available, but more challenging to get an A.

She's very tough. Provides no study guides at all. Her attendance quizzes are very hard and you will often miss points on them and it is as if you didn't go to class. The tests are extremely difficult given that you basically have to read over every topic due to her minimal description of what is on the test. A very difficult class.

This Accounting class was terrible. The teaching itself isn't bad, the way she conducts the class is poor. Every day, an in-class participation quiz is difficult (she tracks your location, attempting these at home is not an option). She provides no study guides, only vague 'concepts'. if you get on her bad side, she can be very disrespectful.

This professor is great! I liked her class despite not really having an interest in going into healthcare accounting after graduation. No extra credit. The group project is tough but with a good group you guys will get through it. She is a stern professor but she is still nice. Make sure to study in advance for her exams.

Ann is a great lecturer. She is engaging and asks great questions. A very good high level course in auditing. Unfortunately, she was not clear enough on her grading for the class, so multiple students got B's when they were expecting A's. Had to do with participation points. She's an easier grader but beware: probably no EC.

Ann made the class fun, which was enough to keep me going through the semester. Her stories and practice assignments were helpful for learning overall, and not just for the exam. 440 is forgiving, do not let the healthcare topics intimidate you.
SHE IS THE BEST!!! You will learn so much and she shares some really cool stories.

Ann is awesome: a straightforward, no-nonsense lecturer who has a wealth of knowledge (and a secret sense of humor). Accounting is challenging - it seems like many reviews are based on course difficulty more than her abilities as a teacher. Don't skip class, do the dang homework, and double-check your answers for accuracy and you'll do well!

ACCT200 in general takes a lot of studying outside of class. But with McGrath I truly felt like I was teaching myself everything. She doesn't really explain concepts in lectures and the lectures are extremely monotone and boring. If you take ACCT200 with McGrath prepare to spend most time studying for this class only.

Took ACCT 200 and I think it's a great class. She is a very nice person and responded to emails fast. The "bigger" projects aren't hard they are just time-consuming. This class and this subject is something you just have to practice to understand. She is intimidating but extremely nice and helpful

Ann was amazing and was very knowledgeable about the subject because she has loads of experience in the field. The class is tough but if you do the homework and show up to class you will be more than fine for the exams. She is also extremely helpful in office hours & will go over in detail what you got wrong on your quizzes/exams and why.

In-class quizzes every class other than presentation/test days. Tests are reasonable and similar to the quizzes. Two group projects. Ann is knowledgeable and experienced in the courses that she teaches, and always knows how to crack a good joke or two! I would definitely take this professor again.
This professor is awwwwful, never choose her class if you have choices. She teaches classes in an extremely boring way.
Horrible
Shes AMAZING
Just take notes and the exams are easy

Paul is a great guy and gives good lectures. The only gripe I have is that the class is only graded off 4 exams, 3 projects, and a handful of quizzes. The projects and quizzes are easy and there is extra credit. Exams are sometimes badly worded or confusing, but he mentions everything in class. Overall, Paul is a good professor who cares.

He is not a bad professor, the exams just carry heavily in the class. There is a ton of extra credit work which you absoulety need to get high grades on to have a good grade in the class. There is a couple memo assignments, lots of guest speakers. Exams are open book and open note so take really good notes you will need them.
Got an 89.89% in the class and he refused to round my grade. Horrible professor comes off rude and narcissistic. Exams are hard for no reason and bad lectures. Only good thing is extra credit.
Prof doesnt care about his students, the class consists of one group paper that has almost no instructions and three exams. Thats all.
Anybody can write a review without an account.  Don't believe everything you read.  Hiding behind your fake reviews
Avoid this professor at all costs. Narcissistic and does not care about the success of his students.

I personally would not take him again. Fabulous lectures - entire class is test based, there is no mercy. He doesn't round grades. He is the definition of narcissist. Test questions are based on lectures, none of the questions make sense and are all over the place. Even if you show up 100% and pay attention, still not guaranteed an A.
Please do not take him. No lenience with rounding grades. Class is entirely exam based and hard to get higher than a 70 on the exams. Open note and exam questions are NOT straight forward.

So I took this class for a business credit to find out it doesn't count as a gen ed. The professor DOES NOT care if you succeed and genuinely acts like he could care less about you as a person. he literally said that his wife told him that he is too hard on his students during my lecture. and the class was easy fs but there is no leniency at all.

Although this class isn't too hard, the exams are hard and to get a good score on your exam you HAVE to be in class or you'll fail. I'm a current freshman and he isn't lenient with rounding grades and has no empathy for his students. This will affect my Eller application and I may not get my first choice in Eller now because of the harsh grading.
I was so close to an B, but he refused to round to it. Lowkey a narcissist and the dept head so good luck getting an appeal. Test questions made no sense
Very engaging lecturer and explains material well, but comes across as a big narcissist with little consideration for others. Strong presence in the classroom, just not the most empathetic.

I would not recommend taking this course. Despite being only 1.5 points away from an A, the professor refused to round my grade, even though I completed all the extra credit, consistently attended class, took detailed notes, and earned 100% on the memos. While he is kind and engaging, he is strict and not flexible with grading.

The professor himself is great, his lectures are entertaining which makes the hour go by pretty fast. The exams are what make up your grade and he doesn't provide much practice. Attendance is definitely mandatory because he presents slides that he waits to release. Exams are really hard, I was averaging 70's even though I studied so much.

One of the best business professors at this school. Super cool guy, makes lectures entertaining and you are only graded by 4 one page papers, and 3 open note exams. I really like Prof Melendez and how he utilizes gvuest speakers which is refreshing as many other of my classes never did this. Great guy and cool class

Class is easy and an easy ethics credit for Business majors for the requirement. Reason for the low ranking is that Dr. Melendez owns the book for the class you use and seems like a narcissist a lot of the time. But if you can get past that it's and easy class as long as you take notes.

Great professor and lecturer. You are graded on four memos and three exams. If follow the template you'll receive an A on the memos. The tests are not cumulative, they follow a certain set of modules, and they are open note. But there are things like case-studies that are not in the notes, so attendance is encouraged to have all the class material.

Best teacher I've ever had at UA. You need to go to lectures, but they're engaging and interesting. Everything is applicable to real life, and the assignments are pretty easy. As long as you take the exams and HW seriously, you'll get an A. He responds to emails quickly, and he is happy to answer questions after class. Super funny and nice.

This class was so ridiculously easy. In the beginning I was a little scared because there's only 4 grades in the gradebook (3 tests and 1 group project), but he lets you have typed notes for the tests so if you take good notes you will definitely pass. Just use ctrl+f, I got almost 100% on every test.

One of my favorite professors. Genuinely cares about the well-being of his students. Offers lots of extra credit for BNAD 302, which is an extremely test-heavy class. There are 300 points worth of exams (3x100) and 100 points worth of memo assignments (4x25). The exams can be difficult but are open-note. I'd advise copying the lecture slides for it

Love this professor so much. His lectures are engaging, and he is a personable person. He's an inspiration and a connection I'm blessed to have. Just go to class, take notes, and you will ace the exams. Depending on the course, you might have to do light reading outside of class; however he is very clear with what he expects and how he grades!

I took the 3 week version of this course so there was a lot of material to cover, but as long as you kept up with the weekly lectures, the workload was super manageable with just a weekly test and extra credit.  The only thing was that we were told we needed to purchase the textbook, but then didn't use it at all - all exams were based on lectures.

He is an amazing professor I loved him! Attendance is not mandatory however he does not post the slides so you have to go to class to get the material. However the lectures were interesting and easy to follow. You get as many notes as you want for exams so as long as you go to class and take notes you will get an A.

BNAD 302 is an easy class if you go to class and write down every word on the slides. Grade is made up of 3 open-note exams and 4 easy memo assignments based on guest lectures. He offers extra credit and makes his lectures funny. The only thing I didn't like was if you sit in the front he often touches you (hug, fist bump, knock on the shoulder).

Overall, this is a fairly easy class. Open note tests (notes have to be on paper/printed though), practically all you need to know on the test is covered in lecture (you can get by without the textbook), and he is very kind, engaging, and willing to answer questions during lecture.

Paul is one of the best lecturers at the UofA. The tests were all based on the lectures and case studies, so you always know what will be on them. He also provides lots of extra credit opportunities for going to ethics events outside of class. Overall some of the most interesting and engaging lectures I've been to, and he is always super friendly.

Melendez's class is very simple. Write down every word of the lecture slides and you will ace the exams, which are open note -- they are the same word for word. He keeps his lectures interesting even in not-so-interesting topics and does care about his students. I would definitely recommend this class!

One of my favorite professors, so easy to listen to and learn from. There are 5 one page memos (Super Easy), 4 open-note exams on the class notes, and 2 extra credit opportunities. If you go to class it's an easy A. Never bought the textbook his lectures are all about it.

Great Professor with interesting lectures. Be sure to write everything down for open note tests bc IT WILL BE ON THE TEST. Not too much homework but need to go to class because lectures are not posted. Got almost 8% in extra credit to offset hard tests.

Professor Melendez is a decent professor and offers good extra credit opportunities but the class was exam heavy and I found the content to not be completely related to the textbook. With only 4 assignments and no attendance points it can be difficult to earn a good grade.
He teaches business ethics, but scams you out of buying a $250 textbook that gives you 5 years of not needed access. On top of that, he wrote it. He gets money from it and we never once used it.
If you have the chance to take one of his classes, PLEASE DO! He's absolutely amazing, and I wish I could take anotehr class with him.

Dr. Melendez is one of the best professors I've ever had. He's friendly, his lectures are really engaging, and he does a good job connecting the concepts in class to real-world issues. It's very easy to keep up in this class as long as you take good notes and do the weekly reading. I'd recommend this class to any student, business or non-business!

Dr. Melendez is an amazing professor. He has tons of experience teaching and it really shows! He's someone who really cares about what he's teaching and supporting his students, which really makes it easy to learn. For my class, all the tests were open book, note, and internet. If you have a chance to take his class, you'll definitely be happy.

The class was interesting and Professor Melendez is a kind guy. However, the lack of assignments made it really hard to do well. Im a near straight a student but did poorly on the last test and ended with a b, even though i did extremely well on everything else.

Melendez is a great professor and the lectures are usually pretty interesting and engaging. The one thing about this class that is a little frustrating is that there isn't a lot of test prep. I recommend studying everything and taking a lot of notes because what is on the tests are inconsistent with what Melendez will tell you to/ not study.

Paul is a great guy and professor and loves what he teaches. Getting the book is mandatory to do well on the exams, read those and make sure you take detailed notes during lecture because he will ask for detail on exams. Lectures are mandatory but he usually lets us out 15-20 early every time.

Dr. Melendez is extremely passionate about Ethics and tries his best to get you a good grade. Offers multiple extra credit opportunities, but only for a certain amount of students. Exams are very difficult if you do not go to class. Find a friend to get notes from if you are not going to be there because no slides are posted.
Difficult because the exams are not based on what he talks about in class.

Professor Melendez is by far one of my favorite professors. Attendance isn't mandatory, but you really need to attend lectures to do well because he doesn't post his powerpoints. Attending isn't hard though because he speaks so well and talks about things that are actually meaningful. There's so much extra credit too!

Honestly Dr. Melendez is a great teacher and is very passionate about ethics, but this is the worst class i have ever taken. He easily goes off on a tangent during class and does not cover much for the exams. If you dont go to class, you will have no way to access notes unless you have a deans excuse, 30+ points of extra credit are offered though

No homework, extra credit through the roof, and fascinating lectures. Considering we had no homework the entire semester (seriously), I was able to just turn my ears on and learn all the dos and don'ts of the business world. He was extremely respectful to our class, the faculty, and the subject of ethics. Great professor, but an even better person!
Some of the tests questions are a little unfair but overall interesting class.

Dr. Melendez truly cares about what he is teaching and it shows in his lectures. Many extra credit opportunities, gives in-class overview of exam the day before which was extremely helpful, very little HW. He also brings in many guest speakers which help relate his course to real world situations. 10/10 would recommend for all business students!

Dr. Melendez is a great professor. He is really passionate about the business ethics and has really powerful lectures. As others have said, 4 or 5 one page memos on guest speakers as well as three exams throughout the semester. You must attend class though, powerpoints are not available on D2L so take good notes.

You need to attend class or you will not be prepared for the tests.  The tests are challenging but he does a "review" before each one, if you take good notes and STUDY you will do fine on the tests.  The lectures are interesting enough.  He offers extra credit and the memo's he assigns are very easy.  I would recommend taking this class.

Student say it's a hard class but literally: 1. Listen to his lecture (never more than 50 min top) 2. Study the notes and the readings he SAYS WILL BE ON TEST.  3. Do extra credit- easy 4. Boom 99%  Literally have to do nothing or not even study to not get an A. He's a wonderful guy too. My fav prof yet.

Likable guy. Pretty good lecturer- asks questions and makes it sorta interesting. The problem with the class is that the tests are really hard and rely on memorizing things that are really irrelevant. It evens out because there is a ridiculous amount of extra credit you can get, though.
If you work and go to school DO NOT take this class until you quit working.  Not worth the headache of trying to make up the assignments if your work schedule interferes.
Nice guy but the class sucks

If they're going to make this class mandatory, then they should at least give you another teacher as an option for this course.  The majority of your grade is based on exams and the exam is not based on the discussions in class it's based on philosophy this is not a philosophy class it's a management ethics class I think somebody needs to tell him
ehh class. It's alright, sort of boring but I could see it being useful in life. Try your best to pay attention and take notes to help study for the tests.

the class itself isn't hard. 3 tests, 4 memos. lots of extra credit. just do the reading and you have to go to class because that's where the test info comes from. not so bad. lectures are long. can be an easy A. failed the first test but got As on the other 2 cause I actually studied. do all extra credit. that changed by grade and saved me

Really engaging professor. You must attend class because there is a lot of material covered in each lecture. The tests really are not that hard so long as you do all of the supplemental readings before the test. Good notes are a must! Very interesting course because he teaches in stories, not lectures. I would take this class again any day!

Professional, clear, smart, helpful, fun. You need to take good notes, read the lectures, and study hard for this class and pay attention. Do every extra credit. Class is not easy if you don't work hard. You really learn a lot. Good instructor, good class.
Great teacher, class is very interesting. Tests are easy and come straight from lecture notes so attend class and you will be fine. No homework other than a few reviews.
