Class was terrible.  He made the tests so hard it was unbelievable.  My worst class, BUT... he does make up for it by giving so much extra credit.  I got D's on all the tests but still got a B in the class. Hard but also hard to fail.

Attendance is definitely mandatory. Tests are based solely on the lectures and the cases from the book. Even with attending every class and taking notes, the tests can be very difficult. Melendez wants his students to do well so do the extra credit he offers to the class.

I don't understand how anyone can dislike Dr Melendez and his class.  I'v taken a million different business classes and they are all so BORING.  He makes classes very interesting.  You HAVE to attend class and you HAVE to take notes.  He will help you out as much as he can with extra credit but most of the exams come directly from lecture notes.

I really disliked this class. Mr. Melendez gets rave reviews for doing nothing. His lectures are awfully boring. He spends most of the time rambling. He invites in crazy guest speakers who are only there to promote themselves or their company. DO NOT read the book, just the cases in the book. Tests are based off of class notes and book cases.

Not an easy A. I was eventually able to receive an A, but I had to go to class everyday, sit up front, record lectures, do the extra credit, and haggle with TA's just to earn my points. If you don't study for these exams you'll earn a C at best.

An easy class.  There are three exams. ATTENDANCE is MANDATORY.  Make sure you take good notes in class (most will appear on the exams). Required readings is MANDATORY as well. There are three exams during the semester. It will test you on the lectures and the readings.  Take advantage of the extra credit offered. By then, there will be no more!

Super easy class, but you HAVE to take notes (almost all questions come directly from lectures) or you are screwed.  Interesting for the most part, and he brings in guest speakers from dif companies.  Really enjoyed the class.

This class requires good note taking!! Most of the questions from the test come straight from the notes if you were paying attention.  The lectures are boring, but stick it through so you can end up with a good grade. Dont bother reading the book, but read the case studies for sure!

Yest this class may be a bit slow at times, however he really wants you to understand the material, and it's definitely easy to grasp. If you go to class and take notes you can EASILY get all As on the exams

Easy class as long as you take good notes (he doesn't post them online) and read the cases. His test questions come straight out of his bullet point notes, as well as some of his essay questions. Easy if you're a good note memorizer...he also gave 20+ points extra credit which just made that class a breeze.
Take this class for an easy A!! If you just take good notes and study the notes the night before and read the cases you'll ace the tests and he gives extra credit!

I took PA 206 with him too, and both classes were fantastic. Attend lectures, because it's the structure for the exams, but if you take good notes and study, you'll get an A+ easy. Great professor, awesome guest speakers, excellent class. Definitely worthwhile!
gives a lot of extra credit i failed the first test but still ended with a high B

I *really* disliked this class. I found it boring, pedantic, & utterly confusing. Melendez always pushed his own views and didn't allow students their own opinions. He focused more on what was HE thought was wrong with philosophies rather than the actual philosophies. I studied endlessly for exams and got 100%s on m/c ?s but did terrible on essays.
Melendez is the best teacher i have had in my life. take this, oh and go to costa rice with him too.
Lectures are interesting and there is not much hw. This is an easy class but there are essay portions on each test which suck

Best teacher I've had so far in college. He ended the 2.5 hour class after 1.5 hours on a long day, so you always got out at least and hour early. He's clear and concise while describing the material, and he's great at connecting with students. The subject didn't interest me, but he made it worth coming to class. For tests, STUDY HARD.

Paul melendez is by far one of the best professors at the U of A. I will truely miss his class. His test are entirely of his lectures. I dod not read a single page of the textbook he required for the class. Just be aware his multiple choice questions can get quite tricky. I will take any future classes I can with Dr. Melendez.

Great guy who cares about what he is teaching and is a good lecturer! You have to take notes during lecture for tests because nothing is posted online, make sure your memos aren't late! But easy and interesting class, take it!

Professor Melendez is a really nice guy and his class is super easy. It is really straight forward and he gives you all of the essay questions that are on the test prior to taking it. the material is also pretty easy. If you go to class it is definitely an easy A

He's passionate about the topic. Lectures are short. He can be a little hard to understand with his metaphors. Never uses the text. He posts any outside readings online. Gives the essays in advance. Go to class and take great notes, that's the key.

Professor Melendez is probably the best teacher I've ever had. His class is actually really interesting if you join in discussions and pay attention. It was so easy to get an A in his class. As long as you take awesome notes and write out all the essays before the test, you will do well. I would suggest bringing your computer, he talks fast.
Pretty cool professor, easy to talk to and very friendly. The course itself is very dry and boring, but he tries to make it somewhat exciting. The guest speakers are all pretty good though.
Lame professor, lame class. Totally useless and Melendez treats students as if they were four. Waste of tuition, take another class.
I didn't really like the class. The lectures were short and not very exciting. It gets rid of an ethics requirement, but not particularly usefully beyond that.
Great Professor. Interesting and I strongly suggest you take the class.
Dont even bother buying the book. I didn't use it once, and never needed to.

Paul is an awesome prof.  If you are a weak note taker, invest in a voice recorder.  His tests are completely based on lecture.  The MC portions are easy, but his essays can be brutal.  Don't waste money on the text either.  Just go to lectures and you get the easy A/B.

Very nice guy and really cares about the ethics of business. Do the reading, it will be on the exam in essay form and is helpful to follow along in lecture. Notes aren't posted online so make sure you attend all/most classes. The only problem was 2 different TA's grading exams differently; 1 TA hadn't even read the material the question was about.

The textbook was a small inexpensive one, something completely not heard of these days.  There were numerous of other readings throughout the course.  The class was interesting and full of information.

Melendez is awesome! He makes the class very interesting and is quick to clarify and help his students understand. Class usually got out early. DO THE EXTRA CREDIT! It will help you get an A without trying too hard. Class discussions were very insightful. Take this class!
He was very nice and interesting. Sometimes he difficult to hear in the big lecture room. Tests all based on notes. Very generous with extra credit.
Big Fan of Melendez! Class was one night a week and usually ended early. Tests are from lecture and readings. Extra Credit will guarantee you an A! Information is generally interesting and familiar.

First, DO NOT BUY THE TEXTBOOK!!! He tests from lectures so take notes and ask questions to clarify things. He is usually pretty cool, some of his tests are really hard but he usually drops "problem" questions. Good class.

great class! he is such a great teacher and is so funny, interesting, and nice! TONS OF EXTRA CREDIT. you need to go to class though b/c everything for tests are from lectures. make you sure you do the turnitin.com assignments b/c they are worth 25 pts. i accidently missed one and that isi prob one reason why i didn't get a B. TAKR THIS COURSE!

This class is the best business emphasis credit avialable.  I took it two days a week and class was out at least 30 minutes every day.  No need to read book.  Tests are off notes and some short readings.  If he does not mention the reading in class there is no need to read it.  Essay questions are given before test to allow you to prep

Paul is an awesome teacher. His class is easy as long as you attend EVERY class and take good notes. You only need to study a couple of hours for his tests and offeres extra credit that bumps you up a full letter grade.

Great teacher. GO TO CLASS. Take good notes and brush up on writing skills. He offers extra grade to earn the A. This is a real college course. If your lazy dont bother. I highly recommend this class.
Geat guy. Take good notes and go to class! I went to every class and would only study for about 2 hours before the test. I gat an A-. You dont need to buy the book.
Great guy!  Very good at lecturing.  Tests can be a bit tricky and the essays are very harshly graded.  He does offer a good amount of extra credit to make up for the tests.

The class is interesting but i hated the tests; they've got multiple choice questions about the most irrelevant miniscule details ever, such as "in what year was such and such book written??"  Honestly i think it's more important to knw what the book is ABOUT but whatever.  paul is a cool guy
Great professor! lectures very well and exams are easy if youve been to class and took notes on lecture

Exams are very hard but he offers a lot of extra credit and easy memo assignments that help improve your grade. If you do the extra credit you will get an A or B even if you fail the tests. There is no final!

Melendez makes a boring subject pretty interesting.  Do not worry so much about buying the book, his tests are straight from lecture.  Reading the book will not substitute for attendance in class because his tests are completely from lecture.  Study for the essay questions or you will be sorry.

Not the most interesting class I've ever taken, and Melendez' teaching style doesn't do much for me. Overall though, it is a fairly enjoyable class with movie days and guest speakers. The exams are pretty difficult. But extra credit and memo assignments help boost your grade. He is a very fair grader,doesn't want to see kids who try in class fail.

He is a great speaker and it is a great class to take. It is very important information to know. Lectures are usually quick for how long they are. He gives you time to take notes. Basically know all the names, etc. on your notes, briefly do the readings and always go to class, and you'll be fine.
explains his stuff really well, but you have to attend all of the lectures, not even kidding.  his tests are kind of hard, but gives some quality extra credit.
tests are way too hard. TA who grades essays is ruthless and hates men
Over half the class failed his midterm. He says he'll give us extra credit opportunities to bump us back up, but it's still pretty frustrating.
Over half the class failed his midterm. He says he'll give us extra credit opportunities to bump us back up, but it's still pretty frustrating.

This class is alright, Paul's teaching style is annoying (notes from his spoken word). Tests are decently hard, if you took great notes you'll do fine. He lets class out 20-30 min early EVERY class. The material is somewhat interesting.
Great Professor, easy to go to class and fun vibes.

Prof. Michaels is the bomb. She inspires her students and walks the very careful line of being challenging and providing tough feedback you need to hear. The class is unusual in its structure - balancing industry experts and challenging assignments. This class is transformative and the best class I've had in my graduate program so far.

All I can say is WOW. Get ready to be impressed - Prof. Michaels is what you heard she is: transformational, inspiring, and a master ninja when it comes to communication. This class is hard, but in the good way. Get ready to find out your blind spots and then some. This is a game changer of a class. Two thumbs up. Marisa is amazing.

You can't get much luckier than to have Marisa as a professor - she is whip smart, hilarious, and transformational. Get ready to roll your sleeves up and work - BCOM 510 is hard work, but it is worth it. Her expertise in all things communication is a master class in how to succeed at work. She truly transformed my professional persona and life.

Should not be teaching at the graduate level. I have learned nothing from her. She is more the ringleader of a circus than a professor. She does not teach anything herself, but brings in a parade of actually qualified guests to teach in her stead. Group projects are a huge time investment for very little learning.
I love Marisa, she is the best!

She explains the assignments in class so as long as you're paying attention you should be good. She also does peer review grading in class and gives you a good idea of what she is looking for. She says to get the book, but she posts enough resources that it wasn't necessary. Be prepared to write a lot. There is a paper due almost every week.
Marisa is a very nice teacher! Although BCOM214 is not a easy class, she tried her best to help students.

BCOM 214 is a difficult class but Marisa makes the class simple and easy to understand. She sets up her class so you only need to show up once a week. She is also very helpful with giving feedback as long as you seek her out. Definitely recommend Marisa if there is space in her class. Assignments are difficult but you will learn a lot in this class

This class was not for me. If you like getting no feedback and being graded harshly this is the class for you. She'll take points off for things as little as commas. She doesn't give direction and grades too hard for a simple writing class that is mandatory. She also can come across as rude at times so for your sake PLEASE spare yourself!

If you can avoid her as a teacher do!! She is one of the toughest graders and gives you zero feedback on drafts until they are already submitted. She will find anything and everything to mark you for and makes it incredibly hard to get good grades. She also never really teaches in class so you are left in the dark on what to do and how to succeed.

Just don't take her class. She always gives the feedback AFTER our assignments were graded. And she and the TAs ARE REALLY HARD GRADERS!!!!!! And the Lockdown Browser really SUCK!!!!! It even can't autosave!!! DON'T TAKE THIS CLASS EVER!!!!

There are a lot of written assignments, basically one due every week. She is very very picky and critical, but gives amazing feedback so you will learn a lot from her. She splits the class up so you occasionally only have it once a week. But i stress the importance of attending feedback fridays. She has a good personality is enjoyable to listen to.

She is very knowledgeable about business writing and is very kind in her feedback, but is very willing to help students who are struggling with the assignments. This class is harder than expected and the writing assignments take more time and effort than expected because the grading is pretty tough, but just try your best and you should be good.

Marisa is an awesome professor! I took this class as an elective going into Eller, and I highly reccommend! Marisa is hilarious and keeps the material interesting! A pretty easy class, and it was so much fun to go to class because of Marisa's personality!

Ugh Paul. His class was honestly just a headache. The content really isn't any harder than the other upper division accounting courses, but Paul sure does make it feel like that. He's your only option though, so just study well for the exams and try to participate because that means a lot to him.
SO much information covered in so short a time. No study guides.

This class was very challenging as someone new to statistics. The instructor gave little explanation of concepts, so I often had to teach myself. In class examples used Excel commands, but in person exams required solving problems by hand, which felt disconnected and left me unprepared as he will not show you how to do anything on paper.

This professor gets a bad rep because the class is boring, he's not necessarily the most entertaining lecturer, but he makes jokes and tries to engage students. If you took AP Stats in high school and did good you're chilling for this class a lot of the concepts you probably already know.
The most boring lectures I've ever attended in my life. You will have to teach yourself the whole unit before the exam.

profesor Molnar was a pretty boring professor and lectures were not engaging at all. he teaches in a way that's pretty difficult to learn as he also teaches very quick making it difficult for many students to succeed. as well as he puts grades in very late almost up to a month after assignments which makes it hard to gage your progress in the class
Just a good dude, prepares you well for the exams. One of those nerdy guys that makes jokes that not a lot of people laugh at but inside they're funny.
Lectures are really boring but try to follow along with them, especially when he starts doing stuff on excel. Not a hard class though

Molnar cares a lot about his students success, and is a great teacher and a great guy, however the class itself is very useless and not benefit you in the slightest. The course concepts in general are very hard and tests make up the majority of the grade. Overall it's simply the course itself thats the problem, not the professor.
Lectures are a little boring but it's not a hard class. Show up, pay attention, do the homework, go along with his excel examples during class and you should be good.

DO NOT take this course, the course isnt online anymore. The lectures are dead boring and he just reads off of the slide shows in the most boring voice imaginable. Be ready to teach yourself the entire course, the professor is the mose unhelpful person ever and i dont know how he is allowed to teach.

By far the worst professor I have ever experienced. He rambles on every class, changes up the order of notes so it's hard to follow along, and basically just reads the already confusing slides the whole class in a monotone depressed voice. DO NOT take the course with this professor if you want to actually be taught instead of teaching yourself.

This professor's teaching style is not interactive, making it hard to grasp key concepts.He mostly reads from the slides without adding explanations or discussions.The course also lacks enough materials for exam preparation, making success difficult.Students often need to rely on outside resources to understand the content, don't take

This guy doesn't know how to explain any of the material, he messes up often on lectures and causes you to get lost, only good thing he does is reply fast to you. If you can wait to take this class till summer I would recommend that.

Professor Molnar is really good at communicating the important concepts you need to know for future class in the business program. He covers the material at a good pace, and reinforces the concepts with a lot of homework assignments. He doesn't beat around the bush, and makes it easy to understand exactly what you need to do. Great class.
very very very boring class

Mr Molnar is a nice and caring guy but his lectures are really boring. Class is pretty easy and homework has infinite attempts. Easy A but you'll have to do most of learning on your own. I would take him again coz another prof who taught my friend was a tough grader and gave lots of homework. Do not cheat on exam he will know you cheated.

Professor Molnar seems like a nice enough guy, but his lectures are mind-numbingly boring. Tests are open note so those are not too bad, but they are heavily weighted. Personally, I got almost nothing out of the lectures and did most of the necessary learning outside of class.
Very slow grader

He's an alright teacher, but you can expect to be teaching yourself a lot of the content in the course. It's not a very tough course, especially since Excel is heavily used, homework has unlimited tries, and exams are open note. The case assignments were pretty easy, but a bit time consuming and didn't feel very educational. Pretty boring lectures.

He's not horrible. He is a really kind guy, he is just really misguided when it comes to teaching. This class is genuinely so useless and nobody learns anything in it. It's not his fault, this is just a bad class. Promise Molnar doesn't really make it a lot harder than it needs to be, he isn't trying but can we even blame him this course sucks

Molnar is smart and really cares but he is just a very boring lecturer. I didnt mind it too much but i know a lot of people hated him. It might also be that it was a 9am. Class is easy though and his quizzes are super light

The main issue of the course (that Molnar is aware of) is that the homework system gives you 1 attempt only, so all of the 9 "homework" assignments are really quizzes. There is little opportunity to "learn" without going to office hours, and students with jobs, clubs, and other involvements might want to consider a different professor.

This is for BNAN 277. Class is an utter waste of time with this professor. He reads directly off poorly put-together slide shows and provides sketchy online calculators as resources. He's also never "available" for office hours even though he's posted the times he was supposed to be available. Save your time and money and don't take Molnar.
Bnad276 was an easy class but he was a very boring at lectures. His tests were easy though and he gave us lot of cheat excel sheats that helped get me high marks on exams.
So so boring and unhelpful. Solely reads from slides, doesn't explain well at all, is difficult to understand, and assigns lots of homework.
Just don't

You will automatically regret taking this class with Professor Molnar on the first day of the semester. Unhelpful, has a chronic sinus infection, sweats profusely, reads directly from slides, homework system is very outdated tedious to complete, grade is mostly determined by tests. Do yourself a favor and don't choose professor Molnar.

Please don't take this professor unless it is your last resort. He has a chronic sinus infection and can't be understood in class, he reads directly from the slides, is an incredibly slow grader, and is even less helpful in any office hour interaction.

Adam Molnar is not a good professor. He only reads from slides, is difficult to understand, not accessible for office hours, offers online calculators as solutions, uses an outdated and difficult homework system, and struggles to do anything beyond the bare minimum of what's required.
Awful, just so boring and hard to understand, skims through slides, does not explain anything. i can tell he gets it, but no one else in the class does.

If his teaching style works for you, he is great and you won't have issues.
HOWEVER, I don't think it works for many, as you have to piece information together as you go, which I found fun and engaging but I don't think most people will. Just study, review the notes and he's pretty good.

Adam Molnar might be one of the worst teachers I have ever had in my entire experience of learning. The lectures were very boring. Not to mention, he reads entirely off of his slides and has the most sassy comebacks for everything. Anyway do yourself a favor and don't take this class with him. Only plus: attendance not taken every class

Works really hard to be available to talk to and ask questions about the material and homework. Provides plenty of time for questions and practice during class. The lectures can get boring as the subject is fairly dry and his voice is very monotone.
Bored to tears... this class is unbelievably boring and the teacher makes really no effort to make this class engaging at all.

I really enjoyed this class and Dr. Molnar cares about his students. Sometimes class may be a little boring, but he is always open to questions during and before/after class. This class can be confusing, but he always gives clear responses. Don't be afraid to ask for help! The exams are open note and online, and he gives plenty of extra credit.

Professor Molnar is a great lecturer because of his clear explanations of difficult concepts. He does a great job showing how to use excel for the math and data visualizations. Overall a super nice guy who has a great sense of humor. Would definitely recommend taking his class.

Molnar is nice and knows his material, but he's not the greatest at teaching it. His class can be pretty boring and the lectures aren't always taught like a typical math class, which can make it difficult to understand. He does provide easy extra credit options and his tests are online and open-note, but if you struggle with math, take another prof
Cool guy, the quizzes are all spread out, and you get one retake. So even if you do bad on one, it won't tank your grade. You can also do the quizzes from home.

Professor Molnar has been by FAR one of the best professor I have ever had. Yes he reads the slides word for word, but he always asks the students if they have any questions and he always gives a clear response. His exams are open note so make sure you take notes during class and have them prepared for exam day. 10/10 professor.

The professor clearly understands the content, but he is not always effective at clearly communicating the concepts during lectures. Doing the provided practice problems is really helpful got comprehension.
Very boring
sucks. just sucks. do yourself a favor and don't take his class.

While he is monotone, his lectures and slides always gave clear criteria for what formulas to use, just try to digest WHY things are happening, and the class will make sense. He provided lots of Excel instruction too. Literally just use those examples for the HW and exams and you will be fine. Offered SO much EASY extra credit opportunities.

Prof. Molnar is neither a good professor or an approachable human being. If you get an A in the class, like me, it was all luck. His tests determine your grade and the little homework he gives, you will wait a week and a half minimum to receive a grade. He reads slide word for word. DO NOT take BNAD276 with Molnar.

THIS IS MY 3RD TIME TAKING STATS AND DR. MOLNAR IS THE REASON ITS FINALLY MY LAST! He is the absolute sweetest & wants his students to pass. His lectures are for sure material heavy but, he offers SO much help outside of class and extra credit. Exams are open notes and easier than any stats exam I have taken. Test review sessions will be your bff

Dr. Molnar has been one of the nicest professors I have had so far at The University of Arizona. He really cares about his students and their success by offering Office hours in person and over zoom, and ways to earn extra credit. He made this difficult course more enjoyable and less stressful.

Dr. Molnar is a very nice guy, but he doesn't know how to teach. All he does is read off the very confusing slides, which makes it extremely hard to understand the material. It is still possible to finish with a good grade, but you will likely have to learn the material yourself by taking good notes & doing the homework, cases, and practice tests.

Hes a good guy but he didnt do a good job at teaching the class. Hes super smart and clearly understands what hes talking about but he makes it very hard to understand if its your first time seeing the material. If theres any chance you can switch teachers. Do it. Reads solely off the slides and attendance is required. Pay attention.

Overall nice guy but just does not know how to teach. He went to Harvard and is way too smart to be teaching this class. He goes into detail about totally irrelevant things and above the class's skill level. His lectures are extremely monotone and hard to follow for more than 10 minutes without zoning out. It is very difficult to get an A.

Molnar is a pretty nice, approachable person that's passionate for stats, but he doesn't know how to teach a class. He doesn't explain the material in a coherent way and it's not sufficient to do well on exams, which are 70% of the grade. I didn't even know how to write notes because he reads off the slides and doesn't give further explanation.

Professor Molnar is by far one of the more difficult instructors I've had. In terms of teaching style, his lectures drag on and are very boring, and do not do a very great job of conveying information that is necessary to prepping you for an exam. The fact that exams take up 70% of your total grade, is downright atrocious to say the least.

He literally has no assignments except for the HW which is only 10 points SO if you do not do good on the tests your grade will be greatly impacted. His class is very boring and does not explain concepts in a way that understandable. His tests cover examples that were skimmed in class. When you go to office hours he just meets you with sass.

Stats is not rocket science. why does this man teach? no clue. he talks with no purpose. I have a good grade in this class but this goof gives no room for us to improve. we have no homework so the one problem we learn in class is all we go by for exams. if you have him don't be worried he confuses everyone. also he's sassy asf. this is my burn book

Molnar is a really nice and funny guy, but his lectures are pretty boring. He has 4 online tests which weren't very hard, his homeworks are graded easy and quick, and he rarely takes attendance. A pretty easy class and an alright teacher, just expect long boring lectures

Prof Molnar is the a the worst prof for 276 he is new to teaching the class as the previous teacher left. go with a diff prof he is actually terrible and just reads his lectures off the slides no contest or explanations tp help with understanding

There are only total of 400 base points which are from homework and four tests. You can get up to 10 extra credits but is nearly useless cause once you did bad in one exam, you are far from getting an A. If you want easy A, just run as fast as u can, take another class.

The lectures are quite dull and the professor is very unresponsive to email inquiries. Attendance is required, which is frustrating because the class is so easy that the lectures don't feel necessary for understanding the material.

Great professor and an awesome class. This was a morning class, and every morning you would start by answering questions. If you were late, you missed those points. If you missed the questions, you could not make them up. Each week there was a quiz due on Friday The professor also started a mentoring group, which I attended, it is obvious he cares.

Professor is fine, the class just gets insanely boring and by the third week you won't want to attend lecture. Lectures are mostly just him reading through a 100 slide slideshow and could last from 1-3 hours. Wireshark was the main thing I got out of this class. Quiz and exam questions, find the answers in the book.

So, the semester is ending, and Professor Glade was very passionate throughout the course. The class is a weekly quiz, something like 20 questions over the book, a midterm, and three presentations on a topic related to cybersecurity. I did not originally like the presentations, but he made them fun. You have a week to do the assignments.
Pretty straightforward class. Good textbook chosen. Very clear instructions for the Wireshark portion of the class.

Ok some of the reviews here are from some very salty people. I think he is one of the best professors in the program. So anyhow the final is intense in this class and just about everything rests on the final. It's not very difficult but it is long. In a good way thou I don't know how to explain it. Very worthwhile.
If anything happens in your life that requires you to focus on something more important than his BS class, you will get nowhere with him. He does not care. Never teaches anything anyways.

Every week, you will take apart a piece of malware from week 1 to week 14. The final exam takes forever but is all in the book, and the malware exercise was something new. Galde looks to be the only one teaching this class. The work is heavy compared to other courses. You need to be able to run a VM for this class, that is for sure.
Final took an entire day to complete and he covered nothing in his lectures, just rambled and tried to be funny. Forgot he was teaching students.
Good lectures. Most long, but some short. The quiz is off of the book, and every other week, it is a Wireshark quiz. I've never used it before, but does walk-throughs.
I took this course the first time it was offered. Every week, there is a discussion post and a quiz. The Final is an add-on from the midterm, which was interesting.
Most entertaining professor you will ever have in the best and worst ways possible.
Prof is pretty chill guy but his lectures can be a little boring. Be careful as tests are huge amount of grade.

First exam 30% of grade second exam 35%. Exams focus in on very specific things ignoring a lot of other stuff you learn. Todd's a really chill guy very funny but sometimes a little too disorganized. Hard to read his handwriting in class. Attendance optional but sometimes extra credit worksheets.

Attendance wasn't mandatory but I would recommend going as he sometimes gave random EC for showing up. His lectures arent bad but I don't feel like they help you for the exam at all. His exams are extremely hard and make up 65% of your grade.

Don't listen to the reviews. This guy is actually the funniest professor I've ever taken at U of A. If every professor was like him, there would probably be a 2000% drop in depressed students at the U of A.

Super test heavy and the study guides do not fully prepare you for tests. We had 2 tests that were worth 75% of our grade and both were in-person. Also did not post the 2nd test's study guide until the day before the test.

Professor Neumann is one of my favorite professors at UA, but he is terrible at preparing you for the exams. There were three tests, and each one was significantly more difficult than the study guides he provides. It's important that you intuitively understand the material, because the exams will test your knowledge far more than the homeworks.

so unorganized and didn't really teach lol. class was honestly a joke. two exams (2nd one was half take home), discussions each week, and pearson homework. didn't really learn anything and felt so unprepared for exams because the class wasn't serious.

Learn from my mistake and take ECON 330 online with a different professor. Todd means well, but is very unorganized. The two exams are 65% of the final grade, and the study guides have zero resemblance to the questions on the tests he writes.

Don't take his word on anything he says about what you will be tested on, because it holds no value.

Todd Neumann is a great guy but an exceptionally frustrating professor. He gives the class homework to prepare for the exams which are filled with problem sets that appear to be relatively doable. He then proceeds to test you on completely different material.

The best professor hands down! Enjoyed each second of class because it was never boring or useless. He cares about students and doesn't make it hard on us. Go to class, complete reviews and you should be fine can complete homework weeks in advance, offers extra credit this course was the best 10/10!!

The 2 exams are worth 60% of your grade but they were take home. There is a group project that was pretty simple. He gave extra credit opportunities. Discussions were through packback but as long as you did them it seemed you got credit. Pretty dry material but that is expected. He was available to answer questions. Do the work and you'll be fine.
Such an amazing professor. Attendance not mandatory, he is funny and chill. Go to reviews and understand the study guide and you will be fine. Answers emails very fast also.
Todd is perfect !!!!!!

This professor deserves the most amazing holidays. This class had a lot of extra credit, the homework wasn't bad, and the discussions were easy and fun to write. There are two tests but his study guides are very similar. Attendance was not mandatory so you can take a few days off. He is also so funny, nice, and interesting to listen to.

Lectures will add a lot   of context to slides so make sure you show up most of the time, but you can afford to miss class once in a while. Loves cracking jokes and very chill guy. Homework for my class in particular was weekly but other classes Ive had with him were very light on homework.
Professor Neumann was a super chill teacher. Graded on three homework and three take home tests. Would recommend taking his class.

The class focused on Macroeconomics theory, and we were briefly presented with dozens of equations on a PowerPoint slide. Come time for the tests, there would be mathematical problems which we never did in class. No examples on how to complete said problems were ever presented making the few exams and homework extremely difficult and confusing.

Very chill professor, lectures were often laid back, but very clear and informative. Homeworks were pretty easy and you had multiple attempts at each problem. Exams are really easy if you study the previous exam problems (even easier for our class since he made the exams take-home at the last minute). Great class overall.
If you can manage actually going to class and doing all the assignments, Professor Neumann makes the class very simple. Generous amounts of extra credit and a nice guy.

Extremely disorganized, cancelled class and office hours like a third of the time. The lectures only covered a portion of what was on the homework and exams, you had to sift through a 20 year old textbook to find the rest. Course was pretty easy, just very logistically frustrating. Seems like he just had a bad semester based on other reviews.
Todd is a legend. If you get As on the first two exams, you dont need to do the final. His exams are similar to the quizzes too and he does offer extra credit too.

Professor Neumann is one of the most engaging professors I have had at the UA. At first, I was not sure what to expect as I have not generally been into ECON before. This man really knows his stuff and knows how to explain it in a way that a novice would understand. Prepares you for exams and is all around a cool guy! I would def take him again.

This is econometrics not statistics. He should know better. However this class was a waste of time. He didn't teach us, he just tried to show us how much he knows. Graduate students laugh at how bad this program is because of him. Yet somehow undergrad students some how qualify for this. This is not econometrics this is stats. Get some help.

While Professor Neumann is nice and accessible outside class for when you're doing the quizzes. I am not a giant fan of the way the exams are formatted since theres like 100 questions and you need to scan each page for the exams and submit it all as one pdf file which is ridiculous. Same applies for the quizzes but other than that its an ok course.

professor doesnt drop anything, constantly moves due dates closer with not a lot of time to prepare and sometimes forgets to tell you. tests take way too much time and you'll be working to the last minute

Highly intelligent guy. Class attendance is somewhat necessary but it will not hurt you to skip on occasion. Only homework is online through one of the Pearson books, which you do not need to read. Tests are pretty easy and he automatically gave me an A in the class and did not make me take the final because I had a high A near the end.

Todd is great. His sense of humor is hilarious. The dude loves his diet coke, diet coke and more diet coke LMFAO. He moves through material quite fast, so its hard to keep up at times, but hes more than willing to clarify and help you understand if you ask him. We used mindtap for homework, and 1 Exam in mindtap, and 2 written parts for exam 1/2.
material is hard but teaches well

This is the least organized class I've taken at UA. D2L page is made up of: syllabus, textbook link, in-class worksheets (doesn't post solutions), and one long slide deck. No study guides provided for any exams, even though they cover up to 6 chapters. Took over a month to receive 1st exam grade back. Tests are ridiculously long and difficult.

Great professor. Lectures are interesting, and attendance is taken a few times over the course of the semester. Homework helps you study because it's last semester's exams. This class is way easier than 361. I never bought the textbook, and the material isn't that heavy. This is my second class with this professor, definitely recommend!

I did not study hard in this class but if u did, u can get a B easily. The professor is not fun but the class is not such hard. Although I am an international student, the class is still good to take. ( zhe jie ke zhen shui )
Half of each exam was written and ridiculously hard every time. The online (MyLab) portion of the exams however, is much more reasonable.

Professor Neumann is clearly an intelligent man but he is not able to parlay that into overall teaching skills for his students. You'll often be lost by how things may overlap during his course but he tries to make it simple for you if you ask. Be ready for very dry lectures that will make you zone out. I would not recommend his courses.

Definitely on the more difficult side for a Gen Ed, but not impossible. Make sure you memorize all the questions in the homework because they will be on the test. There are only a few grades, so everything counts. Also show up to lecture because he has random in-class extra credit.

This class was really hard. Not an easy A for gen ed. Hes a cool teacher but the tests are really hard and specific. If you like Econ then take it but its not easy. He gives a lot of extra credit though

Graded on tests and hw. Tests are cumulative. Doesn't take attendance, doesn't post lecture PPTs online. The book is confusing so going to class is really how you learn. He'll take time to explain if anyone is confused. Always does a review before tests, posts a study guide. Gives half credit on exams and there's a curve. Not too exciting of a guy.
Lectures aren't really isnt necessary if you read the book but homework is very similar to tests
TEST IS HARD and no review works keys

Your entire grade is based on your tests and homework. Goes through slides way too quickly in lecture, and doesnt tell you when homework is due. Even assigned homework DURING spring break, and didnt tell anybody. Gives a huge study guide for tests, but no answer key or reviewing done in class. Difficult class with bad teacher. DONT TAKE.

You really should be going to lecture even if you took econ in high school. His lectures are very long and get boring quick but his humor and content gets you through it. Work with someone on the hws, it helps info stick in your brain better and the tests will be easier if you do. Look out for the essay q's and ex credit counts!! Don't buy the book
if you want to get good grade in test, just review your homework.

Dr. Neumann's class is entirely lectures. There are no in-class activities, and your grade is based on 3 exams and 3 homework sets. Even though the subject can be dry, he tells a lot of funny stories that make things more interesting. He genuinely cares about his students and I'd recommend taking his class if you feel okay with calculus.

One hell of a professor loves what he does and even if the class is 250+ people still makes you feel like your the only one there is a great teacher lectures are amazing. Just take notes during lecture and you should be fine or take pictures of his slide everything he talks about on slides is on hw and test
Class is optional. If you took econ in high school, youll be fine. Pretty funny dude who loves Diet Coke and will talk about it all the time

Goes through slides very fast! Attendance is not mandatory but trust me, if you dont go, you will be lost! Very few assignments so the tests and RARE homework grades really make or break you. You really never use the "required" textbook, so it isnt worth your money, unless you feel that you cant follow him in class and need to read that chapter
He makes a hard class a lot easier to do well in
Really kind to his students.

I've never been an economics person, but Dr. Neumann's class was awesome. Very laid back and all of the exams were online. With a reasonable amount of effort I ended up getting an A. Overall, he's a great guy and was always available when needed. Definitely take him if you can!
This class was awesome! A lot of extra credit and a lot of good information that will help you in the future. This professor is hilarious and knows his stuff.
He's pretty awesome. Great guy, great examples, always willing to help. He can be a little disorganized at times, but bear with it, he's awesome. Just read the book and you can't complain. at all.
Homework and tests online. Homework can be time consuming, but pretty easy. He uses a site called Aplia. Definitely a good elective to take if you've got a heavy semester, or just need this class.

He sort of neglects his classes. He never explains how your grade is calculated, he makes you go in to office hours for that. I never went to class (other than when I needed to) and I got an A. He gives so much extra credit...
Todd is really chill & engaging. His class is WAY easier than Reff's. Homework takes forever, but all exams are online & you can google the exact questions & answers. Would definitely recommend.

I thought this class was easy but it was anything but. I spent at least 2 hours on each assignment only to get 40% on most of them. He explains some things in class but not everything, and if you email him, expect 1-2 words in reply. Wrecked my GPA.
I thought this class would be easy but it was actually pretty difficult. He is the best Econ professor but just a heads up that it is a lot of work and if you're not good at Econ than good luck!

Mr. Neumann is one of the best professors I have had. His class is extremely interesting, and he is very engaging and interactive. I am interested in this topic, however, I believe that even if you aren't, the class is very enjoyable. Great class to open your eyes. Also homeworks are a piece of cake and exams are exactly like the homework.

Pretty easy A. For those complaining that this class wasn't an easy A: if you want anything easier, go to community college. You can't just expect to study 0 hours for anything and get an A. The exams are almost exactly like the homework (some are the same questions, just different numbers). In class writings are a joke too. Fun class though.

I also took this class because I thought it would be easy based on the past reviews and I had an interest in econ. It's actually very hard and a lot of effort is required. I barely have a C and I've attended every class and pay attention. You better be very good at econ or just have good luck.

Took this class because of his reputation for an easy class. He changed the whole class because of this reason. He is knowledgable in the field of economics but in my opinion he doesn't know how to teach. This class isn't the same as it was in the past so don't take it expecting an easy A. There's more effort required now.

Neuman was a great professor and teaches exactally in line with the book. Lectures could get a bit confusing but book can clarify any question. only grades are 3 homework assignments (from the book) due the day of the test and 3 MC tests and 3 essay (short answer) tests. Extra credit available at the end. curves tests. Overall a fairly easy class

He wants students to do well and tries not to make it too hard. The online test questions are exactly as the hmwk assignments before the test. Start hmwk assignments early, if you go ask for help after trying the problem he will help. Three essay exams, but people did not do well on them, this was the first semester he did essay exams.

Went to class 3 times only for the in class essays and got a B rounded to an A. Tests and finals are the same as hw. Dont go to class just spend time learning the course when you do the homeworks which are once a week and are not that challenging.
Three Exams that are easy as long as you study the homework its the same exact thing. A lot of homework but very easy. Take this class For a easy B and work a little bit for an A

There's only 3 essay exams that you have to attend in person. You could skip lectures, but I would recommend going because his slides aren't too detailed. He is very thorough in class. Online reviews can get time consuming, but the questions are exactly the same in the exams. He also gives lots of extra credit. Overall a good easy course.

Dr. Neumann is a great lecturer. The class was very interesting, always made it worthwhile to show up. Limited homework, he prepares you very well for the tests as well. As others have said the class is very laid back, enjoyable. He posted the lecture slides online but they mainly consist of bullet points so it's better to be there in person.

This class is definitely an easy A. This semester, my friend convinced him to post the lecture slides, so going to class is NOT necessary AT ALL. You only have to go on test days. There's no real final, just three online tests and three "essay exams" which are like a paragraph. The online homework program is really easy to use.
Neumann is a great guy, but I would join Reff's class. This class gets really tough really fast drop it if you can.

Super easy class! Tests, homework, and class notes are all online, I went to lecture up to the 2nd exam. I feel bad for not going to more lectures because Todd is a very knowledgeable, helpful, and overall really great guy. Some homework can be time consuming (especially the reviews) but the exams are nearly identical to the review assignments!

Very laid back class and easy if you somewhat understand the material. He curves the tests, which is nice. Homework only before every test. Three tests and the final. If you don't want to put a lot of effort into a class, this would be the class to take.

This class is easy, if you pay attention and do the homework. Honestly there is no reason not to do get at least a B. My only complaint is that sometimes he uses metaphors that don't make very much sense, but other than that a fairly good lecturer.

Although it's quite alright to finish this course totally at home, I still need to recommend people to attend his course as much as possible. He's definitely a great lecturer. The absence is actually a sort of loss. However, it is still easy to get an A even though you're not going every time. Highly recommended.

This was by far the easiest class I've taken at UA. Probably cause of my prior knowledge of economics, I went to class two times all semester. For orientation, and the final. Just keep up on your Aplia assignments and when the tests come around partner up and knock em out. If you can't pass this class you shouldn't be in college.

Dr. Neumann is super easy, nice, helpful, and prompt with email responses.  The class was of no interest to me, but at least it was easy to get an A.  It was basically online: the "experiments," the assignments, and the tests.  Grades are pretty fair, and he extends assignments sometimes.  Overall, Dr. Neumann wants his students to succeed.
great professor, clear about expectations, there was a lot of online homework but it was simple.  Easy A.

easiest econ professor. tests are online and available for a few days usually. he's funny, nice, works with you on deadlines. i stopped going to class, im bad at econ in general and i'll still get an a.do the homeworks and experiments and you'll be fine. would take more classes with him if i could.

Going to class isn't mandatory although the notes aren't online so going might be a good idea. All of the tests aren't online though which makes the class so much easier. Good professor too, would HIGHLY recommend.

Dr Todd is such an amazing teacher! If you get a chance to take his econ class do so! I wish he taught econ 300 as well.  He makes the class interesting and he is really funny.  And he loves diet coke.

Overall great professor and a great class to get your "graduate degree" feet wet. Expect 3-4 lectures that are about 45 minutes total weekly, for reading, expect 1-2 chapters in the online book. Speed ramps up toward the end. Show up, put in effort and you will ace the class. His focus is on understanding the concepts rather than definitions.

He builds up to topics in a logical way. There are some substantial group cases, be prepared to do some extra group meetings. Reading the book supplements the lecture material nicely. Tests were very fair but you do need to study concepts. The professor was very accessible if you had questions. Overall I learned a lot and loved the class.

You definitely need to know how to navigate Excel and know simple functions. One thing I would change in this class is the way feedback is given when assignments are graded. It is very broad and does not really inform you or help you for your next assignments. I did like his video discussions. They were easy to follow and not very long.

My only complaint was this class was only half a semester. Jesper gives great lectures where students are encouraged to participate. Ensure you know how to use a spreadsheet before taking this class (and if you are old enough to be attending college you are old enough to understand spreadsheet formulas).

The textbook is short, and to the point. Take good notes on it and use notes to create cheat sheet for test. Test is heavy on theory and application so don't worry about memorization and instead focus on understanding and applying. Tests are hard but not impossible. Discussions are light. Teach is VERY responsive to questions.

He was an amazing teacher, extremely kind and really cares about the material he is teaching. Some of the tests were confusing be he allows you to use notes on exams. Really like him as a teacher and the material is interesting.

Professor Noussair is a great professor. I would say his material is perfect, but the delivery tends to waver. Regardless he's a great lecturer and keeps his content concise and easy to understand. Your grade is essentially derived from going to the class. Would take again.
Not a lot of classwork, open notes tests. Pays to have the book and at least skim it
Get to do economics experiments in class. Open note tests, cool topics

This class is pretty easy if you are able to read the text book and attend classes. Tests are open notebook which is helpful. If you pay attention you are able to understand the concepts, and if you ask a question it will get answered.

Fantastic professor, very good lectures, very accessible if you have questions in class. Makes everyone feel welcome, very clear grading criteria. I found this class pretty easy if you do all the readings and quizzes and attend. Interesting content, and would highly recommend.
Meaning Professor, boring, Unreasonable teaching style
Don't like the way he lectures, he'll keep asking us for thumbs up.

Professor is clearly respected by staff and was likely an excellent teacher 10 years ago. Now his course is a disorganized mess. Grade is determined almost entirely by group project.
Had an essay as part of the project at the end of the semester. D2L described it as a "detailed report". Clarification from TA 3 days before deadline said 20 pages.

He was probably a lot better in his prime, but his class currently is very disorganized. For grades, there are 2 assignments worth 10% each, midterm and final for 15% each, and the group project is worth 50%. There's no feedback or grades released for any of the assignments so its really difficult to gauge how well you're doing as you go along.
Although difficult, a genuinely kind person and a very knowledgable professor. Who take him again for a different course but would not take this course again with any other teacher.

The course is 1 of the worst I have taken. For it being an MIS elective, it is very tough, very unorganized, and the exams take MULTIPLE hours to do b/c they are take home. Grading is very difficult and the guest lecturers are very boring. Don't take this class if you want an easy elective. There is little to no help. No grades issued throughout.

How do I start?
Her class is horrible. Takes long time to grade. Tests are extremely hard. Reads off slides, doesn't teach. Gives 0 opportunities to practice Python besides a timed graded assignment that occurs twice over 14 weeks. 

The only positives:
Her cute dogs that appeared on random class days and a decent amount of extra credit.
boring class, strict rubric, unclear calendar
Class was a basic python class. Patti was a fun woman who brought her dogs in. I had already taken python classes before so i hated this class. just a lot of reading slides

Honestly, I've never seen any professor or resource explain Python as confusingly and complicated as Ota. I am very displeased with how she teaches the course. You could be needing a simple script and instead of explaining the what we need to understand, she just uses redundant and complicated code when you can achieve the same results easier

This is for MIS503. Instructor backtracked with completely different responses. No feedback was given on practice quizzes, so it was impossible to learn the correct approach. The course lacked structure for beginners: Slides were confusing and not practical. No hands-on coding demonstrations were provided.

Below par is putting it politely. You will learn basically nothing in this class, exams and quizzes routinely have questions with typos, of which I was never given points back on. She made factually incorrect statements that you could correct with 10 seconds on google. She's a nice person but a truly bad teacher.

Very kind lady who maybe incorporates a little too much math and arithmetic for my liking as a Python beginner. Tests aren't as intimidating as you might anticipate: purely multiple-choice and open note. Start on programming assignments early.

She's so knowledgeable, especially in Python. Class material may seem confusing at first, but revisiting the slides before tests helps bc they're very clear. Tests are open-book, no open questions, and easier than expected. You don't need perfect grades for an A, just focus on the programming assignments and review the slides. She's great!

A very sweet person who was very fun. She brought her very cute dogs into brighten everyone's day. That being said actually teaching was different. Very much reading off of slides. Which is a problem when they don't give examples in python itself of how the code works.

Nice person, not a good class.

Edit: I didn't learn in this class on review

Awful just awful. Coding bootcamps are better than this. How can a professor just read off slides of PYTHON? This is unacceptable and eller college needs to seriously improve their MIS department. Fire her and start over

Class is not difficult at all, but is a pretty absurd amount of writing in the 7 week version. Probably wouldn't recommend if you're looking for a cruise gen-ed; I averaged close to 2300 words a week of writing about very mind-numbing "problem solving".

Super easy A if you hit the word counts, though.

Dr. Ota gets a bad rep, but she's such a great professor who really cares about her students. I don't think this class was very difficult. She's a pretty straightforward grader and none of the grades I received were a surprise. She's very understanding and offers a lot of extra credit. The only thing is, you need to come to class every day.

Patti is genuinely a nice person, but I kind of get the feeling that she isn't best person to teach a python class. That said, She is a clearly a caring, knowledgeable and intelligent person. I suspect she would be far happier and better equipped for a class teaching pure mathematics.
Patti is nice, the lecture was not tough. But I feel that the TA was better at answering questions for homework.

She DOES read off slides, and sometimes she may need time to read off her own codes, but as long as you pay attention to the class, the exam materials are not difficult, and you'll get good grades. If you communicate she will understand. very open to feedback and communications. She deserves respect. I personally enjoyed her class.

Truly an awful professor. Her teaching style inhibits actual learning of the material. Not quite sure that she even know how to code in python, she just reads off slides and offers little to no context or teaching advice surrounding the subject.

This class was exhausting to go to. Patti is a very dry professor and does not keep the class engaged at all. I've had great coding classes before but this was not one of them. I would have been much better off taking a course through some online code academy. All she does is read off the slides. Forced participation too.

Easy A. None of the work is difficult or takes long. The content was okay. She is very nice but definitely disorganized. None of the class syllabi/calendars had been updated for years and she never ended up correcting a grading mistake of mine. Still, it's very easy and there's even extra credit in puzzle games if you somehow end up needing it.

This was the worst class I have taken. Professor doesn't know the material at all and should not be teaching this class. She had her TA teach an entire chapter because she didn't know the material. Don't bother asking questions in class. She doesn't know the answer. The only way to pass is to read the book front to back and ignore her lectures.

Sometimes the class was interesting, more often it wasn't. It's really hard to truly get into the class live online, and she's not very technologically advanced but she's a very sweet lady. Didn't have any difficulty at all and didn't spend too much time in the class. You'll have tedious assignments. Easy A for a gen ed. No stress at all.

Very nice lady but has zero business teaching this class. She may be the worst person to ever teach a computer science oriented course. 3/4 of the class was fast asleep. Will make you never want to code another day in your life. Extremely boring and just busy work. Will gain zero knowledge taking this course.

Patti is a great woman, but her lectures are pretty boring. She reads off of pre-made slides that she could have just posted online. Class is ridiculously easy. Everything is graded off of completion except for the quizzes which are worth small points. I probably put in ten hours of work for the entire semester.

Patti is a real gem. As long as you come to class and make effort to connect with her and the other students, you will have a blast. Make sure to read the textbook!! The quiz games like jeopardy and others are crucial for you to succeed, and you get extra points! Very fun class that helps you learn about yourself.

Patti is a great teacher. This class was a super easy A. There are only 3 quizzes and they are open book, and plenty of review games in class. Lectures aren't boring and there are plenty of extra credit opportunities. Homework was super easy because they were mostly opinion based questions. Highly recommend for an easy gen ed.

I love Patti, and honestly if you respect her she'll respected you. Plus she's well known in Eller so I recommend being on her good side. Easy A and Tons of fun extra credit like movie nights with food. She also brings in a lot of food to class.

Patti's class sucks for the most part, but you will not get lower than a C for sure. She is very lenient on the projects and gives plenty of extra credit. Her exams are awful and most people fail them, but they are a small portion of your overall grade. In office hours, she will sometimes just help you get the answer for projects.

Her class is very clear especially since I took it online. Her directions are on point. I knew what to do when. She emails and posts if there are changes and is a very easy grader. Just do the work and you will get an A.

Obviously meant for first semester college students. I took during my 4th semester and it was a joke! so easy! you have to buy the book though- I rented an online version for 20 bucks and its awesome because I can just Ctrl-F the answers to the 3 quizzes. I recommend just do not forget to do the assignments and read the schedule.
Super easy teacher. If I ever had any questions, she always responded quickly and gave me an answer. Just pay attention to the requirements for the assignments and it's an easy A.
Awful. Just plain awful. Avoid at all costs. She really has no business teaching anymore.

Has no business teaching this class. when asked a question she rarely knows the answer or has to solicit the TA's or other students. If you took the book or PPT's away she'd be useless. She's a nice enough person if you stay on her good side, but if you don't class will suck. Eller should find a teacher that knows Java to teach the class.
Very nice lady she just had no business teaching the class. Listen to the other comments and seriously teach yourself java before the semester starts...because it's going to be a long one.

Shes a nice teacher who means well, but has very little knowledge of Java whatsoever. Merely reads from PP slides in class at very fast rate and attendance is required because group exercises due everyday in class. If you end up in this class I recommend teaching the material to yourself before class because you will not learn it from her.

Doesn't know or understand Java to save her life. Worst programming teacher I have ever had. Always asks TA's cause she doesn't know anything. Just reads the book in powerpoint slides. Worthless. If there was more worse things to say about her I would. She thinks we should just magically know it but when she doesn't teach it its very hard. BAD!

She really cares that her students learn the material. If you don't seem to be able to follow her explanations she asks her TAs to help, more than most teachers would do. She is more concerned that the students learn Java then just teaching her class as most teachers do. By far one of the best teachers at UA.

She is a kind person however a pretty bad Java professor. It seems as if she doesn't know the material at all, hence the reason she refers to her TAs for just about any question asked. She apparently had taught other courses and I cannot vouch for anything else but MIS 301, but there are some students in the class who would be a better teacher.
If you are a CS minor/major with a Java background you might pass a test. Ask a question, she defers to her TA's, She runs the Powerpoint slides that's about it.

Karen is so helpful. For every Homework/Assignment I would go to office hours unless you are super confident in your work. The content of this class is very challenging because it is so different from the accounting we are used to. Overall she is a great teacher. One downside is that it takes forever for grades to go in. Only 2 exams which is nice.

Great Professor, as long as you do the homework and read the textbook the tests should be a breeze, spent 2 hours a week at most on the accounting homework and reading. Extra credit homework every unit up to 3 points which is nice, and there are 9 attendance checks and 2 of them are dropped. She is a clear expert in accounting and a great prof
rips zynachinos

Very different accounting class with tough subject material that requires critical thinking. Lectures can be hard to follow at times and tend to run a few minutes over. Use office hours!! Karen is incredibly helpful in office hours, but get ahead (particularly on group projects) because her office fills when deadlines approach.

Makes lectures very clear and repeats information multiple times if you don't understand it on the first go. Goes over by a couple minutes every lecture. Breaks down the info well and believes that everyone can get an A in the class. Definitely a pretty easy class with a discussion every week and office hours if you need them. Don't need textbook!

If HW or concepts confuse you, attend her office hours for help. Practice problems from class and HW to excel on exams, and study her slides. Take notes in class and stay on top of group projects. Always attempt answers you don't know--better to get partial credit than none. She extended due dates a few times to avoid conflicting with other exams.

This class was super tough in the beginning, but it gets way easier after the first exam. Her lectures are soooo boring and sometimes I felt like she was rambling. I had a hard time listening to them.
She is a really nice person and really cares. However, accounting is not enjoyable but that is not her fault. All I gotta say.

Karen is an amazing professor unfortunately, the content in 461 was something I didn't enjoy. The content is focused on trends regarding improper business models. Karen was popular during office hours but, I was always able to get my questions answered. The exams, group projects, and quizzes require deep critical thinking that may frustrate people.

Class was really difficult and different from everything else in ACCT up to now, but an A is possible if you read the book and work a lot outside of class. Karen is very intelligent and SO helpful. Some of the best resources I've seen a professor offer. Lectures can be too long and not explained well, but just TALK to her or go to office hours!

This class is really difficult because its not like anything you've ever learned yet in accounting, but it starts making more sense as the semester goes on. Karen's lectures are frustrating bc she will go on and on about one thing and then there won't be enough time in class to cover the rest of the material and then will make you stay late.

Karen Otto is one of the most charismatic professors I have ever had. She teaches accounting very well in her lectures. If taking ACCT 200 with her go to all the lectures and do the extra credit and an A is totally achievable.
Karen was a good professor. The class is hard but she tried her best to make it understandable to us. She gave lots of materal to prepare us for the exams

Accounting can definitely be hard in the beginning, but go to office hours or do the lab because Karen wants to help. Extra credit and homework will help you on the exams, but go to lecture if you want to do well. Everything was explained very well and enjoyed her class.

For this class, it was important not to fall behind. Karen Otto provided lots of resources to understand the material. Overall good teacher and you just have to put effort into the class to get a good grade.

Super manageable if you actually attend lecture. She goes through her slides really quickly but does a bunch of example problems that help with the homework. You get what you put into this class so do the extra credit assignments to study for the exams and sign up for the optional labs!

She is approachable and very knowledgable in info systems. HW can be lengthy, so make sure you get a head start or you'll be struggling to complete it in one day. Exams weren't too difficult if you study the material and pay attention during lectures. She's also easily accessible via ask the instructor on D2L & quick to respond.

Otto is a pretty good professor she deserves to have higher rating in my opinion. Acct 200 is one of the harder pre-business classes which is probably the reason for the bad reviews. You have to actually study.

Karen Otto is a very good teacher. The material is difficult, but her lectures make difficult concepts a lot easier to understand. There are lots of opportunities to ask her questions, and lots of small homework assignments that are easy if you take notes and pay a little bit of attention during lectures. Just be sure to stay on top of deadlines.
Otto is good at what she does but her lectures are super fast. Make sure to do all of the assigned homework because I got a 70 and two 50s on the exams and still passed
Very simple as long as you show up. Pull your own weight in the class and you'll do fine. She is quick to respond if you ever have a question and really cares about your learning
Really good at teaching the material as long as you show up. You get what you put in to this class. It is a sep up in difficulty, but manageable if you want to learn the material.

Everyone giving this professor nice ratings must be TA's. Do yourself a favor, and avoid taking her class if you can. I honestly think she gets satisfaction of seeing students struggle. Attendance is not mandatory but if you skip you wont pass. She have long boring lectures and rushes through them, course material are not well organized. 3/10. 4 me

Prof. Otto is very knowledgeable in the subject matter. She offers extra credit and a lot of extra help, even on the weekends. ACCT 200 is a very challenging class with a lot of content. Prof Otto does ramble in class and does not make the class engaging at all. In addition, she comes off as rude and condescending during lecture and emails.

Karen Otto was an amazing accounting professor; her lectures were all very clear, and she was always accessible for questions. We always knew what was going to be on exams and how to prepare for them. Overall a very positive experience and I would highly recommend taking accounting with Karen Otto.

This professor was amazing. If you're nervous about the subject but willing to attend class, listen to the lectures, and complete the homework- this will be the easiest A. I can't say enough good things about this Professor Otto.

Feedback is awful on written, opinion based assignments. Grades incredibly hard on specific areas that drips your grade dramatically. For a graduate theory based class the focus should be on an understanding of the topics, not appeasing the instructor of their opinions. Not dropping grades to feel powerful.

The material is tricky at first but if you pay attention in class and study you'll be fine. Also, the hard stuff comes first in the semester, then it gets easier (as far as exam material goes). Group projects are tedious and stressful. She talks too much during lecture. The class would benefit more from active learning rather than listening to her.
Good lecturer, but of course accounting will be kind of hard

Overall, Karen is really nice and more than willing to try to help you if you're struggling with the material. Her lectures can get a bit boring because she just rambles on and on about the content, she's definitely a tell rather than show type of professor. Luckily, there's lots of opportunities to bring up your grade in this class.

Otto is a great professor overall - have nothing negative to say about her. Acct200 can be a tricky class but if you study, do the homework, and put in the effort then you will be successful. Would highly recommend Otto for Acct200.

If you put in effort in this class, you will be fine. Otto is an amazing professor and does her best in the limited class time to get us to understand the concepts. ACCT isn't easy, but if you put in the work outside of class, it will come to you. Do the lab and EVERY extra credit HW assignment and you will succeed. Literally finished with a 102%.
Decent professor who suffers from all around boring subject matter

Otto is a good teacher, but you have to work hard to get a good grade. Her lectures are long and she doesn't stop once to take a breath but it's definitely possible to get an A. DO EVERY PIECE OF EXTRA CREDIT

I am sure those who complain don't pay attention in class, this a hard class by itself, she does a great job explaining if you fall behind it will be your fault, don't expect anybody to hold your hand, but if you pay attention try to read the textbook but more importantly do your Homework, you will be totally fine.

Do NOT take this class if you can not focus in big 300 person lecture halls. She just reads word for word off of the powerpoints and if she is running out of time she skips half the powerpoint and tells us to learn it ourselves. She goes over the slides way too fast and her class is made for her students to fail.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! Her tests are extremely hard and she does not lecture well at all! You will FAIL if she is your teacher. Most of the class fails every exam! Go take it SOMEWHERE else!

She pretty much just reads lecture slides word for word. Very monotoned voice so it's difficult to follow. She will talk super fast and skip slides making it hard to keep up with notes. She's kind and it seems like she wants the students to succeed, however, the average grade on our tests is around a 76% and the exams are extremely difficult.

Prefer her over Stussie. A lot of memorizing and understanding how/why everything works but it gets confusing so flashcards and studying is vital.The 3 exams make up a majority of the grade, chegg will help you with everything,GO TO CLASS for clicker points,DO THE OPTIONAL LAB. Def go to lectures close to the end cuz she gives extra points

She really does want to help students but her class is absolutely terrible. She never posts actual rubrics and her tests are incredibly difficult. Watch her prerecorded lectures because they are a million times more helpful than her actual lectures in class. You cant avoid taking this class as an accounting major but be prepared for hating it.

She is passionate about accounting, but she can't make you interested if you're not interested. The assignments are clearly laid out at the start of the semester. Warning she does make you print out certain assignments to hand write. A clicker subscription and online textbook subscription are required. Attendance is required.

Accounting is a class when the professor mentions to do something to do it. The readings in the textbook aren't mandatory but help out a lot. When she recommends to make notecards do it. This class will take extra time to really understand what is going on, but she is very helpful if you have questions, and there is extra credit given in homework.

Otto Is a good professor teaching a difficult course. Accounting 200 is difficult but she does her best to provide her students with the best materials possible to pass in this class. With that being said, your grade is a reflection of the effort you put in to all the assignments
Great professor, very good at teaching. Optional labs help a ton. Exam reviews are very good and detailed.
grading criteria is bleak and the projects are not very clear cut so getting a good grade is sometimes confusing

Karen is super kind, friendly and helpful even during the pandemic. Not many gradables, and homework could be hard, so come to the office hours. She gives around 1% extra credit, but they are not free, you have to do some extra HW for it.

She doesn't make the content easy for her students. This is the hardest class Ive ever taken. Lots of documents which make it hard to keep up, her lecturing is not effective. Not easy to pass. Extremely difficult, little gradeables, no EC. Her exams are very hard and the activities/HW are impossible without office hours. Just really overwhelming.
