Probably the hardest professor Ive dealt with at University of Arizona. She makes you memorize everything in the class and doesnt give any equations or help on the exams. The final exam is worth 33% of your grade and they dont curve it. Would recommend taking this course at a community college or not with Karen memorize or fail the class Otto.

Pretty much what you'd expect for ACCT200. Karen did a solid job, mostly just taught from the prerecorded lectures. Class is pretty tough but that's typical. Overall pretty average experience, she didn't leave me blown away but I also don't have anything bad to say. If I had to take ACCT200 again I wouldn't mind taking it with her.

Karen was meh. Her lectures never engaged me and did most of the work by learning it on my own or having my friends explain it to me. Definitely a doable class but Karen makes it hard to show up every day.

Karen is great at giving feedback and making clear guidelines. Lectures can be dry, especially when going over new topics. She and the TA are easy to contact if something isn't clear or you need help on a project or homework.

Accounting isn't an easy course at all but compared to the other accounting professors she seems to be the best and Stessi is good too. Don't take Blanchard. Work hard in the class and do the lab and go to campus tutoring for extra help because listening in the lecture can be very difficult because its way too long.
Karen just isnt effective at lecturing- bottom line. She just talks and talks and talks and isnt good at actually teaching the subject. Its mostly white noise.

She's the only one who teaches 461 so you have no choice. BUT she is a great teacher who simplifies topics so you can understand them. There are very few gradeables so you have to be on top of it from the beginning of the semester if you want to get an A.
Dr. Otto was super fun and I hope I can be in another class that she teaches. Just go to class, do the lab, and do the homework and you'll be fine.
HORRIBLEEEEEEEEE

Karen Otto is one of the best teachers I've had at U of A! Her lectures are very engaging which makes it much easier to learn. However, you must pay attention to truly understand the course content. Therefore, attendance is pretty important. Overall, Karen is a very reasonable professor, especially when it comes to grading.

If you have to take accounting 200 at UA take it with Otto!! She made accounting as easy as possible and truly cares about her students. I prepared myself to have a difficult time in this class but attending her lectures and office hours made this course so much easier. The tests are difficult, but she makes things as easy as possible.

She doesn't necessarily make the content easy for her students. While she does take time to explain it! She does not connect the lectures to every day and color coats slides which makes everything even harder to understand. Overall she does give a lot of assistance but does not make it easy to pass. Her exams  very hard and effect your grade badly

Otto definitely makes accounting a much easier subject to understand. The class is difficult only because accounting is difficult. She tries to make it as simple as possible and it seems like shes always willing to help.

One of the best professors I had at the U of A. I loved her class and she didn't make accounting seem scary or impossible. Loved her encouragement and passion for accounting!!! Take her class if you can!!!!

Amazing Professor, really cares about the students. Very Helpful. Difficult class to begin with but she makes it as easy to get the information to learn the material as possible. One of the best professors i have taken at the U of A. She really cares about her students

I love Otto!! Accounting is hard but she makes it manageable. She randomly selects 6 days where she takes attendance. Also, there are in-class clicker questions on specified days, so definitely show up on those days! There is a lot of homework but I feel it was all helpful when studying for tests. Definitely sign up for lab!! It helps so much.

Ok, Karen really does care and does want the students to succeed. But OMG all she does is talk and talk. the more she talks the more difficult she makes things out to be. And once she goes over the subject, she expects you to be a master at that subject. Overall, not a good teacher.

Professor is OK. The class is pretty information heavy on its own and going to class doesn't really help. You have to teach everything to yourself. If there wasn't random attendance points and clicker questions I wouldn't have even gone to class. Overall she is not particularly helpful but I think it has more to do with the class structure than her

Super chill professor, very helpful and nice. She's genuinely great, but the accounting class itself is really tough and you either get it or you don't (I've done better in physics and calc). 3 exams and the final counts for 1/3 of your grade. Attendance is taken, and you also get a lot homework.

She just goes over the powerpoint in lecture that you can find online with fill-in the blanks. Accounting is straightforward in this class, so it was super easy. Online homework once or twice a week, 2 attempts each. Exams were easy if you went over study guide. She has an accent that is super noticeable. Clicker questions once week. So easy.

Otto is overall a good professor. Office hours help a lot so if you have the chance, go to her. Exams can be tricky so focus on what she says in the lecture, and for exam 2 just memorize the question sheet she provides. Project is not bad at all. Very easy for an AccountingMIS double major.

Great Professor. This is the first accounting class I've enjoyed. She gives detailed explanations and her lectures are what is tested on. You must attend the lectures to pass. There are two group projects that last the whole semester but if you do it right, you can easily get a B, or an A if you put in the effort.

This is a hard class. But all Acct 200's are hard. She slows stuff down a little in lecture. Her office hours are normally too crowded to be worth going to. Tests are hard but if you study more then just the night before you can pass. Take the optional lab it will help even if it seems tedious.

This class covers a lot of information and reading the book is crucial. Professor  Otto does a great job going over information in class and answering and questions. She is super approachable and makes this class more interesting then then the average professor would. This class was required and I'm really glad I took it with Karen Otto

Very fair and reasonable with her expectations and grading of assignments. The tests are difficult, but they are very fair. (In my opinion, pretty easy.) She tells you exactly what's going to be on the test. So, try to go to every class, or know someone who does. Use TA office hours to get HW checked prior to submitting  Easy As on HW

Karen is an amazing teacher! It is very beneficial to go to class. Karen is so straight forward and clear. You will never be confused about what you have to do to earn a good grade. She will always help in any way she can.

Great professor! I enjoyed her class very much- the first accounting class that didn't include journal entries, but rather procedures. Good for people who like to memorize diagrams and processes. There is a big project throughout the whole class relating to building a database, but overall not a bad class!
Soooooo boring! Does not interact with the class she just drones on and on and on and on in a monotone voice. Important class but soooooo boring!
boring material and hard if you don't stay on top of it...ps: 10 bucks says the comment below me is a guy...typical.
The material is horribly boring. She is a hottie though!
Straightforward, very willing to help outside of class. Material a bit mundane, but thats not her fault.

Dr. P is the best lecturer I have taken! Her lectures are fun and engaging, and she truly cares for her students and wants them to succeed. She is very accessible outside of class, and offers multiple extra credit opportunities. Exams are weighted heavily, but if you take notes from the textbook and her lectures, you will be just fine. :)

Paradise is an engaging professor, but the content itself is relatively stale and she mentions one of her past job positions a few too many times. So long as you take good notes and review the day before exams, it's very easy to be successful in this course. The occasional interactive activities were also fun, albeit not very helpful content-wise.

Dr. Paradise is one of the best lecturers Eller has! Erin is very knowledgeable, friendly, and an all around amazing and caring instructor. Her lectures are far from boring, and she made this class very enjoyable. 10/10 recommend! 

Thank you Dr. Paradise for all you do!
She is super engaging and caring about her students. Her classes are mostly lectures and she lets you use notes for the tests. I would definitely take again!
Really good teacher and always waits after class if students have questions. She also lets you use notes on tests.

Dr. Paradise is an awesome lecturer, she is the most engaging big lecture professor I've had. She cares about her students' well-being and is a great person. She is very approachable and genuine. The exams are open note, but be cautious about losing track of time searching for answers.

She goes over so much content in such little time and the class only is graded on the exams and the case. The exams were open note and open textbook but I feel like this class is so hard because everything is situational. Overall if you really lock in with the textbook and lectures you'd be fine. Just sucks all this effort for a dumb class.
GREAT HUMAN. Hate the class, but love the person.
My favorite professor this year! She was super kind and made sure the lecture was fun and engaging. The content was easy but sometimes boring. Super easy A!

Do all the extra credit. The class isn't hard but the exams are difficult as there is no study guide and she has diff material in the textbook and lectures. She/her preceptors are kind of tough graders on the projects. You do need to show up to class as there are in class activities. Overall shes friendly!

I recommend taking a different professor he is very critical on the written portion of the quizzes and grades harshly when its not specific enough but only when its too specific he accuses you of cheating.

Grade is based on 6 quizzes and 2 exams. If you miss class then you'll miss a lecture and fail a quiz. Quizzes are easy as long as you write down everything he says to study! I passed without the textbook doing this. He drops the 2 lowest quiz grades which is very helpful. Very passionate and funny professor

Took zoom class. Class sessions are basically him talking through slideshow of different art pieces. He defines key terms used (will be on quizzes). Attendance was mandatory since slides weren't uploaded. I used to not care about art at all until I this class, now I appreciate it much more! Just take notes, pay like 50% attention, & you'll be ok

I took 6 classes with this Professor: Painting, Independent Study, Printmaking, Art History 1 & 2, & Figure Drawing. I was denied an Art Major when I was young, & returned to college at age 66. The Textbook was huge, but thorough. The Professor gave good lectures, sometimes humorous. He inspired me to seek a Degree in Art History. Thanks!

This professor starts the first class by saying that NO ONE in his class ever gets an A unless they are very smart. He teaches his class with passion and it is interesting to listen but his quizzes and work dont match some things he taught. Some of the quizzes are simple as an true or false questions but he makes them complicated.

Bill makes the class interesting. There is a test every week on Thursdays, all you have to do is answer five multiple choice questions and a short paragraph about what you see in the picture he shows. As long as you read some of the stuff in the textbook and write the key terms in your paragraph you'll pass.
Tried to make a boring class interesting. Miss a lecture, fail the quiz. Quiz every Thursday on the previous two lectures. Pretty nice and passionate but take another class for the art credit.

I had professor Bill for my ARH141 class since the beginning I was really confused. We would take notes one class and the next one we had a quiz but the way he grades is really complex/ not clear, even the best guy in class had a B. He is a really cool person but I did not like the class at all.

I've taken almost every class this guy offers. Art History, you don't need the book just pay attention to the lectures. Painting/Drawing, he doesn't always explain what he expects from you so you're kinda on your own. If you really care about painting he will tear you apart with his critiques but so long as you turn in 4 paintings you'll pass

Blomquist is AWESOME, nice&funny. My class was twice a week ,Tues and Thurs, which Tues we would take notes and Thurs would be test day, and take notes right after the test. The test consisted of 5 T/F questions and one art piece analysis. EVERYTHING he says write it down. Without the book, I earned a B+ by studying my notes of EVERYTHING he said.

I was very skeptical while registering for this class but Professor Blomquist really made art history interesting and enjoyable for me. The only graded assignment are weekly quizzes which are 5 true or false and then one picture to describe. The tend to be fairly easy if you actually listen.

Had for painting class. Student-controlled room, you set up easel, have paints - SPECIFIC kind of paints and brushes. He will tell you the material, and assignments. Usually 4-5 paintings for a semester. He is hard about references. You MUST have one, from a master painter. Doesn't teach you, you learn on your own but he will direct you/help/advice

I love this class very entertaining and interesting, Professor explains everything clearly and in a way we can all understand. If you ask for help, he will help. He only gives quizzes on Thursdays based on your notes (make sure to take some notes). You do use the book, but only to study. Plus, if you miss a class you can watch it later online.
Really fun class. Great professor, always made it interesting. would highly recommend,

He is a great professor, extremely clear and detailed. You have to attend every class or fall behind. There's a quiz every week, 5 true&false questions and 1 slide description, you HAVE to nail the true and false question or it will kill your grade. If you do not study for quizzes do not expect and easy A. He assigns no Homework. Easy class overall

Professor Blomquist is excellent! His lectures are lively and very informative. Not only that, he facilitates class discussion very well - you won't be afraid to speak up! Another plus is that he is more concerned about his students understanding the ideas and history of art rather than just memorizing a bunch of little facts. Highly recommended!
Very detailed, but easy. I haven't had to use a textbook yet, everything is shown on slides.
Good teacher. He knows his stuff when it comes to drawing techniques, and I don't think anybody left the class without having improved their drawing skills.

I took the online class for Econ485. Lots of group projects and I got stuck with a bad group. Nothing I could do about it so didn't get a good grade. Made the fact that my group wasn't doing what they were supposed to be doing to the professor and basically got told that there's nothing he can do about it.
Great lecturer that draws comparisons to the real world often and activities that keep you captivated. Also very funny. Class was a great experience.
Great lecturer and his class was overall a very enjoyable experience. Very relatable and funny, and makes sure to draw real world connections during his lectures.

Mark was hands down the best professor I have had at UA. He was very personable to everyone and always made our class laugh. I am one to daze off very easily in class, but his lectures were very easy to follow along. I learned so much from taking his courses. He is such a nice guy and wants everyone to do well. Dont listen to the comments.

The professor is an extremely tough grader, and the grading rubric often did not align with the instructions given for assignments. It felt like we were expected to read his mind to understand what he truly wanted, which was frustrating. There was a lack of clear communication and hard to succeed unless you somehow knew the unspoken expectations.

Expect to do a lot of work for little credit. The class is entirely based on a group project, but the assignment guidelines don't match the professor's expectations. His feedback demands far more detail than the vague outlines suggest, making it hard to meet unclear standards.

Avoid this class with this professor at all costs. His grading is completely unfair and more than half of the overall grade is based on a group project with 3 random people (IN AN ONLINE COURSE). He does not specify the exact requirements for the project in any of the assignment rubrics, leaving it incredibly vague and open to his bias.

Almost 80% of the overall grade is a group project.  It is bad enough that your entire grade relies on three other randomly chosen people, especially online.  To make it worse, his grading is completely asinine. I am an A student getting 70-75% in this course for the most nit-picky, opinionated  things that aren't even on the rubric.

A very unique class. Lots of interaction in the class and high student engagement.  Learned a lot and continually challenged to think out of the box. I signed up for another of Marks classes next semester.

Just wrapped up this class and it was a blast.  Learned a lot, had fun and went out of my way to take another of Mark's classes next semester.  Very friendly and approachable professor.  Much appreciated by his students and can't say enough to convince others to sign up for his classes.

I really got a lot out of this class.  Every lecture brought up new topics and challenged students to rethink their beliefs.  I truly understood the importance of standing out and getting across my unique offer. Thank you for a great class!

Great class!  Not a morning person but Mark made the 8 am start easy.  I did not want to miss class and that says a lot for what has turned out to be a valuable learning experience.  Definitely not your average biz school class in a good way.

This was an awesome course if you are thinking about starting a business in the future.  Mark was great and made the class fun and informative.  Every class was worthwhile and a learning opportunity.  The marketplace at the end of the semester was a great experience.  Highly recommend!

I really enjoyed this class.  Learned a lot about thinking in different ways and having new perspectives.  Never bored and actually looked forward to coming to class every week.  Mark is very approachable and liked by students.
This was a great class!  Taught in a creative way that kept students engaged.  Highly recommend if you can get him.

This class was taught by 2 professors. It seemed like they did not communicate with each other because 1 would give certain directions and due dates and the other would not know. Mark Peterson gave no examples on projects and would grade unfairly. Little to no useful entrepreneurship information was learned, and was a big let down. Do not take.
If you want to do group projects, take this class.

Unfortunately I had a poor experience. My goal was to learn and get something out of this class unfortunately this class had way too many group projects that weighted down the grade. While 1 person may give an A effort not all do. Need to move away from this approach of instructing for adults.

Absolute gem of a professor. He is kind, funny, and very reasonable with assignments. Participation is needed though, I noticed that he didn't seem particularly fond of others in my class who never showed up or who stayed silent the whole time. 10000/10 recommend taking a class with him.
Favorite professor at UA so far. You should definitely take his ENTR 421R class.

Mark is a gem. His class feels like a true college class but if you're not one to participate in class he might not be the one for you though. He expects his students to put in the work but helps us throughout the way. I switched my whole schedule around to take him again next semester - definitely recommend.
Has fun lectures that keep you engaged and then the finals are actually over items that ARE covered during the class.

Sorry, I am not understanding these other reviews. Professor Peterson is literally one of the hardest teachers I have ever had and does not lead a well-structured and organized class. His lectures have nothing to do with the assignments that he grades so difficultly. Not a fan.

Mark is a literal angel. He was so sweet and was kinda funny in a dad kind of way. We only met for class once a week. His lectures were interesting. We had a textbook that had small quizzes and activities to complete (not hard). The major part of this class was the 6 papers that were easy to do if you had chosen a good group.
Group project is 70% of grade. If you're in a bad group contact Professor Peterson week 1 and split off immediately. It's a little more work but worth it.
In class is very chill and easy. Online quizzes are pretty tough. TONS of group projects that are graded pretty tough. Hard to get A if you're in a bad group.

I highly recommend this class. Easy A if you have a strong team and put in 100%. The majority of the grade is a group project. That's why every assignment your group turns in is important. Loved his lectures! By far the best I've ever had!

Mark is the man! It probably helps that he is teaching entrepreneurship, but his classes are engaging and he speaks about real life concepts. He has started a lot of businesses himself so this makes his suggestions more credible. He does different things every class to make it interesting, and plain and simple he's a funny guy.
Asaf makes lectures hard to sit through. He is not good at explaining and he will not respond to his emails.

Professor Plan is a great professor he teaches really well. If you actually do the homework and reading that he assigns and do them sucessfully this class is straight forward. He doesn't trick you on the exams and helps you as much as possible. Economics is not an easy subject. I wouldnt say it was an easy thing to learn but he teaches it well.

This professor's teaching style may not be the best fit for undergrads. Lectures rely heavily on slides and can be hard to follow without extra explanation. Exams are graded fairly by TAs, but use outside resources or online lectures since he is the only option for ECON 361.

This professor is very disorganized and communicates poorly. There's no real schedule, assignments are announced last minute, and he despises emails. Preceptors handle all exams and grading, and he is late to class 75% of the time. The course is easy to pass with infinite notes for exams and multiple homework attempts. I learned absolutely nothing.
Does not explain anything in his lectures, making them a waste of time. Luckily he doesn't grade too harshly, if you write everything word for word from the homework on the three-question exam.
Reads directly from textbook slides, making class a complete waste of time. Very incompetent. Did not learn a single thing in this class

Kinda incompetent. He usually has no idea what's going on, except that using cookies and cream as an example is a perfect analogy for everything he teaches. The good news is that he's pretty wishy washy and easily manipulated; we got open notes for all exams. Overall a very boring class and don't expect more than 3 sentences for the syllabus.

Terrible teacher. The syllabus was a paragraph and wasn't out until two weeks after class started. He never showed up to any exams or office hours. He never knew what he was teaching until the day of. He was very unprepared and unprofessional. I don't know how he's a professor. Avoid him at all costs if you want to learn content.

This professor is a good professor and he explains in a really good way. He cares a lot about his students and wants all students to pass this class. The important thing is that being careful and solving all homework problems. If you ace the homework questions, you can ace the exam as well.
Dawg where are you, we have midterm tomorrow

He doesnt put any effort into his classes AT ALL. The syllabus wasnt up until the second week  and was unclear. You need to learn everything by yourself as he does a really bad job at teaching. He refused to provide a study guide for the tests which have 3-5 questions total which can sink your grade. He also didnt show up to any tests.
Only plus is he doesn't take attendance. Otherwise is useless.

Reads directly from textbook, doesn't do exam reviews, study guides, or show up for exam. Heavily relies on TA's (who don't know material either) and doesn't host his own office hours. Class average always below a 60% on exams, doesn't curve or attempt to do anything differently after. Don't waste your tuition or time with this professor.
Terrible. Reads directly from text book slides. Always contradicts himself and text book. Doesn't know how to lecture or write a test. Don't waste your time with this guy.

*This is a review for Econ 361*
Avoid this professor at all costs. He doesn't understand the material and solely relies on the textbook. He's inaccessible to ask questions and refers you to the TAs that know even less about what's going on. His tests are randomly assembled based on homework questions and not clear.
He mainly uses his notes for the classes. Initially it is not easy to follow and understand. But later we realized what is going on and understand the materials. MWG is recommended for this course.
Terrible experience!!! I do not recommend taking his class.

Podsakoff is a great professor, he is clear-cut and to the point. Really cares about what he is teaching and wants to make sure everyone understands the material fully. Will go out of his way to make sure you can see him if you have questions. BUT class is very hard, and he expects honors material. Hard grader, tough exams, and the group project.

Podsakoff has behaved very politely and nice to us. He is always attentive and whenever I asked him if I could meet with him, he always said yes with a smile on his face. One of the kindest and clearest professors I've ever had. Passionate about what he teaches but he is a hard grader. However, he gives clear instructions as to what he wants.

Alert !!! - Avoid him at all costs!!! Dealing with him is extremely annoying - he is extremely mean. Dealing with him is like dealing with someone who would complain about why you use too much toliet papers, why you drink too much waters, etc. He is an extremely tough grader! DONT WALK AWAY, RUN AWAY!!!!!!

I already miss this class, I felt I learned the most about public health careers in this class and the group I was in all grew close so there was perks. Worth taking the in person version with this professor
Professor sepulveda is my favorite. He's a great teacher and he's got drip fr fr. I wish he taught more courses
Love this class and professor Ramses, he's my favorite.

Take this class, you learn about public health and you make great friends and community. Ramses is the most responsive professor I've had, he gives feedback and support. I wish he taught more courses.

Ramses is a very personable guy. HPS 350 is a hard class to take but he helps because you can tell how much he cares about the things he teaches. He was always up to answer all questions and would often create lighthearted banter with people to create some laughter. Just show up to class or you will easily fall behind and pay attention to quizzes.

Couldn't recommend him enough. We need more teachers like him in public health he is kind to his students and truly cares about everyone's success. There are some big projects in this class, but honestly very manageable. He helps students secure internships outside of class too. Take the in-person class it is worth it, and attendance does count!
Loved his class,I tried the online version and dropped it because it was terrible. This one is worth taking in person if sepulveda is teaching it

Probably the public heath class I got the most out of, you learn about what you will do in the field and I felt more prepared for the job search after. It is pretty dependent on the group work but I had a great time. This was by far my fav class in the major so far.
This was my favorite class this semester, I wish I could take more with him. I hardly get to know any of my public health professors and he was consistent
Fun class, just get down to business when you're in a team and you'll do okay. My favorite class this semester
Like others have said the team projects require you to check in like a lot, but I do understand and like public health much more now if helps he is handsome hehe 😍

the class is tough because its a lot of things covered and you have to rely on your group, but I really enjoyed it. It made me like my major even more, and he is so lovely and approachable when you need to talk to him directly, professor sepulveda made me like public health
Lots of group work but I love his class, something different every day. 2 projects so don't mess any up, he's really charming and nice

Ramses is pretty engaging, his in-class activities do not have a lot of fluff and he connects it to the real world research projects the college is doing. You get graded on 2 team projects so if you dont like your team point it out quick. Very little graded items so make your pop quizzes count, he is very likeable btw, I would take more of his clas

HPS 350 can be overwhelming... a lot of concepts covered, but he makes the class really fun, I feel like I learned a ton of the public health program all together. He is really cool and helps out with projects too. The class is not hard but if you miss 1 day you miss a lot so just know you kind of need to attend to help your team.

Ramses is super nice. His public health course seems slightly tougher than the MAS course but he is very nice, very helpful, and gives great feedback to help you understand. The course helped me understand public health as a whole which was encouraging.

Ramses was a great profeessor and it is evident that he cares about his students and participation. Attendance was mandatory and we had lecture quizzes in class almost every class, they were easy quizzes as long as you were reading the material at home which was short and easy. The course also had 2 group projects. Would take his classes again.

Sepulveda is cool. He cares about participation and with this class it makes sense. It was mandatory for me but I heard a lot of other instructors were way worse so I'm glad I got him for this class. If you are taking lasc make sure you get him.

Ramses cares about you showing up. He is very sarcastic but funny. I went to him with questions about my schedule. He helped me out and we talked about Jordan's for like 40min. He is relatable and a noice fella

I went to Ramses at the end of lasc100x because I had a very bad semester, he gave me advice on classes to take and he even talked to me about exploring majors. A whole lot more than what my useless advisors did. He's the reason I'll return to UofA, cool guy. If you are in wildcat academy and have to take the class get him.
There was a bummer of a group project but overall cool class, I missed class because of a family emergency and he let me make up all the points without penalty.
Such a cutie, he is well spoken and gives great networking advice
he cares about people showing up, super laid back and you learn about campus. told my fraternity brothers to take it lol
Ramses is Awesome great class! Take it! no book, little homework.
Class was easy and interesting, my favorite part was learning about student jobs on campus. I recommend his class.
Super chill dude, would take all his classes if I do. Knows everything about campus kinda scary

His class seemed to be boring at first but then turned into the one I enjoyed the most; I literally likes showing up lol. He was a GA first for willertons class, he looks really young but apparently he's doing doctoral work. Sgpp was a boring class but his 295 one was way fun. Not many professors are young, or keep it real with you like he does!

My friend told me to add this class freshman year, fulfilled a diversity credit, and Ramses is super cool! Writes letters of rec if you need them too. I would find out which one he is teaching next for success and adding him! Best proffffffffffff

Ramses is really funny! apparently he is not teaching this course next semester but he told me he may be teaching another (I hope i am in)!!! Super easy, show up, participate, do weekly journals like 5 and you get an A! FYI TOTES DESERVES THE PEPPER! WAY EASY ON THE EYES!! hehe

Ramses is really funny and he helps the class make resumes and write cover letters. He teaches a lot about stereotypes and how to approach social justice things, what is really cool is when he teaches about employment and uses on-campus jobs as examples.

It's a transition success course, if you can get into this class do it! Ramses doesn't mind giving everyone A's if they earned it and attended class, it doesn't hurt he's super handsome either. #thathair

Ramses is awesome! And if your here Cuz of hotness thing yes he is good looking but above all else the class is like super helpful. The syllabus says focuses on student success and its true, I got a lot of helpful advise and learned so much about what it takes to get success

sooo you like take a course on  how to be a successful student through someone who already has. The lectures are about University life and things that you might need to know in certain situation. Talks a lot about policies and relates his studies to important networking. He has a spicy thing because he is a young handsome guy.

Ramses has the best class! It is mandatory for some but I took it as an elective. He can help you with your schedule way more than an advisor. He keeps it real and is funny. By far my favorite class, I hope I can take the second section.

Ramses first comes off as real laid back, and the class topics seem to be easy. Once the class is in full swing the topics really prepare you for what you see at U of A. He knows the student life, university system and has a lot of tips on finding a job, picking majors, class and parking lol. He's funny too!

Mr. Sepulveda was by far one of my favorite professors, every day before class I'd ask him what we'd be learnin today; he'd always very enthusiastically tell me what I'd be learnin that day. 10/10 class, ask him what you'll learn at the start of every lecture if you take the course.
Mr. Sepulveda cracks many jokes, but has valuable insight when regarding tips and tricks to surviving your freshman year also known as life hacks. Many people say he is attractive, but he is alright.
Mr. Sir Sepulveda was always finding a way to make class fun and interesting. There was never a lot of homework. He is every understanding and he will work with you.
Ramses is awesome! He talks to you about the student life from his perspective and he led good discussions. I love our UAHA familia 2013-2014! Viva los gatos <3
Ramses is informative class is fun so far!
fun class!

Rashan is a nice guy overall. When it comes to grading he is very harsh especially for group presentations. With that being said he is available a lot and wants the best for his students but isn't always clear when you come to him with questions. He's the type of teacher who answers your question with a question and won't give a clear answer.

Professor Rashan is the best teacher I have had. He is incredibly thoughtful and understanding. His class was very beneficial and I learned a lot through attending class and completing assignments. I had a personal issue during the semester and professor Rashan was very accessible outside of class and helped me get back on track. I highly recommend

BCOM314(the public-speaking one) is an equally rewarding and punishing course. You put in the effort you'll do fine, if you don't you'll embarrass yourself and harm your grade. Rashan in particular is an easier grader than other BCOM profs from what I hear; you'll be lucky to have him if you get him.

He is a very good professor, albeit slightly more picky than some other professors when grading assignments. He is also very easy to talk to if you're stuck, and has a great sense of humor and energy in the class. Even though he's picky with grading, he's not a hard grader at all as long as you hit every point on the rubric.

Mr Rashan was my favorite professor this semester. He sincerly cared about his class and wanted us to do our best. He taugh us the improtance of working with new people every class period. He gives the vibes of a cool uncle that just wants the best for you.

Dense material but Sami is caring and offers extra credit before exams and sometimes for attendance. Sami is open to offering more office hours and wants to help students. Lectures are boring, but if you put in the time to practice the concepts, you'll be fine. Online homework, open note quizzes, 2 assignments, & 3 non-cumulative in-test exams.

This claass is entirely memorization-based, when it comes to the quizzes that occur every class. Quizzes consistent of memorizing 4 or 5 vocabulary terms or lists for the chapter and if you dont knoe the obscure list contents, you get a 0. The exams are also based on knowing onscure vocabulary. Expect a harder version of grad audit classes.

Martin is a really nice guy, however, his class was super boring. He spoke in a monotone voice and didn't seem interested in the class at all, even though he has an abundance of experience! Overall though this class wasn't too bad. There is group work, and he does take attendance most days.

Absolutely amazing professor!! I loved being apart of her class; Her lectures were interesting and fun and there were weekly readings along with a final project that was done throughout the course. Super helpful and understanding!

Note that I took this for the MBA-level pricing course (MKTG 556). Roy is awesome! He really cares about the success of his students. I really like that he used cases for discussion and not reports/write-ups. I felt like that helped me learn the material better and at my own pace. Simulation is fun and final is straightforward. Amazing dude!

I learned a lot from this class! I think his syllabus is a little daunting because you're graded by a few things, but he's also a really easy grader, so you don't need to worry about it. This class in particular was a little math-heavy, but he explained it so well that it seemed like everyone got it. I would recommend taking a class with Roy!

Roy is a good dude, just tends to drag on a little bit. Doesn't like to put people on the spot, but I wish he did honestly; would make things a little bit more exciting. Didn't feel like I gleaned a lot from the class, but was overall average!

Roy is a good guy and cares about all of his students, but i didn't learn a dam thing in his class. You have to read a bunch of different cases and his classes drag a lot. I had to take this class in order to graduate.

Roy's class was different than any other I have taken at Eller. His classes are super boring, lectures drag on and on. He is super nice but not much you learn in this class you will find exciting or beneficial. There is only 3 grades in the whole class, so if you blow one you might not do too hot. Just show up to class and listen, you will get an A

I was a preceptor for Roy and he was one of the pleasant and most helpful faculty to me in the marketing department even though he just recently came to the school. He very much cares about his students succeeding--- get to know him.

Roy is the man, great teacher that cares about his students learning more than students showing up or cramming test. Wants the best for each and every student and will work hard to help you get the most from his class. Also, gives you real life experience through interesting case studies.

If you read the book, study slides, and pay attention in class you will get a good grade. Roy is a great person, very educated and humble. His accent is strong and he will lecture all class. If you are interested in the material you will find this class helpful and fun. Exams are hard if you don't study. 6 pop quizzes (drops 1.
He's boring and monotone but not terrible. He does do pop quizzes in class so make sure to do the reading ahead of time. It sucks but actually keeps you on track with the class.
Jason gives great demos and feedback when you are struggling

Awesome professor, cares about students understanding ceramics techniques. Teaches through demonstrations, then goes around the class answering questions for students. VERY hands on class. Mostly about showing up and doing the work. No amount of explaining will help if you're not spending several hours a week in the studio practicing the techniques

he barely taught (left class early) and didn't follow his syllabus, you will only get to watch him show you basic things. his comments on my work weren't constructive at all, and he expects labor from you around the studio, without any guidance. if you want to be creative, he will knock you down. one of the most unsupportive teachers ive ever meet.

Jason is a very helpful professor and you can tell he throughly enjoys teaching this class. This class is a little difficult just because the nature of the work, ceramics is time consuming, so expect to come into the after hours ceramics to work.
he is a mean and difficult person

Professor Hess is an amazing ceramics professor. The class is easy-going and the only required work is everything you do in class. If you fall behind on a project, you can come into studio time whenever you want on weekends. I highly recommend taking ceramics 161 with Hess!

Jason is an awesome teacher. He gives great demos and explanations, and is very caring and understanding. His knowledge and passion for working with clay make him a great mentor if you're trying to explore ceramics for the first time. He'll still give you credit even if you're like me and your projects don't turn out all that great!

Only homework is getting your projects done, but there are quite a few so it is important to stay on top of it. The class overall is very simple. You throw your pieces. They get fired. You critique it at the end of the semester. You have several different assignments with a minimal amount to reach and you are graded from the best of those pieces.
No diversity in his curriculum, teaches the same thing or what he is confortable with. His ego is huge. He doesn't care about inspiring students.

Jason is a super nice guy. He offers feedback and will help you make amazing things. He grades on quality, not effort, but if you ask him for help he will make sure you're pieces are great quality. No matter who you take for this class you will have to come in a couple hours a week to  stay caught up, but it's so fun it doesn't feel like work.
Pretty good ceramics teacher. He expects kind of a lot from even beginning ceramic students. It's a fun class to take!
Amazing ceramics teacher! offers help and instruction very clearly. A great fun class that is definitely worth taking if you don't mind getting a little dirty.
I want my MBA to be worth something, not just a snooze fest.  Diza cares and hits the distance to make sure if you want to learn you level up. Refreshing.

almost everything in this course is a group project/group grade. There are 2 individual assignments and the rest depends on how your group performs and participates with each other (discussions, projects). theres also a group presentation at the end of the course where she chooses the time and day and you have to show up on that day no matter what.

very confusing professor. she would always blame things on D2L not working (grades not being posted for weeks, etc.) even though other courses I was taking had no issue. Very unclear instructions on all assignments.
She's great, take her. You'll enjoy her class.

Diza is a great professor, and you know immediately that she cares about you as a person.  I was surprised at my grade, it was higher than I was expecting.  Almost all of it goes to how willing you are to put yourself out there and admit your shortcomings.  She will help you.  You will HAVE to participate!  No matter what.  It helps!

Tough instructor but, very fair in her grading.  Provides thorough guidance and feedback.  Expect to work hard for your grade and make consistent progress in improving you work and she will take notice.  Stay on top of the course from the start and whatever you do, do not fall behind.
Tough but smart. Suck it up and listen. You'll learn a lot.
She is the best teacher I have ever had.
awesome

Instead of teaching the software skills we need, she focuses on the basics topics we have learned in previous courses. Bad at responding to emails and when asking for help dances around giving you an answer. When a student participates she comes off condescending instead of constructive. VERY unclear on quiz topics to study.
Not the best professor. She does not teach the content well and she does not respond to emails quickly. There is also a new software you need to buy that is pointless.

Jennifer is a very boring lecturer and takes a long time to get her point across. She also gives poor feedback on homework assignments that she grades very hard. She is not very understanding and is slightly rude to students. I can tell that she would be a positive person outside of teaching so maybe its just her sign to retire.

Disrespectful, especially towards the end of the semester. She also belittled and destroyed a student in front of the whole class! Her D2L assignment organization is also terrible and all over the place and she ignored the criteria on her own syllabus!

There was definitely a lack of respect: we were consistently kept 5 to 10 minutes over. During class, a student wanted to confirm the upcoming test was open book. The professor berated her in front of the entire class. When she was told the syllabus included it, the prof then tried to blame undergrads from last year for needing it.
Jennifer is a wonderful person and professor! She cares about her students and even more so cares that they succeed. She just radiates positivity!
Very passionate professor! Loves her students! Pay attention in class, take notes, no laptops, and read the text book and you'll get an A!
Prof. Savary is a great professor. She knows A LOT about what she is teaching and she is also very passionate about it, and that always makes the class better. Cares about her students!

I had her for MGMT 475 and she was an amazing professor. Her lectures are very interesting and actually applicable to life. We don't really have any homework outside of class and her exams are PURELY from the lectures she teaches and other insight from class. Course is clear and she is actually able to make content appealing. I truly love her.

I really enjoyed this class! The lectures are great. The grades are based on a series of papers that are not too difficult as long as you follow directions and start them ahead of time. The final test is pretty easy as she shares the questions the week before. Would definitely recommend this.

Shatzberg is a nice guy, but an awful professor. This was one of the worst classes I've ever taken. It was confusing and pointless. All I did was cram for tests and do better than half of my class (55% avg.) to get an A. I came away with nothing from this other than the university system is broken if guys like this make over $500,000 in salary.

The professor is confusing and uses lectures from the 1990s. He jumps throughout the lectures and it is very hard to follow. The average on the midterm and final was around a 50 on each. I ended up with a B with average grades. If you can, get a tutor. Don't use the book - it's written by the prof and isn't useful. YouTube tutorials can help.

Graded on few things. The class has videos that show him using Windows XP.  The lectures only have one example on how to do a problem and it's always the most complex problem. Normally Professors will work up to the hard problem, maybe give 2 or 3 easier examples and then add complexity along the way.

The lectures are hard to follow, he jumps around and refers back to previous slides and expects you to remember what was on them. He wrote the text book which is also hard to follow. Heavy group projects for case studies, expect about 16 to 20 hour commitment per week. Highly recommend getting a tutor if you're in this class.

He is such a hard teacher, you don't know what grade you will get until after the final. Grades are determined how you compare to your peers. The mean on the final was 50% and midterm percentage was about the same. The only grades are the cases and exams. Cases were essentially free points, we made errors and got 100% on them but this doesn't help

Shows you 1 example & then gives you homework/test problems that have the same parts; but you have to infer how to do it from his example that doesn't fully show all the moving pieces. If you're not already familiar with some of these subjects, be prepared to be confused & work extra hard to have an idea how to do the work.

Jeff is a very nice guy but he is not a very good professor. There are no structure to his lectures and he grades you based off of 7 assignments and 3 exams, but then doesn't input grades until the very end. Good news is that everyone passes this class no matter what. He puts a fat curve at the end. Average scores for his exams were in the mid 50s

Jeff is such a nice guy and Im pretty sure this is his first year back teaching undergrads, which is probably why he assumes were all grad students and know exactly whats going on at all times. His teaching style is horrible - theres hardly any structure to his lectures. Study for tests using the old ones he provides and teach yourself.

This class was pretty chill, there is a good mix of quizzes, tests, and one big group project. The quizzes and tests are not difficult and they are online and open-note. I didn't really learn anything that new and exciting in this class, but it is a solid choice for an elective.

This class was so easy you only have to go to 11 to get full participation points and if you studied for four hours the night before each test you will get an A in the class. The homeworks are through mindtap and if you do them with a group of people you can finish in less than 20 minutes. A lot of the concepts are common sense.
Great teacher
He was one of the best professors I've had!! He was very respective in class and tried his best to help us get the highest grade possible. He offered LOTS of extra credit so make sure you use them!

This class is very interesting and relevant even if youre not a management major. Definitely read the chapters before class/ for the test because the exact wording will be on the test. MindTap are easy 5 points dont miss. Pay attention to in class activities/videos bc theyll be referenced on exams. Meet with preceptors for project paper criteria
He is interesting in lectures and be sure to ask questions if you have them. Exams are straight forward and reliable
His tests are based off of the lectures and he won't post the slides with the blanks filled in! So make sure you go to eery single class. If you do you can defintely get an A in the class!
Great Professor! Cares a lot about his students and is very passionate about teaching. Offers lots of extra credit gives you detailed study guides for exams.

Dr Schilke is kind, and genuinely cares about his students. I recommend doing the extra credit experiments that are offered during the beginning of the semester. He orders his lectures pleasantly so you can keep tabs on what you're going to accomplish in class that day. You do get participation for showing up to class.
The nicest professor at Eller! His class is definitely the easiest of all the first semester eller classes- easy A.
You have to work for the grade but Dr. Shilke gives you all the tools necessary.

Dr. Schilke is a great lecturer who is funny and keeps class interesting! Group work is necessary, but that's business school for ya. Actually try on MindTap, it ends up being a good chunk of your grade. Lots of extra credit. Easy to do well, just put in the work (which isn't too much, like 1 hour a week outside of class).
He's such a caring and hard-working professor. He makes lessons interactive and easy to understand and his tests are reasonable as long as you study. 110% recommend taking his class!

He is a really nice guy but he was very hard to reach outside of class. He tells students to email their preceptors and not to bother him. He also intentionally scheduled his office hours during our MKTG361 class. He has a very unclear grading criteria. Interesting and easy material. Be sure to study for exams using his study guide

Schilke is a breath of fresh air from most other professors. While not perfect, it is easy to surmise what a typical day in his class is comprised of: lecture and in class activity. His zero tolerance for laptops and apple fan-boy syndrome however is a bit antiquated, and he seems uncomfortable doing in-class activities with large class absenses.

Schilke is amazing! He is always excited to be there and keeps class interesting with in class activities. His exams are also very straight forward. Also, the exams exactly follow the study guides he provides. Overall, great class!!

He has a lot of group work. You end up with two separate groups in the class, which is just a mess because the second group starts a month before Finals. The class itself is very dull, however, there are a lot of attendance activities. Do the MindTap in groups of 3 to 4 people because they're really difficult on your own.

Schilke's one of my favorite professors so far. I thought his class would be dull but he makes it interesting and tries to keep the class engaged with activities. It's a shame a lot of students don't feel the need to show up to his class. His tests are a breeze if you actually fill out the powerpoint slide notes and just review those.

Schilke is by far my favorite professor of first semester Eller. Compared to what I've read from 2015/16 he has made this class far more efficient and exciting. The in-class activities are a little cheesy but what he teaches in class almost mimics the tests, read the chapters through once and you'll know the material. I wish he taught more classes.

Prepared to teach yourself ,you will have assignments on chapters you have yet to cover in class. The book is useless. Uncovered chapters count towards your grade. How can you be expected to know what you havent been taught yet? Mom and Dad dont tell you to drive them home until they teach you how to drive.
I was not a big fan of management before but 310A was alright. All the different activities were a nice change from my other classes.
One of the best lectures I ever had. I got many new ideas. This class make you ask big questions.

I took Prof. Schilke's management class in the spring of 2017 and I really enjoyed it. The power point slides are an awesome resource, and he offers many activities that helped me understand the concepts much better.

Oliver is a cool guy and approaches lecture different than other Eller professors. He tries to mix things up and not just lecture on material for an hour. In class assignments happened every day for my semester so be aware of that. The preceptors were super helpful especially with the group projects. Use the provided study guide outline for tests

Took Mgmt 310A with Schilke in 2016. Not sure what kind of experience the other people have had with him, but I really enjoyed his class. He genuinely cares about getting students prepared for their jobs, is very approachable, and overall a very nice guy. I liked the variety of assignments and how the lecture has lots of different elements to it.

I enjoyed the fact that the tests are fair and balanced, with tests representing only 34% of the grade. I also enjoy the fact that he offer's two separate extra credit opportunities, for a total of 20 points, or 2.8% of the grade. He assigns in class assignments on Monday's, so don't skip Monday classes. Wednesday's, you can skip (due to cases).

Oliver is a really nice quirky guy who tries his best to engage his students. Unfortunately, that doesn't happen since everyone is on their phones the entire class. I sorta feel bad because nobody listens to him. Nobody goes to the Wednesday classes because of presentations. Just know definitions for his tests and you'll be fine. Super easy class

Schilke is literally the worst professor at Eller. I dont question his credibility, but hes a horrible instructor. Reads powerpoints every monday and case presentations on wednesday and thats the standard. Cumulative final that he doesnt give a study guide for and everyone fails. Make sure your grade is high before you go into the final

There is only one assigned group presentation on a case. With almost 30 groups in the class, presentations are every weds.  His lectures are pretty pointless as he pretty much reads from his slides. Most people do not pay attention in his class and only attend to sign in on the sign in sheet and then leave. Only one cumulative final on 20 chapters

This class is the worst class I have ever taken in my college career. No one goes to class, the structure is awful, actually there is no structure. One test at the end of the semester, no guidance for that test. Barely any assigned work, that that is assigned is barely even graded. THIS CLASS TAUGHT ME NOTHING, WHAT A WASTE.

Professor Slavin is amazing. His class is quite simple. If you meet the criteria on the assignments, you will get full points. His class consists of DBs, not a lot of exams, and essays. He gives you 2 weeks to finish each module. His class isn't hard, you just got to meet his criteria and you should be good. He gives good constructive feedback.

So great. Each week is a discussion post and a 2-3 page reflection. They are very low stakes but also very educational because he gives high grades with clear, constructive, and thorough feedback. I'm not even a criminal justice major but I have so much appreciation for the field because of him.

Juvenile Justice in the US was not my first Juvenile Justice class but it was the first class I took with Professor Slavin. He is an amazing professor. He is so inspirational. His Juvenile Justice class has made me want to work in Juvenile Justice and I am actively seeking a career in that area of the courts. I wish all professors were this awesome

This professor is so amazing. Grades very quickly and always provides specific feedback and lots of positive reinforcement. Professor Slavin is great at communicating so there is no guessing on what is expected on assignments. I learned so much in his classes. He is one of my all-time favorite professors!

Professor Silverberg is an amazing professor!!! She teaches a smaller lecture class which I recommend you take because it's very hands-on and way more valuable for learning the course material in the class. The class is one of the hardest core class you need to take, so be prepared to have to study a lot.

Professor Silverberg is a standout in the Accounting department. She makes ACCT 200 feel engaging and manageable. She conveys material with absolute clarity, ensuring you aren't just memorizing formulas but actually understanding the 'why' behind the numbers.  Highly recommend if you want a solid foundation in accounting!

Sherri is one of the best, most caring professors at U of A. She was very knowledgeable about accounting and gives lots of resources to succeed in the class. She wants every student to do good in this class and as long as you put in the work you will do just fine.

She's super sweet! Fast talker during her lectures but gives so many resources to help study for exams, which the bigger sections don't get as much. She's very willing to help and does weekly zoom review sessions. Will reach out if you miss class to give you the slides if needed, but mandatory attendance.

Genuinely one of my favorite professors. She really cares about ensuring that you pass. She offers many resources, like extra Zoom practice sessions and office hours, which I recommend attending to do well in the class. The only thing is, if you take her small section, she does care about you being on time and not being late to her class.
Great professor. So sweet, and actually cares about your learning. The class is difficult, but she makes it very manageable.

Sherri was an amazing professor for ACCT200. Any bad reviews are probably because students didn't take advantage of all the resources or help that the class provided. The lectures weren't the most interesting but that's not because of the professor, just the style of the class. Study outside of class and you will get a good grade.

She is a great teacher. Very helpful and understanding if you just ask her or explain your situation. The lectures go by quick which can make it hard to comprehend things, the class has about 150 students but there is a discussion once a week that has about 25 students. There are lots of resources if you use them but the class is hard!

I really liked Sherri. She was super nice & understanding. She cares about her students & if you need help, she is always willing to make time. I didn't love her lectures just because she moved so fast, but also that was because we had limited time, so I understand. Overall, super nice professor, just the lectures were hard because they were fast.

She is the absolute best. So sweet, willing to work with you, and will help you in any way she can to make sure you pass. She's the reason I will be majoring in acct. You NEED to study to understand the material for the test and practice the material. I failed my first test and got above 80s for the remaining 3.
Good teacher and will help you if you talk to her. Has plenty of availabilities for extra help. Teaches the material a little fast but other than that she's great.

Sherri is a wonderful and sweet professor! Yes, the course has a lot of exams and there is a lot of information on it but it is an accounting course! She gives out a lot of extra help through labs, the extra practices, and being super accessible outside of class. She's a funny professor and I suggest you strike up a conversation & get to know her!
Most of the bad reviews are from COVID, she is really nice in person.

Sherri was the nicest professor I've ever had at the UA! She was incredibly kind, helpful, and always accessible. Even now after the class ended, I still stay in touch. The class was done very well and I always felt supported. Four exams you'll need to prep for, but study guides and dedicated review days are sufficient and helpful. Take this class!

Professor Silverberg was an amazing teacher and really helped me succeed in this class. I struggled in the beginning of the year, but she was always quick to reach out to me and offer ways to help. I went from receiving a 68% on the first exam to an 100% on my final due to her above-and-beyond commitment to helping us understand the course content.

She is a wonderful lecturer. It was an even better experience having her in a small section. 

Make sure to review the slides and practice problems before exams. Do not blindly read textbook chapters. Honestly, it is best not to unless you're looking up a definition, but even then, just stick to her lecture slides and your practice problems.

sherri is an amazing professor, I took her class this fall and it was a very easy class. I would say to read the textbook chapters as well as the slides and take notes before class, she can go over the slides pretty quickly. sherri gave out tons of extra practice and did plenty of exam reviews on each chapter. she also has Sunday reviews on zoom.
Take this class with Sherri. You won't regret it!

I attended a small class she hosted last semester and had a good experience. Sherri brings extra online review every Sunday evening, and you'll have plenty of material to prepare for the exam, which I guarantee will help you do well. Shirley will give you four chances to drop the lowest-scoring test, so the final exam is optional.

Sherri is definitely the best teacher you can have for ACCT 200. She's super sweet and cares for all of her students! Take notes on the lecture slides before class because she can go through the slides a little fast. Due to the small class size, she gives individual feedback on chapter assignments which is nice

She is the absolute best. She is so sweet and kind and it is obvious that she really cares about her students. During class, she can go a little fast but she will make sure that you understand the material through practice. She is very accessible outside of class and is very attentive. She is an amazing professor and I would take this class again.
Yes, accounting 200 is a more difficult class but Sherri does everything she can to make it easier. She genuinely cares about her students so if you get the chance to take her you should!

Sherri is one of the kindest instructors I've ever had. Very supportive and encouraging as well as enthusiastic about the material which genuinely does help. As for the material, it will be difficult if you don't put the effort in to practice and understand it. Accounting can be like learning a language and new way of thinking.

Sherri is one of the nicest professors, she cares about your success in her class and is always willing to help. Her class isn't easy and people who don't pass blame her for it, but it's truly not her fault.

She is a very nice and understanding professor, if you come to her with a problem that you are struggling. The exam prep was very helpful in guiding my study habits. They do take occasional attendance, but you are allowed to miss three classes. The optional final exam allows you to replace a low score non-cumulative exam, which I thought was nice.
Super sweet and very passionate about accounting.

Sherri did a great job in teaching the class but accounting itself is just hard. On the other hand she does give out plenty of homework but has the accounting lab mandatory. The accounting lab helped out a lot and she is very understanding as a teacher.

Sherri is the absolute BEST! She knows your name, is super responsive and accessible outside of class, and goes out of her way to help you succeed. She provides so much extra practice, office hours, and extra credit opportunities and is very knowledgeable in her topic. The absolute sweetest professor I've had, definitely take her class!!

I do not understand why Sherri does not have a higher rating. She is such a sweetheart and responds to emails quickly. She has plenty of office hours where she will help you understand anything you are missing. The class is hard, but it is Accounting and is not supposed to be easy. Easily my favorite pre-business teacher.

The professor is very knowledgeable about the course content and is always willing to answer questions and present information in different ways if a student is struggling. The class is mainly in-class lectures with occasional in-class practice problems and weekly online extra practice problems.

She is a wonderful instructor. I took one of her smaller ACCT200 sections, and I was able to receive an A through completing the extra credit and understanding the practice problems she goes over. I recommend focusing on practice problems over reading that long textbook. Boo.

I had Sherri Fall 2022 in a small lecture class for ACCT 200. She is amazing!! She WANTS students to ask her questions, and she has a Sunday Zoom for her classes to go over lecture material. She's very understanding and cares about her students. She also gives out so many resources to help students. I'm taking ACCT 210 with her next semester!

I don't understand why Sherri doesn't have good reviews because she is the best teacher that I've had. Choose her class! She always responded to my emails with a lot of thought and was always available to help. Managerial Accounting is hard itself and you will have to put a lot of time into studying but Sherri was always there to help and explain:)

Not sure where the sentiment in other reviews come from. Sherri is a great teacher who will bend over backwards for you if you demonstrate an earnest effort. This course is generally difficult, but Sherri goes to many lengths to ensure you know the content. She earns a 4 and not a 5 because she does breeze through slides like others have said.

An overall terrible professor. She raced through the slides, so people are lost. I put in long hours to study, but in contrast, Sherri only does the bare minimum. She rudely turned me down when I asked for HW help & sent me a nasty, disrespectful email. I also asked her to fix a D2L mistake, but she made an excuse b/c she's too lazy to do her job.

Silverberg made me fall in love with accounting. Yes lectures can be a bit hard to get through at 2pm but that's any 2pm class. Getting an A is easy if you review the lecture slides and chapter before class then utilize all the practice sheets she provides. Exams are easy as long as you attended lectures and reviewed the chapters.

Sherri is really not a great lecturer, it is hard to stay engaged. It definitely helped to watch recorded lectures she posts on D2L, and go through the slides on your own. She does random attendance, though it isn't worth much of your grade. Otherwise the course is manageable and interesting, she does give extra credit.

Most rude person you will ever meet. Her lectures are so boring that you will wanna fall asleep and she stutters every 5 seconds you can barely understand her. Most teachers will help you out if your close to getting that next letter grade, she will absolutely not. She does not care. She should be more giving and actually recognize that she sucks!

Worst teacher ever. She acts like she cares about you but she doesnt. Because of her I am now changing my major. Dont think you will learn much, shes an old lady, but she teaches the course work so fast that you will need to do 2 hours of extra work to actually know whats going on. If you need an extra point to pass she WILL NOT give it to you!

This professor is a wonderful teacher who truly wanted to see everyone succeed. She gave multiple opportunities for extra credit and always provided quick feedback with lots of TA help as well. Study the problems she puts up and do the homework and quizzes, it's an easy A
very helpful teacher and wants her students to succeed. Office hours are very helpful. definitely a hard class but if you have questions ask or you will be lost.

Professor Silverberg is a great professor for this course. She provides numerous opportunities outside of class to go over material with you if you dont understand it. Its obvious she really cares about her students passing, if you put in effort you will easily pass this course.

I failed ACCT 210 so Sherri S. is my second ACCT 210 teacher and she is the best Accounting Professor out of the other two I had. The class is extremely difficult and you will have to put most of your time and effort in this course. Sherri is good at explaining and answering questions. She goes by the slides fast so take notes beforehand.
She is a good professor. She is very passionate about accounting and that makes it fun to learn from here. I also felt like she was more than willing to give students all the help they needed.

Most unhelpful, uncaring professor. She sent me multiple rude emails and brushed my concerns under the rug. I also tried make an appointment with her because I had class during her office hours, but she replied that she didn't want to meet! - She's so lazy. (Isn't it her job to help students?!)

Her ratings are definitely based on the class and not her as a professor. The class is definitely challenging and a good amount of work but she really lets you know all the resources available for extra help and she's super nice when you ask questions. I think the rating is definitely a little unfair because she really does push you to succeed.

I have no idea what people are talking about... honestly. Might be one of the nicest professors I have ever met. The reviews on here are total slander! Shes given every single opportunity for students to ask questions, go over things, etc.

Her lectures were boring and not helpful as it was just reading off slides and she moved very quick so it's easy to fall behind if she's showing examples. Office hours helped me a lot, but if you don't understand something in the beginning units then you won't pass the class. The final is over all units over the semester so it was very challenging.

She is a nice professor and wants her students to do well, but accounting is by far the hardest class I've ever taken. She was vocal about office hours which helped me a lot, but during the lectures I found myself extremely lost as she didn't go into much detail. It was very boring and just reading off slides. Also, the final is cumulative.

Shes uncaring and an uncommanding public speaker. Belittles students. I asked her to meet for homework help but she responded that she wanted to go home; shes so lazy! I also asked her to fix a mistake in D2L and she sent me a passive aggressive email about how she is on vacation - Bruh, its her job as a professor to fix grading mistakes.

Sherri was one of the best teachers I have had at the University of Arizona. Her lectures are very detailed and she explains accounting so well to make it easy to understand. Yes the class has some work, but if you just commit yourself to two days a week (lecture and homework days) you will be fine. She is a sweet and caring person.

I had this women for online classes this year because of covid-19. I thought I would do good, but the first test I totally failed. If you get her for ACCT250 just make sure you are able to access outside resources because learning online for this class was very difficult and she was not that helpful personally. Her tests are very important!!

Lazy professor & terrible public speaker! - She doesn't command the room or have a strong presence. Races through slides so students cannot take thorough notes. Exams are harder than study guides and more like in-class practice probs. She also made condescending remarks to me because she wanted to be lazy instead of do her job helping students!

The material in this class itself is really not hard, you just need to practice it thoroughly and do LOTS of reading. Sherri is a pretty good teacher, lecture isn't all that helpful if you don't read the book beforehand. Tests are a huge portion of your grade.

Accounting 200 is a hard class and Sherri is not forgiving. The lectures are boring & hard to listen to. Professor SIlverberg is robotic in her lectures and it is easy to let your eyes glaze over. The tests are hard and have a big impact on your grade. Be prepared to work you ass off and study a lot.

Bro she talks so fast and moves on from one thing straight into the next. Clearly knowledgable and nice professor, but literally can't keep up to take notes, it's almost comical at times. Can't take another course with her purely for this reason.

I learned a lot in this class but most importantly during COVID 19 Sherri was one of my more understanding professors and helped a lot during the transition process, and did things for the best of the students. The content can be really hard as time so I HIGHLY recommend taking a lab! The labs were extremely helpful and it was easy to ask questions

I don't know why everyone says ACCT 200 is so hard, this was one of the easiest classes I have taken at UofA. Her lectures are very boring, which makes it hard to follow along and stay awake. Even so, there's no need to worry; as long as you do the homework problems and a tiny bit of studying you should get an A no problem.

This has been one of the hardest classes I've taken so far at UofA. She teaches extremely fast and it can definitely be hard to keep up. It's good to print slides prior coming to class to fill in the blanks during the lecture. The points are very heavy towards your final grade and need to basically ace them to get an A or good grade.

One of the worst teachers out there in the U of A she makes the exams so hard she rushes through the lecture she dosen't care about students and she just wants to make your life tough. Take the class with Rob Stussie he is was better than her.
Ms. Silverberg is fantastic. She is super responsive to answering questions, and her enthusiasm for the material is contagious. WIsh I could take ACCT210 with her.

Sherri is a nice lady but this class was not a great experience. It seems like she is always racing through the lectures because there are wayyy too many slides. She passes out practice problems but does not go over them. The study guides are just recycled versions of practice problems that do not offer any new insight whatsoever.

Sherri is an average professor, she teaches from the book exactly, which is rather boring and uninspiring. I started this semester with a 4.0 and thanks to this course my 4.0 is gone. She makes you memorize every formula for each test and she gives out practice problems for each chapter, but does not go over them with you in class.

One of the worst classes I've ever taken. She goes very quickly in class and does not know how to effectively teach material. You'll have to teach yourself because you won't learn a thing by going to class. Tests are a lot harder than in-class examples. Her study guides are vague and do not prepare you for what will be on the test.

Also, she graded the exams and then proceeded to add our final grade to our permanent record so that we, as a class, had no time to ask questions or even see our final exam. I have taken courses this semester where I learned useful topics and information, but NOT IN THIS COURSE. Do yourself a favor and do NOT take this class with her. -Ok Im done

Not one concept is taught using excel or any accounting software its just information and formulas that you will forget after this class. It should be setup from a conceptual perspective with more vocabulary and concepts, and less word problems that dont use most of the numbers in the question and try to TRICK students.

Exams make up 80% of your grade. If you have one or two bad days, your are toast. I received a 100% on clicker questions and homework, but still received a C in the class. There are so many things I would change, but it doesnt matter because this class is OBSOLETE anyways. No one does paper accounting, this isn't 1960s anymore folks.

First of all, let me begin by saying that I am an A student. I began this class with a 4.0 gpa. This semester has been exceptionally stressful and tremendously frustrating due to the actions and teaching style of Sherri Silverberg. I am not afraid to admit that I received a C, and I wanted to let everyone who sees this website to understand why...

Sherri was a really good professor for this class.
