The lectures are really long and there is a lot of information to understand but she is super accessible and super willing to help with any questions about anything. She is a super nice teacher and lays out everything that will be on the exams which is super nice so you know what to practice.

While the material for the class is hard and yes sure the prof is kind of boring, she is still very nice. Even better she is accessible outside of class and that makes it so she can help you understand the material better.

Horrible teacher. She is confused half of the time she is teaching. You basically teach yourself in this class. You probably wont understand what she is saying until you get the practice problems, and you just reflect off the powerpoints. Save the trouble and choose someone else for Accounting 210.

I didn't understand a work in the class. She is confused all the time. papers are very tough. The slides are so hard to understand. It's one of the classes you have to attend but learn the content yourself. Dont take her she makes the class harder than it is.

She basically teaches what the textbook says (even examples are the same). She does not command the room, and is not very engaging. Uses acronyms and clickers way too much. And she makes several things due at once.

This class isn't that hard. Yes, there are a lot of moving parts in acct, but math is not difficult--it isn't even math! Print lecture slides, go to class (I can't emphasize it enough) & pay attention w/o having to take notes. When studying for exam(s), do ungraded problems on Wiley+ and do extra problem assignments and practice test(s) on D2L.

Sherri is one of the best teachers I have ever had. She inspired me to change my major to accounting. I think that most people exaggerate how hard this class is. If you put in effort and go to class you will do fine. Sherri really cares about her students and wants you to do the very best you can. She is also extremely nice and makes class fun!

This class is as hard as it sounds. I thought it would be impossible but if you go to the lectures, see her at office hours and study study study!!!!!! you can do it! Lectures are usually boring and hard to understand because she goes so fast but she takes attendance and literally every point counts. She truly cares about her students though!

Sherri is the best. This class is hard but not impossible like some of the other reviews here say. All you have to do is study, go to class, and go to office hours where she'll help you with whatever you need. She truly cares and is rooting for all of her students. This class requires a lot of time/hard work but it doable. I am not good at math!

Sherri is the most caring teacher ever. She post everything online and gives you tons of extra problems to practice. The lectures are a little dry, but it isn't hard to teach yourself. She always emails back and is very encouraging. Just be prepared to put in some study hours or you won't pass.

Sherri is one of the best professors I've ever had. She emails you back within the same day, has many office hour times as well as weekly review sessions and even has office hours on the weekends before exams. What professor would do that? This course is not easy but if you pay attention in class, go to office hours, and study you'll be fine.

Sherri is a great professor, but has become super strict about attendance. She is very knowledgeable and has good intentions, but gets tripped up a lot in lecture. Super accessible outside of class and emails back same day within hours. Not my favorite accounting professor I have had.

Multiple choice tests only with plenty of trick questions. 80% of your grade is based off 3 tests, if that isn't biased grading I don't know what is. Taking this class over summer with this professor is a bigger mistake than taking her during a regular semester, you're literally just asking for trouble.

This class is a mission. Sherri is like this nice old lady, but her tests are rough and so is teachin. I went to office hours and did all the problems offered, and barely made a B on the first test. Did all the same for the second test and got a 54. Her class averages are anywhere fro a 55-65 on exams, it's pretty pathetic. The free tutoring sucks.

Kind person but very tough grader. The course is lecture heavy and she goes very fast and writes everything in illegible handwriting. Given the opportunity I suggest you take this class with a different professor.

Sherri Silverberg is an outstanding professor. She is easily accessible via email, which is a huge plus. She is more than willing to help with any problem you have, and is one of the most encouraging professors I've ever had. If you need to take accounting, this professor will explain the most difficult concepts to you with ease. Highly recommend!

her teaching is absolutely terrible. You basically have to teach yourself cause if you don't get something she'll explain it maybe one extra time and then move on. Almost the whole class is confused 24/7. She gives you "opportunities for help" but when you go to accounting tutoring it's almost completely packed with act 210 students and 2 tutors.

Sherri Is super nice, but ACCT 250 is a really hard class. Almost every student is completely lost and she doesn't curve exams even when the class average for exams are low 60%. She tries to be super helpful, but you are going to be teaching yourself all semester or at least trying to.

Sherri is a good teacher. 250 is not easy, you have to put work in, and she knows it. She offers SO much help you'd be crazy not to use it. She answers email questions fast and seems to really care that you are learning. Happy to answers questions in class. You dont have to come, but miss class at your own risk. Super nice and sweet.

Sherri is really sweet and willing to help as much as she can! She does her best to make a really tough class as simple as possible! Go to her office hours, that's what makes the difference! Print out the slides and pay attention in class.

Sherri was honestly one of the best teachers I have had at the U of A. She is so kind and extremely willing to meet with you to answer any of your questions. I understand the class is challenging but if you keep up with it, it is possible to get an A. Do the pre-lecture quizzes and the activities before class. Print out her slides for class too.

Sherri teaches a class that is going to be demanding &hard because it is the nature of the material. However, she has a willingness to help you anytime. She has been available on weekends &will meet you at a time that is convenient for you if her office hours don't work for you. She is so patient &kind and will do anything in her power to help you.

Sherri is hands down the most caring and invested professor I've had at the U of A. Yes, she may go quickly during lecture but she'll slow down if you ask. She is a new professor and just has a lot to learn. She is willing to go out of her way for any student and will do anything to make sure you succeed in the class.

She sure is an accountant, but SHOULD NOT be teaching and INTRODUCTORY level accounting class designed for business MINORS. She goes extremely fast, hand writes the essential things you need to know with handwriting that you cannot read, and acts as if you already know the basics of accounting. DOES NOT prepare you for exams. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS.

If you go to the lecture, pay 100% attention to it, because when she gives examples, there might be numbers suddenly comes out from nowhere. ***BUY/RENT THE TEXTBOOK!!! ***That's the MOST IMPORTANT thing you'll need for this class. You might have 90% chance end up teaching yourself throughout the semester.

You should buy the textbook and be prepared to teach yourself for the entire semester. She is willing to help. But willing to doesn't equal able to. She goes through many examples during classes. If you really enjoy learning from lectures, do not take her class.

Textbook is only a must because you will end up having to teach yourself if accounting is new to you. She is kind and is def certified in solving accounting problems really fast. But many of us are lost in class. The material is beneficial to know in "the real world" it just sucks that lectures are not very effective or helpful

Is she certified in Accounting? Yes. Does that mean she should be teaching it? No. She has proven she knows the tasks of an accountant but she is not much of a teacher/ educator. She jumps right into material as if it wasn't an introductory course. She works through examples quickly without explaining or teaching us. "Limited Term Adjunct Lecturer"

I hated this class because he didn't teach well at all. We were not allowed to have ANY technology in the classroom and he goes through the slides so fast you can't even take notes. His exams set me up to fail because the content he would teach would not match the exams. This class ruined my GPA and was by far the worst course I took that semester.

He's a tough grader but has great lectures. The exams can be a little tough with the amount of info and terms but he curves most of them. Get a lot of points from in class activities. Just do all the HW and read the textbook bc the info is easier to learn from there than in lectures. The group project is graded hard but his class isn't that bad.

I was a preceptor and we were told to grade harshly. We were given no instructions on what to look for until AFTER papers were submitted, so meeting with students was useless bc we never had the entire grading criteria. He would also lower scores and then blame preceptors for it. I promise none of us wanted ppl to have bad grades!

Jerel is the epitome of a teacher on a power trip. He throws a temper tantrum when students have laptops out in class and gives tests that are so wordy for no reason. Jerel is very proud of his class's difficulty and offers minimal extra credit. I don't know anyone who enjoyed his class or teaching style. Hopefully, someone new will teach thisclass

I got a high B in the class. But I got above a 90% on every exam, and every project I completed. The fact I got an A on every major test or assignment but still only got a B in the class tells you everything you need to know about this professor. I had to teach myself this class. He likes to teach 'management theory' from strictly the book.

Here's the reality: Slaughter has high expectations of his students, so no easy A's. That's how it is so if you want to do well, then this is what you do. Go to every class, pay attention, use the textbook, and use his exam study guides. Being a good student will pay off a lot in this class, but being a bad one will make this class miserable.

THANK GOD Slaughter will not be teaching this course next semester. Slaughter made this class difficult for no reason. Confusing test questions & unhelpful preceptors due to his ridiculous rules. I got a 79.5% in the class & OF COURSE, Slaughter does not round up. Avoid taking any class of his. It seems he doesn't want his students to succeed.

I attended almost every class and studied for the exams. I managed to get worse grades despite trying harder. The exams are so frustrating. Although he curved the exams, the entire class struggled nearly the whole semester. I heard nothing but bad rumors about him. I tried hard not to believe them but it's completely true.

Difficult professor, point system made it hard. He does look for feedback and implements valid suggestions which I respect. Every point counts, makes it hard to get an A in the class. Good topics, just do not think the way the course is structured is beneficial or adequate. He mostly has a script identical to the textbook so do the reading.

Slaughter is the worst professor in your first semester of upper division. The exams are extremely difficult and worded so confusingly that it is extremely hard to do well on them. The preceptor for my team was not only rude but did not understand how to grade our projects properly and gave us little to no feedback.

Three exams make up 55% of the grade. Questions are worded terribly. Ex. There will be T or F questions containing two sentences where the first sentence is false and the second sentence is true. He comes into class the next day smiling about how badly the class did on the exams, then will tell you about EC opportunities worth half an exam question

One of the worst lecturers I've ever had. I was thoroughly disappointed with this class considering how much we pay to be there. He has zero personality and is very rude. He wants his students to be engaged in class but his teaching style does not encourage creativity. This class is also unnecessarily challenging.

Slaughter's Tests are a joke, he makes them way too hard to try and prove that he is better than you. Every test got curved because he does not prepare his students. He publicly calls out his students in class and has a no technology policy which is ridiculous for a course that is heavy on definitions and other written note type of questions.

The teacher does not have clear grading criteria whatsoever. The preceptors that are meant to give guidance will give you a thumbs up regardless of the quality of your work and give you false hope, and then actually grade the papers themselves and give you a C. The professor is also extremely pretentious and will publicly shame you in class.

This was the most difficult class I have taken so far at the UofA, mostly because of the very difficult tests and grading criteria for the group projects. Some advice: you NEED to push your preceptor to give you good feedback on the projects. Otherwise, they'll do the bare minimum which could screw you over in the end. Good luck.

If he didn't have unannounced in-class activities, no one would show up. He purposely puts weird wording on his exam questions to prove that his exams are difficult when they really shouldn't be. Almost half of the class either failed or got a D on the final. Not sure why Eller thinks we need a class on how to behave at work.

Worst teacher I have ever had, worst grade I have ever received. He does not care about his students at all, his tests rarely cover what was actually discussed in class. I've heard preceptors are instructed to grade super hard. I hope that the University gives this class to a new professor because he makes me wish I did not attend Eller. A shame.

Good class. You honestly get what you put in. My group did great on the project and I got A's on every exam. People are bitter about that fact that the class wasn't an easy A. Hard work pays off, but very few are actually willing to work for it. Most people complain about the test and project. They're easy if you are good at following instructions.
My first B at the U of A. Slaughter tries really hard to punish students who miss class and grades his projects harshly. General lack of a human element from him.

Content of class is relatively easy. Lectures are boring and professor doesn't make them less so. Pretty boring guy and does not really teach about management, more so psychology and diversity/race. Not an easy A but one of my easier classes for the semester. Did not feel like a business class and I don't feel like I learned anything.

Very kind. I got high As on every exam and Im not a mgmt major. If you want an A, READ THE BOOK, watch the videos, study the ppt. Make sure to not memorize the definition. Think of examples and applications of each word and I promise youll be fine. Theres also lots of extra credit. If you ever took AP Psychology, its very similar to that.

As a MGMT major, Slaughter made me dislike the subject. He focuses heavily on psychology and with online learning, his lecturing can get very dry. His tests are hard if you're not an expert on the material, and after the second test (when a third of the class failed it), he didn't do much. He is really nice, but online learning is just not for him.

I enjoyed Professor Slaughters class. While the lectures can get a little boring at times (everything is of course online), you can tell he is passionate about the subject and wants the students to succeed. People complain about the tests being hard, but they really werent if you read the book, paid attention in class, and used his study guide.
He is caring in person, but online covid it didn't translate that way. His test are hard. lots of extra credit tho, be team leader. lots of content. wish the tests weren't so hard.

In-class work is easy. Exams as well. Homeworks on the other hand are frustrating. They involve frustrating Excel sections on McGraw Hill that require you to input formulas in an extremely specific way. Even Songur is unhelpful at times in trying to help you solve them.
Great introduction to finance. Professor Songuer is well-organized and provides good feedback.  Recommended!

A somewhat challenging class that he makes way too easy. He provides every material you could ever want or need available for the homework and exams. While his lectures are painfully dry, you don't really need to fully engage as long as you preread the textbook chapter hes covering that day.
Dr. Songur is an amazing professor who just wants all his students to do well. He makes his expectations known from the start
Easy A if you apply yourself. Hard to understand and handwriting is ineligble which makes it difficult to learn.
Overall a great class. Take Dr. Songur for BNAD 304!
I really enjoyed this class. The professor is well organized.

Dr. Songur wants his students to do well and allows for students to work at their own pace. Having so many other deadlines and conflicts, it was very advantageous to have the flexibility to work ahead when permitted in his class. He also provides detailed feedback and is very helpful in tying the concepts to actual financial situations and problems

He was a life savior. Attendance and participation matters so you need to go to class. But if you do it's an easy A. Most relatable and coolest professor in Eller. I wish there was more than 5 stars to give for him.

This guy is the goat. Your free A for your first semester of Eller. I think he knows that people who take his class are not finance majors and that his class is mandatory. He probably cares less about the class than we did. Do the assignments, exams, and show up for attendance. The online version taught by another professor was stupid hard.

Go to class and you will get an A, seriously. Your saving grace for first semester. The easiest class you will ever take, and the guy is funny. The only thing that is hard about his class is literally just going to class.
Absolutely love this guy, though truthfully I didn't learn anything in the class
I LOVE THIS GUY. He is so funny and makes the class fun. Gives you a billion opportunities to get an A and you need to be trying hard in order to not get an A tbh. LOVE THIS GUY ONCE AGAIN
The absolute GOAT of first semester Eller. Only one homework assignment and TONS of extra credit. You'd have to try really hard to not get an A in this class.

Your saving grace for first-semester Eller. Crazy amounts of extra credit. Sign up for the stock simulation at the beginning of the semester and do the grade boosters, and you'll get an A without issue.
I really liked the FIN 413 with Dr. Songur. I have developed my excel and finance skills. I highly recommend it.
great class an A is very achievable and he works well with students

Professor Songur was by far the best professor I have taken in Eller. He makes his class easy to pass as long as you do the homework and exams! Its easy to get an A with all the extra credit he provides. I highly recommend.
He's amazing, great at teaching, and overall a great class. Take Songur for Fin360, you definitely won't regret it.

Take this class!!! The professor is very clear on grading and offers so many extra credit opportunities to boost your grade. I was in the three week online course which consisted of a discussion post/reply each week, one quiz each week, an exam each week, and a class reflection at the end of the course. Overall, very fast pace but so worth it.
Best online class experience. The assignments are clear. You learn a lot of useful information.

I had this class in the 2 hour and 45 minute block, so the lectures were brutal to get through. However, he was very knowledgeable, and you can skip some classes if you do not want to go on any particular day. Only a few homework assignments and the tests are not difficult. Excellent professor.

Dr. Songur's class was EASILY one of my favorites during my first semester of Eller. Finance can be a difficult subject to learn, but he goes to great lengths to ensure every student understands the material. He offers a lot of opportunities to boost your grade. It is very clear that he cares for students' well-beings.
Hilmi was a good professor who cared about his students. He also makes it very easy to get an A in this class. There is a lot of extra credit and the tests are very straight forward.

He is a great professor and definitely shows that he cares about how you do in his class. The topic itself is difficult, but he does his best to make sure you understand what he's teaching. He has mini "quizzes" during class and if you don't understand he'll explain. He also has tons of EC which is very helpful.

I absolutely loved this class. He uses top hat, so if you arent feeling well or have to miss a class, someone can send you the code and you wont lose any participation points! His tests are extremely straight forward and also offers 10% extra credit on each test. He is very caring and I would totally take a class with him again! Easy A!!!
Great professor! The class is tedious but fun. Participation matters.

Awesome professor who is extremely patient, kind, and understanding. Being one of the first professors in your Eller career, he stands out as being very laid-back. He really goes above and beyond. There is no reason not to get an A in this class if you make sure to do the work and KNOW THE MATERIAL. Awesome professor. Very glad I had this class.

Very cool teacher, I enjoyed going to his class. The subject was pretty hard for me so his lectures were helpful. I learned a lot. The test are easy if you know how to do the work. He provides everything you need to pass the class.
Dr. Songur is great. He is passionate about what he teaches & makes class enjoyable. Quizzes are difficult but open book, and they prepare you for the exams, which he provides a review sheet for.
Great professor ! Gives great feedback. Quizzes are though and participation matters.

Songur is a really nice prof who wants to see his students succeed. I didn't find his class to be very hard although the quizzes can be a bear sometimes (you get 2-3 attempts). Plenty of EC opportunites, they are all really fun and you learn a lot from doing them too. His lectures can be dry though, I found myself nodding off a few times...

This guy is awesome. Super nice, laid back, he CARES about you learning. He CARES and KNOWS the content VERY well, and it shows. No question goes unanswered, he will break anything down you ask, and he's just great to be around.
His class is pretty solid. The quizzes are really hard. Chegg is 100% the best investment for high quiz grades. The exams are easy and you get a formula sheet.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. never struggled so much in a class. rambles about things they don't pertain to the information that should be taught. His homework and lectures don't relate to the exams. You teach yourself everything in this course. Office hours and study session don't help. He takes so long to respond to emails. Save yourself please!

Nice guy but can't teach. Always got like a C on my first attempt on the hw. Exams are curved so my high C/low B would become an A. Study the hw in prep for the exams, sometimes the questions are the same. Not hard to get an A if you do somewhat good on the exams. Rambles a lot, takes a whole class to get through 1-2 slides.
Worst professor tbh, classes are arranged in a very unorganized way and exams are tougher than the lectures

Get ready to teach yourself everything. Professor Stegeman teaches this course as if everyone already knows what he is talking about. He often goes off on random tangents about things completely unrelated to the course, doesn't use a microphone, and doesn't take the time to explain anything in detail.
If you're able to take his Econ 426 class, take it. It's really easy and if you like financial markets it's pretty interesting.

Do not take his class. He spends most of his time rambling on about pretty much nothing in all of his lectures. If you need to reach him, good luck; he won't respond. Obviously, a very smart guy, but he is too smart for his own good. Basically assumes you know everything, so he brushes over very important topics. Very bad at making things clear.
The worst professor I have ever had!

Professor Stegeman is a very nice guy. However, his tests are super hard due to the fact that he does not teach; he reads off the screen and uses hypotheticals that do not relate to the material. He is always late to class, not to mention on test days. Even had one of our tests postponed on the same day because he was so late. Would NOT recommend.

Stegeman is knowledgeable and very kind, but he is also the definition of a scatterbrain. His lectures are hypothetical scenarios that don't relate to the content, especially not to the exams. The exams are hard to study for because there isn't much information on the lecture slides, and there isn't a textbook. I would not recommend his class

Stegeman is a very scatterbrained professor who can rarely stay on topic and often goes on unrelated tangents that take up the majority of his lectures. He always shows up late to class, and his exams are extremely difficult. However, Mark is a nice person and is knowledgeable on a lot of things, but how to teach a class is not one of them.

This guy is defiantly scatterbrained so its best to catch him in person. Has a lot of good advice on career stuff and smart. Would recommend for a small class. Attend the lectures because he does take some time to post lecture slides but attendance isn't mandatory. Exams are easy.

Lacks communication, does not go over important topics, rambles, etc. He is very nice but did not respond to emails sent when confused on a certain topic. Tests are an important part to the course but are difficult when he doesn't represent information needed.

This professor has no idea what is going on in his own class, I have never had to email the head of a department and for this class I had to do it multiple times. He rarely actually teaches the class and on multiple occasions we spent the entire class time of an hour and 15 minutes on one slide that had no help for the curriculum. DO NOT TAKE THIS.

He is one of my teachers at the UA. He is a super chill guy, down to earth and easy to talk to if youre struggling. We got to watch Spirited Away (anime)& write an essay on it. He gives you ample time to write papers I think there were 4 or less in the semester. Give clear feedback and ways you can improve. Highly suggest taking a class with him!

Super easy class. I am horrible when it comes to writing, but he was staright forward with each and every assignment. Lectures are boring, but it is a writing class so it is expected. Pretty easy to get a good grade in the class.

He really respects and cares about his students, take your paper to his office hours and he will give you useful feedback to revise your paper. Very patient, not a tough grader. Also, he offers extra credits if you have full attendance, which really helped me with getting an A in this class since I'm really bad at writing.
Easy A. Ask him to look over your essay before turning it in and he will tell you what to change. He makes his classroom a very comfortable environment, and is extremely laid back.

Took Dave Rick for English 101 and 102. He is the best. I received above a 95% both semesters by just taking my essays to his office hours once and making his suggested changes. Easy grader, class is boring af but worth taking for the free A. He also offers lots of extra credit, I got over 7% in my 102 class which is a ton!

Mr. Rick is an awesome guy! If you've taken AP English in high school this class will be an easy A. He avoids dumb liberal/PC/feminist tendencies that do exist in the English Department. Easiest and honestly most enjoyable class of the semester!

Professor Rick is a very down to earth guy and really wants to focus on your english skills, no dumb feminism. easy grader, I haven't read any of the reading assignments and we only use them for discussion. extra credit, take essay to office hrs. Weekly journals but free pts if completed. Very flexible with due dates. Such an easy A, must take.

Very easy class if you show up (you get 2% extra credit for not missing any days) also there was a total of 7% of final grade in extra credit. If you take your papers to him he will get you an A or at least a high B and with the extra credit class is easy A. also very fun class he is chill and not a lot of work. asks to bring book but don' have too

I love Rob! He is an amazing professor, and I enjoyed taking his accounting course. He supports his students in various ways, ensuring they have the opportunities and help they need to pass the course. Make sure to pay attention to the material, go to office hours if you can, ask questions, and read the chapter before coming to class.

Overall, a nice guy, gives a good amount of extra credit, so make sure to do it. The tests are weighted pretty heavy and it will take a good amount of studying to do well, but it's definitely possible. Attendance was graded, but wasnt always taken in class. Go to the test review and ThinkTank for sure.
Rob was one of my favorite lecturers I have had at U of A so far.  His lectures are clear, direct, and informative.  Although ACCT 200 can be difficult, he made learning the subject very doable.

Rob was an amazing professor and his lectures were very well structured. I learned so much in this class, and whenever I needed extra help I went to his office hours. He was very helpful and really cares about his students.

I've never taken accounting before and as hard as it was for me to understand Stussie did a great job explaining things. He is very straightforward on what the concepts are, what is going to be on the exams, and what he expects out of his class. He really cares about his students and making sure they succeed in this course.
very good teacher and class but you need to attend lectures and get extra help to do good
hard class but stussie made it pretty simple and is just so awesome king love him take his class.

This class was pretty hard but if you go to lectures and take notes it isn't too bad. Its a big class but Stussie cares a lot and is helpful in office hours. Also PeerPro offers this class for tutoring, I did weekly sessions and got an A in the class.
Took the condensed online class ACCT200(7 weeks), class went by quick but professor had good recorded content and provided lots of opportunity for engagement with both him and his aids.
Loved professor Stussie

Rob is great. ACCT 200 in itself is a rigorous class but Rob does an excellent job in explaining stuff. Please please please go to his class and listen to what he says rather than jotting stuff down. He gives hints if a particular topic will appear on the test. Lots of extra credit too. Take Josh Hunter's discussion session if available.

He is a fast paced lecturer but if you keep up and go to all the lectures you will learn everything you need to for the tests. Do as much of the extra credit as you can and then study a day or two before the tests and you should be okay!

ACCT200 with Prof. Stussie is challenging but rewarding. Attend class, study diligently, and utilize resources like unstuck study to succeed. The exams are tough but the professor cares about student learning.

Great professor! His lectures were very engaging and informative. Difficult, but not impossible to teach yourself most of the material. The discussions were very helpful in reinforcing the content. The homework and comprehensive homeworks were also pretty easy, but you don't get many attempts unfortunately. A lot of exams, but just study a bit.

This course was quite difficult, but Prof. Stussie managed to make much of the content easy to digest. I would reccomend paying attention in the lectures, going to the discussion, and taking notes. Stussie also offers plenty of study opportunities for exams.

This class is challenging, but the professor truly wants the best for all his students. If you focus during his lectures, you'll do really well. The class is test-heavy, but he prepares you well and clearly explains what to expect. He also offers a lot of extra credit opportunities, so make sure to take advantage of them they make a big difference.
Overall, this class was engaging and laid out clearly from the start. If you study, you will do fine.
Great guy, easy A if you can test well.

Accounting is a hard class regardless of the professor, but Stussie is great. He's good at teaching and a really great guy. People giving negative reviews just didn't try hard because there's tons of extra credit and the final is optional. My advice is to make sure you get 100% on all the homework and do the extra credit, it's there for a reason.
Follow the syllabus and this class should be an easy A, but be ready to study and prepare for exams.

While the tests are in-person, making them a bit harder to do well on, this class is still pretty easy and Stussie does a great job keeping students in the loop with when work is due. He also gives ample opportunities for small, but meaningful extra credit and practice before the exams.

Accounting itself is a hard class. I didn't study at all and I was failing my first two exams. If you would like to get C+ you really have to study. After I start to pay attention everything Rob says my next exams were perfect score. He makes everything clear, he really wants his students to be successful. You need to pay attention to his lecture!

Rob was a great professor, taught the material well, and responded to everything quickly. The course itself is pretty irritating if you prefer to learn by reading rather than lecture. You basically have to abandon reading the textbook to keep up, and I wish there were chapter synopsis documents to read instead.

He's the best lecturer I've ever had, engaging & informative. He gives enough opportunities for grade improvement while still making the assignments matter as well. He's the reason I chose accounting as my major. Highly recommended.

The best ran course in my years at UoA. The professor and assistants are very interactive with the class and questions. If you are serious about learning Accounting, I would highly recommend his course. 

That all said, the pace can be extreme, exams are closed notes/book, and there is no way to pass if you don't study diligently.

Great accounting professor. Although a good professor will only take you so far in this class. My biggest advice to achieving an A is doing the optional lab, all of the homework and extra practice, and creating a clear study plan before each exam. Overall he is a create choice when it comes to choosing a professor for your first ACCT course.
you need to let students use a note card for exams, have a heart dude

Very organized professor, the class is difficult but he does a good job providing all information during his lectures although that is all class will be. As long as you attend class regularly and stay attentive in this large class you will pass.
He does a really great job teaching a difficult class!! Go to class, pay attention, and you'll be just fine. You can tell how smart he is and he really wants the best for his students.

Accounting can be a tough class however Stussie made it a smooth transition into the difficulties that may be faced in Eller. He emphasized important material in lectures, and he made it easy to understand the concepts and formulas. Overall, he was a great teacher for a challenging class.
Goes over the material very carefully and slowly so everyone can understand. He really tries to make sure everyone knows the material and tries to tell you exactly what to expect on the tests.
Very hard course, harder than it should be but great teacher

This was a very difficult course, but Professor Stussie made it as manageable as possible. He is very good at teaching the confusing parts of information and provides lots of extra practice opportunities for extra credit and for studying.

ACCT 200 can be a difficult class, but Professor Stussie explains concepts thoroughly and clearly. He is always open to questions, both during and before/after class. If you use the many practice opportunities, you'll do fine in the class. Take the optional lab, as it helps reinforce the material. Exams are not open note, but multiple choice.

Great professor. Accounting 200 is a tough class but he explains the concepts very well. Tests are relatively easy and he prepares you well for them. Lots of extra credit and practice opportunities as well.

Stussie can talk really fast and burn through subjects but overall is an enjoyable professor to listen to. The lectures are fast and dense so you better be focused during class because you will get left behind. Weekly homework and small quizzes and 3 exams with class averages around 70. Pay attention in class and you will be fine.
horrible

Amazing professor who does a great job of introducing you to the new language of accounting. Time consuming class but there are tons of resources presented to you that will make it very easy to get an A if you put in the time. He is super helpful and very kind if he sees you are putting in the effort. Definitely recommend Stussie

Take Stussie for ACCT200! This is a hard class, but Stussie did a great job making sure you have what you need to understand the material. Show up to class and pay attention. Make sure to do the assignments and take advantage of the extra credit. I didn't take the optional lab and was just fine, but it can't hurt. Definitely study for the exams!

Professor Stussie is amazing. Accounting is a hard class but he is the professor you want to get you through it. He offers a lot of resources to help you and goes through the content in an efficient and easily understandable way. If you take this class definitely sign up for the optional lab with Chandler Brown if you can.
Great professor, consistent work but he makes the topics easy.

We had a passive-aggressive exchange via email about him taking points off when I turned in an assignment by typing in my answers in Word instead of writing it by hand and scanning it in - but he got a speeding ticket that morning and he had a change of heart and gave me the points back lol. He's prickly, but he's a good guy, I promise.

Great class and prof. Lecture every day w/ attendance points. Pay attention, do lab, do HW, study, and you will be fine. Not difficult, just all new information that you have to understand. Have to buy clicker and textbook. Nice guy, good teacher, enjoyed the class.
Professor Stussie is very derogatory and talks down to students. He acts like he cares but he clearly doesn't. His class is also very dry which makes it hard to pay attention to.

If you take the time and master the study guide which is not that hard, you will do very well on the test. The study guide has everything. Rob is smart and if you are interested in accounting you will find him interesting. Not a class you should ever skip going to.

Your grade rides on 3 exams. Be prepared for in-class clicker questions. Your main grade comes from exams. Very easy to pass as long as you attend class and pay attention during lectures. He and his TAs are very helpful and understand if you get stuck. Definitely recommend him if you have to take this class.

This class is very difficult and the material is hard but he teaches it better than all the other accounting teachers. If you show up to class, take good notes and study a little before the exams you will be fine.

Stussie is a tough professor. Everything is very test heavy in this class and lectures he moves extremely quick. You need to show up to every class to be successful and take very good notes. He is not approachable whatsoever. I'd recommend taking the lab, extra practice and extra points. If you're not an independent learner, this will be tough.

Stussie is definitely one of the better ACCT teachers at UA. His tests can be difficult cuz there's so many little details. But he gives good homework to help you try and grasp the concepts. Do the homework, do the study guides, look over the lectures, and you'll be fine. He does do random attendance quizzes so be sure to show up to class.

Rob was a rude, uncaring, arrogant professor. I had 100% attendance and studied hard, but still found accounting to be challenging. He was rude and disrespectful. In office hrs, Rob was condescending and unhelpful towards me when I asked him a HW question. In class, he is also rude and treats me with extreme attitude and irritation.

Meh, cool enough class but the guy obviously doesn't care about his students. He really just cares about how much people like him on a surface level rather than people liking him for being a good professor.
I used to like the material before I took this class. I wish I took Karen's class, she has much better reviews and a much better understanding of the materials.

This class was only fairly challenging but professor Stussie made it very difficult to endure. Every interaction with him is like pulling teeth and his jokes never hit. Probably because of his monotone voice. Theres so many things I could say about this professor but in summary he made me hate a topic I actually really liked
Accounting is hard but Rob does his best to post resources to help you be successful. The class is manageable and with a little bit of effort you can do well!

The arrogance behind his monotone lectures drove me up a wall. This is an extremely difficult class for most and he only seems interested in helping when its convenient for him. I was willing to put in the work but just felt the effort was not matched on his end.

Rob Stussie is the most amazing accounting professor ever! He is great about giving extra credit to those that will work for it and encourages those struggling to seek outside help. I went into this class knowing it would be difficult and am so thankful I had him as my professor. I would definitely highly, highly recommend him!!

He is a great lecturer who does a lot to make a challenging topic easier to understand. He goes through many practice problems to show us the exact processes to answer questions. I would advise reading chapters ahead of time though so it all clicks in class. He is also pretty open about what kinds of questions we can expect to see on the exam.

Terrible teacher. Barely helps with any outside resources. Expects you to be able to learn everything just with the work that is given. When I askeed him for extra work and help he was unable to help me.

Rob is such an amazing professor, while accounting definitely isn't for everyone, and I'm assuming that's the reason for the low ratings, Rob definitely makes it much more bearable. He is clear with what will be on the test, has thorough reviews before the exams which are super helpful. Overall definitely recommend taking accounting with him <3

Rob is a fantastic professor, and was great in ACCT200. The class can be challenging for many students, but Rob truly does want to see you succeed, and provides SO many options and opportunities to sharpen your skills in the class. Grading was fine, as there is plenty of content so a few bad grades won't hurt you that bad.

People rating low did not want to put the work into this class and are blaming the professor. It may take a moment but honestly when you get the hang of things the class is fine. Rob has been a great and kind professor. He prepares u very well; hw and study guides are just like the test. Honestly wish I could take my next acct class with him!! :)

Rob is awesome - he teaches very well (albeit "boring") and provides way more material than youll ever need to do well. You absolutely need to work at it, but he provides the resources. My biggest gripe with this class is the use of clickers and the mandatory printing of everything. Otherwise, I'm super happy with the class and Rob.

The content was difficult to learn in 7-weeks, but it is very possible to get an A+ if you dedicate a lot of time to studying. Tests are about 75% of your grade and can be difficult if you do not understand the content. The professor is uninvolved and lectures are extremely boring. He offers about 15 extra credit points if you do extra practice.
Impossible to pass this class.

Rob is a nice man, and is somewhat entertaining at times. Unfortunately, while he is super smart, his lectures are not engaging whatsoever. I hated that he would read through practice problem/slides quickly & simply unveil the answers in the lecture slides. He flies through content, and then asks "any questions?" & says "were making great time!"

Stussie may not have been my favorite, but according to some friends who took this course with the other professor, he is preferable. Really goes through and tries to explain everything, two HW assignments a week plus a larger at exam time,which can be done pretty easily with textbook. Exams proctored but all MC and pretty fair given course level.

Professor Stussie is absolutely incredible! He is, by far, my favorite professor in the Eller College of Management. ACCT 501 is not an easy class, so be prepared to work hard. If you put in the effort, then you will do just fine. Thank you for a great semester, Rob. The U of A is very lucky to have you!
Stussie is probably the best accounting teacher at UofA. A lot of people give him bad ratings because this is a hard class. If you don't put the work in outside of class, you will not do well.

Accounting is definitely not an easy course in general, no matter what prof you have, but Stussie's such a great prof. He really makes the material clear, provides so many resources for students to succeed, and he really knows what he's doing. You definitely have to work in this course, but that's just accounting. Stussie's for sure the prof to get

Stussie is by far my favorite teacher I have had within Eller so far. His class slides contain many examples and make it much easier to understand the difficult concepts. He may come off as intense at times, but you can tell he really loves accounting and knows what he is talking about. I'd strongly recommend taking his class.

It's a hard class like any accounting class and I took this class online but Professor Stussie was great at reminding us of due dates, homework and office hours. He's very understanding and answers anything we are confused on. I really liked how this online class was structured. It is a lot of work and a lot of reading but you will learn a lot!
hard material, but if you work hard you can succeed in a difficult class

Anyone who says Rob was a bad teacher did not put any effort into this class.  Rob is very concise and detail oriented and cares a lot about his students.  With the new online format he offers curves and if youre looking for an easy accounting class you wont find one.  He is very good at teaching the subject, just participate and you will do good

This class gets a bad rep. It's very clearly laid out and there are plenty of extra resources. If you just watch the videos, ask for help, and study for the test you'll be fine. Rob is a really caring guy and will respond to emails super fast. This class does eat up a decent amount of time but it isn't hard work. Just put in the work.
Rob did a great job teaching the material. He strived to understand and meet student needs while maintaining course expectations. He has a very clear voice!
Its the arrogance and lack of actual teaching/effort for me!
Seemed like a nice guy but this class was just ridiculous in an online format. The entire class is basically tests and the lectures are not helpful at all.
super difficult content, but stussie did his best to teach online, offer extra credit and even curve the exams. It is a difficult class and you have to put in way too much work to be successful

Accounting, in general, is a very hard class. This class will make you work for it and you may need to dedicate time outside of class. However, Rob is probably one of the best professors to have for this class. He explains the concepts pretty well and is always willing to help. I would highly recommend having him as your professor.

This professor does not teach at all so prepare to teach yourself. The recorded lectures arent even his. We are placed into breakout rooms each class where no one knows whats happening. Its honestly not even worth going.

So far, I've gotten a 96% first test and an 88% second test. The zoom lab sections r kinda meh. Halfway through the course, I stopped showing up to them, as your random groups tend to be quiet and unhelpful. Found it easier to do the worksheets myself. If you fall behind within the first 3 chapters, I would drop. Catching up seems quite difficult

Rob is the definition of hands-off learning. He taught absolutely nothing the whole semester, and instead expected us to watch recorded lectures from a completely different teacher. I was forced to teach myself everything because he did nothing the whole semester, and his TAs are just as useless. He is arrogant and makes you feel SO stupid 24/7.

Stussie seems to be a nice guy, but I am not really impressed with his course. I don't know if it is because of the pandemic, but I feel like he puts no effort in teaching this class. He put up video lectures and expects you to teach the material to yourself.

To be fair, I am sure the pandemic has had a pretty significant impact on this course. That said, I don't feel like Rob has been helpful at all. Every class he just puts us into breakout rooms to work with each other on problems. There is no real teaching involved and I don't feel like I am learning anything.

Professor Stussie has one of the best structured online accounting courses you will ever take. The lessons are laid out simply, lecture slides are detailed and HW is challenging yet helpful. Tests are built to be difficult, so hard work is required. EC is offered in the form of extra practice! This class is rewarding and difficult, as it should be

I had Rob in 200, and he was the person who made me want to be an accounting major! I was so grateful to have him again for 400B, perfect transition after Katie in 400A. The class is definitely not easy, but just have some good study buddies and get the work/studying done.
Not friendly. Not helpful. Rude!

Rob's a nice guy and did a great job transitioning a hard class to online due to COVID. I enjoyed his clarity explaining challenging topics and his dry sense of humor. He has also been a great mentor to me ever since I had him in acct 200. Highly recommend!

I had heard really great things about Rob and was excited when I found out he was teaching ACCT 400B. Unfortunately, I was quite disappointed to find that he is just okay at best. He is known for being well liked in ACCT 200 so maybe is better at the lower levels? I do agree with the other reviews that he is quite arrogant at times.
Rob is great at dumbing down the concepts and explaining how to do something. Only thing I hated was the daily quizzes which got annoying after the 1st test.
Rob is honestly overrated. This is my second time taking a class with him and while he acts friendly, he is really just full of himself and kind of rude. Avoid him if you can!
If you dont have to take this class dont. He tries to act like hes friendly and there to help but hes not. He acts like hes better than everyone in the room.

Rob is by far the best professor for ACCT 200. He is a great lecturer and the way he explains even the most difficult concepts is always very clear. The class is obviously difficult by nature, but he is always friendly and cares about his students

He is such a great professor. He makes everything so clear in his slides and he explains in such a nice way in the class. Go meet him if you have doubts he will help you. Attendance is very important. he is really nice and sweet.

Robert Stussie is a good and caring professor. He even says on the first day that he understands how difficult the class is and can be. However, he gives good feedback and even helps give enough advice for help. He is also very fun to talk to during office hours and really helps understand how difficult accounting can be unless you practice enough.
Bad personality, rude in office hrs, taught myself the material.
Professor Stussie isnt a bad teacher and explains the content really well so its possible to understand. He doesnt seem to care about the class and acts like he is above everyone in the room.

Overall a really good class and Rob is a great teacher. As long as you attend class and take notes in class you will be in a good spot for tests. This class is difficult but if you actually study and pay attention you will get a good grade.
He does *try* to be friendly and helpful. However, once I made a mistake and he made a snarky, rude comment that makes you feel terrible. I'll never forget how awful he made me feel that day.

Rob makes accounting at 8 am much more bearable - the material is very dry and can be difficult to understand at times. There is a good amount of homework, however the tests will determine your grade. I recommend attending the optional lab and going to every class - lots of material covered every day and a ton of clicker questions

Although, the class is hard and maybe one of the harder subjects I have had so far, he at least understands and is really supportive. He is also really nice and very fun to meet with during office hours. He can give good feedback about why your answers were wrong on the tests and can also help you study for the tests. Along with giving suggestions.

He is extremely stern and you'll see him smile probably 4 times the entire semester. The material is hard to comprehend because he addresses the topics as if people are already familiar with accounting. He speeds through things and doesn't break down the basics. The avg. test grade is 70% and each test is around 20% of our grade.
Great professor 10/10 would take his class again.

Rob Stussie is one of the best professors Ive ever had. He is extremely knowledgeable about the subject and is super passionate as well. He is always available to help his students and is super understanding when you have stuff going on outside of school. The class is tough but if you put in the effort it wont be too bad.
This man is a legend
One of my favorite professors! A really well organized class and Rob inspired interest in accounting.

Super hard class, but required for Eller and RCSC. Stussie is loved by many, but I find him to be pretty arrogant. Lectures are very dry and he is quite monotone, so I had to teach myself most of the material. The class is curved at the end which is nice, but you'll still have to work VERY hard. Go to office hours, think tank, etc
This class is awful and so is the teacher. Periodt.

if you're taking acct, then stussie is your guy. I have struggled here and there throughout the semester but he is really good at teaching this subject and very helpful to where I understand it pretty well now. also would recommend joining the lab that's offered for this class and to just keep up with the material because its easy to fall behind

ACCT 200 is tough but Rob makes it so much better. Although the class is lecture heavy, there are optional labs that help you get a better understanding of the week's materials. Rob makes every class entertaining and gives you the resources to fully understand accounting.

This is definitely one of those classes where you understand it, or you don't. I didn't struggle in this course, but I know others who had a very hard time. Stussie is by far the best professor to have for accounting 200, he is so knowledgeable its insane & his lectures are VERY helpful. Join the optional lab, go to class, study & you'll be fine

This class is difficult as is, but Prof. Stussie made the material easier to understand and easier to follow. The class is a little difficult, but if you stay on top of your reading, and make sure you pay attention in class you should be good!

Rob is probably the best professor I've had at the UofA. He really cares about his students ability to learn and the material he covers. Accounting 200 is not the easiest subject by far, but with Rob you stand the absolute best chance of scoring an A.

Rob is a great teacher. He goes through the material to make sure everyone knows what's going on, and will stop if you have questions. Be sure to do the readings before class and stay on top of it, because the first few weeks of class build up foundation for later chapters.

Stussie is one of the best professors I have had at the U of A. He gives great lectures and speaks of relatable topics to make things interesting. Material is pretty heavy but he tries his best to make it easy to understand. Definitely go to class or things may get difficult. Clicker questions happen a couple of times during the semester!
Gives great examples. Tells stories that are funny & engaging about real life accounting work.
An awesome professor!

Rob is an easy going guy and good lecturer, makes everything understandable. Accounting can be hard but with Rob its not so bad, just show up, do the hw and study hard. Exams aren't the easiest but he makes up for it with the occasional clicker extra credit. Class was very organized down to most minute details, which was appreciated. Best choice.

Rob is an inspiring teacher who gets it! He is flawless in his lecture style and he never messes up. Rob knows exactly what he's talking about and can answer questions in a breeze. He is the best Accounting 200 Prof at UofA and is 100% worth taking.

This is not a fun class to take, because it can be hard but Rob is the nicest prof. I went into tutoring regularly and he was so helpful and really wants his students to succeed. Make sure to pay attention in class or you will drown on the tests, but if you need help go to his office hours! He gives extra credit at the end of semester too :)

Rob is my favorite professor so far at the U of A (i am a sophomore). Would not recommend taking him at 8am because he is very monotone, but knows a great deal about accounting and makes it very relatable to life. Tests are difficult but the class is passable.

I had to GRO ACCT 200, & the 2nd time I had Rob. He's so flexible & understanding! If you talk to him, he'll reopen wiley assignments for credible reasons or emergencies; he really just wants everyone to succeed because he knows the class is hard. He even lowered the TCE response % just so we could get the extra point. CHOOSE HIS CLASS IF YOU CAN!

Who thinks "amazing lectures" in an accounting class? This dry material is brought to you by one of the best professors ever. Cannot believe how much knowledge I gained just by attending class. Stussie is clear and defined. He makes this class more interesting than I could have asked for! It wasn't easy - but class attendance makes the difference!

Rob is a cool teacher. Thought I was gonna get an A in the class, but kind of screwed up on the final. Definitely would take again. Oh yeah, try to take the later time of this class, 8 A.M. is hell to go to.

Rob is a great accounting teacher, but he is monotone, do not take this class early in the day or you'll tend to fall asleep. Accounting classes at UA is generally hard but he tries to make it as understandable as possible. Beware there are not many points in the class, tests mostly make that up so mess one up and you're screwed!

Stussie honestly does his best to make a very challenging class as approachable as possible. He really truly cares about his students and their grades and is always willing to help. Keep up with your work, show up to class, and honestly give this class everything you've got. Accounting is hard but Stussie is the best to take it with! Good Luck!!

Stussie is an awesome professor, Accounting 200 isn't an easy class but he teaches it so well,  and you can tell he know what he is talking about! He moves pretty fast in class but if you have a question he will stop and break it down until you understand.  You have to go to class and pay attention to be successful but he is always there to help!

Stussie is a wonderful teacher. Accounting 200 is a very challenging class and he does all he can to help out the students. He is almost always available to reach and if you go into his office hours he really tries to work with you so you can do well in the class. Acct 200 is designed to be tough but Stussie actually cares for his students grades.

Accounting is a very heavy subject and is needed for several business degrees. Stussie knows his stuff but is very monotone when presenting material. Lectures are very dry but he is willing to answer questions and go further into detail with answers.

Rob Stussie is an amazing instructor. He presents the information very clearly and doesn't make it any more confusing than it has to be. Accounting at the UA is a hard course, but he makes it MUCH more manageable. He is obviously knowledgable and can answer any questions you have. Loved this instructor!

At the U of A, Accounting 200 is a mandatory class for students applying to Eller, and is a rather dry subject if you're not really into accounting. HOWEVER, if you had to take it, Stussie is the best Professor to take it with. He's helpful, caring, and overall a great teacher. BE SURE TO  KEEP UP with the material and PAY ATTENTION in class!!!

At the U of A, Accounting 200 is a mandatory class for students applying to Eller, and is a rather dry subject if you're not really into accounting. HOWEVER, if you had to take it, Stussie is the best Professor to take it with. He's helpful, caring, and overall a great teacher. BE SURE TO  KEEP UP with the material and PAY ATTENTION in class!!!
She really explains what topics will be on the exams and goes into detail for everything covered but will tell you if its important for exam/quizzes

Presumably a smart guy, but a terrible teacher. He never explains the basics or the theory of accounting but gives random, obscure examples during class. The only thing I got out of this class was extra practice plugging numbers into equations that no one in the class understood.
Really good professor, is very knowledgeable in the field and gives many opportunities to get an A in the class. Would definitely take again

Professor Sweo is a knowledgable instructor and has his own way of systematically laying out his course. However, he is pretentious and opinionated when it comes to attesting any of his class expectations. No consideration for missing any deadlines no matter the circumstance. Much of his teachings were unrealistic and lacked real-time relevance. GL
The best teacher at Eller.

Really knowledgeable about what you'll be experiencing in the real world as a manager. He often gives engaging lectures and a lot of optional activities that you can do as much as your heart desires. Just put in the work and show up to class if you want to get the most experience out of this class.
Second class I've taken with Sweo! He's awesome!!
One of the best teachers at Eller, using his own personal career experience to prepare students to navigate job searching after graduation. If you can take him, you will have an amazing experience.
Bob is awesome! Take advantage of the extra credit and do ALL of the assignments. This class is competitive, but an easy A if you push yourself
Bob is a great professor. His lectures are always fun and you can tell he really cares about his students succeeding in the real world. Make an effort and you'll do fine in his class.
Knew his stuff. Dense, well-placed lectures.
No man can pull off a deep V like Bobby Sweo.
Best class I had at university
Sweo is a great professor, I believe he truly knows how to teach a concept without unnecessarily adding stress to a student's life.

Pros:
Allows the option for you to go fully remote at the downside of no field trips or beer tasting 
Allows two re-takes on tests
Cons:
Hard group semester project
Expects those online to participate in class, despite either being rude to those online or taking a long time to answer questions
Will take attendance every time class is held

This professor is so disorganized, doesn't put some things in the gradebook, doesn't know how to teach, this class was supposed to be fun, but turned out to be boring. Doesn't post dates to grade book, last minute email notifications, poor instruction. Lectures were literally 15 minutes and then over, why bother coming? Group project hard w/group.

IF UR GONNA ENROLL LATE DO NOTTTT.MAKES ME WANNA JUMP OFF A CLIFF. HES SO PASSIVE AGGRESSIVE. I ENROLLED INTO THE CLASS TWO WEEKS LATE. great teacher and lectures are interesting but hes very rude off email communication . made the whole late integration super uncomfortable. unless u can handle a grumpy old man i wouldnt recommend.
Prof G is an interactive professor who knows a TON about the topic he teaches. Take this class no matter what your major is.

Prof G. is a boss. It helps if you like the material that the class presents as it is all about urban sprawl, the emergence of cities, etc. You will  learn things you never knew in this class and understand your surroundings (even Tucson) a lot better. Class is not easy though and requires reading and understanding material. STUDY FOR TESTS.
Class can be super interesting assuming your into urban development/politics.  5 pop quizzes make class kind of mandatory but if you don't its doubtful you'll understand anything anyway.

-good guy -tries to make lectures interesting -need to attend class to get info not on slides -written exams, doesn't let you see a poll of possible questions beforehand -no review sessions -cumulative exams -lots of reading needed

If you don't go to class, you will not pass. This class is hard and his grading is annoying. Three exams that are essay question exams.. and they are hard. One 10 page research paper. The guy means well though, very passionate.

The exams are all essay questions. Pretty hard if you don't study and go to class. Though attendance is not required it is crucial for you to understand the material. The 10 page paper is really easy and interesting. He seriously could care less  if you or don't go to class. It's your grade afterall. No need for final if you pass :)

Professor G is really an interesting guy. Class is kinda boring sometimes but he favors students who go. Tests are essay format and are difficult. Final paper is 10 pages but he is a lenient grader as long as you link your topic to the urban community.

You can tell he is from Chicago from his sense of humor style :). He is really direct and straightforward about all of his grading rubrics. Tests are pretty easy, and so is the final paper. This has probably been one of my favorite classes so far at UofA I highly recommend taking any courses he teaches.

Pretty good teacher. Classes are pretty dry, but you can definitely miss a couple of classes. He puts all the slides online. Tests aren't too bad, usually 4 essay questions and you pick 3. There is a 10 pg paper, but he grades them pretty easily. Definitely recommend him.
Great teacher!  Good sense of humor and really cares about teaching. Not hard to get an A. If you pay attention in lecture you're pretty much guaranteed a good grade

mr g is pretty cool, has a dry humor about him but its he me laugh still. lectures with slides and puts them on web so if you miss a day its ok. there are books but you can really get what you need out of the lecture. there are 2 exams  3 essay questions each. theyre not bad and he grades on a curve. only assignment is 10 pg paper due at the end.

Ricardo is the sweetest, most genuine person. He is so knowledgeable and passionate about what he does. This class is structured where you only have to give one presentation during the course and otherwise everything is discussion-based and very chill. I highly recommend taking this with him! I wish I  I could have him again!

Very fun and laid back class to take. Just make one presentation for the semester (about esports, no less!), watch everyone else's presentation, and get an easy A. Would 100% recommend for the honors seminar requirement.
Professor Valerdi is an amazing fellow. He's accessible and fun. 195I was easy honors credit as well!
He's awesome. Class was just an open discussion after two kids presented each week and basically just a fun talk. Take him for this class if possible (ESports Honors Seminar)

I thought I was going to dread his class bc, sport analytics is not my specialty, but it was super enjoyable! His homework and test are very straight forward. He doesnt trick you in anyway. I would for sure take another class by him. There was one exam and no final just a final paper. Also, a homework assignment due every week.

There where no textbooks to be specific but you have to be familiar with "Relentless: by T. S. Grover" and "Mathletics: by Wayne Winston". The course schedule was very detailed, which will allow you to stay on course. Dr. Valerdi is very passionate about his work and students. Would definitely take a class from him again.
Overall the Sports analytics class was extremely enjoyable! I wish there was a second session so I could improve my coding skills and solve additional sports stat questions.

Dr. Valerdi is the best professor out there! He really cares about his students. Lectures are so fun and useful, straight forward HW and grading criteria is crystal clear. Class is interesting, Take home exam and final project only, NO Final exam for this class. Class materials are very well organized. Definitely take all his classes that allowed.

Dr. Valerdi is the coolest professor ever!! He really cares about his students. Lectures are so fun and useful, straight forward HW and grading criteria is crystal clear. Classes are interesting, I felt like I learned a lot with minimum amount assigned HW. I would take all the classed that he's teaching if they are counted for my program.

One os the easiest class I have ever taken. Take this class with him and you will not regret, even if you know nothing about baseball. Class is fun and interesting. Quizzes and homework are supper easy and the exam, everything from the study guide.

By far one of the most responsive and helpful instructors. He held weekly meetings to ensure students were prepared for all assignments each week. Each assignment had clear grading criteria and instructor and TA addressed all questions within 48 hours. This course was enjoyable and teaches you alot about statistics and how to apply them to sports.
The professor is very enthusiastic and knowledgeable in the real of sports analytics. Class is fun and is graded fairly, albeit the assignments and quizzes are very easy.
Good Instructor, explained assignment, clearly spoken. fast paced, I enjoyed the class.

Professor Valerdi's class is easily one of the best courses offered in SIE. His level of organization and professionalism is unmatched. He not only makes the material interesting, he presents it in easily digestible segments. On top of this, he uses a variety of platforms and media to deliver the content, which keeps the course engaging.
I enjoyed doing this course with Prof. Valerdi.

Science of Baseball for honors colloquium. Show up and do one presentation and paper for the semester and you'll pass. Great guy, very nice, interesting topics. Attendance not mandatory but it was such a fun class you want to be there.

Ricardo Valerdi cares about his students and it reflexes in his teaching. Makes learning easy by applying it to real world  and fun situations. All engineering classes come with their difficulties but he makes a hard subject interesting, easy, and approachable.  I would take any class that he teaches purely because of his teaching style.

This was the best course I took my freshman year, hands down. The colloquium he taught was called The Science of Baseball and it was the most engaging class I've ever taken. Each week there will be a presentation of a different baseball topic, sometimes by students and sometimes by guest speakers, you'll always be engaged and will always have fun.

I took Professor Valerdi's science of baseball course as an honors colloquium. The class was engaging and the subject matter was interesting. Different students give presentations on various scientific topics in baseball each week, but the best part of the class was the hands-on activities that Professor Valerdi incorporated.

Valerdi was a great professor. Indeed, he is the best professor I have ever had. He's very clear and comprehensive. HE gives time for students finish homework assignments and and projects are based on what you learned in his lectures. I got an A and was pretty easy.

Professor Valerdi is a really helpful. This class allows you to take as much from it as you want. If you just want an easy A you can do the minimum, but if you use the resources he provides you can learn a lot more. Overall this was a really good class, the focus is on learn instead of the usual overloading of pointless exercises.

The class I took was a Honors Seminar called the Science of Baseball. This class was absolutely amazing. Dr. Valerdi was my favorite professor of this semester. If you love baseball, or if you want to learn about baseball take this class. We got to go to a DBacks game and talk to pro players about what playing in the bigs is like.

- Lots of guest lectures, which can be tedious. His lectures however are useful and go by quickly.   - Understanding of mishaps that inevitably occur during the semester.  - Is one of the few teachers that actually realizes this was not your only class.
one of the beast

He was the best teacher i've had at UofA so far.  He is always willing to help you, even if it's not for something that you need in the class (he helped me with internships).  HIGHLY RECCOMEND this class he is so passionate about what he teaches, even though attendance isn't necessary you will want to go listen to him every week.
Great teacher
Dr. Valerdi is one of the best professors in the department!  Makes the material very understandable, and does a great job filling in the blanks when the textbook doesn't.
Fascinating classes, Very clear instructions and flexible due time. You will have plenty of time to finish your HW and everything
Very knowledgeable of the material.  Ensures that you understand the concepts.  Very helpful and understanding.
Excellent class
Great professor, very clear, very thorough, extremely knowledgeable and helpful. Really enjoyed this course and his teaching style. Wish I could take all my courses with him. Would highly recommend.

Prof. Valerdi is a great instructor with useful experience with the defense industry that he uses with real examples and lessons learned.  Very easy to work with and conveys his knowledge in an easy to understand manner using ideas that relate to everyday experiences.
Great course.  Best class I've ever taken.  Very relevant to my professional career.  Every engineer and leadership professional should take this course. Dr. Valerdi is an excellent teacher.
Great Professor. Lecture includes real world examples. Great class to systems engineering and systems thinking.
Excellent class and great professor.
Friendly professor. Very knowledgeable and nice.

One of the best instructors I have ever had. Lectures are rich on real engineering thinking and examples. Every engineer should take a class like this if they want to be thinking of the big picture not just a component.

One of the best courses in our department in terms of giving a broader view of Systematic Thinking. I love the way Ricardo taught in the class. This course was an awesome and delightful experience for me.

This course was a great introduction, or in my case re-integration, into senior undergrad/graduate level Systems Engineering. Dr. Valerdi kept his lectures interesting and applicable. All around superb experience.
Good course.  Dr. Valerdi was very insightful and I learned a lot in this course.  One of the better college courses I've taken in my 13+ year professional career.

Professor Valerdi was a superb instructor.  His industry experience in Aerospace made his knowledge extremely relevant to real-world considerations in Systems Engineering.  For example, when he teaches about Preliminary Design Reviews (PDR) he does so from a position of real world experience.  I strongly recommend taking a course with Prof Valerdi.
Good instructor.  He knows the subject material very well and can offer insights having studied under and worked with some of the leaders in Cost Estimation.
Uses real world examples and problems throughout the class. Brings industry and academic experience to the classroom.
Very interesting class and Ricardo makes the class clear and engaging.  Very useful material and very applicable in the real world.
Outstanding Professor. If you attend class and stay on top of reading, and use office hours, this is a great class. Very applicable to the real world and a very doable class if you participate.

Dr. Valerdi is an excellent professor. The content is both made interesting and practical through the multi-modal deliveries. This is a class you will remember and use in your profession and in life. Thanks, Prof. Valerdi!

One of the best professors at the SIE department. Like others have mentioned, Dr. Valerdi is very approachable, has genuine interest in teaching and guiding the student and knows how to make the class interest. Lots of current examples. Recommend!
Great class with guest speakers and creative assignments.

This class was very interesting and practical.  It centered around cost modeling of software, but we were given ample exercises involving non software projects.  The homework, midterm exam, and final project all stayed true to the class material.  Professor Valerdi is very approachable and is glad to give guidance--A great professor. I recommend.

Great professor that is helpful and to the point. His lectures don't drag on and only pertain useful information that you need to know. Projects are challenging but he usually allows you to pick the subject matter and is always willing to help. If you have the chance to take his course, do it.

I enjoyed this class and learned a lot. Dr. Valerdi is a good lecturer and one of the best professors I've had for a class. He is very knowledgeable and keeps the class interesting. I recommend this class.

One of the best professors in the department(SIE, U of A). He makes the class as interesting as possible. There was 1 take home midterm and a final project for this course. He is very helpful. Thanks Ricardo for the class.
ONe of the best professors I have had.
One of the best professors in the SIE program at the University of Arizona.
