Prof Valerdi is an amazing professor! One of the best professors I've ever had in college. He actually knows how to effectively teach a class while making the subject interesting. A great person too

His class was very interesting. The hw were open in date till the end of the semester like you can do and submit the all the hw at the end of the semester. His exams were take home exams but little challenging but the grading is not that hard. He explains the material very well.

Very friendly and helpful teacher. Haven't met a teacher before so cool and so much open to students. Very practical and important topics covered in class. Guest lectures and course project are the fun parts in his course.

Ricardo was one of the best teachers I've had in my college career. The course itself is is important, but Ricardo made it interesting, semester project very realistic. Best professor in SIE Dept. hands down!

Although new to the UofA, Prof. Valerdi is raising the bar. This is a class everyone one should take, including professors. It was enjoyable, it was a great balance between the theoretical and practical. I appreciate, Prof. Valerdi's teaching style and will benefit from his class, for the rest of my career. Thank you. Ricardo!
An amazing person as well as an amazing professor. His lectures are helpful and interesting. Everything I learned in the class I got to use in other classes.

Prof. Valerdi is one of my favorite professors at UA. He is very helpful to students, and you can tell he really has a passion for teaching. If you get to know him he is a great asset in industry. The group project in this class is time consuming, almost like a mini-senior design. It depends how much effort you invest in it though.

The most amazing and wonderful professor that I ever seen. I recommend Dr. Valerdi for every student like to get experience from the academic study, he will involve you in the field of study and will submit all his efforts to support you. I have the honor that I am one of his students.

The most amazing and wonderful professor that I ever seen.  One of the best professors in the SIE program at the University of Arizona. Really cool, relaxed, helpful and genius at the same time. Try all his best and knowledge to help and support his student in the academic and practical feilds.  Extremely recommended.

Relaxed, personable, excellent communicator with extensive theoretical and practical knowledge. AND (gasp) an understanding of what effective teaching looks like! I've had maybe one other prof this good in my entire life.
One of the best professors in the SIE program at the University of Arizona.  Really cool, relaxed, and genius at the same time.  Extremely recommended.
Ricardo is a very smart young professor. The course is interesting. Lots of talks were given. Exams are easy.
Learned a lot.  Great Prof.

Professor Van Landuyt engages the class in meaningful discussions that allow you to learn further about audit than as an undergrad student. He is a passionate professor and cares about his students-always open to conversation and questions. Participate and listen in lectures and you will be successful.

By far my favorite professor at U of A! His lectures and exams are very straightforward. As long as you're going to class, and you start studying the material a couple weeks before the test, you'll do fine. He's really good at clearing up areas of confusion so don't be afraid going to him for questions.

Ben does a great job at making audit fun and engaging, even though audit doesn't click with me. Exams are tough and usually have trick answers but do a good job preparing you for AUD CPA. lots of free points through discussions and group project, though they are just pure busy work when time could be better spent elsewhere.

Sangeetha is a great teacher. I struggled to learn BNAN in the huge lecture halls, but this analytics class was smaller, and Sangeetha won't move forward until everyone is solid. It was easy to ask for help, and this semester I gained way more excel skills. The course schedule isn't very accurate though, and you have to participate for a grade.
Sangeetha is super great! I loved her class and she always gave examples of practical applications for what we were learning.
Love her! Her examples are hilarious. I still remember several examples she used for the various topics. Very nice course. Sangeetha is the best!

She is an amazing professor! You definitely have to go to class 95% of the semester to grasp the content, because some of it is definitely advanced excel. However, this is a class that you genuinely WANT to go to because she is always such a positive, engaging, supportive teacher.

Amazing professor, I was scared of excel and felt like I knew nothing even though Eller has 3 Excel prereqs, she is the only teacher where things stuck. She connects everything to real business applications and is always willing to answer questions. You have to show up to class to be successful. I hope to have her again in the future!
She is truly a nice person. Her lectures are amazing.
Didn't expect to like this course, but I actually do like it very much. Very useful course. Even though it is way early at 8, still definitely worth it.
Very knowledgeable. I fell in love with analytics as a result of this course. I specifically asked for her for the MR course next semester.

Overall is a great professor who enjoys teaching her students and is great at it. I learned a lot in her class and she constantly asked if we had questions to make sure everyone was keeping up with the material. Attendance and participation are important in this class to succeed.
Not bad. Sangeetha made the course easy. She seems a bit strict, but is actually very sweet.
Not a fan of the video assignments. she encourages class participation. Good course.
Sangeetha is very sweet and her lectures are SO engaging! This is my second course with her. I wish she would teach an elective.

I really enjoyed the course. She is very busy but always tried and made time when I reached out to her. As long as you show up and listen to her lectures (which are super funny) you will do great in the course.

Her lectures are fun and she is very knowledgeable. Only Prof to provide an opportunity for students to meet one on one for several weeks towards the end so they can improve their performance. Extra credit opportunities too. Take her if you can.

The worst teacher I have had in my entire college career. I have had Sangeetha twice due to being a marketing major and would always take weeks to grade and would not respond to emails. She complains she has a lot on her plate with other classes but as a student, I also have other classes and things going on. She takes weeks to grade and is harsh.

Sangeetha is one of the best professors I have had at UA. You definitely have to work for your grades and it's not an easy A but you learn SO much. Her lectures are so engaging. If you have the opportunity to take any class with her as a professor I would HIGHLY recommend it. She slays.
One of the best professors I have had.she is a bit fast through the subjects. Seems intimidating but she genuinely is concerned about all her students.

If you don't rate this professor well then you're a bad student honestly. I suck at Excel. I missed more classes than I should have. But I watched her lectures and asked my classmates for help. Exams are easy if you spend a few hours studying and she grades everything pretty easily. She's a sweetheart and is enthusiastic. Would 100% take again.

Sangeetha is one of Eller's best. She is incredibly smart and passionate about what she does. She does a great job of encouraging class participation to help students apply consumer behavior concepts to their everyday lives. Her exams can be a bit challenging, but overall, it's an easy and interesting class.
Best professor I have ever had! So nice and understanding to all students.

I had her for 376 and am so glad I had her for 450. She made the class so interesting and always tells funny stories for example of concepts. Her class is based off super easy homework assignments and 4 tests. Take her if you can!! I wish I could take another class with her

Sangeetha is an amazing professor who really cares about her students! As long as you explain to her your circumstances she is very understanding and will always want what's best for you. The course is excel heavy so as long as you pay attention and do the work you'll be fine. Overall, she's a great professor who enjoys teaching this subject!

Learning about consumer behavior with Sangeetha is an honor. Her lectures are interactive and very applicable to modern pop culture. Her exams are exactly what she teaches in class. This class is overall one of the most enjoyable I have taken at Eller and her students appreciate her interest in our own opinions and thoughts.

Sangeetha is a very straight forward lecturer. All of the content on exams and homework is clearly gone over in class. There are not too many assignments, just enough to learn the topics. I enjoy her class and find that as long as you're paying attention to her, you will get good grades.

Sangeetha is a great professor and her class is very useful. If you zone out during class and fall behind on the lesson, it can be difficult to catch back up. Thankfully she posts recorded lectures online. Tests are about what you'd expect, and homework is easy if you know the content. Not the most exciting class, but very practical.

Sangeetha is an amazing professor. She made concepts easy to understand with her examples and class discussion. She is one of the most caring professors I've had and you can tell she wants the best for all her students! Highly recommend taking her!

Sangeetha is by far one of my favorite professors in Eller. She cares about all her students and wants everyone to succeed. Excel is tough to learn, but she made it easy to understand. The homework is very similar to what you do in class, so it's very doable. She is always willing to help and is an amazing person all around!

I had a great experience with Sangeetha and was so happy that she was my professor for this class. She really spends the time on the material to make sure her students understand the concepts. She has good real-life examples to help you remember different terms. I still remember some till this day.

Sangeetha is by far my favorite professor. Excel is just tough to learn, but Sangeetha does a great job teaching it. She really cares about her students and their mental health. I requested to take MKTG 450 with her because she is just the best.

Sangeetha gets a bad rep but is amazing. Students struggle with this class because it's all excel, but she explains everything thoroughly. The worst part of this class is the students. If you put in the effort you will get an A. The only criticism I have is that she's a bit disorganized, but she was also teaching 5 classes, so it's not her fault.

Im not sure why Sangeetha has such a low rating. Shes the only professor I had in my first semester Eller that cares. She gave us weeks off when we were stressed to take self care time. She also makes excel super easy to understand. She really cares for her students and her class is one of my favorites. I requested her next semester too.

Sangeetha is one of the best professors at UofA. I was required to take her 376 class and loved her so much that Ive taken every other class she offers. Very knowledgeable, and she genuinely cares about her students success. Even if its not a topic youre passionate about, youre sure to learn a lot from her

Sangeetha is the best professor I've ever had in college. I've been in three of her classes and make sure I enroll in any class that she is teaching. Truly cares about student success as long as you participate and try in class. Exams are easy if study the study guide she posts. I will miss her once I graduate from the UofA.

Compared to other professors, Sangeetha is the most unorganized, incompetent professor. Makes mistakes in formulas and students have to correct her. Unhelpful, dismissive, and cold in office hrs. A huge red flag that she often finishes grading at 2-4AM! Once, she waited until midnight-2AM the night of the midterm to submit assignment grades!

Sangeetha is my absolute favorite professor at the U of A! She truly cares about her students understanding the material, but more importantly she cares about her students outside of the classroom. Also, she does not give trick questions on exams. Everything she tests you on is from the lectures.

Unorganized, uncaring, hostile. Our Final was on Wednesday, but she only posted the study guide a few days before. Screams and yells, sending passive aggressive emails in all caps, like REMEMBER TO SEND PARTICIPATION TRACKERS! LATE SUBMISSIONS WILL *NOT* BE ACCEPTED! Also, her feedback on assignments is unhelpful, negative, & passive aggressive.

In office hours, I tried to talk about how I dont have anyone to study with, but then she YELLED at me, IGNORED my point, and went on a tangent! Bruh, she is the one who said she was happy to see teams study together on Discord at the beginning of the year. She is DISRESPECTFUL, rude, inconsiderate, and doesnt listen!

Towards the end of the semester, she doesn't care about teaching, as she tends to show up late and post last minute study guides. Also marks off points for criteria that aren't on the rubric, which is frustrating. Doesn't pay attention, as there are typos and mistakes in D2L. Sometimes disrespects and talks down to students.

Condescending and puts down students. Starts half-heartedly running the class as the semester ends by posting last minute study guides and announcements. Becomes careless and lazy, as seen in her late arrivals, unhelpful advice, and low effort organization of class structure. Makes All my other professors are more organized than her.

Rude and belittles students. Unhelpful feedback and grading criteria. Very last minute and disorganized. Very un-engaging public speaker. Towards the end of the semester, she stops caring and frequently arrives late and posts last minute assignments and study guides. Stays up late, so she frequently makes mistakes and misses important details.

Inattentive - Makes many mistakes, which I have to remind her multiple times but she ignores and doesn't listen. Unhelpful feedback results in lower grades. Sometimes asks invasive questions. As the semester ends, starts slacking and becomes more unorganized and shows up late. Very unorganized - posts announcements and study guides last minute.

I loved her! She cares a lot and offered sooooo soooo many opportunities for you to receive a better grade on assignments and tests. Her slides were really helpful for all the exams and she did an awesome job at making sure the class all understood.
She does't know how to teach!!! Her slides are full of words, I don't think she takes a breath once during her lectures. Rambles all class about hospital technology.

Dr. Wallendorf is the most intelligent professor I've ever had. Her slides are minimal but test Qs come directly from what she says in class. You need to stay engaged in class but in my opinion the lectures were so interesting I never even once thought about checking my phone. She's passionate about her work and always answers questions extensively
Skip this course if you can and run.

I can recommend to anyone not to take Melanie Wallendorf's consumer behavior course. There is zero guidance, no interest on behalf of the teacher, her way just is rude and disinterested in the students. Expect to be treated a low life.
Professor Melanie Wallendorf is not interested in teaching something of value but rather uses the classroom as a tool to intimidate.

Honestly the worst teacher I've had to date. Her teaching style was mundane and critical. She reads off a piece of paper the entire class about a bunch of unrelated information from like 1980, and then has no idea what to say when anyone asks a question. Pretty rude individual and her actions and personality make students not want to participate.

Wallendorf is the worst Eller teacher I have ever had honestly worst teacher ever in my life. She lectures only on random facts and stats she finds online that aren't related to the class. Reads straight from those facts and never knows answers to questions people have. Also tests on specific details from lectures almost impossible to know. Horribl

Mrs. Wallendorf's class was atrocious and I left most classes feeling as if I didn't learn a thing. I have a 4.0 GPA and am now pre-med but I barely pass MKTG 450 because the exams weren't built on the lectures at all. MAKE SURE TO CIRCUMVENT THIS CLASS!
DON'T WASTE YOUR TIME. Her materials are outdated and boring, which she tries to overcome with tough exams. Professor Wallendorf also came across pretty aggressive and disrespectful to our cohort.
Horrible teacher. Eller should have someone else teach this class.

Amazing professor! She really knows her stuff & has a wealth of knowledge to pass on. I really enjoyed the class and the information we have learned. Of all my classes in Eller, this one has really stuck with me. I did not like: treated us like kindergartners (assigned seats, no bathroom during class and more). Pay attention, keep up, take notes!

I loved Dr. Wallendorf! Her class was definitely challenging, but she teaches the material well. You have to pay attention to everything she says in lecture, take notes, and know the bold words in the book. Ask her questions during lecture and she'll answer them. Best class during second semester Eller.

I don't know what's worse, her or her tests. The tests were impossible due to the amount of material on each. She is rude, stubborn and not helpful which make her hard to work with. She had us write suggestions on how to improve the course midway of the semester and she ignored everyone's suggestions. FIND ANOTHER TEACHER IF YOU CAN!

I was excited to take this class because I want a career in consumer behavior, but she completely destroyed the topic. She has lots of information, but in my opinion is often times useless. The readings are outdated and lengthy. Exam questions are tricky and unclear. She does take criticism well.
She just tries to cram as much information as possible. So much that you don't really have a shot at actually learning anything.
All I can say is avoid if possible.

Hands down the worst professor I have ever had. Makes me regret choosing Eller/UofA. She injects her own biases into the lessons, quotes out of date data and is counter-productive to actually learning. She teaches concepts that oppose what we've already learned and her classes are painful to sit through. Transfer out of her class if you can.
Now I am beyond her class I see how she preppared us for later classes.  Great covereage of current topics.  It is not an easy class but it gets you ready for other classes and for job interviews.

The tests were ridiculous. It is impossible to learn TedTalks, their authors and lessons from each lecture, plus every single definition in the book, plus readings per class, plus lecture of about 5 pages each then know that all for tests plus a comprehensive final.

Overall she is a wealth of information, but the way she treats here students and delivers the ciriculum is absolutly rediculous and impossible to follow in any realistic matter. You can study for days and still fail her exams beacuse thats how she sets them up! If you have options, take them...RUN!

Dr. Wallendorf knows her stuff, she is extremely knowledgeable about the subject. However, her teaching methods are very ineffective, she conducts class logistics like its a kindergarten class and tests are VERY difficult because she covers huge amounts of material in each section so your never sure exactly what to study.
She really wants students to learn.  If you don't like that, don't take this course.  Get a major that doesn't expect you to learn anything.

The strictest teacher I have EVER met my whole life. She treats us like kinder-gardeners. We have assigned seats and name tags. We aren't even allowed to go to the toilet during class. She doesn't follow anything from the book, but tests us on it anyways. Be prepared to feel like her dog.

This class is a make or break based on the company you work with and the group members you are lucky, or unlucky, to get. I really feel like the professor did not want to teach this class, and I was really disappointed that this class did not go in depth into analysis. Everything you learn in this class can be learned for free on the web.

Many of my classmates thought he was too harsh but I think he is just too passionate about what he teaches and expects you to listen and learn. I wasn't happy about my grade but his class was interesting regardless.

I am making 2 parts to this. Let me start by saying I have NEVER done a rating before but I have never been treated with such disrespect in my life. It's honestly embarrassing UofA would hire someone who loves to watch their students fail like he does. There is no study guide ever, although every exam the class would fail but he blamed it on "us"

Has way too high of expectation during a global pandemic. Likewise, he did not actively teach the course. When over half the class failed exams, he would blame his entire class instead of actually trying to teach better. "Study better" was all he could provide for us in terms of exceeding in his class. DO NOT TAKE, he has too much pride in himself

Caleb puts off the vibe that he doesn't care about student's success. He doesn't really teach and promotes the idea that this course should be somewhat "self-taught". I was looking forward to this class and was disappointed in the lack of excitement in the course from the professor. Participation grade is confusing, and the assignment instructions.

This was Caleb's first semester teaching Digital Marketing 423. Previously I heard this was an enjoyable course, however I cannot say the same with Caleb. The simulation on Stukent does not provide clear graded context, Caleb could not answer what we were being graded on, as a result many of us performed poorly. Good luck preparing for exams too!

I had limited prior knowledge of regression but learned a great deal in this course. The material is delivered through lectures and applied exercises rather than a textbook. I gained experience with Stata, and the small class size made the instructor approachable, responsive, and supportive.

This was my first experience with hands on research surveys and projects, and I loved it. The course is not for everyone, it is for really learning how to do research, and she is very good at following up if you have an issue. There were great discussion forum instructions, and she answered every question I had, and I asked a lot of questions.

Awesome teacher, super caring and smart. However, she needs to come in for exam days and project presentations. The TA does a great job for the massive responsibilities he was given, but he is not the teacher and struggles to do it on his own.

Most disappointing class I've ever taken. Her online lectures didn't match the assignments at all, and asking questions felt pointless because her discussion replies were dismissive and belittling. I came in excited to learn, but she gave us nothing. No clarity, no support, just constant stress and frustration. Not fit to teach graduate students.

At first, this class was good. She was very caring. Very rushed lectures at times. However, come project and exam time, she is absent. Her TA, Tyler, was rude and inconsiderate of our time, always late. She doesn't grade a single thing, which leaves her TA to ruthlessly grade when he doesn't know the criteria. She values research over class.

Warren is one of the best at Eller. She is caring and almost always available to respond to questions. I emailed this woman at 2 am and she still answered in about an hour!! The course is not easy but not as complicated as I thought. She is an interesting person. Different from most I've seen. If it had a textbook it would've been easier to learn.

The class started great but she gave up halfway through. She canceled so many class sessions that 110 points were removed from the syllabus. We spent $100 on an SPSS license and learned next to nothing about how to use it. She does not provide rubrics for projects, so grading seems arbitrary. It's evident she prefers research over instruction.

I hope she reads these and knows how much she makes a difference in her students lives. She is real and personable and is not afraid to bring up difficult issues like mental health. She cares about every one of her students and makes sure that they are heard and they are ok. I wish all our professors were like her

I was very stressed about taking this course as I hate mathematics and anything related to it. Interestingly it is not about maths. It is more about understanding how to collect useful data and find answers. Professor Warren made it clear and understandable. Even though the attendance wasnt mandatory I went to all the sessions and I Sialkot dont!

Warren is good at her job, and is personable and fun. But the course is a pain, its a lot of work. The upside is youll be graded for every little thing and at the end of the course its clear how the final grade is calculated. I would have given her a 5 in any other course. Wish she taught capstone or more fun subjects.

I took this course thinking Im going to hate it but instead I got so inspired by Nooshin that I even started to like stats! She is real and honest, she teaches the course well but her passion clearly is more than just teaching the material. She wants students to take more from her class and I think most of us did. More of this type of profs pls!

She is different from all other professors at Eller. She is down-to-earth, approachable, serious, and disciplined at the same time. Our group had inside problems and she was aware. She helped keep it fair for us. Difficult class because there are many assignments but it helps with the participation grade. Do them and you will get your participation

If only we could take this course before our Econ courses. I am now understanding what they have been telling us in those Econ courses. Nooshin makes the complicated econometrics models easier to understand giving practical examples. She is helpful inside and outside of the class. Is easy-going and approachable. I wish she taught other courses too

A gem! I was expecting this class to be super difficult, but it was really easy. She is super understanding for grading, assignments, questions, etc. If there is a bad quiz score, she will round. She truly wants everyone to succeed and is more than generous with her grading on assignments. Participate and put in the effort and you are good!

The class is not as difficult as I expected but it has lots of assignments, quizzes and project. I guess it helps to have your grade dispersed. Nooshin herself is approachable and knowledgeable. I asked her questions out of class many times and she has been very responsive and helpful. I wish she taught other courses I could take. She is chill

I'm currently taking this course. don't know my grade yet but I do know whoever says Nooshin Warren doesn't care about her student has either never met her or has no idea what they are talking about! She is extremely considerate and caring about her students well being. she talks about difficult issues in class. Course is good but she is fantastic.

I took her Regressions class. This class is a must for any marketer that wants to understand the numbers behind BI reports. This class also makes both the Marketing Research class and the Relationship Marketing classes much easier to understand. Ensure you have a good, basic understanding of running regressions before taking this class.

Warren seemed to try some days, and not care at all on others. Her instructions were extremely unclear and she would grade harshly. She did not take negative feedback well. Claimed to care but never listened to students actual concern.

She is kind and caring but serious and tough. I liked the course a lot because it will help me with my internship but mostly I liked her since she was helpful and always answered my questions and emails. Lots of homework and projects but not too bad. -

The course itself isn't too difficult, but Nooshin unfortunately was unimpressive. Instruction guidelines were extremely unclear and that would end up hurting your grade on assignments. I could tell that she cared about her students and I really wanted to like her and this course, but the lack of preparedness and unclarity made it difficult.
Dr. Weber is a knowledgeable instructor and gives a lot of opportunity for extra credit. He may seem a little strict, but at least you will actually learn from his class.
Dr. Weber is a great professor and knows what he's talking about. The class might be boring if you have no interest in computers, but overall a great class and instructor.

Weber is sort of nice but acts like we're children and he takes forever to get through slides that don't cover any important content (almost all the content needed for tests is in video lectures). He does provide a good amount of drops & extra credit.
A bit smug and can not think that his assignments are useful for students. Stop using MyLab

This is my first time ever I wanted to speak on RateMyProfessor. The problem of this class is the 3 exams are nothing like what you did for assignments and exam review questions. There are only very few questions that were in the review. The End of Course Survey ends before the last exam so I can't even provide the feedback.

This class uses software for some assignments that multiple students in the past have had issues with, so why still use it? Did an assignment where all students received a 100% with or without completion, and I was not granted extra credit or excused from a future assignment, which was unfair. Refused to answer a question I had.
Every negative review is correct, but in addition to that, "Assignment 2: Advanced MS Excel Simulation" is genuinely the worst assignment I have ever been forced to complete in my entire life.

The worst professor I have ever had the displeasure of taking a class from. He is super slow to present his couple of slides AND we have to watch playposit lectures on top of that. Theres also many in class assignments that he says we should ask questions about, but he is SO rude when you do ask a question. Very inaccessible in and out of class!
Yes, he presents slowly. But honestly great material, super organized instructor, and friendly guy. Good class all around

This class is one of the biggest jokes. It it takes him a million years to get through basic slides, we get it let's move on already. We don't learn anything during the "lectures" for the exam. We have to watch hour long lectures outside of class despite it being an IN-PERSON class. Pointless case comp to stroke his ego. Did seem nice though

I can't remember how many hours of my life I have wasted on something as useless as this class. He takes 75 minutes to explain 10 slides, most of which have little to do with the material needed for exams. We are required to watch playposit lectures OUTSIDE of class time because clearly he doesn't have a clue how to do his job. nice guy but bruh
Class was super easy(im an mis major but still had to take it). He had a good class structure.

Not a very caring professor. Lots of video lectures, tests, assignments. Creates online space according to how HE thinks it would work best but not actually what works for the students and he could care less.

Weber set student up for success to learn R from the ground up. I have no coding experience, but was confident in the assignments due to the comparable lectures/examples. Lectures have some light humor. The group project was the most difficult part of the class since there was no single "right way" to do it.

Weber is fine. His assignments are rather long and tedious. Grading is generally detailed, exams are heavily weighted on memorization. The material is quite easy though and he puts many of the same questions in the review so as long as you do the review you'll do fine

Tbh he wasn't a bad prof, his lectures were a little boring. An easy A tho, since he offered 90 EC points (in addition to curving exam and case comp scores). He's very kind and caring, the exams were just hard to study for

Incredibly easy class. So much extra credit offered + every test was curved by at least 10 points. I got an A without doing the case comp OR taking the final (only one gets dropped). Multiple drops for assignments, HW, etc. For tests, review playposit vids (most important) and 2x speed through lecture vids. Boring lectures, rarely paid attention.

The exams in this class pull content from WAY too many different sources to keep up with for studying. You've got to worry about his in-class lectures, his video lectures, stuff from homework assignments, AND the textbook. Each source I just named has original stuff from it that may be featured in an exam. Too much.

This class is a joke. The tests are on things he doesnt even teach in person. There is 0 point to the in person sessions but he takes attendance so you cant miss. Its all on random topics each week that I'll never use again in my life unless I want to make students miserable by being a MIS professor too.

One of the worst teachers I've ever taken. He doesn't teach anything during class, instead he talks about stuff that is not relevant to the exams. Was very difficult when trying to figure out our groups for in class activities. Unfortunate we have to have him for a mandatory class. I really disliked his teaching style and his class.

Professor Weber is a great guy. He was willing to answer every reasonable question I had. My only suggestion is that the cheat sheet for the final should be allowed to be typed. It seems a bit counterintuitive but maybe I'll frame it alongside my degree one day. RStudio is another great skill to add to the tool belt in MIS.

David is actually one of the worst professors I have had at the University of Arizona. Whether it is the fact he teaches you like a child or that he never refers to what will be on exams in class and rather makes you watch 2 hour lecture videos in your free time. He prefers to use class to yap about irrelevant topics nothing to do w MIS. Fire him.

One of the worst professors I've ever had 
Attendance is mandatory and nothing on tests is discussed in class any concept tested on has to be learnt out of class 
No technology allowed and TA' and David would call people out in class for checking their phones or having laptops open 
Would avoid at all costs honestly he needs to be let go

David means well but I became very easily frustrated with the homework assignments because they use old software. During lecture, he does not teach subjects as he assigns us video lectures to watch at home. Although, David does allow drops for a decent amount of assignments and provides extra credit for each one turned in early.

Easily the worst professor I have ever had. He doesn't allow technology in the class. THE CLASS IS MANAGEMENT INFORMATION SYSTEMS. Makes zero sense to me. He doesn't actually teach MIS topics in class. Instead he makes all the lectures homework online  and expects you to understand everything he talks about. TRY AND AVOID HIM! Take ONLINE!!!!!!!!!!

Worse professor ever, he talks about nothing relates to content and test in class but will assign videos that can last up to an hour long for you to watch at home. Assignments are crazy long with applied skills that you won't ever need again in your life.

Worst professor I've had in a while. The lectures are pointless and don't relate to the tests or content, however, he makes you watch hour-long videos at home to learn. He's a nice guy, but he wastes a lot of time trying to come across as nice and fun for a college professor in a huge lecture hall.

This class is extremely difficult. The tests are terrible and I don't even know where he came up with those questions. He doesn't teach at all in class and instead rambles about the most unnecessary things. He makes your watch long 1+ hour long videos that has content. Don't understand why he wouldn't just teach it in class? Homework takes forever.

Worst professor, class, and TAs ever. Never taught, learned nothing, and grading is irritatingly nitpicky. Case Comp was a MESS and he j lets the rando stranger judges dictate ur grade. Got an A because of EC, but still, wish I had learned ANYTHING. Class meets once, but everyone wouldve preferred two days just to have him actually teach something.

Weber is without a doubt the worst professor I've ever had in all of the departments I've taken classes from. He relies solely on his TAs, making them teach content unrelated to exams. He refuses to engage with the students, coming across as callous and uncaring. He will take constructive feedback and get super defensive. Just see it through.

He is a nice person, but I did not like the structure of this class at all. He relied very heavily on the TA's to only do chapter overviews, which was not beneficial because the material was very complex. The exams were extremely difficult, but the assignments were pretty guided.

Nice guy overall but you don't learn anything from in-person lectures. All learning is done outside of class using the textbook or long, boring recorded lectures from previous years. Assignments are tedious and don't teach much because all you do is follow very very strict procedures.

Weber means well, but he relies so much on his TA's. You don't learn anything from the lectures in class and the homework is always confusing bc of this. The exams are additionally really hard. Good guy, fine class, just don't expect to get a good grade

Unfortunately this class is not optional. Weber is arguably the worst professor I've had at U of A. Not only are his tests impossible but he doesn't teach. His lectures are two slides long that he makes his TA's teach, and then the tests are on literally anything in the textbook. Also good luck to Fall 24, he wants to implement a no computers rule!

If you're a good student, your non-A grades will come from either Weber or Currim in the MISGA program - you're actually more likely to get below an A in Weber's courses. The final exam was so bad last year that it collectively dropped everyone by a letter grade. I recommend typing your cheat sheet to save you from handwriting it.

Professor Weber is a good teacher and is very accessible/cares about teaching. I would recommend him overall. I was caught off guard by the exams, which are disproportionately more difficult than work in class/HW. That being said there is a ridiculous amount of points so you can afford to do mediocre on exams and get an A.

I was excited to take this class to learn how to code. His lectures were great and I learned a ton, but got stuck in a group that did absolutely nothing for the group project. He said he would grade fairly on contribution, but I did not feel he did. He gave no feedback on the final and this ended up being the only B I've gotten in the program

Labs and quizzes are deceptivily easy as is the class until the final. You can use one page of hand written notes, check that again, yes hand written code for that matter. Final covers maybe 1/3 of learned material and given a strict time limit to code. Group project and final are worth 380 points out of 700. Group project lacks data guidelines.

The first professor I have ever rated on this website, because he is just AWESOME! I had taken several machine learning courses before this course, and MIS545 is actually the best one for beginners. He made comprehensive lectures and labs that would inspire you. The level of difficulty stays at a mild balance between too hard or too easy.

Dr. Weber is amazing! Stern, kind, respectful. He is strict on attendance, timeliness, and academic integrity. We only had class once a week. He posted short lecture videos and example problems for the homework. His D2L was so organized and he made his expectations and grading crystal clear. I learned a lot. Dr. Weber is the best! I recommend him.
A great and fascinating professor, but be prepared to work, and to write out a lot of calculus. Make sure to ask questions about how things work, as he teaches primarily in equations.

This guy didn't look prepared for class. Usually released the class early since he had nothing to talk about at the end.  Very unclear in his explanations. Luckily I took some classes before. I  almost learned nothing new in his class. A guy even walked out of the classroom in the middle of his lecture.
Attend all classes, she is very helpful in office hours, reading the textbook helps, need to know and understand derivatives.

Professor Xiao is intelligent and communicated the concepts of the course clearly and concisely. The class had two midterms and a final presentation, alongside several problem sets throughout the year. I highly recommend taking this class if you're at all interested in antitrust economics and industrial organization.
I'm a C student, and i recieved an A. She is the kindest, and best Professor!!!
Pro.Xiao is perfect, but her TA is not that good

very difficult class. a lot of mathematical concepts. Mo has a very awkward teaching style where no concepts are taught logically. you just end up copying huge equations down and hoping they don't show up on test. There are three midterms (one is dropped), and 6-7 quizzes. She has a heavy accent and is very difficult to comprehend in class.

Relatively hard class with some tough concepts.  Prof likes to use math/calc a lot so don't take this class if you suck at math.  Only the top midterm, final, and top 4 pop quizzes count for your grade.  Virtually no homework.
Jeff IS the man. Would highly recommend Jeff as he truly cares about his students and has a wonderful sense of humor!

Just study the slides (effectively) and complete in-class practice problems before the exams. Also, attend all your classes on time, as participation is important. Finish your assignments before the due dates, and you should be good to go. This capstone was not designed to be difficult, but make sure you understand what's going on. Great guy.

I was in his 400B but it doesn't allow an option for that. The TA review sessions were an extreme help for overall confusion. Additionally, I wish his office hours were more than 30 minutes but, even then I am able to get most of my questions answered. The exam format is excellent as it is extremely similar to in-class exercises and quizzes.

There are many words that come to mind when I think of the Jeff (Jiewei) Yu. Funny, brave, intelligent, confident. He is the embodiment of the man I wish to be. I look at Jeff and I just know that he can destroy any problem that life throws his way. God bless his soul and may he reign supreme.

Why does this guy have bad reviews? Jeff is the man! He was so fair and nice, and really cared. He encouraged your learning and success. In most classes I find that I succeed in spite of the instructor; Jeff was the key driver for most of the class doing exceptionally well! Take Jeff, you won't regret it! Top accounting instructor at Eller for sure

Jeff is one of the kindest most understanding professors I have met. Jeff easily became my favorite undergrad professor. He expects you to work as you should in a upper level accounting class. Do the work and come to class and you will ace his class. He also will happily teach you about real world application beyond the class content.

This guy is condescending but grades based on participation. Why would students volunteer in class if he is going to shoot down your answer rudely? He also has a group assignment worth a major part of your grade so get ready to depend on random people for a good portion of your grade. I wish I could have taken another professor.

One of the most confusing professors at Eller, he doesn't feel confident about the material plus he is very disorganized, he only copies answers from his answer sheet to the board and let student copy it, not even bothering himself and posting it on Blackboard, he should NOT be teaching a capstone class,  he has no idea about the assignment

David is new to teaching FIN360, so the semester started out very hard. But, David was very adaptable and ended up removing short answer questions and providing many extra credit opportunities. As long as you understand the core concepts it is not bad, definitely go to office hours at the beginning if you are confused because material builds off!

This was his first time teaching this course so I will give him so grace. I like the set up of the class as it is 5 exams and 10 homeworks so it doesn't require that much time besides studying for exams. There is also 30 extra credit points. His lectures are slightly unorganized but most of the exams are similar to practice problems

great guy but seems to still be getting the hang of handling a large capacity undergraduate course. I think the class will continue to get better as he makes it his own. Lectures felt put together in a very odd and confusing way, seemingly overcomplicating concepts. If you get maths and fundamental finance theory the class is a walk in the park.

Feel grossly unprepared for completing homework assignments and tests. The lectures are impossible to follow and quite frankly inadequate at providing information necessary to complete assignments. Rarely have I felt the need to write a review of a professor, but no one knows what is going on and it shouldn't be this difficult.

The material is very elementary and easy to grasp for anyone in upper division, but holy crap are the lectures long. Spent 3 hours talking about simple interest, compounding interest, and annuities. Lectures are painful to watch because it feels like someone is constantly dangling keys in front of your face, so to speak.

This professor is not qualified to be teaching finance. Most of the lectures were spent on personal stories about his "partner" rather than course material. If you don't have a fast computer or a Mac, you're at a big disadvantage for the Ex Prep assignments; the professor won't compromise on this.
